He gave up his beautiful wife, nice children, great friends and an enormous empire. Although these things were very difficult to give up, Mahārāja Bharata was so exalted that he gave them up just as one gives up stool after evacuating. Such was the greatness of His Majesty. SB 5.14.43

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha māna-śūnyatā—
utkṛṣṭatve ’py amānitvaṃ kathitā māna-śūnyatā||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Pridelessness is defined as follows:

Pridelessness means “in spite of having a high position, remaining humble.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā pādme—
harau ratiṃ vahann eṣa narendrāṇāṃ śikhā-maṇiḥ |
bhikṣām aṭann ari-pure śvapākam api vandate ||33||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Pridelessness is illustrated in the Padma Purāṇa:

King Bhagīratha, though the crest jewel among kings, went out begging at the house of his enemies, and offered respects to the dog-eaters, because he had rati for the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha āśā-bandhaḥ—
āśā-bandho bhagavataḥ prāpti-sambhāvanā dṛḍhā ||34||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Confidence is defined as follows:

Confidence means “firm assumption that one will attain the Lord.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrīmat-prabhupādānāṃ—
na premā śravaṇādi-bhaktir api vā yogo ’thavā vaiṣṇavo
jñānaṃ vā śubha-karma vā kiyad aho saj-jātir apy asti vā |
hīnārthādhika-sādhake tvayi tathāpy acchedya-mūlā satī
he gopī-jana-vallabha vyathayate hā hā mad-āśaiva mām ||35||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of confidence is the following statement of Sanātana Gosvāmī:

I do not have prema or the practices of hearing or chanting in bhakti. I have no practice of meditation of Viṣṇu in the aṣtāṅga-yoga process, nor do I have practices of jñāna or varṇāśrama duties. I do not even have good birth to execute these actions properly. But since you are most merciful to the least qualified, O dear lover of the gopīs, though I have impure desires, my aspiration for You continues to agitate me.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha samutkaṇṭhā—
samutkaṇṭhā nijābhīṣṭa-lābhāya guru-lubdhatā ||36||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Longing is now defined:

Longing means “having intense greed for attaining service to the Lord.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛte (54)—
ānamrām asita-bhruvor upacitam akṣīṇa-pakṣmāṅkureṣv
ālolām anurāgiṇor nayanayor ārdrāṃ mṛdau jalpite |
ātāmrām adharāmṛte mada-kalām amlāna vaṃśī-svaneṣv
āśāste mama locanaṃ vraja-śiśor-mūrtiṃ jagan-mohinīm ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of longing for the Lord is given in Kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛta (54):

I long to see that young Kṛṣṇa who enchants the universe with His bent, black eye brows, with His thick eye lashes, with His attractive, shifty eyes, with His soft words which melt the heart, with his sweet, red lips, and with the intoxicating sound emanating from the clear notes of His flute.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha nāma-gāne sadā ruciḥ, yathā—
rodana-bindu-maranda-syandi-dṛg-indīvarādya govinda |
tava madhura-svara-kaṇṭhī gāyati nāmāvalīṃ bālā ||38||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Taste for chanting the name of the Lord is illustrated:

O Govinda! Today, the young girl with a sweet voice, whose lotus eyes are streaming with tears of honey, is singing Your names.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tad-guṇākhyāne āsāktiḥ, yathā kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛte—
mādhuryād api madhuraṃ
manmathatā tasya kim api kaiśoram |
capalyād api capalaṃ
ceto bata harati hanta kiṃ kurmaḥ ||39||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Attachment to discussing about the Lord’s qualities is described in the Kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛta (88):

That youthful Kṛṣṇa, with the qualities of Cupid, who is extremely sweet and extremely fickle, has stolen my heart. What should I do?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tad vasati-sthale prītiḥ, yathā padyāvalyām—
atrāsīt kila nanda-sadma śakaṭasyātrābhavad bhañjanaṃ
bandha-ccheda-karo ’pi dāmabhir abhūd baddho ’tra dāmodaraḥ |
itthaṃ māthura-vṛddha-vaktra-vigalat-pīyūṣa-dhārāṃ pibann
ānandāśru-dharaḥ kadā madhu-purīṃ dhanyaś cariṣyāmy aham
||40||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of attachment for the abode of the Lord is found in the Padyāvalī:

“Nanda’s house was here. This is where Kṛṣṇa broke the cart. Here is where Dāmodara, who cuts material bondage, was bound up by ropes.” When will I be fortunate enough to wander about in Mathurā with tears in my eyes, drinking such streams of nectar flowing from the mouth of an elder of Mathurā? Padyāvalī 121

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
api ca—
vyaktaṃ masṛṇitevāntar lakṣyate rati-lakṣaṇam |
mumukṣu-prabhṛtīnāṃ ced bhaved eṣā ratir na hi ||41||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
However, it should be said:

If softness of the heart, the symptom of rati, becomes clearly visible in persons desiring liberation, or in other unqualified persons, it is not real rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vimuktākhila-tarṣair yā muktair api vimṛgyate |
yā kṛṣṇenātigopyāśu bhajadbhyo ’pi na dīyate ||42||
sā bhukti-mukti-kāmatvāc chuddhāṃ bhaktim akurvatām |
hṛdaye sambhavaty eṣāṃ kathaṃ bhāgavatī ratiḥ ||43||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: How can rati appear in persons having desires for enjoyment or liberation? Those persons do not perform pure bhakti. Rati is sought out by those liberated from all desires and is not given immediately by Kṛṣṇa even to the devotees, since it is most secret.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kintu bāla-camatkāra-karī tac-cihna-vīkṣayā |
abhijñena subodho ’yaṃ raty-ābhāsaḥ prakīrtitaḥ ||44||
pratibimbas tathā cchāyā raty-ābhāso dvidhā mataḥ ||45||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though this semblance of rati is very astounding to the innocent, those in knowledge understand what it really is by seeing the characteristics. This is called raty-ābhāsa, a semblance of rati. This semblance of rati has two types: reflection (pratibimba) and splendor (chāyā).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra pratibimbaḥ—
aśramābhīṣṭa-nirvāhī rati-lakṣaṇa-lakṣitaḥ |
bhogāpavarga-saukhyāṃśa-vyañjakaḥ pratibimbakaḥ ||46||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Pratibimba or reflection is described as follows:

When there are apparent qualities of rati, accompanied by desires for enjoyment and liberation, it is called the pratibimba (reflection) raty-ābhāsa. This pratibimba raty-ābhāsa awards the goals of enjoyment and liberation to those persons without their having to exert effort.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
daivāt sad-bhakta-saṅgena kīrtanādy-anusāriṇām |
prāyaḥ prasanna-manasāṃ bhoga-mokṣādi rāgiṇām ||47||
keṣāṃcit hṛdi bhāvendoḥ pratibimba udañcati |
tad-bhakta-hṛn-nabhaḥ-sthasya tat-saṃsarga-prabhāvataḥ ||48||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This reflection of the moon of bhāva appears in the hearts of some persons who are attached to enjoyment or liberation, but who become somewhat satisfied by following the aṅgas of bhakti through occasional association with real devotees. That moon of bhāva is situated in the sky of the real devotee’s heart, and it appears as a reflection in the non-devotee for some time by its impressions.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha chāyā—
kṣudra-kautūhala-mayī cañcalā duḥkha-hāriṇī |
rateś chāyā bhavet kiṃcit tat-sādṛśyāvalambinī ||49||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The splendorous type of semblance of bhāva is now described:

That which has some similarity to real rati, which possesses a small amount of interest in the Lord, which is unstable, and which destroys suffering, is called chāyā-raty-ābhāsa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
hari-priya-kriyā-kāla-deśa-pātrādi-saṅgamāt |
apy ānuṣaṅgikād eṣa kvacid ajñeṣv apīkṣyate ||50||
kintu bhāgyaṃ vinā nāsau bhāva-cchāyāpy udañcati |
yad abhyudayataḥ kṣemaṃ tatra syād uttarottaram ||51||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This chāyā-raty-ābhāsa appears sometimes even in ignorant people by a combination of performing actions dear to the Lord, observing the festivals of the Lord, residing in the dhāma of the Lord, and associating with the devotees of the Lord. Even this chāyā-raty-ābhāsa, which eventually bestows auspiciousness to those people, appears only with great good fortune.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
hari-priya-janasyaiva prasāda-bhara-lābhataḥ |
bhāvābhāso ’pi sahasā bhāvatvam upagacchati ||52||
tasminn evāparādhena bhāvābhāso ’py anuttamaḥ |
krameṇa kṣayam āpnoti kha-sthaḥ pūrṇa-śaśī yathā ||53||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: By the great mercy of a dear devotee of the Lord, the semblance of bhāva suddenly becomes real bhāva. By offending that devotee, even the best bhāvābhāsa gradually wanes just as the full moon in the sky gradually wanes.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kiṃ ca—
bhāvo ’py abhāvam āyāti kṛṣṇa-preṣṭhāparādhataḥ |
ābhāsatāṃ ca śanakair nyūna-jātīyatām api ||54||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Moreover, it should be stated:

By an offense against the dearest devotee of the Lord, even real bhāva will be destroyed, if the offense is grave. If the offense is medium, the bhāva will turn to bhāvābhāsa. If the offense is slight, the bhāva will become an inferior type.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
gāḍhāsaṅgāt sadāyāti mumukṣau supratiṣṭhite |
ābhāsatām asau kiṃ vā bhajanīyeśa-bhāvatām ||55||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: By intimate association with a person who strongly desires impersonal liberation, real bhāva becomes bhāvābhāsa, or becomes worship of the self as the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ata eva kvacit teṣu navya-bhakteṣu dṛśyate |
kṣaṇam īśvara-bhāvo ’yaṃ nṛtyādau mukti-pakṣagaḥ ||56||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: It is seen sometimes that new devotees, absorbed in the goals of different types of liberation, identify themselves with the Lord momentarily, during dancing or other acts of devotion.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sādhanekṣāṃ vinā yasminn akasmād bhāva īkṣyate |
vighna-sthagitam atrohyaṃ prāg-bhavīyaṃ susādhanaṃ ||57||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Bhāva sometimes appears suddenly in a person without performance of sādhana or mercy, and without scriptural knowledge. It should be inferred that, in a previous life, some obstacle interrupted that person’s skillful sādhana, and in this life the obstacle has finally been removed.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
lokottara-camatkāra-kārakaḥ sarva-śaktidaḥ |
yaḥ prathīyān bhaved bhāvaḥ sa tu kṛṣṇa-prasādajaḥ ||58||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That bhāva which is more amazing than anything in this world, which gives all powers, and which is very deep, is caused by the mercy of Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
jane cej jāta-bhāve ’pi vaiguṇyam iva dṛśyate |
kāryā tathāpi nāsūyā kṛtārthaḥ sarvathaiva saḥ||59||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If some apparent fault is seen in a person who has developed real bhāva, one should not be hostile to him, because he has accomplished the goal in all respects.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā nārasiṃhe—
bhagavati ca harāv ananya-cetā
bhṛśa-malino ’pi virājate manuṣyaḥ |
na hi śaśa-kaluṣa-cchaviḥ kadācit
timira-parābhavatām upaiti candraḥ ||60||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Thus, it is said in the Narasiṃha Purāṇa:

A person who is dedicated completely to the Lord may show, externally, serious contamination (but internally he is pure). The full moon, though marked by the figure of a rabbit, is never overcome by darkness.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ratir aniśa-nisargoṣṇa-prabalatarānanda-pūra-rūpaiva |
uṣmāṇam api vamantī sudhāṃśu-koṭer api svādvī ||61||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Rati is naturally and eternally unstable because of its continuous, ever-increasing desires for pleasing the Lord and it is full of bliss. Manifesting this instability in the form of a variety of sañcāri-bhāvas, it is tastier than millions of moons.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
samyaṅ-masṛṇita-svānto mamatvātiśayāṅkitaḥ |
bhāvaḥ sa eva sāndrātmā budhaiḥ premā nigadyate ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When bhāva becomes extremely condensed, it is called prema by the learned. It softens the heart completely and produces extreme possessiveness of the Lord in the experiencer.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā pañcarātre—
ananya-mamatā viṣṇau mamatā prema saṅgatā |
bhaktir ity ucyate bhīṣma-prahlādoddhava-nāradaiḥ ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
In the Pañcarātra, it is said:

Prema is defined by Bhīṣma, Prahlāda, Uddhava and Nārada as that bhāva which has possessiveness related to Viṣṇu (or any other form of the Lord) and to no one else.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhaktiḥ premocyate bhīṣma-mukhyair yatra tu saṅgatā |
mamatānya-mamatvena varjitety atra yojanā ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The analysis of the grammar of the above verse is as follows: Bhāva is called prema by Bhīṣma and others where there is possessiveness (mamatā) related to (saṅgatā) to Viṣṇu and where possessiveness of other things (ananya-mamatā) is absent.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: bhāvottho ’ti-prasādotthaḥ śrī-harer iti sa dvidhā ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This prema for the Lord has two types: that arising from bhāva and that arising from mercy.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra bhāvotthaḥ—
bhāva evāntar-aṅgāṇam aṅgānām anusevayā |
ārūḍhaḥ parama-utkarṣam bhāva-uttaḥ parikīrtitaḥ ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Prema arising from bhāva is explained:

That bhāva which reaches the highest excellence by continual service using all essential aṅgas[151] is called “prema arising from bhāva.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra vaidha-bhāvottho, yathā ekādaśe—
evaṃ-vrataḥ sva-priya-nāma-kīrtyā
jātānurāgo druta-citta uccaiḥ |
hasaty atho roditi rauti gāyaty
unmāda-van nṛtyati loka-bāhyaḥ ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of prema arising from vaidha-bhāva is supplied from the Eleventh Canto:

Having performed his vows according to vaidhi-bhakti, chanting the holy name of his beloved Lord, he develops great attachment. As his heart melts with ecstatic love, he laughs very loudly, cries, or shouts. Sometimes he sings and dances like a madman, for he is indifferent to public opinion. SB 11.2.40

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rāgānugīya-bhāvottho, yathā pādme—
na patiṃ kāmayet kañcid brahmacarya-sthitā sadā |
tam eva mūrtiṃ dhyāyantī candrakāntir varānanā ||7||
śrī-kṛṣṇa-gāthāṃ gāyantī romāṇcodbheda-lakṣaṇā |
asmin-manvantare snigdhā śrī-kṛṣṇa-priya-vartayā ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Prema arising from bhāva, based on rāgānuga-sādhana, is described in the Padma Purāṇa:

In this manvantara period, the beautiful-faced Candrakānti observed continuous celibacy, and continuously meditated only on the form of Kṛṣṇa, thinking, “One should not desire anyone else as a husband.” She sang songs about Him with hairs standing on end. She developed complete affection for Kṛṣṇa by hearing stories about Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha harer atiprasādotthaḥ—
harer atiprasādo ’yaṃ saṅga-dānādir ātmanaḥ ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Prema arising from the great mercy of the Lord is explained:

The great mercy of the Lord includes such things as the Lord giving His association to the devotee.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā ekādaśe —
te nādhīta-śruti-gaṇā nopāsita-mahattamāḥ |
avratātapta-tapasaḥ mat-saṅgān mām upāgatāḥ ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
In the Eleventh Canto, prema arising from the Lord’s mercy (in this case, association) is illustrated as follows:

The persons I have mentioned did not undergo serious studies of the Vedic literature, nor did they worship great saintly persons, nor did they execute severe vows or austerities. Simply by association with My devotees and Me, they achieved Me. SB 11.12.7

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: māhātmya-jñāna-yuktaś ca kevalaś ceti sa dvidhā ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Prema arising from the Lord’s mercy has two types: that endowed with knowledge of the Lord’s powers, and that endowed with only knowledge of the Lord’s sweetness.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha ādyo, yathā pañcarātre—
māhātmya-jñāna-yuktas tu sudṛḍhaḥ sarvato ’dhikaḥ |
sneho bhaktir iti proktas tayā sārṣṭyādinānyathā ||12||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Prema endowed with knowledge of the power of the Lord, arising from the Lord’s mercy, is described in the Pañcarātra:

Affection for the Lord more than anyone else, which is very firm, and includes knowledge of the Lord’s powers, is called bhakti. By that bhakti, a person attains powers in the spiritual world as well as other rewards. There is no other way of attaining such things.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kevalo, yathā tatraiva—
mano-gatir avicchinnā harau prema-pariplutā |
abhisandhi-vinirmuktā bhaktir viṣṇu-vaśaṅkarī || iti | ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Prema with knowledge of the Lord’s sweetness, arising from the Lord’s mercy, is described as follows in the Pañcarātra:

That bhakti inundated with prema,[155] with continuous, spontaneous desires to please the Lord free of desires for other results (even the desire to see His powers), brings even Viṣṇu under control.[156]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mahima-jñāna-yuktaḥ syād vidhi-mārgānusāriṇām |
rāgānugāśritānāṃ tu prāyaśaḥ kevalo bhavet ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those who have practiced vaidhi-bhakti (and then get the great mercy of the Lord) attain prema with knowledge of the Lord’s powers. Those who have practiced rāgānuga-bhakti (and then get the great mercy of the Lord) usually attain prema with knowledge of the Lord’s sweetness.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ādau śraddhā tataḥ sādhusaṅgo ’tha bhajanakriyā |
tato ’narthanivṛttiḥ syāt tato niṣṭhā rucis tataḥ ||15||
tathāsaktis tato bhāvas tataḥ premābhyudañcati |
sādhakānām ayaṃ premṇaḥ prādurbhāvaḥ bhavet kramaḥ ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: First, there is association with devotees, by which one gains faith. Then, one associates with the devotees to learn the scriptures, and then practices bhakti. The anarthas are then destroyed. Steady bhakti without confusion follows. Desire for the Lord (ruci) appears. This is followed by spontaneous desire for the Lord (āsakti). This becomes bhāva and then prema. This is the progression for manifesting prema for those performing sādhana-bhakti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dhanyasyāyaṃ navaḥ premā yasyonmīlati cetasi |
antarvāṇībhir apy asya mudrā suṣṭhu sudurgamā ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This ever fresh prema arises in the heart of a person who is very fortunate. That person’s behavior is very difficult to comprehend even for those knowledgeable of scriptures.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ata eva śrī-nārada-pañcarātre, yathā—
bhāvonmatto hareḥ kiñcin na veda sukham ātmanaḥ |
dukhaṃ ceti maheśāni paramānanda āplutaḥ ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Therefore, in Nārada-pañcarātra it is said:

O goddess Pārvatī, the person who is mad with love of the Lord, being absorbed in the highest bliss, does not know at all his own happiness or distress.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
premṇa eva vilāsatvād vairalyāt sādhakeṣv api |
atra snehādayo bhedā vivicya na hi śaṃsitāḥ ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Sneha and other advanced stages are the manifestations of prema, but because they are rare, even in those who have practiced bhakti, the distinctions will not be described here.[158]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śrīmat-prabhupadāmbhojaiḥ sarvā bhāgavatāmṛte |
vyaktīkṛtāsti gūḍhāpi bhakti-siddhānta-mādhurī ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Śrī Sanātana Gosvāmī, my master, has clearly described all the sweetness of the conclusions of bhakti in Bṛhad-bhāgavatāmṛta, though it is very esoteric.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
gopāla-rūpa-śobhāṃ dadhad api raghunātha-bhāva-vistārī |
tuṣyatu sanātanātmā prathama-vibhāge sudhāmbu-nidheḥ ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
May the eternal Lord in the beautiful form of a cowherd boy, who distributes his mood of love to Rāma and other forms, be pleased with this first part of the Bhakti-rasāmṛta-sindhu.

Second Meaning:

May the person named Sanātana Gosvāmī, who glorified Gopāla Bhaṭṭa Gosvāmī and Rūpa Gosvāmī and bestowed kṛṣṇa-prema to Raghunātha dāsa Gosvāmī, be pleased with this first section of the ocean of nectar!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prabalam ananya-śrayiṇā niṣevitaḥ sahaja-rūpeṇa |
agha-damano mathurāyāṃ sadā sanātana-tanur jayati ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
May the eternal form of Kṛṣṇa, killer of Aghāsura, served strongly by His natural beauty, which is attractive even without ornaments, and which is non-different from His very self, remain with all attractive features eternally in the district of Mathurā.

Second Meaning:

May Sanātana Gosvāmī, who conquers all sins and is served with devotion by his younger brother Rūpa, who has taken shelter of him alone, remain eternally in Mathurā district.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rasāmṛtābdher bhāge ’smin dvitīye dakṣiṇābhidhe |
sāmānya-bhagavad-bhakti-rasas tāvad udīryate ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The second part of the sweet ocean of rasa is called the Southern Section. It describes the general rasas within devotion to the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asya pañca-laharyaḥ syur vibhāvākhyāgrimā matā |
dvitīyā tv anubhāvākhyā tṛtīyā sāttvikābhidhā |
vyabhicāry-abhidhā turyā sthāyi-saṃjñā ca pañcamī ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are five waves or chapters in this Southern Section of the ocean. The first deals with vibhāva; the second with anubhāva; the third with sāttvika-bhāva; the fourth with vyabhicāri-bhāva; and the fifth with sthāyi-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
athāsyāḥ keśava-rater lakṣitāyā nigadyate |
sāmagrī-paripoṣeṇa paramā rasa-rūpatā ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The Southern Section describes how rati (bhāva) for the Lord (sthāyi-bhāva), which has been described previously, takes on the form of the highest rasa through nourishment by the ingredients of vibhāva, anubhāva, sāttvika-bhāva and vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vibhāvair anubhāvaiś ca sāttvikair vyabhicāribhiḥ |
svādyatvaṃ hṛdi bhaktānām ānītā śravaṇādibhiḥ |
eṣā kṛṣṇa-ratiḥ sthāyi-bhāvo bhakti-raso bhavet ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This rati for Kṛṣṇa, called the sthāyi-bhāva, takes on a pleasurable nature in the hearts of the devotees by the vibhāvas, anubhāvas, sāttvika-bhāvas and vyabhicāri-bhāvas, through activities such as hearing, and then becomes bhakti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prāktany ādhunikī cāsti yasya sad-bhakti-vāsanā |
eṣa bhakti-rasāsvādas tasyaiva hṛdi jāyate ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The taste for bhakti-rasa arises in the heart of a person who has had experiences of pure bhakti in previous and present life.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhakti-nirdhūta-doṣāṇāṃ prasannojjvala-cetasām |
śrī-bhāgavata-raktānāṃ rasikāsaṅga-raṅgiṇām ||7||
jīvanī-bhūta-govinda-pāda-bhakti-sukha-śriyām |
premāntaraṅga-bhūtāni kṛtyāny evānutiṣṭhatām ||8||
bhaktānāṃ hṛdi rājantī saṃskāra-yugalojjvalā |
ratir ānanda-rūpaiva nīyamānā tu rasyatām ||9||
kṛṣṇādibhir vibhāvādyair gatair anubhavādhvani |
prauḍhānanda-camatkāra-kāṣṭhām āpadyate parām ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Rati, which is the very form of ānanda, appears in the hearts of devotees who have been purified of all faults by bhakti, whose hearts have become joyful (hlādinī) and bright (saṃvit), who have developed great relish for the Bhāgavatam and for the association of others who have taste for Kṛṣṇa, whose life and soul becomes the profound happiness of devotion at the feet of Govinda, and who have become absorbed in actions such as kīrtana, imbued with the Lord’s mercy. This rati, strengthened by past and present life impressions of bhakti, then attains a state of relish by realizing vibhāva, anubhāva, sāttvika-bhāva and vyabhicāri-bhāva in relation to Kṛṣṇa, and finally attains the highest, astonishing peak of profound bliss.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kintu premā vibhāvādyaiḥ svalpair nīto ’py aṇīyasīm |
vibhāvanādy-avasthāṃ tu sadya āsvādyatāṃ vrajet ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: However, prema, even becoming slightly pleasurable by a slight mixture of vibhāva and the other ingredients, quickly becomes fully tasteful.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra vibhāvādi-sāmānya-lakṣaṇam –
ye kṛṣṇa-bhakta-muralī-nādādyā hetavo rateḥ |
kārya-bhūtāḥ smitādyāś ca tathāṣṭau stabdhatādayaḥ ||12||
nirvedādyāḥ sahāyāś ca te jñeyā rasa-bhāvane |
vibhāvā anubhāvāś ca sāttvikā vyabhicāriṇaḥ ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The general characteristics of the ingredients are as follows.

In tasting rasa, the causes of rati such as Kṛṣṇa, His devotee, and the sound of the flute are called vibhāvas. The effects of rati, such as smiling are called anubhāvas and the eight symptoms such as being stunned are called sāttvika-bhāvas. The assistants such as self-criticism are called vyabhicāri-bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra vibhāva –
tatra jñeyā vibhāvās tu raty-āsvādana-hetavaḥ |
te dvidhālambanā eke tathaivoddīpanāḥ pare ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In rasa, the vibhāvas should be known as the causes of relishing rati. They take the form of support (ālambana) of two types and stimuli (uddīpana).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tad uktam agni-purāṇe (alaṅkāra section, 3.35) –
vibhāvyate hi raty-ādir yatra yena vibhāvyate |
vibhāvo nāma sa dvedhālambanoddīpanātmakaḥ ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
This is mentioned in the Agni Purāṇa (Alaṅkāra section, 3.35):

Vibhāva refers to the two types of ālambana--the person in relation to whom the rati and other elements are experienced (viṣaya), the person in whom the rati and other elements are experienced (āśraya) and to uddīpana, the stimuli by which rati is experienced.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ālambanāḥ –
kṛṣṇaś ca kṛṣṇa-bhaktāś ca budhair ālambanā matāḥ |
raty-āder viṣayatvena tathādhāratayāpi ca ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Ālambanas are described as follows:

The wise consider the ālambanas to be Kṛṣṇa, as the object of love experienced in rati, and His devotees, as the experiencer (subject) of rati (the five major and seven secondary sthāyi-bhāvas).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra śrī-kṛṣṇaḥ –
nāyakānāṃ śiro-ratnaṃ kṛṣṇas tu bhagavān svayam |
yatra nityatayā sarve virājante mahā-guṇāḥ |
so ’nyarūpa-svarūpābhyām asminn ālambano mataḥ ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Now Kṛṣṇa as the object of rati is discussed:

Kṛṣṇa, Svayam Bhagavān, is the crest jewel of heroes, in whom all great qualities shine eternally. He is considered the ālambana or support for rati (viṣaya) through His svarūpa and through other forms that He may assume.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
anyarūpeṇa, yathā –
hanta me katham udeti sa-vatse, vatsa-pāla-paṭale ratir atra |
ity aniścita-matir baladevo vismaya-stimita-mūrtir ivāsīt ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The “other forms” are now explained as follows:

“How is it that I have developed rati for the calves and the cowherd boys similar to my rati for Kṛṣṇa?” In this way Balarāma with indecision remained struck with wonder.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha svarūpam –
āvṛtaṃ prakaṭaṃ ceti svarūpaṃ kathitaṃ dvidhā ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The svarūpa as the ālambana is now discussed:

The svarūpa takes two forms: covered and manifested.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra āvṛtam –
anya-veśādinācchannaṃ svarūpaṃ proktam āvṛtam ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The covered svarūpa is explained:

The svarūpa is called covered when it is covered or disguised by others’ clothing.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tena, yathā –
māṃ snehayati kim uccair mahileyaṃ dvārakāvarodhe’tra |
āṃ viditaṃ kutukārthī vanitā-veśo hariś carati ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of a disguised svarūpa is given:

Why does this queen in the inner chambers of Dvārakā attract me so much? Ah! I can understand that Kṛṣṇa has assumed the dress of a queen out of curiosity and is wandering about the palace.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prakaṭa-svarūpeṇa, yathā –
ayaṃ kambu-grīvaḥ kamala-kamanīyākṣi-paṭimā
tamāla-śyāmāṅga-dyutir atitarāṃ chatrita-śirāḥ |
dara-śrī-vatsāṅkaḥ sphurad-ari-darādy-aṅkita-karaḥ
karoty uccair modaṃ mama madhura-mūrtir madhu-ripuḥ ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of the manifested svarūpa is given:

This sweet form of the enemy of the demon Madhu gives me intense bliss. He has a neck like a conch shell, beautiful eyes envied by the lotuses, and the bodily glow of the dark tamāla tree. His head is sheltered by an umbrella, His chest is marked with the śrīvatsa whorl, and His hands are marked with cakra, conch and other symbols.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha tad guṇāḥ –
ayaṃ netā suramyāṅgaḥ sarva-sal-lakṣaṇānvitaḥ |
ruciras tejasā yukto balīyān vayasānvitaḥ ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The qualities of Krṣṇa will be described:

The hero Kṛṣṇa has beautiful limbs, has all auspicious bodily features, is pleasing to behold, possesses vitality, is strong and is endowed with ideal age.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vividhādbhuta-bhāṣā-vit satya-vākyaḥ priyaṃ vadaḥ |
vāvadūkaḥ supāṇḍityo buddhimān pratibhānvitaḥ ||24||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: He knows a variety of astonishing languages, is truthful, speaks in a pleasing manner, is eloquent, learned, intelligent, and filled with new ideas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vidagdhaś caturo dakṣaḥ kṛtajñaḥ sudṛḍha-vrataḥ |
deśa-kāla-supātrajñaḥ śāstra-cakṣuḥ śucir vaśī ||25||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: He is aesthetic, clever, skillful, and grateful. He keeps his vows, is knowledgeable of time, place and person, sees through the eyes of scripture, is pure, and controls His senses.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sthiro dāntaḥ kṣamā-śīlo gambhīro dhṛtimān samaḥ |
vadānyo dhārmikaḥ śūraḥ karuṇo mānya-mānakṛt ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: He is persevering, patient, tolerant, inscrutable, steadfast, uniform, generous, virtuous, heroic, compassionate, and respectful to persons worthy of respect.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dakṣiṇo vinayī hrīmān śaraṇāgata-pālakaḥ |
sukhī bhakta-suhṛt prema-vaśyaḥ sarva-śubhaṅkaraḥ ||27||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: He is compliant, modest, bashful, the protector of those who take shelter of Him, happy, friend of the devotees, controlled by love, and the benefactor of all.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pratāpī kīrtimān rakta-lokaḥ sādhu-samāśrayaḥ |
nārī-gaṇa-manohārī sarvārādhyaḥ samṛddhimān ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: He is glorious, renowned, the object of attraction for all, the shelter of the devotees, attractive to women, worshipable by all, and endowed with the greatest wealth.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
varīyān īśvaraś ceti guṇās tasyānukīrtitāḥ |
samudrā iva pañcāśad durvigāhā harer amī ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: He is the most important and the controller. These fifty qualities of Kṛṣṇa which have been listed are difficult to fathom, like the ocean.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
jīveṣu ete vasanto ’pi bindu-bindutayā kvacit |
paripūrṇatayā bhānti tatraiva puruṣottame ||30||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: These qualities are present even in the jīvas to a very small degree now and then. However, they are present in full in the Supreme Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathā hi pādme pārvatyai śiti-kaṇṭhena tad-guṇāḥ |
kandarpa-koṭi-lāvaṇya ity ādyāḥ parikīrtitāḥ ||31||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In this way, in the Padma Purāṇa, Śiva tells Pārvatī about the qualities of Kṛṣṇa, starting with His beauty, which is greater than that of ten million Cupids.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eta eva guṇāḥ prāyo dharmāya vana-mālinaḥ |
pṛthivyā prathama-skandhe prathayāñcakrire sphuṭam ||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The earth also describes the qualities of Kṛṣṇa clearly and extensively in order to instruct the deity of dharma, in the First Canto.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā prathame –
satyaṃ śaucaṃ dayā kṣāntis tyāgaḥ santoṣa ārjavam |
śamo damas tapaḥ sāmyaṃ titikṣoparatiḥ śrutam ||33||
jñānaṃ viraktir aiśvaryaṃ śauryaṃ tejo balaṃ smṛtiḥ |
svātantryaṃ kauśalaṃ kāntir dhairyaṃ mārdavam eva ca ||34||
prāgalbhyaṃ praśrayaḥ śīlaṃ saha ojo balaṃ bhagaḥ |
gāmbhīryaṃ sthairyam āstikyaṃ kīrtir māno ’nahaṅkṛtiḥ ||35||
ime cānye ca bhagavan nityā yatra mahā-guṇāḥ |
prārthyā mahattvam icchadbhir na viyanti sma karhicit ||36||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In Him reside (1) truthfulness, (2) cleanliness, (3) intolerance of another’s unhappiness, (4) the power to control anger, (5) self-satisfaction, (6) straightforwardness, (7) steadiness of mind, (8) control of the sense organs, (9) responsibility, (10) equality, (11) tolerance, (12) equanimity, (13) faithfulness, (14) knowledge, (15) absence of sense enjoyment, (16) leadership, (17) chivalry, (18) influence, (19) the power to make everything possible, (20) the discharge of proper duty, (21) complete independence, (22) dexterity, (23) fullness of all beauty, (24) serenity, (25) kindheartedness, (26) ingenuity, (27) gentility, (28) magnanimity, (29) determination, (30) perfection in all knowledge, (31) proper execution, (32) possession of all objects of enjoyment, (33) joyfulness, (34) immovability, (35) fidelity, (36) fame, (37) worship, (38) pridelessness, (39) being (as the Personality of Godhead), (40) eternity, and many other transcendental qualities which are eternally present and never to be separated from Him. SB 1.16.27-30

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: atha pañca-guṇā ye syur aṃśena giriśādiṣu ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Now, five qualities of Kṛṣṇa, which will also be present in Śiva and others when they are the Lord’s expansions, will be listed.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sadā svarūpa-samprāptaḥ sarva-jño nitya-nūtanaḥ |
sac-cid-ānanda-sāndrāṅgaḥ sarva-siddhi-niṣevitaḥ ||38||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: He is always situated in His eternal form; He is omniscient; He is forever young; He has a body made of condensed eternity, knowledge and bliss; and He possesses all siddhis (mystic powers).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
athocyante guṇāḥ pañca ye lakṣmīśādi-vartinaḥ |
avicintya-mahā-śaktiḥ koṭi-brahmāṇḍa-vigrahaḥ ||39||
avatārāvalī-bījaṃ hatāri-gati-dāyakaḥ |
ātmārāma-gaṇākarṣīty amī kṛṣṇe kilādbhutāḥ ||40||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Now will be listed the amazing qualities present in Kṛṣṇa but present also in Nārāyaṇa, and the puruṣāvatāras. He has inconceivable, great energies; He is the form which expands over tens of millions of universes; He is the source of the numerous avatāras; He rewards even the enemies He kills; and He attracts the ātmārāmas. These qualities become even more astonishing in Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sarvādbhuta-camatkāra-līlā-kallola-vāridhiḥ |
atulya-madhura-prema-maṇḍita-priya-maṇḍalaḥ ||41||
trijagan-mānasākarṣi-muralī-kala-kūjitaḥ |
asamānordhva-rūpa-śrī-vismāpita-carācaraḥ ||42||
līlā-premṇā priyādhikyaṃ mādhuryaṃ veṇu-rūpayoḥ |
ity asādhāraṇaṃ proktaṃ govindasya catuṣṭayam ||43||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The four extraordinary qualities possessed by Govinda alone are as follows. He is a wave-filled ocean of the most astonishing display of pastimes. He is surrounded by loving associates decorated with incomparably sweet prema. He plays sweet notes on His flute which attract all the minds in the three worlds. He astonishes all moving and non-moving entities with the beauty of His form to which there is no equal or superior. His extraordinary qualities are His special pastimes, His devotees endowed with abundant prema, the sweetness of His flute and the sweetness of His form.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
evaṃ guṇāś catur-bhedāś catuḥ-ṣaṣṭir udāhṛtāḥ |
sodāharaṇam eteṣāṃ lakṣaṇaṃ kriyate kramāt ||44||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The sixty-four qualities in four divisions will each be described with examples.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: ślāghyāṅga-sanniveśo yaḥ suramyāṅgaḥ sa kathyate[164] ||45||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person who is endowed with praiseworthy bodily parts is called “beautifully-limbed.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
mukhaṃ candrākāraṃ karabha-nibham uru-dvayam idaṃ
bhujau stambhārambhau sarasija-vareṇyaṃ kara-yugam |
kavāṭābhaṃ vakṣaḥ-sthalam aviralaṃ śroṇi-phalakaṃ
parikṣāmo madhyaḥ sphurati murahantur madhurimā ||46||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: What sweetness the form of Murāri reveals. His face is like the moon. His thighs are like elephant trunks. His arms are as sturdy as the bases of pillars. His hands are the object of praise for lotuses. His chest is as broad as a door. His hips are massive and His waist is thin.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: tanau guṇottham aṅkottham iti sal-lakṣaṇaṃ dvidhā[165] ||47||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Good indications or auspicious characteristics are of two types: bodily features (guṇottham) and the markings on the hands and feet (aṅkottham).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: guṇotthaṃ syād guṇair yogo raktatā-tuṅgatādibhiḥ ||48||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Guṇottham refers to such things as redness or elevation of a limb.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
rāgaḥ saptasu hanta ṣaṭsv api śiśor aṅgeṣv alaṃ tuṅgatā
visāras triṣu kharvatā triṣu tathā gambhīratā ca triṣu |
dairghyaṃ pañcasu kiṃ ca pañcasu sakhe samprekṣyate sūkṣmatā
dvātriṃśad-vara-lakṣaṇaḥ katham asau gopeṣu sambhāvyate ||49||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O friend! I see that your child has thirty-two auspicious marks on His body. How is it possible that such a child could be born in a cowherd’s house? Seven places on his body are red. Six limbs are elevated. Three limbs are broad. Three limbs are short. Three limbs are deep. Five limbs are long. Five limbs are fine.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
aṅkottham –
rekhāmayaṃ rathāṅgādi syād aṅkotthaṃ karādiṣu ||50||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Aṅkottham refers to lines such as a cakra on the hands or feet.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
karayoḥ kamalaṃ tathā rathāṅgaṃ
sphuṭa-rekhāmayam ātmajasya paśya |
pada-pallavayoś ca vallavendra
dhvaja-vajrāṅkuśa-mīna-paṅkajāni ||51||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O king of the cowherd men! See, on the hands of your child, there are clear lines of the lotus and cakra, and on His feet there are marks of a flag, thunderbolt, goad, fish and a lotus.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(3) ruciraḥ –
saundaryeṇa dṛg-ānanda-kārī rucira ucyate ||52||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Rucira means “He gives bliss to the eye by His beauty.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
yad dharma-sūnor bata rājasūye
nirīkṣya dṛk-svastyayanaṃ tri-lokaḥ |
kārtsnyena cādyeha gataṃ vidhātur
arvāk-sṛtau kauśalam ity amanyata ||53||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: All the demigods from the upper, lower and middle universal planetary systems assembled at the altar of the rājasūya sacrifice performed by Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira. After seeing the beautiful bodily features of Lord Kṛṣṇa, they all realized that whatever Brahmā had previously created had now completely entered into Kṛṣṇa. SB 3.2.13

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
aṣṭānāṃ danujabhid-aṅga-paṅkajānām
ekasmin katham api yatra ballavīnām |
lolākṣi-bhramara-tatiḥ papāta tasmān
notthātuṃ dyuti-madhu-paṅkilāt kṣamāsīt ||54||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Or, another example:

If the bee-like eyes of the gopīs fall upon one among the eight lotus-like bodily parts of Kṛṣṇa, the enemy of the Dānavas, they will not be able to rise from the thick honey of His beauty.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(4) tejasā yuktaḥ–
tejo dhāma prabhāvaś cety ucyate dvividhaṃ budhaiḥ ||55||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wise say there are two meanings of tejas: dhāma (effulgence) and prabhāva (conquering the enemy).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra dhāma–
dīpti-rāśir bhaved dhāma ||56||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Dhāma refers to effulgence.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
ambara-maṇi-nikurambaṃ viḍambayann api marīci-kulaiḥ |
hari-vakṣasi ruci-niviḍe maṇirāḍ ayam uḍur iva sphurati ||57||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though the Kaustubha jewel, the king of jewels, discredits the sun by its shining rays, it appears like only a star on the Lord’s chest, which shines much brighter than the Kaustubha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prabhāvaḥ –
prabhāvaḥ sarvajit-sthitiḥ ||58||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Prabhāva refers to His capacity to conquer all others.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
dūratas tam avalokya mādhavaṃ
komalāṅgam api raṅga-maṇḍale |
parvatodbhaṭa-bhujāntaro ’py asau
kaṃsa-malla-nivahaḥ sa vivyathe ||59||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Seeing Mādhava with His tender body from a distance in the wrestling arena, the group of wrestlers, though endowed with chests greater than mountains, became agitated with fear.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(5) balīyān –
prāṇena mahatā pūrṇo balīyān iti kathyate ||60||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Balīyān means “filled with great strength.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
paśya vindhya-girito ’pi gariṣṭhaṃ
daitya-puṅgavam udagram ariṣṭam |
tula-khaṇḍam iva piṇḍitam ārāt
puṇḍarīka-nayano vinunoda ||61||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: See! Lotus-eyed Kṛṣṇa has thrown to a far distance, like a piece of cotton, the greatest demon Ariṣṭa, who is heavier and higher than the Vindhya mountain range.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
vāmas tāmarasākṣasya bhuja-daṇḍaḥ sa pātu vaḥ |
krīḍā-kandukatāṃ yena nīto govardhano giriḥ ||62||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

May the left hand of lotus-eyed Kṛṣṇa, which has lifted Govardhana Hill like a ball, protect you!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(6) vayasānvitaḥ –
vayaso vividhatve ’pi sarva-bhakti-rasāśrayaḥ |
dharmī kiśora evātra nitya-nānā-vilāsavān ||63||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though Kṛṣṇa is endowed with all ages which become most excellent, the age of kaiśora, ever-fresh, endowed with all pastimes, manifesting all good qualities, and the shelter of all rasas, is considered the best.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
tadātvābhivyaktīkṛta-taruṇimārambha-rabhasaṃ
smita-śrī-nirdhūta-sphurad-amala-rākā-pati-madam |
darodañcat-pañcāśuga-nava-kalā-meduram idaṃ
murārer mādhuryaṃ manasi madirākṣīr madayati ||64||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Filled with the joy of recently manifested youth, the sweetness of Murāri, defeating the spotless, full moon with the effulgence of His smile, and made soft with a hint of the fresh sports of Cupid, gives great joy to the minds of the sweet-eyed gopīs.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(7) vividhādbhuta-bhāṣāvit –
vividhādbhuta-bhāṣāvit sa prokto yas tu kovidaḥ |
nānā-deśyāsu bhāṣāsu saṃskṛte prākṛteṣu ca ||65||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person who knows the languages of various countries, saṃskṛta, the vernacular and the languages of animals is called a knower of astonishing languages.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
vraja-yuvatiṣu śauriḥ śaurasenīṃ surendre
praṇata-śirasi saurīṃ bhāratīm ātanoti |
ahaha paśuṣu kīreṣv apy apabhraṃśa-rūpāṃ
katham ajani vidagdhaḥ sarva-bhāṣāvalīṣu ||66||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa, śauri, expresses Himself in the vernacular to the young gopīs of Vraja, in saṃṣkṛta to the respectful Indra, and in the colloquial dialect to the animals, to the people of Kāśmīra and to the parrots. How amazing! How has he become expert in all these languages?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(8) satya-vākyaḥ –
syān nānṛtaṃ vaco yasya satya-vākyaḥ sa kathyate ||67||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person is called a speaker of the truth whose words are never false.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
pṛthe tanaya-pañcakaṃ prakaṭam arpayiṣyāmi te
raṇorvaritam ity abhūt tava yathārtham evoditam |
ravir bhavati śītalaḥ kumuda-bandhur apy uṣṇalas
tathāpi na murāntaka vyabhicariṣṇur uktis tava ||68||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: You said, “O Kunti! I will bring back your five sons to you from the battlefield alive and with great honor.” Your statement has come true. O Murari! Even though the sun may become cold and the moon may become hot, Your words will never be untrue.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
gūḍho ’pi veṣeṇa mahī-surasya
harir yathārthaṃ magadhendram ūce |
saṃsṛṣṭam ābhyāṃ saha pāṇḍavābhyāṃ
māṃ viddhi kṛṣṇaṃ bhavataḥ sapatnam ||69||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though disguising Himself as a brāhmaṇa, Kṛṣṇa told the truth to Jarāsandha. “O king of Magadha! Understand that it is I, Kṛṣṇa, your enemy, accompanying the two sons of Pāṇḍu.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(9) priyaṃvadaḥ –
jane kṛtāparādhe ’pi sāntva-vādī priyaṃvadaḥ ||70||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: “Speaking in a pleasing manner” means speaking in a pleasing manner even to those who have offended.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kṛta-vyalīke ’pi na kuṇḍalīndra
tvayā vidheyā mayi doṣa-dṛṣṭiḥ |
pravāsyamāno ’si surārcitānāṃ
paraṃ hitāyādya gavāṃ kulasya ||71||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O king of the snakes! Though I have afflicted you, do not find fault in Me. For the good of the cows, worthy of respect by the devatās, you should live far from here.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(10) vāvadūkaḥ –
śruti-preṣṭhoktir akhila-vāg-guṇānvita-vāg api |
iti dvidhā nigadito vāvadūko manīṣibhiḥ ||72||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wise say that there are two types of eloquence: speaking which is pleasing to the ear, and speaking with clever meaning.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyo, yathā –
aśliṣṭa-komala-padāvali-mañjulena
pratyakṣara-kṣarad-amanda-sudhā-rasena |
sakhyaḥ samasta-jana-karṇa-rasāyanena
nāhāri kasya hṛdayaṃ hari-bhāṣitena ||73||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of speaking in a pleasing manner:

O friends! Whose heart will not be stolen by the words of Kṛṣṇa, sweet with clear, soft pronunciation, an elixir of intense nectar in the placement of each syllable, a medicine, giving life to the ears of all people by its sweet intonation?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dvitīyo, yathā –
prativādi-citta-parivṛtti-paṭur
jagad-eka-saṃśaya-vimarda-karī |
pramitākṣarādya-vividhārtha-mayī
hari-vāg iyaṃ mama dhinoti dhiyaḥ ||74||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of the skilful meaning of words is as follows:

The words of Kṛṣṇa, skilful at changing the hearts of His adversaries, superior to all in extinguishing all doubts in the universe, authoritative and economical, endowed with many meanings, have today made all my mental functions blissful.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(11) supaṇḍityaḥ –
vidvān nītijña ity eṣa supaṇḍityo dvidhā mataḥ |
vidvān akhila-vidyā-vin nītijñas tu yathārha-kṛt ||75||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Being knowledgeable has two aspects: knowledgeable of all branches of all subjects and knowledgeable of proper conduct.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyo yathā –
yaṃ suṣṭhu pūrvaṃ paricarya gauravāt
pitāmahādy-ambudharaiḥ pravartitāḥ |
kṛṣṇārṇavaṃ kāśya-guru-kṣamābhṛtas
tam eva vidyā-saritaḥ prapedire ||76||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of knowing all branches of knowledge:

Previously the clouds, in the form of Brahmā and others, by expertly serving with reverence the ocean, in the form of Kṛṣṇa, produced rivers of knowledge. Now those rivers of knowledge are flowing from the mountain of Sāndīpani back into the ocean of Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
āmnāya-prathitānvayā smṛtimatī bāḍhaṃ ṣaḍ-aṅgojjvalā
nyāyenānugatā purāṇa-suhṛdā mīmāṃsayā maṇḍitā |
tvāṃ labdhāvasarā cirād gurukule prekṣya svasaṅgārthinaṃ
vidyā nāma vadhūś caturdaśa-guṇā govinda śuśrūṣate ||77||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O Govinda! The bride of knowledge with fourteen branches whose lineage is distributed by the four Vedas, and which includes the smṛtis, is made brilliant by the six aṅgas, is followed by the saḍ-darśaṇas, is assisted by the Purāṇas, and decorated with the karma and jñāna-kāṇḍas. This bride known as knowledge, seeing You eager for gaining her association (knowledge) at the house of Your guru, desires to serve You--finding this opportunity after a long time.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dvitīyo, yathā –
mṛtyus taskara-maṇḍale sukṛtināṃ vṛnde vasantānilaḥ
kandarpo ramaṇīṣu durgata-kule kalyāṇa-kalpa-drumaḥ |
indur bandhu-gaṇe vipakṣa-paṭale kālāgni-rudrākṛtiḥ
śāsti svasti-dhurandharo madhupurīṃ nītyā madhūnāṃ patiḥ ||78||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The second type of learning, knowledge of conduct is illustrated:

The lord of the Madhus, Kṛṣṇa, is death for the thieves; the spring breeze for the pious; Cupid for the young women; a desire tree for the poverty-stricken; the cooling moon for His friends; the fire of final destruction in the form of Rudra for the enemies. He protects Mathurā and Dvārakā by His judicious conduct (in relation to all people.)

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(12) buddhimān –
medhāvī sūkṣmadhīś ceti procyate buddhimān dvidhā ||79||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Buddhimān means “the capacity to absorb knowledge” and “possessing fine intelligence (acuity).”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra medhāvī, yathā –
avanti-pura-vāsinaḥ sadanam etya sāndīpaner
guror jagati darśayan samayam atra vidyārthinām |
sakṛn nigada-mātrataḥ sakalam eva vidyā-kulaṃ
dadhau hṛdaya-mandire kim api citravan mādhavaḥ ||80||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of the ability to absorb knowledge:

Mādhava, going to the house of Sāndīpani, His guru living in Avantipura, in order to teach the proper method to those who desire knowledge, received all the knowledge in the temple of His heart after just one recitation by the guru. How astonishing it is!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sūkṣma-dhīḥ, yathā –
yadubhir ayam avadhyo mleccha-rājas tad enaṃ
tarala-tamasi tasmin vidravann eva neṣye |
sukhamaya-nija-nidrā-bhañjana-dhvaṃsi-dṛṣṭir
jhara-muci mucukundaḥ kandare yatra śete ||81||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of having fine intelligence:

This Kālayavana cannot be killed by the Yadus. By running away from him into the dimly lit cave, I will bring him there. In that cave decorated with waterfalls, Mucukunda is sleeping. When Mucukunda opens his eyes, being rudely awakened from comfortable sleep by Kālayavana, he will destroy this enemy with his glance.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(13) pratibhānvitaḥ –
sadyo navanavollekhi-jñānaṃ syāt pratibhānvitaḥ ||82||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Pratibhānvitaḥ (possessed with creativity) means immediate, novel manifestation of ideas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā padyāvalyāṃ –
vāsaḥ samprati keśava kva bhavato mugdhekṣaṇe nanv idaṃ
vāsaṃ brūhi śaṭha prakāma-subhage tvad-gātra-saṃsargataḥ |
yāminyām uṣitaḥ kva dhūrta vitanur muṣṇāti kiṃ yāminī
śaurir gopa-vadhūṃ chalaiḥ parihasann evaṃvidhaiḥ pātu vaḥ ||83||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Padyāvalī (283) –

Rādhā said: O Kṛṣṇa, where do You stay (vāsa) now?

Kṛṣṇa said: O Rādhā, with bewitching eyes! Can you not see that I am wearing my cloth (vāsam)?

Rādhā said: How crafty You are! I am talking about Your residence, not your cloth!

Kṛṣṇa said: O Rādhā with natural sweet aroma! I am fragrant (vāsa) by touching your limbs.

Rādhā said: O cheater! Where did You stay during the night (yāminyām uṣitaḥ)?

Kṛṣṇa said: How could I be stolen by the night, which does not even have a body (yāminyā muṣitaḥ)?

In this way may Kṛṣṇa, who joked with Rādhā using tricky words, protect you!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(14) vidagdhaḥ –
kalā-vilāsa-digdhātmā vidagdha iti kīrtyate ||84||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: One whose mind is absorbed in the sixty-four arts such as dancing and singing, and in various amusements, is called vidagdha (aesthete).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
gītaṃ gumphati tāṇḍavaṃ ghaṭayati brūte prahelī-kramaṃ
veṇuṃ vādayate srajaṃ viracayaty ālekhyam abhyasyati |
nirmāti svayam indrajāla-paṭalīṃ dyūte jayaty unmadān
paśyoddāma-kalā-vilāsa-vasatiś citraṃ hariḥ krīḍati ||85||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Look! Kṛṣṇa is composing songs and dancing. He is making riddles, playing the flute, stringing garlands, and drawing pictures. He is making magical objects and winning at dice against persons showing pride. Kṛṣṇa, the residence of pastimes of unlimited arts, is now enjoying His leisure.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(15) caturaḥ –
caturo yugapad-bhūri-samādhāna-kṛd ucyate ||86||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A clever person is one who brings about a solution to many problems simultaneously.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
pārāvatī-viracanena gavāṃ kalāpaṃ
gopāṅganā-gaṇam apāṅga-taraṅgitena |
mitrāṇi citratara-saṅgara-vikrameṇa
dhinvann ariṣṭa-bhayadena harir vireje ||87||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa gives bliss to all the cows by composing cowherd songs. He pleases the gopīs by the movement of His brow. He brings joy to His friends by heroic actions. All of these simultaneously give fear to Ariṣṭāsura (seeing how fearless Kṛṣṇa remains).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(16) dakṣaḥ –
duṣkare kṣipra-kārī yas taṃ dakṣaṃ paricakṣate ||88||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: An expert is a person who does very quickly what is difficult to do.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrī-daśame –
yāni yodhaiḥ prayuktāni
śastrāstrāṇi kurūdvaha |
haris tāny acchinat tīkṣṇaiḥ
śarair ekaika-śastribhiḥ ||89||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from the Tenth Canto:

Lord Hari then struck down all the missiles and weapons that the enemy soldiers threw at Him, O hero of the Kurus, destroying each and every one with three sharp arrows. SB 10.59.17

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
aghahara kuru yugmībhūya nṛtyaṃ mayaiva
tvam iti nikhila-gopī-prārthanā-pūrti-kāmaḥ |
atanuta gati-līlā-lāghavormiṃ tathāsau
dadṛśur adhikam etās taṃ yathā sva-sva-pārśve ||90||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

“O killer of the Agha demon! Please dance only with me!” Desiring to fulfill this request by all of the gopīs, Kṛṣṇa quickly produced a multitude of gopīs and Himself going to a suitable place and performing the dance--but in such a manner that each of the gopīs without doubt saw Him at her side alone.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(17) kṛtajñaḥ –
kṛtajñaḥ syād abhijño yaḥ kṛta-sevādi-karmaṇām ||91||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A grateful person is one who acknowledges others who have done service.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā mahābhārate –
ṛṇam etat pravṛddhaṃ me hṛdayān nāpasarpati |
yad govindeti cukrośa kṛṣṇā māṃ dūra-vāsinam ||92||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from the Mahābhārata (5.58.21):

Draupadī cried out “O Govinda!” though I was situated far away. That crying out has created an ever-increasing debt which does not leave My heart.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
anugatim ati-pūrvaṃ cintayann ṛkṣa-mauler
akuruta bahumānaṃ śaurir ādāya kanyām |
katham api kṛtam alpaṃ vismaren naiva sādhuḥ
kim uta sa khalu sādhu-śreṇi-cūḍāgra-ratnam ||93||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Though Jāmbavān had offended Kṛṣṇa, the Lord, remembering his service in the past during the time of Lord Rāma, married his daughter and gave him great respect. Since the well-bred never forget what little service is rendered to them, what then can be said of Kṛṣṇa, who is the head jewel among all well-behaved persons?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(18) sudṛḍha-vrataḥ –
pratijñā-niyamau yasya satyau sa sudṛḍha-vrataḥ ||94||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person who is true to his promises and to his perpetual vows is called “fixed in vows”.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra satya-pratijño, yathā hari-vaṃśe –
na deva-gandharva-gaṇā na rākṣasā
na cāsurā naiva ca yakṣa-pannagāḥ |
mama pratijñām apahantum udyatā
mune samarthāḥ khalu satyam astu te ||95||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Being true to His promises is illustrated in Hari-vaṃśa (2.68.38) –

O Nārada! All of the devas, Gandharvas, Rākṣasas, asuras, Yakṣas and Pannagas are trying to make Me break My promise, but they cannot do so. May My promise to you be fruitful!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
sa-helam ākhaṇḍala-pāṇḍu-putrau
vidhāya kaṃsārir apārijātau |
nija-pratijñāṃ saphalāṃ dadhānaḥ
satyāṃ ca kṛṣṇāṃ ca sukhām akārṣīt ||96||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Very easily Kṛṣṇa, the enemy of Kaṃsa, made Indra bereft of the Pārijāta tree and pleased Satyabhāmā. He also made Yudhiṣṭhira devoid of enemies and pleased Draupadī. In this way, He fulfilled His promises.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
satya-niyamo, yathā –
girer uddharaṇaṃ kṛṣṇa duṣkaraṃ karma kurvatā |
mad-bhaktaḥ syān na duḥkhīti sva-vrataṃ vivṛtaṃ tvayā ||97||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of being true to perpetual vows:

O Kṛṣṇa! You vowed that Your devotee will never suffer. You have illustrated that by performing the difficult task of lifting Govardhana Hill.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(19) deśa-kāla-supātrajñaḥ –
deśa-kāla-supātrajñas tat-tad-yogya-kriyā-kṛtiḥ ||98||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The knower of place, time and person is one who performs actions suitable to the time, place and person.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
śaraj-jyotsnā-tulyaḥ katham api paro nāsti samayas
trilokyām ākṛīḍaḥ kvacid api na vṛndāvana-samaḥ |
na kāpy ambhojākṣī vraja-yuvati-kalpeti vimṛśan
mano me sotkaṇṭhaṃ muhur ajani rāsotsava-rase ||99||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There is no time compared to the autumn season in the moonlight. There is not place of amusement equal to Vṛndāvana in the three worlds. There are no lotus-eyed women like the young women of Vraja. Considering this, My heart longs for the taste of the rāsa dance.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(20) śāstra-cakṣuḥ –
śāstrānusāri-karmā yaḥ śāstra-cakṣuḥ sa kathyate ||100||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person with “the eyes of scripture” is the person who performs his actions according to the rules of scripture.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
abhūt kaṃsa-ripor netraṃ
śāstram evārtha-dṛṣṭaye |
netrāmbujaṃ tu yuvatī-
vṛndān mādāya kevalam ||101||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The eyes of scripture which belong to Kṛṣṇa, the enemy of Kaṃsa, exist only to see the practical action in a given situation, and His lotus eyes exist only to bewilder the young women.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(21) śuciḥ –
pāvanaś ca viśuddhaś cety ucyate dvividhaḥ śuciḥ |
pāvanaḥ pāpa-nāśī syād viśuddhas tyakta-dūsaṇaḥ ||102||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are two types of purity: pāvana and viśuddha. Pāvana means he who destroys sin and viśuddha means he who is without faults.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra pāvano, yathā pādme –
taṃ nirvyājaṃ bhaja guṇa-nidhiṃ pāvanaṃ pāvanānāṃ
śraddhā-rajyan-matir atitarām uttamaḥ-śloka-maulim |
prodyann antaḥ-karaṇa-kuhare hanta yan-nāma-bhānor
ābhāso ’pi kṣapayati mahā-pātaka-dhvānta-rāśim ||103||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Purification of sin (pāvana) is illustrated in the Padma Purāṇa:--

With intellect purified by faith and all sincerity, please intently worship Kṛṣṇa, an ocean of good qualities, whose glory destroys all darkness, who purifies those who purify others. When just the semblance of His name appears in the heart, it destroys the greatest accumulation of sins, just as the light prior to the rising of the sun destroys all darkness.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
viśuddho, yathā –
kapaṭaṃ ca haṭhaś ca nācyute
bata satrājiti nāpy adīnatā |
katham adya vṛthā syamantaka
prasabhaṃ kaustubha-sakhyam icchasi ||104||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Viśuddha (faultlessness) is illustrated as follows:

O Syamantaka jewel! There is no deception in Kṛṣṇa to try to take you from Satrājit, and there is plenty of miserliness in Satrājit to keep you. Then why do you desire so forcibly to make a friendship with the Kaustubha jewel today?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(22) vaśī
vaśī jitendriyaḥ proktaḥ ||105||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A subjugator is one who controls his senses.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā prathame –
uddāma-bhāva-piśunāmala-valgu-hāsa-
vrīḍāvaloka-nihato madano ’pi yāsām |
saṃmuhya cāpam ajahāt pramadottamās tā
yasyendriyaṃ vimathituṃ kuhakair na śekuḥ ||106||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
This is illustrated in the First Canto:-

Although the queens’ beautiful smiles and furtive glances were all spotless and exciting, and signified deep love, and although they could conquer Cupid himself by making him give up his bow in frustration, other women who approached Kṛṣṇa with false smiles and glances could not agitate the senses of the Lord. SB 1.11.37

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(23) sthiraḥ –
āphalodayakṛt sthiraḥ ||107||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: He is called sthira or persevering who works steadily until realizing his goals.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
nirvedam āpa na vana-bhramaṇe murārir
nācintayad vyasanam ṛkṣa-vilapraveśe |
āhṛtya hanta maṇim eva puraṃ prapede
syād udyamaḥ kṛta-dhiyāṃ hi phalodayāntaḥ ||108||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa did not loathe wandering in the forest looking for the Syamantaka jewel, and did not consider fear on entering the cave of Jāmbavān. Taking the jewel, He returned to Dvārakā. Those of steady intelligence persevere in their work until attaining the result.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(24) dāntaḥ –
sa dānto duḥsaham api yogyaṃ kleśaḥ saheta yaḥ ||109||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who endures difficult but necessary suffering is called patient.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
gurum api guru-vāsa-kleśam avyāja-bhaktyā
harir ajagaṇad antaḥ komalāṅgo ’pi nāyam |
prakṛtir ati-durūhā hanta lokottarāṇāṃ
kim api manasi citraṃ cintyamānā tanoti ||110||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though Kṛṣṇa’s body was very soft, He did not consider in His heart the intolerable difficulties of living at the house of guru, because of His sincere devotion. One becomes amazed on contemplating the inscrutable character of extraordinary people.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(25) kṣamāśīlaḥ
kṣamāśīlo ’parādhānāṃ sahanaḥ parikīrtyate ||111||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person who endures the offenses of others is called tolerant.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā māgha-kāvye –
prativācam adatta keśavaḥ
śapamānāya na cedi-bhūbhṛte |
anuhuṅkurute ghana-dhvaniḥ
na hi gomāyu-rutāni keśarī ||112||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Śiṣupāla-vadha, Māgha-kāvya (16.25):

Though Śiṣupāla criticized Kṛṣṇa hundreds of times, Kṛṣṇa did not give any response. Though the lion replies to the thunder, it does not pay attention to the howl of the jackal.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā yāmunācārya-stotre –
raghuvara yad abhūs tvaṃ tādṛśo vāyasasya
praṇata iti dayālur yac ca caidyasya kṛṣṇa |
pratibhavam aparāddhur mugdha sāyujyado ’bhūr
vada kim apadam āgatas tasya te ’sti kṣamāyāḥ ||113||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example from Stotra-ratna of Yāmunācārya (60) –

O Rāmacandra, best of the Raghu dynasty! You were so merciful to the crow which pecked Sītā’s breast, but which then offered respects to You.[168] O Kṛṣṇa, so forgetful of others sins! You gave attractive impersonal liberation to Śiṣupāla who offended You for many lifetimes. Tell me what offense exists in him that You would not tolerate?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(26) gambhīraḥ –
durvibodhāśayo yas tu sa gambhīraḥ itīryate ||114||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person whose intentions are difficult to comprehend is called inscrutable.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
vṛndāvane varābhiḥ stutibhir nitarām upāsyamāno ’pi |
śakto na harir vidhinā ruṣṭas tuṣṭo’thavā jñātum ||115||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When Brahmā worshipped Kṛṣṇa by offering the best praises in Vrndāvana, Kṛṣṇa remained silent. Brahmā could not understand if Kṛṣṇa was satisfied or angry with him.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
unmado ’pi harir navya-rādhā-praṇaya-sīdhunā |
abhijñenāpi rāmeṇa lakṣito’yam avikriyaḥ ||116||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Though Kṛṣṇa, while lifting Govardhana Hill, was intoxicated with the new liquor of Rādhā’s love, even omniscient Balarāma could not notice any indications of change.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(27) dhṛtimān –
pūrṇa-spṛhaś ca dhṛtimān śāntaś ca kṣobha-kāraṇe ||117||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who is fully satisfied in his desires, or who is peaceful when there is cause for agitation by control of his mind, is called steadfast.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyo –
svīkurvann api nitarāṃ yaśaḥ-priyatvaṃ
kaṃsārir magadha-pater vadha-prasiddhām |
bhīmāya svayam atulām adatta kīrtiṃ
kiṃ lokottara-guṇa-śālinām apekṣyam ||118||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The fully satisfied person is illustrated in the following:

Though Kṛṣṇa is fond of fame, He gave that incomparable fame to Bhīma in the killing of Jarāsandha. What remains to be desired by those of superior character?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dvitīyo, yathā –
ninditasya dama-ghoṣa-sūnunā
sambhrameṇa munibhiḥ stutasya ca |
rājasūya-sadasi kṣitīśvaraiḥ
kāpi nāsya vikṛtir vitarkitā ||119||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of being peaceful, in spite of causes of agitation:

In spite of being rebuked by Śiśupāla and being praised by the sages in the assembly of the rājasūya sacrifice, Kṛṣṇa showed such steadiness that the kings present could not detect any change in Kṛṣṇa’s appearance.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(28) samaḥ –
rāga-dveṣa-vimukto yaḥ samaḥ sa kathito budhaiḥ ||120||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The learned say that a person who is free of attraction and disgust is called impartial.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrī-daśame–
nyāyyo hi daṇḍaḥ kṛta-kilbiṣe ’smiṃs
tavāvatāraḥ khala-nigrahāya |
ripoḥ sutānām api tulya-dṛṣṭer
dhatse damaṃ phalam evānuśaṃsan ||121||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from the Tenth Canto:

The punishment that this offender has been subjected to is certainly just. After all, You have incarnated within this world to curb envious and cruel persons. You are so impartial that You look equally upon Your enemies and Your own sons, for when You impose a punishment on a living being You know it to be for his ultimate benefit. SB 10.16.33

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
ripur api yadi śuddho maṇḍanīyas tavāsau
yaduvara yadi duṣṭo daṇḍanīyaḥ suto ’pi |
na punar akhila-bhartuḥ pakṣapātojjhitasya
kvacid api viṣamaṃ te ceṣṭitaṃ jāghaṭīti ||122||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O best of the Yadus, if Your enemy does something correctly You reward him, and if Your son becomes spoiled You punish him. You are the protector of all people and without prejudice. Thus partiality can never occur in Your actions.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(29) vadānyaḥ –
dāna-vīro bhaved yas tu sa vadānyo nigadyate ||123||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who is very charitable is called generous.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
sarvārthināṃ bāḍham abhīṣṭa-pūrtyā
vyarthīkṛtāḥ kaṃsa-nisūdanena |
hriyeva cintāmaṇi-kāmadhenu-
kalpa-drumā dvāravatīṃ bhajanti ||124||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wishing jewel, the desire cow and the desire tree, being made useless by Kṛṣṇa, who fully satisfies all the supplicants’ desires, serve Dvārakā in shame.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
yeṣāṃ ṣoḍaśa-pūritā daśa-śatī svāntaḥ-purāṇāṃ tathā
cāṣṭāśliṣṭa-śataṃ vibhāti paritas tat-saṅkhya-patnī-yujām |
ekaikaṃ prati teṣu tarṇaka-bhṛtāṃ bhūṣā-juṣām anvahaṃ
gṛṣṭīnāṃ yugapac ca baddham adadād yas tasya vā kaḥ samaḥ ||125||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

In each of the sixteen thousand one hundred and eight palaces with their queens, every day at one time Kṛṣṇa would give in charity thirteen thousand and eighty-four ornamented young cows with first-born calves. Who can be equal to Him in charity?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(30) dhārmikaḥ –
kurvan kārayate dharmaṃ yaḥ sa dhārmika ucyate ||126||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who follows the principles of dharma and engages others in doing so also is called a virtuous man.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
pādaiś caturbhir bhavatā vṛṣasya
guptasya gopendra tathābhyavardhi |
svairaṃ carann eva yathā trilokyām
adharma-sparśāṇi haṭhāj jaghāsa ||127||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

O king of the cowherd men! The bull of dharma with four legs under Your protection has flourished to such an extent that, going everywhere in the three worlds, it has forcefully gobbled up the grass of irreligion.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
vitāyamānair bhavatā makhotkarair
ākṛṣyamāṇeṣu patiṣv anāratam |
mukunda khinnaḥ sura-subhruvāṃ gaṇas
tavāvatāraṃ navamaṃ namasyati ||128||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O Mukunda! You have performed so many sacrifices and continually attracted all the devatās that the wives of the devatās, feeling suffering in separation from their husbands, are praying to Buddha, Your ninth avatāra, to appear to stop sacrifices.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(31) śūraḥ –
utsāhī yudhi śūro ’stra-prayoge ca vicakṣaṇaḥ ||129||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A hero is a person who is energetic in fighting and skilful in use of weapons.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyo, yathā –
pṛthu-samara-saro vigāhya kurvan
dviṣad aravinda-vane vihāra-caryām |
sphurasi tarala-bāhu-daṇḍa-śuṇḍas
tvam agha-vidāraṇa vāraṇendra-līlaḥ ||130||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of enthusiasm in fighting is illustrated first:

O destroyer of Agha! Destroyer of sin! With Your trembling trunk-like arms, submerging Yourself in the lake of the expansive battlefield, playing in the forest of lotuses composed of Your enemies, You appear to be playing like the king of the elephants.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dvitīyo, yathā –
kṣaṇād akṣauhiṇī-vṛnde jarāsandhasya dāruṇe |
dṛṣṭaḥ ko ’py atra nādaṣṭo hareḥ praharaṇāhibhiḥ ||131||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of being expert in handling weapons:

Within a second, one could not see any soldier among the ferocious phalanx of akṣauhiṇis of Jarāsandha, any person who was not bitten by the snake-like arrows of the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(32) karuṇaḥ --
para-duḥkhāsaho yas tu karuṇaḥ sa nigadyate ||132||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person who cannot tolerate the suffering of others is called compassionate.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
rājñām agādha-gatibhir magadhendra-kārā-
duḥkhāndhakāra-paṭalaiḥ svayam andhitānām |
akṣīṇi yaḥ sukhamayāni ghṛṇī vyatānīd
vṛnde tam adya yadunandana-padma-bandhum ||133||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of karuṇa:

I offer my respects to the compassionate son of the Yadus, who like the sun made all the kings’ eyes blossom with joy. These kings had blinded themselves with tears, arising from the impenetrable darkness of suffering caused by being imprisoned by Jarāsandha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
skhalan-nayana-vāribhir viracitābhiṣeka-śriye
tvarābhara-taraṅgataḥ kavalitātma-visphūrtaye |
niśānta-śara-śāyinā sura-sarit-sutena smṛteḥ
sapadya-vaśa-vartmaṇo bhagavataḥ kṛpāyai namaḥ ||134||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

I offer my respects to the compassion of the Lord, who, because of Bhīṣma’s remembrance of Him while laying the bed of sharp arrows, immediately appeared, beyond His own control. He rushed towards Bhīṣma while forgetting His position as God, His body bathed in a shower of tears.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(33) mānyamāna-kṛt –
guru-brāhmaṇa-vṛddhādi-pūjako mānyamāna-kṛt ||135||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who worships guru, brāhmaṇas, and the elderly is called a respectful person.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
abhivādya guroḥ padāmbujaṃ
pitaraṃ pūrvajam apy athānataḥ |
harir añjalinā tathā girā
yadu-vṛddhān anamat kramādayam ||136||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa first offered respects to the lotus feet of His guru. He then offered respects to His father and elder brother. Then with folded hands and words, He offered respects to the elders of the Yadu dynasty in proper order.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(34) dakṣiṇaḥ –
sauśīlya-saumya-carito dakṣiṇaḥ kīrtyate budhaiḥ ||137||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person who is mild due to his excellent nature is called compliant by the intelligent.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
bhṛtyasya paśyati gurūn api nāparādhān
sevāṃ manāg api kṛtāṃ bahudhābhyupaiti |
āviṣkaroti piśuneṣv api nābhyasūyāṃ
śīlena nirmala-matiḥ puruṣottamo ’yam ||138||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The Supreme Lord, who is pure hearted by His very nature, does not see the serious offenses of His servant, but He accepts even a little service as a great thing. He does not find fault even in those of low character.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(35) vinayī –
auddhatya-parihārī yaḥ kathyate vinayīty asau ||139||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who is devoid of arrogance is called modest.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā māgha-kāvye (13.7) –
avaloka eṣa nṛpateḥ sudūrato
rabhasād rathād avatarītum icchataḥ |
avatīrṇavān prathamam ātmanā harir
vinayaṃ viśeṣayati sambhrameṇa saḥ ||140||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Māgha-kāvya:

Seeing that Yudhiṣṭhira wanted to alight in haste from his chariot on seeing Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa Himself, by getting down from His chariot first out of great respect for Yudhiṣṭhira, showed more modesty than anyone else.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(36) hrīmān –
jñāte ’smara-rahasye ’nyaiḥ kriyamāṇe stave ’thavā |
śālīnatvena saṅkocaṃ bhajan hrīmān udīryate ||141||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person is called bashful who is shy when praised by others, or when he thinks others are aware of His secret love affairs. This is because of modesty or his unfathomable nature.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā lalita-mādhave (9.40) –
darodañcad-gopī-stana-parisara-prekṣaṇa-bharāt
karotkampādīṣac calati kila govardhana-girau |
bhayārtair ārabdha-stutir akhila-gopaiḥ smita-mukhaṃ
puro dṛṣṭvā rāmaṃ jayati namitāsyo madhuripuḥ ||142||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From Lalita-mādhava:

All glories to the enemy of Madhu, who made Govardhana Hill shake slightly by the weight of glancing upon the expanse of the gopīs’ upraised breasts and who hung His head in shame when He saw Balarāma smiling before Him, while He was being praised by the fearful cowherd men.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(37) śaraṇāgata-pālakaḥ –
pālayan śaraṇāpannān śaraṇāgata-pālakaḥ ||143||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: He who protects those who surrender is called protector of the surrendered.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
jvara parihara vitrāsaṃ tvam atra samare kṛtāparādhe ’pi |
sadyaḥ prapadyamāne yad indavati yādavendro ’yam ||144||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O fever (weapon), though you are an offender in this battle, give up your fear, because the best of the Yadus, Kṛṣṇa, acts like the moon towards those who completely surrender to Him.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(38) sukhī –
bhoktā ca duḥkha-gandhair apy aspṛṣṭaś ca sukhī bhavet ||145||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who is an enjoyer and is not touched by a trace of sorrow is called a happy person.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyo, yathā –
ratnālaṅkāra-bhāras tava dhanada-mano-rājya-vṛttyāpy alabhyaḥ
svapne dambholi-pāṇer api duradhigamaṃ dvāri tauryatrikaṃ ca |
pārśve gaurī-gariṣṭhāḥ pracura-śaśi-kalāḥ kānta-sarvāṅga-bhājaḥ
sīmantinyaś ca nityaṃ yaduvara bhuvane kas tvad-anyo ’sti bhogī ||146||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The enjoyer is illustrated first:

O best of the Yadus, the quantity of Your jeweled ornaments cannot even be imagined by Kubera, the lord of wealth. The singing and dancing taking place at Your door cannot be imagined by Indra, even in his dreams. At Your side constantly are beautiful women who enjoy all your attractive limbs, who are decorated with the tips of Your moon-like fingernails, and who are thus superior to the wife of Śiva. In this world there is no enjoyer like You.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dvitīyo, yathā –
na hāniṃ na mlāniṃ nija-gṛha-kṛtya-vyasanitāṃ
na ghoraṃ nodghūrṇāṃ na kila kadanaṃ vetti kim api |
varāṅgībhiḥ sāṅgīkṛta-suhṛd-anaṅgābhir abhito
harir vṛndāraṇye param aniśam uccair viharati ||147||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
“Being without a trace of sorrow” is illustrated next:

O wives of the brāhmaṇas! Not even a trace of sorrow can touch Kṛṣṇa, for in Him there is no destruction, no decrease, no suffering in household affairs, no cause of fear, and no worry. He does not know any of the suffering of this world. He is eternally engaged in amusing Himself in Vrndāvana with beautiful women, who were perfect, the best of friends, and held the highest love.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
(39) bhakta-suhṛt –
susevyo dāsa-bandhuś ca dvidhā bhakta-suhṛn mataḥ ||148||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are two ways in which Kṛṣṇa is friendly to devotees: being easily served and being a friend of His servant.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyo, yathā viṣṇu-dharme –
tulasī-dala-mātreṇa jalasya culukena ca |
vikrīṇīte svam ātmānaṃ bhaktebhyo bhakta-vatsalaḥ ||149||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of being easily served from Viṣṇu-dharma:[170]

If the devotees offer only water and tulasī leaves to the Lord, the Lord, being affectionate to the devotees, puts Himself under the control of the devotees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dvitīyo, yathā prathame –
sva-nigamam apahāya mat-pratijñām
ṛtam adhikartum avapluto rathasthaḥ |
