bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Beating is the cause of moha (vyabhicāri-bhāva) and pralaya (sāttvika-bhāva). It is also the effect of augrya (ferocity, vyabhicāri-bhāva). Other states should be understood similarly.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
trāsa-nidrā-śramālasya-mada-bhid-bodha-varjinām |
sañcāriṇām iha kvāpi bhaved raty-anubhāvatā ||202||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The vyabhicāri-bhāvas of trāsa, nidrā, śrama, ālasya, mada arising from intoxication, and bodha arise “somewhat” as the effects of rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sākṣād-rater na sambandhaḥ ṣaḍbhis trāsādibhiḥ saha |
syāt parasparayā kintu līlānuguṇatākṛte ||203||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Rati has no direct relationship with the six vyabhicāri-bhāvas just mentioned. Rati has a relationship with them only because they support rati for encouraging pastimes.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vitarka-mati-nirveda-dhṛtīnāṃ smṛti-harṣayoḥ |
bodha-bhid-dainya-suptīnāṃ kvacid rati-vibhāvatā ||204||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Vitarka, mati, nirveda, dhṛti, smṛti, harṣa, and the type of bodha arising from destruction of ignorance become “somewhat” the cause of rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: paratantrāḥ svatantrāś cety uktāḥ sañcāriṇo dvidhā ||205||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The vyabhicāri-bhāvas may be either dependent or independent.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra paratantrāḥ –
varāvaratayā proktāḥ paratantrā api dvidhā ||206||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The dependent vyabhicāri-bhāvas are either superior or inferior.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra varaḥ –
sākṣād vyavahitaś ceti varo ’py eṣa dvidhoditaḥ ||207||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The superior, dependent vyabhicāri-bhāvas are either direct or indirect.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra sākṣāt –
mukhyām eva ratiṃ puṣṇan sākṣād ity abhidhīyate ||208||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That superior vyabhicāri-bhāva which nourishes a primary rati is called a direct, superior dependent vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
tanuruhālī ca tanuś ca nṛtyaṃ
tanoti me nāma niśamya yasya |
apaśyato māthura-maṇḍalaṃ tad-
vyarthena kiṃ hanta dṛśor dvayena ||209||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

What is the use of eyes which do not see Mathurā, hearing whose name my hairs stand on end?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vyavahitaḥ –
puṣṇāti yo ratiṃ gauṇīṃ sa vyavahito mataḥ ||210||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That superior, dependent vyabhicāri-bhāva which nourishes a secondary rati is called an indirect (vyavahita), superior, dependent vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
dhig astu me bhuja-dvandvaṃ bhīmasya parighopamam |
mādhavākṣepiṇaṃ duṣṭaṃ yat pinaṣṭi na cedipam ||211||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: I am Bhīma. How unfortunate are my two arms, strong as iron beams, if they cannot crush the evil Śiṣupāla, the enemy of Kṛṣṇa?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: nirvedaḥ krodha-vaśyatvād ayaṃ vyavahito rateḥ ||212||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the above verse, nirveda (self-deprecation) is under the control of the secondary rati of anger. Thus it is called indirect, or vyavahita.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha avaraḥ –
rasa-dvayasyāpy aṅgatvam agacchann avaro mataḥ ||213||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the vyabhicāri-bhāva is not a component of either a primary or secondary rasa (does not nourish the rasa), it is called an inferior, dependent vyabhicāri-bhāva.[214]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
lelihyamānaṃ vadanair jvaladbhir
jaganti daṃṣṭrāsphuṭad-uttamāṅgaiḥ |
avekṣya kṛṣṇaṃ dhṛta-viśvarūpaṃ
na svaṃ viśuṣyan smarati sma jiṣṇuḥ ||214||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When Arjuna saw the universal form of Kṛṣṇa which was crushing the heads of the living entities in the universe with its teeth situated in its shining mouths, his mouth became dry and he forgot himself.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ghora-kriyādy-anubhāvād ācchādya sahajāṃ ratim |
durvarāvirabhūd bhītir moho yaṃ bhī-vaśas tataḥ ||215||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Uncontrollable fear appeared from Arjuna experiencing the frightful actions of the universal form. This covers the rati of Arjuna. The vyabhicāri-bhāva of moha is under the control of a fear which is not a secondary rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha svatantrāḥ –
sadaiva pāratantrye ’pi kvacid eṣāṃ svatantratā |
bhūpāla-sevakasyeva pravṛttasya kara-grahe ||216||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Svatantra (independent) vyabhicāri-bhāvas:

Though all the vyabhicāri-bhāvas are dependent to some degree, they manifest some independence. Though the employees of the king are dependent on the king, at the time of collecting the king’s taxes or during marriages, they show independence from the king.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhāvajñai rati-śūnyaś ca raty-anusparśanas tathā |
rati-gandhiś ca te tredhā svatantrāḥ parikīrtitāḥ ||217||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those knowledgeable of rati divide the independent vyabhicāri-bhāvas into three types: devoid of genuine rati, influenced by genuine rati and having a trace of rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra rati-śūnyaḥ –
janeṣu rati-śūnyeṣu rati-śūnyo bhaved asau ||218||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Devoid of genuine rati:

When vyabhicāri-bhāvas are displayed in a person devoid of genuine rati, but not inimical to Kṛṣṇa, it is called a rati-śūnya-svatantra-vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrī-daśame–
dhig janma nas trivṛd-vidyāṃ dhig vrataṃ dhig bahujñatām |
dhik kulaṃ dhik kriyā-dīkṣāṃ vimukhā ye tv adhokṣaje || atra svatantro nirvedaḥ | ||219||

tatra raty-anusparśanaḥ –
yaḥ svato rati-gandhena vihīno ’pi prasaṅgataḥ |
paścād ratiṃ spṛśed eṣa raty-anusparśano mataḥ ||220||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Influenced by genuine rati:

When a vyabhicāri-bhāva appears spontaneously but devoid of even rati-gandha (the third type of independent vyabhicāri-bhāva, having genuine rati as the indirect cause), but is related to genuine rati because the experiencer has permanent genuine rati, it is called raty-anusparśana-svatantra-vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
gariṣṭhāriṣṭa-ṭaṅkārair vidhurā vadhirāyitā |
hā kṛṣṇa pāhi pāhīti cukrośābhīra-bālikā || ra trāsaḥ | ||221||

atha rati-gandhiḥ –
yaḥ svātantrye ’pi tad-gandhaṃ rati-gandhir vyanakti saḥ ||222||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When a vyabhicāri-bhāva shows a touch of rati even though it remains independent, it is called rati-gandhi-svatantra-vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
pītāṃśukaṃ paricinomi dhṛtaṃ tvayāṅge
saṅgopanāya na hi naptri vidhehi yatnam |
ity āryayā nigaditā namitottamāṅgā
rādhāvaguṇṭhita-mukhī tarasā tadāsīt ||
atra lajjā | ||223||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Bashfulness:

“O my daughter! I know why you are wearing that yellow cloth on your body. Do not try to hide from me (your attraction for Kṛṣṇa).” When Mukharā told this to Rādhā, Rādhā quickly hung her head and covered her face with the edge of her cloth to hide her shame.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ābhāsaḥ punar eteṣām asthāne vṛttito bhavet |
prātikūlyam anaucityam asthānatvaṃ dvidhoditam ||224||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When these vyabhicāri-bhāvas manifest inappropriately in unqualified persons, they are actually semblances or ābhāsas of vyabhicāri-bhāvas. There are two types of inappropriateness: appearing in persons hostile to Kṛṣṇa and being improperly credited.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra prātikūlyam –
vipakṣe vṛttir eteṣāṃ prātikūlyam itīryate ||225||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Unfavorable vyabhicāri-bhāvābhāsa:

When the bhāvas are present in those hostile to Kṛṣṇa they are called prātikūlya (unfavorable).[220]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
gopo ’py aśikṣita-raṇo ’pi tam aśva-daityaṃ
hanti me hanta mama jīvita-nirviśeṣam |
krīḍā-vinirjita-surādhipater alaṃ me
durjīvitena hata-kaṃsa-narādhipasya ||
atra nirvedasyābhāsaḥ | ||226||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of hostile manifestation of self-deprecation:

When the cowherd boy without training killed the horse demon who was my very life and soul, what is the need for me, the unfortunate king Kaṃsa, who defeated Indra as play, to maintain my life?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
ḍuṇḍabho jalacaraḥ sa kāliyo
goṣṭha-bhūbhṛd api loṣṭra-sodaraḥ |
tatra karma kim ivādbhutaṃ jane
yena mūrkha jagadīśateryate ||227||

atha anaucityam –
asatyatvam ayogyatvam anaucityaṃ dvidhā bhavet |
aprāṇini bhaved ādyaḥ tiryag-ādiṣu cāntimam ||228||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are two types of inappropriateness: non-existent (asatya) and unqualified (ayogya). “Non-existent” means attributing vyabhicāri-bhāvas to inanimate objects (which cannot have emotions). “Unqualified” means attributing the bhāvas to animals (who are unqualified for higher emotions).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra aprāṇini, yathā –
chāyā na yasya sakṛd apy upasevitābhūt
kṛṣṇena hanta mama tasya dhig astu janma |
mā tvaṃ kadamba vidhuro bhava kāliyāhiṃ
mṛdnan kariṣyati hariś caritārthatāṃ te ||
atra nirvedasya | ||229||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Self-deprecation in trees:

“My life is useless since Kṛṣṇa has not enjoyed the shade of my branches even once.” “O kadamba tree! Do not lament! You will attain success in life when Kṛṣṇa jumps from your branches to punish Kāliya.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tiraści, yathā –
adhirohatu kaḥ pakṣī kakṣām aparo mamādya medhyasya |
hitvāpi tārkṣya-paksaṃ bhajate pakṣaṃ harir yasya || atra garvasya | ||230||

vahamāneṣv api sadā jñāna-vijñāna-mādhurīm |
kadambādiṣu sāmānya-dṛṣṭyābhāsatvam ucyate ||231||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Consciousness, discrimination, and the experience of sweetness described in the kadamba tree and other objects should be understood to be only a semblance to those items because they have only consciousness without discriminating powers.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhāvānāṃ kvacid utpatti-sandhi-śābalya-śāntayaḥ |
daśāś catasra etāsām utpattis tv iha sambhavaḥ ||232||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Sometimes four stages are seen in the manifestation of vyabhicāri-bhāvas: appearance, conjunction, conflict and disappearance. Appearance (utpatti) means the initial manifestation of the vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
maṇḍale kim api caṇḍa-marīcer
lohitāyati niśamya yaśodā |
vaiṇavīṃ dhvani-dhurām avidūre
prasrava-stimita-kañculikāsīt || atra harṣotpattiḥ | ||233||

yathā vā –
tvayi rahasi milantyāṃ sambhrama-nyāsa-bhugnāpy
uṣasi sakhi tavālī mekhalā paśya bhāti |
iti vivṛta-rahasye mādhave kuñcita-bhrūr
dṛśam anṛju kirantī rādhikā vaḥ punātu || atrāsūyotpattiḥ | ||234||

atha sandhiḥ –
sarūpayor bhinnayor vā sandhiḥ syād bhāvayor mūrtiḥ ||235||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When two versions of the same bhāvas or two differing bhāvas mix it is called bhāva-sandhi.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra sarūpayoḥ sandhiḥ –
sandhiḥ sarūpayos tatra bhinna-hetūtthayor mataḥ ||236||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the same vyabhicāri-bhāva arises from two different causes and joins, it is called sarūpa-sandhi.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
rākṣasīṃ niśi niśāmya niśānte
gokuleśa-gṛhiṇī patitāṅgīm |
tat-kucopari sutaṃ ca hasantaṃ
hanta niścala-tanuḥ kṣaṇam āsīt ||
atrāniṣṭeṣṭa-saṃvīkṣākṛtayor jāḍyayor yutiḥ ||237||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Jaḍya caused by favorable and unfavorable circumstances:

In the evening, the dead demoness lay on the earth and your son was sitting on her breast laughing. When Yaśodā heard this, for some time she remained motionless.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha bhinnayoḥ –
bhinnayor hetunaikena bhinnenāpy upajātayoḥ ||238||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When two differing vyabhicāri-bhāvas arising from the same or differing cause join together it is called bhinna-bhāva-sandhi

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha eka-hetu-jayoḥ, yathā –
durvāracāpalo ’yaṃ dhāvann antar bahiś ca goṣṭhasya |
śiśur akutaścid bhītir dhinoti hṛdayaṃ dunoti ca me || tatra harṣa-śaṅkayoḥ | ||239||

tatra bhinna-hetujayoḥ, yathā –
vilasantam avekṣya devakī
sutam utphulla-vilocanaṃ puraḥ |
prabalām api malla-maṇḍalīṃ
himam uṣṇaṃ ca jalaṃ dṛśor dadhe || atra harṣa-viṣādayoḥ sandhiḥ | ||240||

ekena jāyamānānām anekena ca hetunā |
bahūnām api bhāvānāṃ sandhiḥ sphuṭam avekṣyate ||241||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: It is also seen that many bhāvas can join together, arising from one cause or many causes.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra eka-hetujānāṃ, yathā –
niruddhā kālindī-taṭa-bhuvi mukundena balinā
haṭhād antaḥ-smerāṃ taralatara-tārojjvala-kalām |
abhivyaktāvajñām aruṇa-kuṭilāpāṅga-suṣamāṃ
dṛśaṃ nyasyanty asmin jayati vṛṣabhānoḥ kula-maṇiḥ || atra harṣautsukya-garvāmarṣāsūyānāṃ sandhiḥ | ||242||

aneka-hetujānāṃ, yathā –
parihita-hari-hārā vīkṣya rādhā savitrīṃ
nikaṭa-bhuvi tathāgre tarka-bhāk smera-padmām |
harim api dara-dūre svāminaṃ tatra cāsīn
mahasi vinata-vakra-prasphura-mlāna-vaktrā || atra lajjāmarṣa-harṣa-viṣādānāṃ sandhiḥ | ||243||

atha śābalyam –
śabalatvaṃ tu bhāvānāṃ saṃmardaḥ syāt parasparam ||244||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When many bhāvas conflict with each other, the state is called śābalya.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
śaktaḥ kiṃ nāma kartuṃ sa śiśur ahaha me mitra-pakṣānadhākṣīd
ātiṣṭheyaṃ tam eva drutam atha śaraṇaṃ kuryur etan na vīrāḥ |
āṃ divyā malla-goṣṭhī viharati sa kareṇoddadhārādri-varyaṃ
kuryām adyaiva gatvā vraja-bhuvi kadanaṃ hā tataḥ kampate dhīḥ || atra garva-viṣāda-dainya-mati-smṛti-śaṅkāmarṣa-trāsānāṃ śāvalyam | ||245||

yathā vā –
dhig dīrghe nayane mamāstu mathurā yābhyāṃ na sā prekṣyate
vidyeyaṃ mama kiṅkarī-kṛta-nṛpā kālas tu sarvaṅkaraḥ |
lakṣmī-keli-gṛhaṃ gṛhaṃ mama hahā nityaṃ tanuḥ kṣīyate
sadmany eva hariṃ bhajeya hṛdayaṃ vṛndāṭavī karṣati || atra nirveda-garva-śaṅkā-dhṛti-viṣāda-maty-autsukyānāṃ śāvalyam | ||246||

atha śāntiḥ –
atyārūḍhasya bhāvasya vilayaḥ śāntir ucyate ||247||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When a bhāva which has become prominent disappears, it is called bhāva-śānti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
vidhurita-vadanā vidūna-bhāsas
tam aghaharaṃ gahane gaveṣayantaḥ |
mṛdu-kala-muralīṃ niśamya śaile
vraja-śiśavaḥ pulakojjvalā babhūvuḥ || atra viṣāda-śāntiḥ | ||248||

śabdārtha-rasa-vaicitrī vāci kācana nāsti me |
yathā-kathañcid evoktaṃ bhāvodāharaṇaṃ param ||249||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though my words do not have variety in meaning or flavor, examples of the vyabhicāri-bhāvas have been presented to indicate their essential nature as much as possible.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
trayastriṃśad ime ’ṣṭau ca vakṣyante sthāyinaś ca ye |
mukhya-bhāvābhidhās tv eka-catvāriṃśad amī smṛtāḥ ||250||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The forty-one chief bhāvas or emotions are these thirty-three vyabhicāri-bhāvas, the seven secondary sthāyi-bhāvas,[222] and the one mukhya-sthāyi-bhāva of the devotee.[223]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śarīrendriya-vargasya vikāraṇāṃ vidhāyakāḥ |
bhāvāvirbhāva-janitāś citta-vṛttaya īritāḥ ||251||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The transformations of mind created by the appearance of these forty-one bhāvas are said to create transformations in the body and all of the senses.[225]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kvacit svābhāviko bhāvaḥ kaścid āgantukaḥ kvacit |
yas tu svābhāviko bhāvaḥ sa vyāpyāntar-bahiḥ-sthitaḥ ||252||
mañjiṣṭhādye yathā dravye rāgas tan-maya īkṣyate |
atra syān nāma-mātreṇa vibhāvasya vibhāvatā ||253||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: One bhāva is natural to the person, and other bhāvas are incidental. The natural bhāva spreads internally and externally just as red color is completely identical with naturally red substances. Thus, the natural bhāva becomes very apparent just by a slight contact with the cause (Kṛṣṇa).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
etena sahajenaiva bhāvenānugatā ratiḥ |
eka-rūpāpi yā bhakter vividhā pratibhāty asau ||254||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: By this natural bhāva, rati (attraction or love) is appears. Though rati is one in speaking about it generally, it appears in various forms when wishing to describe its various qualities.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
āgantukas tu yo bhāvaḥ paṭādau raktimeva saḥ |
tais tair vibhāvair evāyaṃ dhīyate dīpyate ’pi ca ||255||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Just as white cloth appears red when red dye is applied to a cloth, the incidental bhāvas become situated in the devotees by various causes and then become visible.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vibhāvanādi-vaiśiṣṭyād bhaktānāṃ bhedatas tathā |
prāyeṇa sarva-bhāvānāṃ vaiśiṣṭyam upajāyate ||256||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Because of the variety of causes and other elements and the differences in the devotees, there is great diversity in every one of the bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vividhānāṃ tu bhaktānāṃ vaiśiṣṭyād vividhaṃ manaḥ |
mano ’nusārād bhāvānāṃ tāratamyaṃ kilodaye ||257||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There is a variety of minds of the various types of devotees (devotees in śānta, dāsya etc.). In accordance with the variety of minds, there are also various gradations of the appearance of the bhāvas because of various natures such as gariṣṭha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
citte gariṣṭhe gambhīre mahiṣṭhe karkaśādike |
samyag-unmīlitāś cāmī na lakṣyante sphuṭaṃ janaiḥ ||258||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Even if these bhāvas were to appear strongly in hearts which are gariṣṭha (heavy), gambhīra (deep), mahiṣṭha (expansive) or karkaśa (hard) in nature, ordinary people could not perceive the bhāvas clearly because there would be no external transformations of body or senses.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
citte laghiṣṭhe cottāne kṣodiṣṭhe komalādike |
manāg-unmīlitāś cāmī lakṣyante bahir ulbaṇāḥ ||259||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When these bhāvas arise even slightly in hearts which are laghiṣṭha (light), uttāna (spread on the surface), kṣodiṣṭha (small) and komala (soft), they can be recognized externally because of extreme transformations of the body and senses.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
gariṣṭhaṃ svarṇa-piṇḍābhaṃ laghiṣṭhaṃ tula-piṇḍavat |
citta-yugme ’tra vijñayā bhāvasya pavanopamā ||260||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The heavy heart is like a pile of gold. The light heart is like a pile of cotton wool. The bhāvas act like wind in relation to these two types of hearts.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
gambhīraṃ sindhuvac cittam uttānaṃ palvalādivat |
citta-dvaye ’tra bhāvasya mahādri-śikharopamā ||261||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The deep heart is like the ocean, and the shallow heart is like a pond. The bhāvas are like pinnacles of high mountains for these two types of hearts.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pattanābhaṃ mahiṣṭhaṃ syāt kṣodiṣṭhaṃ tu kuṭiravat |
citta-yugme ’tra bhāvasya dīpenebhena vopamā ||262||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The expansive heart is like a city and the small heart is like a hut. Bhāva is like a lamp or an elephant for these two types of hearts.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
karkaśaṃ trividhaṃ proktaṃ vajraṃ svarṇaṃ tathā jatu |
citta-traye ’tra bhāvasya jñeyā vaiśvānaropamā ||263||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are three degrees of hardness: like a thunderbolt, gold and lac. Bhāva is like fire in relation to these three types of hard heart.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atyanta-kaṭhinaṃ vajram akutaścana mārdavam |
īdṛśaṃ tāpasādīnāṃ cittaṃ tāvad avekṣyate ||264||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The thunderbolt is extremely hard and never becomes soft. This hardness is seen in the hearts of those who perform severe austerities.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
svarṇaṃ dravati bhāvāgnes tāpenātigarīyasā |
jatu dravatvam āyāti tāpa-leśena sarvataḥ ||265||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Gold becomes liquid from intense heating. By the very strong heat of bhāva, this heart becomes soft. Lac becomes soft with very little heat. With a little bhāva, this heart becomes soft.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
komalaṃ ca tridhaivoktaṃ madanaṃ navanītakam |
amṛtaṃ ceti bhāvo ’tra prāyaḥ sūryātapāyate ||266||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Softness is of three degrees: like bee’s wax, butter and nectar. In relation to these, bhāva is like the heat of the sun.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
draved atrādya-yugalam ātapena yathāyatham |
dravībhūtaṃ svabhāvena sarvadaivāmṛtaṃ bhavet |
govinda-preṣṭha-varyāṇāṃ cittaṃ syād amṛtaṃ kila ||267||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Bee’s wax and butter become liquid from different degrees of heat from the sun. Nectar is naturally liquid. The hearts of the dearest devotees of Govinda are naturally soft like nectar.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kṛṣṇa-bhakti-viśeṣasya gariṣṭhatvādibhir guṇaiḥ |
samavetaṃ sadāmībhir dvitrair api mano bhavet ||268||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The mind of some devotee may be constantly influenced by a mixture of two or three of the above conditions such as gariṣṭha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kintu suṣṭhu mahiṣṭhatvaṃ bhāvo bāḍham upāgataḥ |
sarva-prakāram evedaṃ cittaṃ vikṣobhayaty alam ||269||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: But when the principal sthāyi-bhāva becomes very prominent, all types of hearts become completely disturbed by the vyabhicāri-bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā dāna-keli-kaumudyām (4) –
gabhīro ’py aśrāntaṃ duradhigama-pāro ’pi nitarām
ahāryāṃ maryādāṃ dadhad api harer āspadam api |
satāṃ stomaḥ premaṇy udayati samagre sthagayituṃ
vikāraṃ na sphāraṃ jala-nidhir ivendau prabhavati ||270||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The devotee is like the ocean. As Viṣṇu resides in the milk ocean, so the Lord resides in the heart of the devotee. As the ocean is deep or unfathomable, the heart of the devotee is inscrutable, not revealing its qualities. As the ocean is unwearied, so the devotee is continuous in his service. As the ocean is difficult to cross, but has a permanent shore, so the devotee’s qualities are difficult to enumerate, but he appears to limit those qualities. But when the devotee develops full prema he cannot prevent the transformations arising from that prema, just as, the ocean cannot prevent the rising of the tide when the moon rises from the ocean

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
aviruddhān viruddhāṃś ca bhāvān yo vaśatāṃ nayan |
su-rājeva virājeta sa sthāyī bhāva ucyate ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That bhāva which, controlling other favorable bhāvas such as hāsya and contradictory bhāvas such as krodha, presides in the manner of an efficient ruler, is called the sthāyi-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sthāyī bhāvo ’tra sa proktaḥ śrī-kṛṣṇa-viṣayā ratiḥ |
mukhyā gauṇī ca sā dvedhā rasa-jñaiḥ parikīrtitā ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In this context, the rati directed towards Kṛṣṇa is called the sthāyi-bhāva. Those knowledgeable of rasa say that there are two types of sthāyi-bhāva: mukhya (primary) and gauṇa (secondary).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra mukhyā –
śuddha-sattva-viśeṣātmā ratir mukhyeti kīrtitā |
mukhyāpi dvi-vidhā svārthā parārthā ceti kīrtyate ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Mukhya-rati (Primary rati)

A rati which is śuddha-sattva-viśeṣātmā (composed of the hlādinī and saṃvit śaktis) is called a primary rati. Though this is the primary rati, it takes two forms: svārtha and parārtha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra svārthā –
aviruddhaiḥ sphuṭaṃ bhāvaiḥ puṣṇāty ātmānam eva yā |
viruddhair duḥkha-glāniḥ sā svārthā kathitā ratiḥ ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That primary rati which clearly nourishes itself with non-contradictory bhāvas and becomes intolerably depressed with sorrow by contradictory bhāvas is called the svārtha-rati (nourishing itself).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha parārthā –
aviruddhaṃ viruddhaṃ ca saṅkucantī svayaṃ ratiḥ |
yā bhāvam anugṛhṇāti sā parārthā nigadyate ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The same primary rati which, restricting itself, accepts both non-contradictory and contradictory bhāvas (which then become prominent), is called parārtha (nourishing the other bhāvas).[227]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śuddhā prītis tathā sakhyaṃ vātsalyaṃ priyatety asau |
svaparārthyaiva sā mukhyā punaḥ pañca-vidhā bhavet ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A primary rati in these two forms has five varieties: śuddha, prīti (or dāsya), sakhya, vatsala (or vātsalya) and priyatā (or madhura).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vaiśiṣṭyaṃ pātra-vaiśiṣṭyād ratir eṣopagacchati |
yathārkaḥ pratibimbātmā sphaṭikādiṣu vastuṣu ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The rati takes on a specific type (one of the five) according to the individual nature of the devotee. Just as the sun takes on various forms when reflected through crystals and other items, the rati takes on various forms when manifesting in different individuals.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra śuddhā –
sāmānyāsau tathā svacchā śāntiś cety ādimā tridhā |
eṣāṅga-kampatā-netrāmīlanonmīlanādi-kṛt ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Śuddha-rati:

The first rati, called śuddha-rati, has three types: sāmānya, svaccha and śānta.[228] It produces quivering of the body, and closing and opening of the eyes.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra sāmānyā –
kañcid viśeṣam aprāptā sādhāraṇa-janasya yā |
bālikadaiś ca kṛṣṇe syāt sāmānyā sā ratir matā ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sāmānya-śuddha-rati:

The rati manifested in ordinary people and children for Kṛṣṇa is called sāmānya-rati or ordinary rati. It does not have the distinguishing qualities of even svaccha-rati or śānta-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
asmin mathurā-vīthyām udayati madhure virocane purataḥ |
kathasva sakhe mradimānaṃ mānasa-madanaṃ kim eti mama ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

O friend! Please tell me why my mind, like bee’s wax, has become very soft. Is it because the sweet sun, Kṛṣṇa, has risen on the street in Mathurā in front of me? I can see no other cause for this happening.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
tri-varṣā bālikā seyaṃ varṣīyasi samīkṣyatām |
yā puraḥ kṛṣṇam ālokya huṅkurvaty abhidhāvati ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O old woman! See that child, just three years old, who, on seeing Kṛṣṇa in front of her, is running after Him and calling out.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha svacchā –
tat-tat-sādhanato nānā-vidha-bhakti-prasaṅgataḥ |
sādhākānāṃ tu vaividhyaṃ yāntī svacchā ratir matā ||12||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Svaccha-śuddha-rati:

When rati manifests many varieties because the practitioner associates with various types of devotees and performs various practices, it is called svaccha-rati (transparent).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yadā yādṛśī bhakte syād āsaktis tādṛśaṃ tadā |
rūpaṃ sphaṭikavad dhatte svacchāsau tena kīrtitā ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When a devotee’s rati, like a clear crystal, becomes similar in form to that of the devotee to whom he is attached, it is called svaccha-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kvacit prabhur iti stuvan kvacana mitram ity uddhasan
kvacit tanaya ity avan kvacana kānta ity ullasan |
kvacin manasi bhāvayan parama eṣa ātmety asāv
abhūd vividha-sevayā vividha-vṛttir āryo dvijaḥ ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

One brāhmaṇa fixed on following the injunctions of scripture sometimes praised the Lord as master, sometimes joked with Him as a friend, sometimes protected Him as his son, sometimes craved for Him as a lover, and sometimes meditated in his heart on the Lord as Paramātmā. In this way, by various modes of service, he was endowed with various inclinations of mind.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
anācānta-dhiyāṃ tat-tad-bhāva-niṣṭhā sukhārṇave |
āryāṇām atiśuddhānāṃ prāyaḥ svacchā ratir bhavet ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those very pure persons whose hearts are fickle because of lack of a particular taste for the ocean of happiness of the higher rasas and who thus must conduct themselves according to the rules of scriptures generally develop svaccha-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha śāntiḥ –
mānase nirvikalpatvaṃ śama ity abhidhīyate ||16||

tatha coktam –
vihāya viṣayonmukhyaṃ nijānanda-sthitir yataḥ
ātmanaḥ kathyate so ’tra svabhāvaḥ śama ity asau ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
It is said by the ancients:

That nature by which a person is situated in the bliss of his own ātmā after giving up pursuit of material objects is called śama.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prāyaḥ śama-pradhānānāṃ mamatā-gandha-varjitā |
paramātmatayā kṛṣṇe jātā śānta-ratir matā ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The rati which arises in persons with a predominance of śama (ātmā-jñāna), which is devoid of even a trace of possessiveness for the Lord, but which produces attraction for Kṛṣṇa in the form of Paramātmā, is called śānta-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
devarṣi-vīṇayā pīte hari-līlā-mahotsave |
sanakasya tanau kampo brahmānubhavino ’py abhūt ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

When Nārada sang about the pastimes of the Lord on his vīṇā, Sanaka’s body began to tremble, even though he was a brahma-jñānī.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
hari-vallabha-sevayā samantād
apara-vargānubhavaṃ kilāvadhīrya |
ghana-sundaram ātmano ’py abhīṣṭaṃ
paramaṃ brahma didṛkṣate mano me ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Because of serving the devotees, I have given up the happiness of liberation as insignificant and, surpassing the impersonal brahman, I desire to see the dark-hued Lord, the highest form of brahman.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
agrato vakṣyamāṇais tu svādaiḥ prīty-ādi-saṃśrayaiḥ |
rater asyā asamparkād iyaṃ śuddheti bhaṇyate ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That rati which is not mixed with the tastes found in the other types of rati starting with prīti-rati, which will be explained later, is called śuddha-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha bheda-trayī hṛdyā rateḥ prīty-ādir īryate |
gāḍhānukūlatotpannā mamatvena sadāśritā ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The three types of rati –prīti, sakhya and vatsala—are pleasing to the heart. They arise from deep friendliness to the Lord and are always endowed with possessiveness of the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kṛṣṇa-bhakteṣv anugrāhya-sakhi-pūjyeṣv anukramāt |
tri-vidheṣu trayī prītiḥ sakhyaṃ vatsalatety asau ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When rati (with deep friendship and possessiveness) is found in three types of devotees—recipients of mercy, friends and elders—it becomes prīti-rati, sakhya-rati and vatsala-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra netrādi-phullatva-jṛmbhaṇodghūrṇanādayaḥ |
kevalā saṅkulā ceti dvi-vidheyaṃ rati-trayī ||24||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In these three types of rati there is opening of the eyes, stretching the limbs and unsteadiness. These three types have two varieties: kevala and saṅkula.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra kevalā –
raty-antarasya gandhena varjitā kevalā bhavet |
vrajānuge rasālādau śrīdāmādau vayasyake |
gurau ca vrajanāthādau krameṇaiva sphuraty asau ||25||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Kevala-rati:

When the rati has no trace of other types of rati it is called kevala-rati (pure rati). In Vraja, it is found in Kṛṣṇa’s servants such as Rasāla, in friends such as Śrīdāma and elders such as Nanda.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra saṅkulā –
eṣāṃ dvayos trayāṇāṃ vā sannipātas tu saṅkulā |
udbhavādau ca bhīmādau mukharādau krameṇa sā |
yasyādhikyaṃ bhaved yatra sa tena vyapadiśyate ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Saṅkula-rati:

When two or three of the three types of rati are found together in a person, it is called saṅkula-rati (mixed rati). It is found in Uddhava, Bhīma and Mukharā. A person is identified by the rati which is most prominent.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha prītiḥ –
svasmād bhavanti ye nyūnās te ’nugrāhyā harer matāḥ |
ārādhyatvātmikā teṣāṃ ratiḥ prītir itīritā ||27||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Prīti-rati:

When persons identify themselves as inferior to the Lord they are called the recipients of mercy (anugrāhyā). Their rati, in which Kṛṣṇa is perceived as worthy of worship, is called prīti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: tatrāsakti-kṛd anyatra prīti-saṃhāriṇī hy asau ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This prīti-rati produces attachment for the object of worship and destroys affection for other objects.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā mukunda-mālāyām (8)–
divi vā bhuvi vā mamāstu vāso
narake vā narakāntaka prakāmam |
avadhīrita-śāradāravindau
caraṇau te maraṇe ’pi cintayāmi ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Mukunda-mālā:

O destroyer of the demon Naraka! Wherever I live, according to Your desire, in heaven, on earth or in hell, I will remember even at the time of death Your two feet whose beauty defeats the lotuses blooming in the autumn season.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sakhyam –
ye syus tulyā mukundasya te sakhāyaḥ satāṃ matāḥ |
sāmyād viśrambha-rūpaiṣāṃ ratiḥ sakhyam ihocyate |
parihāsa-prahāsādi-kāriṇīyam ayantraṇā ||30||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sakhya-rati:

Those who identify themselves as equal to Mukunda are called sakhas or friends. Their rati, with familiarity arising from the sense of equality, is called sakhya-rati. In this rati, there is loud laughing, joking and no sense of reserve.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
māṃ puṣpitāraṇya-didṛkṣayāgataṃ
nimeṣa-viśleṣa-vidīrṇa-mānasāḥ |
te saṃspṛśantaḥ pulakāñcita-śriyo
dūrād ahaṃpūrvikayādya remire ||31||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

Today, when I went to see the flowering forests of Vṛndāvana, my friends were suffering by even a moment’s separation from Me. From far off, they said, “I will be touched by Him first! I will be touched by Him first!” With hairs standing on end, they played in this way.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
śrīdāma-dor-vilasitena kṛto ’si kāmaṃ
dāmodara tvam iha darpa-dhurā daridraḥ |
sadyas tvayā tad api kathanam eva kṛtvā
devyai hriye trayam adāyi jvalāñjalīnām ||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Śrīdāma said: Your pride has become impoverished enough in being defeated by the strength of my arms. Having boasted, say goodbye to the queen called shame (and defeat me).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vātsalyam –
guravo ye harer asya te pūjyā iti viśrutāḥ |
anugraha-mayī teṣāṃ ratir vātsalyam ucyate |
idaṃ lālana-bhavyāśīś cibuka-sparśanādi-kṛt ||33||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Vatsala-rati:

Those persons whose rati identifies them as superiors to the Lord are known as pūjya, worthy of respect or elders. Their rati, which gives mercy to Kṛṣṇa, is called vātsalya or vatsala. In this rati, there is protecting Kṛṣṇa, blessing Him, kissing Him and touching Him.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
agrāsi yan-nirabhisandhi-virodha-bhājaḥ
kaṃsasya kiṅkara-gaṇair girito ’py udagraiḥ |
gās tatra rakṣitum asau gahane mṛdur me
bālaḥ prayāty avirataṃ bata kiṃ karomi ||34||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

The forest is filled with inimical Kaṃsa’s servants, who are more solid than mountains. In that dense forest, my tender boy goes constantly. Oh! What should I do?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
sutam aṅgulibhiḥ snuta-stanī
cibukāgre dadhatī dayārdra-dhīḥ |
samalālayad ālayāt puraḥ
sthiti-bhājaṃ vraja-rāja-gehinī ||35||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Yaśodā, whose heart was soft with affection and whose breasts flowed with milk, caressed her son Kṛṣṇa, while holding His chin in her fingers.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha priyatā:
mitho harer mṛgākṣyāś ca sambhogasyādi-kāraṇam |
madhurāpara-paryāyā priyatākhyoditā ratiḥ |
asyāṃ kaṭākṣa-bhrū-kṣepa-priya-vāṇī-smitādayaḥ ||36||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Priyatā-rati:

That rati found in the doe-eyed women and which is the root cause of eight types of enjoyment between the women and Kṛṣṇa is called priyatā-rati. It is also called madhura-rati. In this rati, there are side-long glances, moving the eyebrows, and affectionate words and slight smiles etc.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā govinda-vilāse –
ciram utkaṇṭhita-manaso rādhā-mura-vairiṇoḥ ko ’pi |
nibhṛta-nirīkṣaṇa-janmā pratyāśā-pallavo jayati ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Govinda-vilāsa:

For a long time, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa have been longing to see each other. All glories to the new sprout of hope of being able to see each other alone

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathottaram asau svāda-viśeṣollāsamayy api |
ratir vāsanayā svādvī bhāsate kāpi kasyacit ||38||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: These five types of rati progressively (from śuddha to priyatā-rati) become more blissful by increasing tastes. The particular taste arises in a devotee according to his previous experiences.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha gauṇī –
vibhāvotkarṣajo bhāva-viśeṣo yo ’nugṛhyate |
saṅkucantyā svayaṃ ratyā sa gauṇī ratir ucyate ||39||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Gauṇa-rati (Secondary Rati):

When a different emotional state arising from the excellence of the ālambana (vibhāva) manifests while the primary rati subdues itself, it is called a secondary rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
hāso vismaya utsāhaḥ śokaḥ krodho bhayaṃ tathā |
jugupsā cety asau bhāva-viśeṣaḥ saptadhoditaḥ ||40||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The seven specific emotions are hāsa (humor), vismaya (astonishment), utsāha (fortitude), śoka (lamentation), krodha (anger), bhaya (fear) and jugupsā (disgust or hatred).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
api kṛṣṇa-vibhāvatvam ādya-ṣaṭkasya sambhavet |
syād dehādi-vibhāvatvaṃ saptamyās tu rater vaśāt ||41||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Since they are under the control of the primary ratis, Kṛṣṇa acts as the cause for the first six of these bhāvas, but Kṛṣṇa cannot be the cause of the seventh secondary rati, jugupsā or disgust. The cause of disgust is the material body or other objects.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
hāsādāv atra bhinne ’pi śuddha-sattva-viśeṣataḥ |
parārthāyā rater yogād rati-śabdaḥ prayujyate ||42||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though these seven bhāvas are different from the primary svārtha-ratis composed of śuddha-sattva-viśeṣa (mukhya-svārtha-ratis), when these seven emotions are conjoined with a primary rati which takes a secondary role as parārtha, the word rati is used to describe the condition of these seven.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
hāsottarā ratir yā syāt sā hāsa-ratir ucyate |
evaṃ vismaya-raty-ādyā vijñeyā ratayaś ca ṣaṭ ||43||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When hāsa predominates over a mukhya-rati (which becomes parārtha), it is called hāsa-rati. The other six secondary ratis should be similarly understood.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kañcit kālaṃ kvacid bhakte hāsādyāḥ sthāyitām amī |
ratyā cāru-kṛtā yānti tal-līlādy-anusārataḥ ||44||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When hāsa and other emotions take on beautiful forms by the influence of a primary rati in a particular devotee in a particular pastime, and remain for some time, they can be considered sthāyi-bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tasmād aniyatādhārāḥ sapta sāmayikā ime |
sahajā api līyante baliṣṭhena tiraskṛtāḥ ||45||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Therefore, these seven emotional states manifest for a short time in a person, and are not fixed in any particular person.[233] Even though these seven emotions manifest spontaneously, they disappear by being covered by contrary bhāvas arising from the primary rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kāpy avyabhicarantī sā svādhārān sva-svarūpataḥ |
ratir ātyantika-sthāyī bhāvo bhakta-jane ’khile |
syur etasyā vinā-bhāvād bhāvāḥ sarve nirarthakāḥ ||46||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When a primary rati in its essential form does not leave the devotee, it is considered to be the continuous or ātyantika-sthāyi-bhāva. This is present in all types of devotees. Without that continuous sthāyi-bhāva, all other bhāvas such as hāsa are dysfunctional.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vipakṣādiṣu yānto ’pi krodhādyāḥ sthāyitāṃ sadā |
labhante rati-śūnyatvān na bhakti-rasa-yogyatām ||47||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though the secondary bhāvas become sthāyi-bhāvas in the enemies of Kṛṣṇa, they are not suitable for bhakti-rasa since they are without a primary rati (positive attraction to Kṛṣṇa).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
aviruddhair api spṛṣṭā bhāvaiḥ sañcāriṇo ’khilāḥ |
nirvedādyā vilīyante nārhanti sthāyitāṃ tataḥ ||48||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Because all the thirty-three vyabhicāri-bhāvas starting with nirveda, though not contaminated with hostile emotions, disappear on their own after some time in the devotees, they are not classed as sthāyi-bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ity ato mati-garvādi-bhāvānāṃ ghaṭate na hi |
sthāyitā kaiścid iṣṭāpi pramāṇaṃ tatra tad-vidaḥ ||49||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though some persons would like to consider mati, garva and other vyabhicāri-bhāvas to be sthāyi-bhāvas, they are not classed as such. Bharata Muni and others are the authority for this statement.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sapta hāsādayas tv ete tais tair nītāḥ supuṣṭatām |
bhakteṣu sthāyitāṃ yānto rucir ebhyo vitanvate ||50||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The seven secondary emotions, being nourished greatly by vibhāvas, anubhāvas, sāttvika-bhāvas and vyabhicāri-bhāvas, take up the status of sthāyi-bhāvas in the devotees and produce a taste in the devotees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathā coktam –
aṣṭānām eva bhāvānāṃ saṃskārādhāyitā matā |
tat-tiraskṛta-saṃskārāḥ pare na sthāyitocitāḥ ||51||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Therefore, it is said:

In a devotee, one of the five primary sthāyi-bhāvas and the seven secondary bhāvas, together making eight bhāvas, produce lasting impressions (even though they may externally disappear for some time). Since the impressions of the vyabhicāri-bhāvas disappear after they are covered by these eight, the vyabhicāri-bhāvas are not considered to be sthāyi-bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra hāsa-ratiḥ –
ceto-vikāso hāsaḥ syād vāg-veṣehādi-vaikṛtāt |
sa dṛg-vikāsana-sauṣṭha-kapola-spandanādikṛt ||52||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Hāsa-rati:

When there is cheerfulness of the heart from irregularity of speech, dress or actions, it is called hāsa. In this state, the symptoms are opening of the eyes fully, and quivering of the nose, lips and cheeks.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kṛṣṇa-sambandhi-ceṣṭotthaḥ svayaṃ saṅkucad-ātmanā |
ratyānugṛhyamāṇo ’yaṃ hāso hāsa-ratir bhavet ||53||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When hāsa arises from actions related to Kṛṣṇa and the primary rati assumes a subdued role, hāsa becomes hāsa-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
mayā dṛg api nārpitā sumukhi dadhni tubhyaṃ śape
sakhī tava nirargalā tad api me mukhaṃ jighrati |
praśādhi tad imāṃ mudhā cchalita-sādhum ity acyute
vadaty ajani dūtikā hasita-rodhane na kṣamā ||54||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

Kṛṣṇa said, “O beautiful woman! I swear to you that I have not even looked at the yogurt. But your bold friend is sniffing my mouth in vain. Give instructions to your friend so that she does not accuse innocent persons like me.” When the gopī servant heard these words, she could not restrain her laughter.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vismaya-ratiḥ –
lokottarārtha-vīkṣāder vismayaś citta-vistṛtiḥ |
atra syur netra-vistāra-sādhūkti-pulakādayaḥ |
pūrvokta-rītyā niṣpannaḥ sa vismaya-ratir bhavet ||55||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Vismaya-rati:

On seeing something unusual the mind may inquire “What could this be?” This disposition is called vismaya or wonder. In this state the symptoms are widening of the eyes, uttering words like “very good very good” and standing of hairs on end. The relation of vismaya to vismaya-rati is the same as hāsa to hāsa-rati.[236]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
gavāṃ gopālānām api śiśu-gaṇaḥ pīta-vasano
lasac-chrīvatsāṅkaḥ pṛthu-bhuja-catuṣkair dhṛta-ruciḥ |
kṛta-stotrārambhaḥ sa vidhibhir ajāṇḍālibhir alaṃ
para-brahmollāsān vahati kim idaṃ hanta kim idam ||56||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

When Brahmā saw all the calves and cowherd boys manifest as forms of the Supreme Brahman--as Nārāyaṇa forms with four hands wearing yellow garments and marked with the śrīvatsa, who were being praised by all the inhabitants of the universes including many Brahmās--he became astonished and uttered, “What is this? What is this?”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha utsāha-ratiḥ –
stheyasī sādhubhiḥ ślāghya-phale yuddhādi-karmaṇi |
satvarā mānasāsaktir utsāha iti kīrtyate ||57||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Utsāha-rati:

Firm and immediate attachment of the mind to activities such as battle, charity, compassion and dharma, whose results are praised by the saintly people, is called utsāha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kālānavekṣaṇaṃ tatra dhairya-tyāgodyamādayaḥ |
siddhaḥ pūrvokta-vidhinā sa utsāha ratir bhavet ||58||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In this state, impulsiveness (not looking back in time), impatience and eagerness manifest. Utsāha relates to utsāha-rati in the same way hāsa relates to hāsa-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kālindī-taṭa-bhuvi patra-śṛṅga-vaṃśī
nikvāṇair iha mukharī-kṛtāmbarāyām |
visphūrjann agha-damanena yoddhu-kāmaḥ
śrīdāmā parikaram udbhaṭaṃ babandha ||59||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

When the air resounded with the sound of the flute, horn and patra (blades of grass) on the bank of the Yamunā, Śrīdāma, desiring to fight with Kṛṣṇa, began roaring, and bound up his waist firmly.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha śoka-ratiḥ –
śokas tv iṣṭa-viyogādyaiś citta-kleśa-bhavaḥ smṛtaḥ |
vilāpa-pāta-niḥśvāsa-mukha-śoṣa-bhramādi-kṛt |
pūrvokta-vidhinaivāyaṃ siddhaḥ śoka-ratir bhavet ||60||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Śoka-rati:

Intense pain in the heart arising from separation from a dear one with thoughts that the beloved has perished is called śoka or lamentation. In this state wailing, falling on the ground, heavy breathing, drying of the mouth, and confusion manifest. Śoka has the same relation to śoka-rati as hāsa to hāsa-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrī-daśame –
ruditam anu niśamya tatra gopyo
bhṛśam anutapta-dhiyo ’śru-pūrṇa-mukhyaḥ |
rurudur anupalabhya nanda-sūnuṃ
pavana upārata-pāṃśu-varṣa-vege ||61||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from the Tenth Canto:

When the force of the dust storm and the winds subsided, Yaśodā’s friends, the other gopīs, approached mother Yaśodā, hearing her pitiful crying. Not seeing Kṛṣṇa present, they too felt very much aggrieved and joined mother Yaśodā in crying, their eyes full of tears. SB 10.7.25

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
avalokya phaṇīndra-yantritaṃ
tanayaṃ prāṇa-sahasra-vallabham |
hṛdayaṃ na vidīryati dvidhā
dhig imāṃ martya-tanoḥ kaṭhoratām ||62||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

My heart did not become shattered on seeing Kṛṣṇa, dearer that a thousand lives, bound up by Kāliya. How hard my heart is!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha krodha-ratiḥ –
prātikūlyādibhiś citta-jvalanaṃ krodha īryate |
pāruṣya-bhrū-kuṭī-netra-lauhityādi-vikāra-kṛt ||63||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Krodha-rati:

Flaming up of the heart from encountering opposition is called krodha or anger. In this state, rough behavior, frowning and reddening of the eyes manifest.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
evaṃ pūrvoktavat-siddhaṃ viduḥ krodha-ratiṃ budhāḥ |
dvidhāsau kṛṣṇa-tad-vairi-bhāvatvena kīrtitā ||64||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Krodha-rati arises from krodha in the same way that hāsa-rati arises from hāsa. It has two types: where the stimulus for krodha-rati is Kṛṣṇa and where the stimulus is the enemy of Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha kṛṣṇa-vibhāvāḥ, yathā –
kaṇṭha-sīmani harer dyuti-bhājaṃ
rādhikā-maṇi-saraṃ paricitya |
taṃ cireṇa jaṭilā vikaṭa-bhrū-
bhaṅga-bhīmatara-dṛṣṭir dadarśa ||65||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Anger stimulated by Kṛṣṇa:

When Jaṭilā recognized Rādhā’s shining pearl necklace around the throat of Kṛṣṇa, she frowned ferociously and glanced at Kṛṣṇa in a terrifying manner.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tad-vairi-vibhāvāḥ, yathā –
atha kaṃsa-sahodarogra-dāve
harim abhyudyati tīvra-heti-bhāji |
rabhasād alikāmbare pralamba-
dviṣato ’bhūd bhrū-kuṭī-payoda-rekhā ||66||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Anger stimulated by an enemy:

When the blazing forest fire, who was actually the brother of Kaṃsa, surrounded Kṛṣṇa, a frown of anger appeared on the forehead of Balarāma like a cloud bank in the sky.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha bhaya-ratiḥ –
bhayaṃ cittāticāñcalyaṃ mantu-ghorekṣaṇādibhiḥ |
ātma-gopana-hṛcchoṣa-vidrava-bhramaṇādikṛt ||67||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Bhaya-rati:

When the heart manifests extreme unsteadiness after committing an offense or seeing fearful creatures, it is called bhaya or fear. In this state, the attempt to hide oneself, drying up of the heart, fleeing and confusion manifest.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
niṣpannaṃ pūrvavad idaṃ budhā bhaya-ratiṃ viduḥ |
eṣāpi krodha-rativad dvi-vidhā kathitā budhaiḥ ||68||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wise say that bhaya is related to bhaya-rati just as hāsa is related to hāsa-rati. As with krodha, bhaya-rati has two types: bhaya whose cause is Kṛṣṇa and bhaya whose cause is the enemy of Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra kṛṣṇa-vibhāvāḥ –
yācitaḥ paṭimabhiḥ syamantakaṃ
śauriṇā sadasi gāndinī-sutaḥ |
vastra-gūḍha-maṇir eṣa mūḍha-dhīs
tatra śuṣyad-adharaḥ klamaṃ yayau ||69||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Kṛṣṇa as the cause of fear:

When Kṛṣṇa asked Akrura for the Syamantaka jewel in the assembly in a friendly way, Akrura, who was hiding the jewel in his clothing, could not answer. He became fearful of Kṛṣṇa, understanding that Kṛṣṇa knew he was hiding the jewel. His mouth dried up and he became sad.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
duṣṭa-vibhāva-jāḥ, yathā –
bhairavaṃ bruvati hanta hanta gokula-
dvāri vārida-nibhe vṛṣāsure |
putra-gupti-dhṛta-yatna-vaibhavā
kampra-mūrtir abhavad vrajeśvarī ||70||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Enemy of Kṛṣṇa as the cause of fear:

When Vṛṣāsura, like a storm cloud, roared in a frightening manner at the entrance of Gokula, Yaśodā, thinking of the protection of her son, began to tremble.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha jugupsā-ratiḥ –
jugupsā syād ahṛdyānubhavāc citta-nimīlanam |
tatra niṣṭhīvanaṃ vaktra-kūṇanaṃ kutsanādayaḥ |
rater anugrahāj jātā sā jugupsā-ratir matā ||71||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Jugupsā-rati:

Restriction of the heart arising from experiencing disgusting things is called jugupsā or disgust. In this state spitting, curling the lips, and uttering contemptuous words manifest. When this jugupsā appears because of rati, it is called jugupsā-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
yadavadhi mama cetaḥ kṛṣṇa-pādāravinde
nava-nava-rasa-dhāmany udyataṃ rantum āsīt |
tadavadhi bata nārī-saṅgame smaryamāne
bhavati mukha-vikāraḥ suṣṭhu-niṣṭhīvanaṃ ca ||72||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

Since my heart has become eager to play at the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa who is the abode of ever-fresh rasa, when I think of association with women, my mouth curls up in disgust and I spit.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ratitvāt prathamaikaiva sapta hāsādayas tathā |
ity aṣṭau sthāyino yāvad rasāvasthāṃ na saṃśritāḥ ||73||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: As long as one among the five primary ratis along with the seven secondary ratis does not attain the state of rasa in an individual, the eight are called sthāyi-bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
cet svatantrās trayas-triṃśad bhaveyur vyabhicāriṇaḥ |
ihāṣṭau sāttvikāś caite bhāvākhyās tān asaṅkhyakāḥ ||74||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If they remain independent, then the thirty-three vyabhicāri-bhāvas, the eight ratis mentioned above and the eight sāttvika-bhāvas are called forty-nine bhāvas or emotional states.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kṛṣṇānvayād guṇātīta-prauḍhānanda-mayā api |
bhānty amī triguṇotpanna-sukha-duḥkha-mayā iva ||75||
tatra sphuranti hrī-bodhotsāhādyāḥ sāttvikā iva |
tathā rājasavad-garva-harṣa-supti-hasādayaḥ |
viṣāda-dīnatā-moha-śokādyās tāmasā iva ||76||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: These forty-nine states of mind are completely transcendental to the guṇas of matter, and are filled with spiritual bliss, being linked to the appearance of Kṛṣṇa. However, it may appear as if some of these states such as garva (pride), harṣa (jubilation), supti (sleep) and hāsya (joking) arise from the mode of passion, and others such as viṣāda (despair), dīnatā (lowness), moha (bewilderment) and śoka (lamentation) arise from the mode of ignorance.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prāyaḥ sukha-mayāḥ śītā uṣṇā duḥkha-mayā iha |
citreyaṃ paramānanda-sāndrāpy uṣṇā ratir matā ||77||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among the bhāvas, it would seem that those such as harṣa are filled with happiness and others such as viṣāda are filled with sorrow. But the astonishing fact is that rati filled with sorrow is considered to be the highest, most intense bliss.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śītair bhāvair baliṣṭhais tu puṣṭā śītāyate hy asau |
uṣṇais tu ratir atyuṣṇā tāpayantīva bhāsate ||78||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The rati predominated by sorrow (such as śoka-rati), when nourished by strong joyful vyabhicāri-bhāvas, becomes joyful. The sorrowful rati, nourished by sorrowful bhāvas such as viṣāda, becomes more sorrowful, and appears to give suffering.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ratir dvidhāpi kṛṣṇādyaiḥ śrutair avagataiḥ smṛtaiḥ |
tair vibhāvāditāṃ yadbhis tad-bhakteṣu raso bhavet ||79||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The primary and secondary ratis produce vibhāvas, anubhāvas, sāttvika-bhāvas and vyabhicāri-bhāvas through hearing, experiencing or remembering Kṛṣṇa. All of these combine to become rasa in the devotees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā dadhy-ādikaṃ dravyaṃ śarkarā-maricādibhiḥ |
saṃyojana-viśeṣeṇa rasālākhyo raso bhavet ||80||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: As yogurt becomes rasāla by mixing with the particular ingredients of sugar and pepper, the two types of rati become rasa by combination with the elements of vibhāva, anubhāva, sāttvika-bhāva and vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tad atra sarvathā sākṣāt kṛṣṇādy-anubhavādbhutaḥ |
prauḍhānanda-camatkāro bhaktaiḥ ko ’py anurasyate ||81||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Thus through that rasa, the devotees directly experience an astonishing, deep bliss arising from realization of Kṛṣṇa and related things.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sa raty-ādi-vibhāvādyair ekībhāva-mayo ’pi san |
jñapta-tat-tad-viśeṣaś ca tat-tad-udbhedato bhavet ||82||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though the ratis and elements starting with vibhāva become one entity in the state of rasa, there is still awareness of their difference because of their original separate identities.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā coktam –
pratīyamānāḥ prathamaṃ vibhāvādyās tu bhāgaśaḥ |
kgacchanto rasa-rūpatvaṃ militā yānty akhaṇḍatām ||83||
yathā marica-khaṇḍāder ekībhāve prapānake |
udbhāsaḥ kasyacit kvāpi vibhāvādes tathā rase ||84||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
It has been said:

First, the ingredients have distinct forms but when they mix and attain the form of rasa, they assume oneness. However, when pepper and sugar are mixed together in a drink one can still recognize pepper and sugar. Similarly, in rasa though vibhāva and the other elements become one entity in rasa, they can be recognized in a subtle form.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rateḥ kāraṇa-bhūtā ye kṛṣṇa-kṛṣṇa-priyādayaḥ |
stambhādyāḥ kāra-bhūtāś ca nirvedādyāḥ sahāyakāḥ ||85||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa and His devotees act as the causes of rati (sthāyi-bhāva). Spontaneous actions such as paralysis and actions involving intellect are the effects of rati. Self-deprecation and other minor emotions are the accompanying factors.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
hitvā kāraṇa-kāryādi-śabda-vācyatvam atra te |
rasodbodhe vibhāvādi-vyapadeśatvam āpnuyuḥ ||86||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When these mix together and transform into rasa they give up the names of cause and effect and assume the names of vibhāva, anubhāva, sāttvika-bhāva and vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rates tu tat-tad-āsvāda-viśeṣāyātiyogyatām |
vibhāvayanti kurvantīty uktā dhīrair vibhāvakāḥ ||87||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those conditions which cause (vibhāvayanti) rati (relationship of love) to become very suitable for relishing particular tastes are called vibhāva (excitants) by the wise.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tāṃ cānubhāvayanty antas tanvanty āsvāda-nirbharām |
ity uktā anubhāvās te kaṭākṣādyāḥ sa-sāttvikāḥ ||88||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those elements such as glancing, accompanied by the sāttvika-bhāvas, which produce fullness of the rati produced by the vibhāva--in other words, which spread within the mind an additional relish (anubhāvayanti)--are called anubhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sañcārayanti vaicitrīṃ nayante tāṃ tathā-vidhām |
ye nirvedādayo bhāvās te tu sañcāriṇo matāḥ ||89||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those mental conditions such as nirveda (self-deprecation) which produce further variety in the rati (sañcārayanti) induced by the vibhāvas and made more enjoyable by the anubhāvas, are called sañcāri-bhāvas or vyabhicāri-bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eteṣāṃ tu tathā-bhāve bhagavat-kāvya-nāṭyayoḥ |
sevām āhuḥ paraṃ hetuṃ kecit tat-pakṣa-rāgiṇaḥ ||90||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those attracted to poetics say that hearing skillful poetry related to the Lord and seeing literary dramas related to the Lord are the main causes for realizing the nature of all these elements in the devotee and Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kintu tatra sudustarka-mādhuryādbhuta-sampadaḥ |
rater asyāḥ prabhāvo ’yaṃ bhavet kāraṇam uttamam ||91||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: However, the ultimate cause of understanding these elements is the influence of rati directed to the Lord, which is inconceivable, sweet, and most astonishing.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mahā-śakti-vilāsātmā bhāvo ’cintya-svarūpa-bhāk |
raty-ākhyā ity ayaṃ yukto na hi tarkeṇa bādhitum |
bhāratādy-uktir eṣā hi prāktanair apy udāhṛtā ||92||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The ancient authorities have given the following statement from Mahābhārata as an example to show that rati, which is the manifestation of the hlādinī śakti, which is beyond the conceivable--deriding even liberation and giving joy to the Lord Himself--should not be defiled by material logic.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathoktam udyama-parvaṇi –
acintyāḥ khalu ye bhāvā na tāṃs tarkeṇa yojayet
prakṛtibhyaḥ paraṃ yac ca tad acintyasya lakṣaṇam ||93||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Thus the Mahābhārata, Udyama-parva says:

One should not analyze the inconceivable bhāvas by material logic. Those bhāvas which are beyond the material realm are said to be inconceivable.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vibhāvatādīn ānīya kṛṣṇādīn mañjulā ratiḥ |
etair eva tathā bhūtaiḥ svaṃ saṃvardhayati sphuṭam ||94||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Attractive rati, making Kṛṣṇa and other things into vibhāva and the other elements, clearly increases itself by these elements.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā svair eva salilaiḥ paripūrya balāhakān |
ratnālayo bhavaty ebhir vṛṣṭais tair eva vāridhiḥ ||95||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: It is similar to the ocean which, nourishing the clouds by its water, nourishes itself by rain coming from the clouds.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nave raty-aṅkure jāte hari-bhaktasya kasyacit |
vibhāvatvādi-hetutvaṃ kiñcit tat kāvya-nāṭyayoḥ ||96||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the enjoyer of poetic works newly develops a sprout of rati, those poetic works become somewhat of a cause for realizing vibhāva and the other elements.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
harer īṣac-chruti-vidhau rasāsvādaḥ satāṃ bhavet |
rater eva prabhāvo ’yaṃ hetus teṣāṃ tathākṛtau ||97||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The devotees develop a taste for rasa simply by a little hearing about the Lord. In these acts of hearing, the strength of rati causes realization of vibhāva and the other elements.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mādhuryādy-āśrayatvena kṛṣṇādīṃs tanute ratiḥ |
tathānubhūyamānās te vistīrṇāṃ kurvate ratim ||98||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Rati reveals Kṛṣṇa and things related to Him as the shelter of qualities (such as sweetness), and Kṛṣṇa, after being experienced in that way, increases the rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atas tasya vibhāvādi-catuṣkasya rater api |
atra sāhāyikaṃ vyaktaṃ mitho’jasram avekṣyate ||99||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Because rati and the other elements mutually reveal each other in this way, it is always seen that the sthāyi-bhāva (rati), vibhāvas, anubhāvas, sāttvika-bhāvas and vyabhicāri-bhāvas clearly assist each other.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kintv etasyāḥ prabhāvo ’pi vairūpye sati kuñcati |
vairūpyas tu vibhāvāder anaucityam udīryate ||100||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If there is any deformity in the vibhāva or other elements, the power of rati is curtailed. Deformity means that there are unsuitable qualities in vibhāva and other elements.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
alaukikyā prakṛtyeyaṃ sudurūhā rasa-sthitiḥ |
yatra sādhāraṇatayā bhāvāḥ sādhu sphuranty amī ||101||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Since the activities of rasa are by nature non-material, they are difficult to understand. The various ratis and other elements create a complete identity between the emotions of the contemporary devotee with previous devotees depicted in scripture.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ sva-para-sambandha-niyamānirṇayo hi yaḥ |
sādhāraṇyaṃ tad evoktaṃ bhāvānāṃ pūrva-sūribhiḥ ||102||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The ancient sages have described the unrestricted identity of the bhāvas between the present and past devotees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tad uktaṃ śrī-bharatena –
śaktir asti vibhāvādeḥ kāpi sādhāraṇī-kṛtau |
pramātā tad-abhedena svaṃ yayā pratipadyate || iti | ||103||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Bharata Muni has said:

In the matter of identification, there is an indescribable power in vibhāva and the other elements, by which the audience becomes non-different from the characters depicted on the stage.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
duḥkhādayaḥ sphurantyo ’pi jātu bhāntaḥ svīyatayā hṛdi |
prauḍhānanda-camatkāra-carvaṇām eva tanvate ||104||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though previous devotees’ sufferings appear in the heart of the present devotee as his own suffering, those sufferings also produce an astonishing taste of intense bliss.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
parāśrayatayāpy ete jātu bhāntaḥ sukhādayaḥ |
hṛdaye paramānanda-sandoham upacinvate ||105||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That is because when the devotee perceives the happiness of others, it gives rise to incomparable bliss in his heart.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sad-bhāvaś ced vibhāvādeḥ kiñcin-mātrasya jāyate |
sadyaś catuṣṭayākṣepāt pūrṇataivopapadyate ||106||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If just a little of vibhāva and the other elements related to the associates of the Lord appear in the devotee, immediately he achieves completeness from the appearance of the vibhāvas, anubhāvas, sāttvika-bhāvas and vyabhicāri-bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kiṃ ca –
ratiḥ sthitānukāryeṣu laukikatvādi-hetubhiḥ |
rasaḥ syān neti nāṭya-jñā yad āhur yuktam eva tat ||107||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: However, it is correct when the literary experts say that rati depicted in characters through literary works will not in itself produce rasa, since mundane aspects are involved.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
alaukikī tv iyaṃ kṛṣṇa-ratiḥ sarvādbhutādbhutā |
yoge rasa-viśeṣatvaṃ gacchanty eva hari-priye ||108||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Rati for Kṛṣṇa is most uncommon, more blissful than the most blissful rati for the avatāras, and attains the highest rasa in combination with His devotee.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
viyoge tv adbhutānanda-vivartatvaṃ dadhaty api |
tanoty eṣā pragāḍhārti-bharābhāsatvam ūrjitā ||109||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In separation, this rati develops its full form of rasa in astonishing bliss, and since it does not give up this form at all, any suffering is an appearance only.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatrāpi vallavādhīśa-nandanālambanā ratiḥ |
sāndrānanda-camatkāra-paramāvadhir iṣyate ||110||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That rati which has the son of Nanda as the object reaches the height of most intense bliss.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yat-sukhaugha-lavāgastyaḥ pibaty eva sva-tejasā |
rameśa-mādhurī-sākṣātkārānandābdhim apy alam ||111||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: One drop of this happiness of Vraja Kṛṣṇa, by its power, drinks up the ocean of happiness embodied in the husband of Rukmiṇī, just as Agastya Muni drank the ocean to assist the devatās.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kiṃ ca –
paramānanda-tādātmyād ratyāder asya vastutaḥ |
rasasya sva-prakāśatvam akhaṇḍatvaṃ ca sidhyati ||112||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Because rati and the other elements are non-different from the hlādinī śakti, rasa also is self-revealing and consists only of rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pūrvam uktād dvidhā bhedān mukhya-gauṇatayā rateḥ |
bhaved bhakti-raso ’py eṣa mukhya-gauṇatayā dvidhā ||113||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: It has previously been stated that rati has two types: primary and secondary. Therefore, rasa also has primary and secondary types.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pañcadhāpi rater aikyān mukhyas tv eka ihoditaḥ |
saptadhātra tathā gauṇa iti bhakti-raso ’ṣṭadhā ||114||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though there are five types of primary rati, only one is considered since only one is manifested as most prominent in an individual devotee. The one primary rati combines with the seven secondary ratis to form eight ratis, which produce eight rasas (for one person).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra mukhyaḥ –
mukhyas tu pañcadhā śāntaḥ prītaḥ preyāṃś ca vatsalaḥ |
madhuraś cety amī jñeyā yathā-pūrvam anuttamāḥ ||115||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Mukhya-rasa (Primary Rasa):

The primary bhakti-rasas are five: śānta, prīti, preyo, vatsala and madhura. The order of excellence is from first to last.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha gauṇaḥ –
hāsyo ’dbhutas tathā vīraḥ karuṇo raudra ity api |
bhayānakaḥ sa bībhatsa iti gauṇaś ca saptadhā ||116||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Gauṇa-rasa (Secondary Rasa):

There are seven secondary rasas: hāsya (humor), adbhuta (astonishment), vīra (enthusiasm), karuṇa (lamentation), raudra (anger), bhayānaka (fear) and bībhatsa (disgust).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
evaṃ bhakti-raso bhedād dvayor dvādaśadhocyate |
vastutas tu purāṇādau pañcadhaiva vilokyate ||117||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Thus there are a total of twelve primary and secondary rasas, but in the Purāṇas only five are mentioned.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śvetaś citro ’ruṇaḥ śoṇaḥ śyāmaḥ pāṇḍura-piṅgalau |
gauro dhūmras tathā raktaḥ kālo nītaḥ kramād amī ||118||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The twelve rasas have twelve colors as follows: white (śānta), multi-colored (prīti), orange (preyān or sakhya),[246] crimson (vatsala), indigo (madhura), light yellow (hāsya), yellow or green (adbhuta),[247] gold (vīra),[248] purple (karuṇa), red (raudra), black (bhayānaka) and blue (bībhatsa).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kapilo mādhavopendrau nṛsiṃho nanda-nandanaḥ |
balaḥ kūrmas tathā kalkī rāghavo bhārgavaḥ kiriḥ |
mīna ity eṣu kathitāḥ kramād dvādaśa devatāḥ ||119||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are twelve deities assigned to the twelve rasas as follows: Kapila (śānta), Mādhava (prīti or dāsya),[249] Upendra (preyān or sakhya), Nṛsiṃha (vatsala), Kṛṣṇa (madhura), Balarāma (hāsya), Kūrma (adbhuta), Kalkī (vīra), Rāma (karuṇa), Parāśurāma (raudra), Varāha (bhayānaka) and Mīna (bībhatsa).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pūrter vikāra-vistāra-vikṣepa-kṣobhas tathā |
sarva-bhakti-rasāsvādaḥ pañcadhā parikīrtitaḥ ||120||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are five tastes in the bhakti-rasas: pūrti, vikāśa, vistāra, vikṣepa and kṣobha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pūrtiḥ śānte vikāśas tu prītādiṣv api pañcasu |
vīre ’dbhute ca vistāro vikṣepaḥ karuṇograyoḥ |
bhayānaka ’tha bībhatse kṣobho dhīrair udāhṛtaḥ ||121||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The learned say that pūrti (satisfaction) is manifested in śānta-rasa, vikāśa (brightness) is manifest in all the rasas from prīti to hāsya, vistāra (expansion) is manifest in vīra-rasa and adbhuta-rasa, vikṣepa (distraction) is manifest in karuṇa-rasa and raudra-rasa, and kṣobha (disturbance) is manifest in bhayānaka-rasa and bībhatsa-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
akhaṇḍa-sukha-rūpatve ’py eṣām asti kvacit kvacit |
raseṣu gahanāsvāda-viśeṣaḥ ko ’py anuttamaḥ ||122||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though all the bhakti-rasas are the embodiment of pure happiness, among the rasas there is sometimes a special, deep, incomparable taste.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pratīyamānā apy ajñair grāmyaiḥ sapadi duḥkhavat |
karuṇādyā rasāḥ prājñaiḥ prauḍhānanda-mayā matāḥ ||123||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though the completely ignorant people and those with mistaken knowledge think immediately that rasas such as karuṇa are full of grief, those persons with knowledge of rasa say that these rasas are full of profound bliss.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
alaukika-vibhāvatvaṃ nītebhyo rati-līlayā |
sad-uktyā ca sukhaṃ tebhyaḥ syāt suvyaktam iti sthitiḥ ||124||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: It is well established by the knowers of rasa that, by the speeches of the devotees and by the nature of rati itself, karuṇa, bhayanaka and bībhatsa will produce happiness, since those rasas have the nature of manifesting vibhāva (Kṛṣṇa) and other elements which produce extraordinary, astonishing bliss.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathā ca nāṭyādau –
karuṇādāv api rase jāyate yat paraṃ sukham |
sucetasām anubhavaḥ pramāṇaṃ tatra kevalam ||125||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
This is confirmed by statements in Nātya-śāstra:

The absolute proof that karuṇa and the other negative rasas produce happiness is the experience of the soft-hearted devotees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sarvatra karuṇākhyasya rasasyaivopapādanāt |
bhaved rāmāyaṇādīnām anyathā duḥkha-hetutā ||126||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If karuṇa-rasa did not give rise to happiness, then Rāmāyaṇa would be a cause of grief for the bhāvaka-bhaktas, since that and other works reveal karuṇa-rasa throughout.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathātve rāma-pādābja-prema-kallola-vāridhiḥ |
prītyā rāmāyaṇaṃ nityaṃ hanumān śṛṇuyāt katham ||127||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If Rāmāyaṇa is a cause of grief, why should Hanumān, who is an ocean filled with the waves of Rāma’s lotus feet, continually hear Rāmāyaṇa with pleasure?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
api ca –
sañcārī syāt samāno vā kṛṣṇa-ratyāḥ suhṛd-ratiḥ |
adhikā puṣyamāṇā ced bhāvollāsā ratiḥ ||128||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Addenda:

If the rati of the associates of Rādhā directed to Rādhā is equal or less than their rati directed to Kṛṣṇa, the rati directed to Rādhā is called sañcāri-rati, nourishing the rati towards Kṛṣṇa. If the rati of Rādhā’s associates directed to Rādhā is greater than that directed to Kṛṣṇa, and is constantly increasing, though it is still a sañcāri-bhāva, it is called bhāvollāsa-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
phalgu-vairāgya-nirdagdhāḥ śuṣka-jñānāś ca haitukāḥ |
mīmāṃsakā viśeṣeṇa bhaktyāsvāda-bahirmukhāḥ ||129||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those whose bhakti has been completely burned up by false renunciation, those who are dried up jñānīs, those who are absorbed in logic and disputing, and particularly those who are mīmāṃsakas, are excluded from tasting bhakti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ity eṣa bhakti-rasikaś caurād iva mahā-nidhiḥ |
jaran-mīmāṃsakād rakṣyaḥ kṛṣṇa-bhakti-rasaḥ sadā ||130||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Just as one very carefully protects a great treasure from thieves, the devotees protect bhakti-rasa from the withered mīmāṃsakas, since they are totally unqualified for relishing bhakti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sarvathaiva durūho ’yam abhaktair bhagavad-rasaḥ |
tat-pādāmbuja-sarvasvair bhaktair evānurasyate ||131||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Rasa directed to the Lord is very difficult to understand for those with no devotion. Those who have dedicated themselves to the lotus feet of Bhagavān can taste bhakti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vyatītya bhāvanā-vartma yaś camatkāra-kāra-bhūḥ |
hṛdi sattvojjvale bāḍhaṃ svadate sa raso mataḥ ||132||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That which becomes even more intensely relished in the heart made bright with hlādinī and saṃvit śaktis (attainment of bhāva), after surpassing the stage of distinguishing the constituent bhāvas, and which becomes more astonishing in bliss than bhāva, is called rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhāvanāyāḥ pade yas tu budhenānanya-buddhinā |
bhāvyate gāḍha-saṃskāraiś citte bhāvaḥ sa kathyate ||133||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That which the wise person who has dedicated his intelligence only to the Lord experiences in the heart, which realizes vibhāva and the other elements as separate entities, through deep impressions of previous bhakti, is called bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
gopāla-rūpa-śobhāṃ dadhad api raghunātha-bhāva-vistārī |
tuṣyatu sanātanātmā dakṣiṇa-vibhāge sudhāmbunidheḥ ||134||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: May the eternal person who manifested the beautiful form of a cowherd boy and distributed His bhāvas to the form of Rāma as well, be pleased with the southern section of the ocean of nectar.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dhṛta-mugdha-rūpa-bhāro bhāgavatārpita-pṛthu-premā |
sa mayi sanātana-mūrtis tanotu puruṣottamas tuṣṭim ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
May the Supreme Lord with eternal form, endowed with attractive beauty and abundant prema, which He gives to the devotees, be pleased with me!

May Sanātana Gosvāmī, who bears the burden of the fool Rūpa, and who had great attachment to the Bhāgavatam, be pleased with me!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rasāmṛtābdher bhāge ’tra tṛtīye paścimābhidhe |
mukhyo bhakti-rasaḥ pañcavidhaḥ śāntādīr īryate ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the third section of this book, called the Western Division of the ocean of sweet rasa, the five types of primary bhakti-rasa starting with śānta are described.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ato ’tra pāñcavidhyena laharyaḥ pañca kīrtitāḥ |
athāmī pañca lakṣyante rasāḥ śāntādayaḥ kramāt ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In this western section, the five waves are named according to the five types of rasa. Thus, following in succession, the chapters or waves are designated as the five rasas starting with śānta.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra śānta-bhakti-rasaḥ –
vakṣyamāṇair vibhāvādyaiḥ śamināṃ svādyatāṃ gataḥ
sthāyī śānti-ratir dhīraiḥ śānta-bhakti-rasaḥ smṛtaḥ ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If the sthāyi-bhāva called śanta-rati mixes with the elements of vibhāva and others which will now be described, and is relished by persons possessing śama, the wise call this śānta-bhakti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prāyaḥ svasukha-jātīyaṃ sukhaṃ syād atra yoginām |
kintv ātma-saukhyam aghanaṃ ghanaṃ tv īśa-mayaṃ sukham ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Generally, these practitioners possess the happiness of impersonal Brahman, realizing it as the cause of everything. However, such impersonal happiness is dilute, whereas the happiness related to the Lord with form and qualities is intense.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatrāpīśa-svarūpānubhavasyaivoru-hetutā |
dāsādi-van-manojñatva-līlāder na tathā matā ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The main cause of śānta-rasa arising in these persons is realization of the form of the Lord. The main cause of dāsya and other rasas appearing in the devotees is appreciation of the Lord’s attractive pastimes. Though this is also one factor in the śānta-bhakta’s realization, it is not the main factor.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ālambanāḥ –
caturbhujaś ca śāntāś ca asminn ālambanā matāḥ ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: According to the wise, the four-armed form of the Lord (viṣaya) and the śānta-bhaktas (āśraya) are the ālambanas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra caturbhujaḥ –
śyāmākṛtiḥ sphurati cāru-caturbhujo ’yam
ānanda-rāśir akhilātma-sindhu-taraṅgaḥ |
yasmin gate nayanayoḥ pathi nirjihīte
pratyak-padāt paramahaṃsa-muner mano ’pi ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The four-armed form:

This attractive four-armed form, filled with bliss and dark blue in complexion, is an ocean in which the jīvas are the waves. If He is seen by the parama-haṃsa sages, their minds will leave the impersonal brahman and become absorbed in all His qualities.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
saccidānanda-sāndrāṅga ātmārāma-śiromaṇiḥ |
paramātmā paraṃ brahma śamo dāntaḥ śucir vaśī ||9||
sadā svarūpa-samprāpto hatāri-gati-dāyakaḥ |
