vibhur ity ādi guṇavān asminn ālambano hariḥ ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The ālambana (viṣaya) in śānta-rasa is the Lord with an eternal form of knowledge and bliss, most attractive to the ātmārāmas. He is the Paramātmā, the param brahman, free from all passions, tolerant, pure, sense-controlled, eternally fixed in spiritual form, who rewards even the enemies He kills, and who is greater than the whole universe.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha śāntāḥ –
śāntāḥ syuḥ kṛṣṇa-tat-preṣṭha-kāruṇyena ratiṃ gatāḥ |
ātmārāmās tadīyādhva-baddha-śraddhāś ca tāpasāḥ ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The śānta-bhaktas are of two types: the ātmārāmas who have attained rati for Kṛṣṇa due to the mercy of Kṛṣṇa and His dear devotees, and the performers of austerities who have developed firm faith in the path of bhakti (also by mercy of the devotees).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha ātmārāmāḥ –
ātmārāmās tu sanaka-sananda-mukhā matāḥ |
prādhānyāt sanakādīnāṃ rūpaṃ bhaktiś ca kathyate ||12||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The sages headed by Sanaka and Sananda are considered ātmārāmas. Because Sanaka and the other Kumāras are the main ātmārāma-śānta-bhaktas, their forms and bhakti will now be described.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra rūpam –
te pañcaṣābda-bālābhāś catvāras tejasojjvalāḥ |
gaurāṅgā vāta-vasanāḥ prāyeṇa sahacāriṇaḥ ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their forms:

The four Kumāras are children five years old, shining with effulgence, golden in complexion, and almost naked. They wander about together.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ca bhaktiḥ –
samasta-guṇa-varjite karaṇataḥ pratīcīnatāṃ
gate kim api vastuni svayam adīpi tāvat sukham |
na yāvad iyam adbhutā nava-tamāla-nīla-dyuter
mukunda sukha-cid-ghanā tava babhūva sākṣāt-kṛtiḥ ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their bhakti:

O Mukunda! Until we realized Your astonishing form of knowledge and bliss with the complexion of a new tamāla tree, we were absorbed in the indescribable brahman beyond the senses and modes of material nature.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha tāpasāḥ –
bhaktir muktyaiva nirvighnety ātta-yukta-viraktatāḥ |
anujjhita-mumukṣā ye bhajante te tu tāpasāḥ ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Tāpasa-śānta-bhakta:

The practitioners of austerity are those who worship the Lord while practicing yukta-vairāgya without giving up the desire for liberation, since obstacles to bhakti are destroyed by attaining liberation.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kadā śaila-droṇyāṃ pṛthula-viṭapi-kroḍa-vasatir
vasānaḥ kaupīnaṃ racita-phala-kandāśana-ruciḥ |
hṛdi dhyāyaṃ dhyāyaṃ muhur iha mukundābhidham ahaṃ
cidānandaṃ jyotiḥ kṣaṇam iva vineṣyāmi rajanīḥ ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

When will I take pleasure in living in a cave or under a tree, wearing a kaupiṇa and eating simple roots and fruits, meditating in my heart on the effulgent form of knowledge and bliss called Mukunda, who gives liberation, in this way passing all my days and nights in few seconds?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhaktātmārāma-karuṇā prapañcenaiva tāpasāḥ |
śāntākhya-bhāva-candrasya hṛd-ākāśe kalāṃ śritāḥ ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The performers of austerities, having received mercy from the devotees and ātmārāmas, hold in the sky of their hearts the moon of bhāva called śānta.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha uddīpanāḥ –
śrutir mahopaniṣadāṃ vivikta-sthāna-sevanam |
antar-vṛtti-viśeṣo ’sya sphūrtis tattva-vivecanam ||18||
vidyāśakti-pradhānatvaṃ viśva-rūpa-pradarśanam |
jñāni-bhaktena saṃsargo brahma-satrādayas tathā |
eṣv asādhāraṇāḥ proktā budhair uddīpanā amī ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Uddīpanas:

The learned say that the unique stimuli for śānta-rasa are hearing the principal Upaniṣads, living in a solitary place, the appearance of pure mental functions, contemplating the truth, putting emphasis on jñāna-śakti (power of knowledge), visualizing the universal form, associating with jñāna-miśra-bhaktas, and discussing the Upaniṣads with similar persons.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra mahopaniṣac-chrutiḥ, yathā –
akleśāḥ kamala-bhuvaḥ praviśya goṣṭhīṃ
kurvantaḥ śruti-śirasāṃ śrutiṃ śruta-jñāḥ |
uttuṅgaṃ yadu-pura-saṅgamāya raṅgaṃ
yogīndrāḥ pulaka-bhṛto navāpy avāpuḥ ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of hearing the Upaniṣads:

Having entered the assembly of Brahmā and hearing the Upaniṣads, the nine yogendras, learned in the Vedas, free of suffering, and with hair standing on end, then developed a great desire to meet the members of the Yadu dynasty.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pādābja-tulasī-gandhaḥ śaṅkha-nādo mura-dviṣaḥ |
puṇya-śailaḥ śubhāraṇyaṃ siddha-kṣetraṃ svarāpagā ||21||
viṣayādi-kṣayiṣṇutvaṃ kālasyākhila-hāritā |
ityādy uddīpanā sādhāraṇās teṣāṃ kilāśritaiḥ ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The incomparable fragrance of the tulasī at the feet of the Lord, the sound of the Lord’s conch, holy mountains, holy forests, holy places, the Gaṅgā, the temporary nature of things of this world, the power of time to destroy everything are stimuli common for the dāsa-bhaktas as well as the śānta-bhaktas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha pādābja-tulasī-gandho, yathā tṛtīye (3.15.43) –
tasyāravinda-nayanasya padāravinda-
kiñjalka-miśra-tulasī-makaranda-vāyuḥ |
antar-gataḥ sva-vivareṇa cakāra teṣāṃ
saṅkṣobham akṣara-juṣām api citta-tanvoḥ ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The fragrance of tulasī, from the Third Canto:

When the breeze carrying the aroma of tulasī leaves from the toes of the lotus feet of the Personality of Godhead entered the nostrils of those sages, they experienced a change both in body and in mind, even though they were attached to the impersonal Brahman understanding. SB 3.15.43

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha anubhāvāḥ –
nāsāgra-nyasta-netratvam avadhūta-viceṣṭitam |
yuga-mātrekṣita-gatir jñāna-mudrā-pradarśanam ||24||
harer dviṣy api na dveṣo nātibhaktiḥ priyeṣv api |
siddhatāyās tathā jīvan-mukteś ca bahu-mānitā ||25||
nairapekṣyaṃ nirmamatā nirahaṅkāritā kathā |
maunam ity ādayaḥ śītāḥ syur asādhāraṇāḥ kriyāḥ ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Anubhāvas:

Staring at the tip of the nose, behaving like a renunciate, walking while looking four meters in front on the ground, showing the jñāna-mudrā (joining the thumb and forefinger together), absence of hatred even towards the enemies of the Lord, lack of intense attachment to the devotees of the Lord, showing great regard for the final destruction of material bondage (siddhatā) and for living without the influence of the gross and subtle bodies, indifference, non-possessiveness, lack of false ego, and silence are some of the special anubhāvas of a person with sānta-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra nāsāgra-nayanatvaṃ, yathā –
nāsikāgra-dṛg ayaṃ puro muniḥ
spanda-bandhura-śirā virājate |
citta-kandara-taṭīm anākulām
asya nūnam avagāhate hariḥ ||27||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of glancing at the tip of the nose:

This sage in front of us walks about while glancing at the tip of his nose, while raising and lowering his head. By this it appears that the Lord has entered into his steady heart.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
jṛmbhāṅga-moṭanaṃ bhakter upadeśo harer natiḥ |
stavādayaś ca dāsādyaiḥ śītāḥ sādhāraṇāḥ kriyāḥ ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The śīta and other anubhāvas[3] that the śānta-bhakta shares with the dāsas and others are yawning, stretching the body, giving instructions to the devotees, offering obeisances to the Lord, and reciting praises of the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra jṛmbhā, yathā –
hṛdayāmbare dhruvaṃ te
bhāvāmbara-maṇir udeti yogīndra |
yad idaṃ vadanāmbhojaṃ
jṛmbhām avalambate bhavataḥ ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Yawning:

O Yogīndra! Certainly, the sun of bhāva must have risen in the sky of your heart, for your lotus mouth is now blossoming with a yawn.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sāttvikāḥ –
romāñca-sveda-kampādyāḥ sāttvikāḥ pralayaṃ vinā ||30||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sāttivika-bhāvas:

In śānta rasa all the sāttvika-bhāvas such as standing of hair on end, perspiration, shaking of the body occur. However, fainting (pralaya) does not occur.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha romāñco, yathā –
pāñcajanya-janito dhvanir antaḥ
kṣobhayan sapadi biddha-samādhiḥ |
yogināṃ giri-guhā-nilayānāṃ
pudgale pulaka-pālim anaiṣīt ||31||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Hair standing on end:

The sound of the Pāñcajanya conch agitated the hearts of the yogīs living in the mountain caves. It broke their samādhi and made their hairs stand on end.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ nirabhimānānāṃ śarīrādiṣu yoginām |
sāttvikās tu jvalanty eva na tu dīptā bhavanty amī ||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the bodies of the yogīs devoid of false identity, the sāttvika-bhāvas manifest as jvalita,[5] but not dīptā.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sañcāriṇaḥ –
sañcārino ’tra nirvedo dhṛtir harṣo matiḥ smṛtiḥ |
viṣādotsukatāvega-vitarkādyāḥ prakīrtitāḥ ||33||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In śānta-rasa, the sañcāri-bhāvas of nirveda, dhṛti, harṣa, mati, smṛti, viṣāda, autsukya, āvega and vitarka and others appear.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra nirvedo, yathā –
asmin sukha-ghana-mūrtau param-
ātmani vṛṣṇi-pattane sphurati
ātmārāmatayā me vṛthā
gato bata ciraṃ kālaḥ ||34||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Nirveda (self-disgust):

Though Kṛṣṇa, Paramātmā, the form of concentrated bliss, resides in Dvārakā, I am so unfortunate! Thinking myself an ātmārāma, I have wasted so much time.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sthāyī –
atra śānti-ratiḥ sthāyī samā sāndrā ca sā dvidhā ||35||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sthāyi-bhāva:

In śānta rasa, the sthāyi-bhāva is śānta-rati. It has two types: sama (ordinary) and sāndra (intense).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyā, yathā –
samādhau yoginas tasminn asamprajñāta-nāmani |
līlayā mayi labdhe ’sya babhūvotkampinī tanuḥ ||36||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sama-śānta-rati:

When I playfully revealed Myself to the yogī absorbed in asamprajñāta-samādhi, his body began to shake to the extreme.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sāndrā, yathā –
sarvāvidyā-dhvaṃsato yaḥ samastād
āvirbhūto nirvikalpe samādhau |
jāte sākṣād yādavendre sa vindan
mayy ānandaḥ sāndratāṃ koṭidhāsīt ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sāndra-śānta-rati:

In nirvikalpa-samādhi, after destroying all ignorance, I experienced complete bliss. But when I saw Kṛṣṇa directly, the bliss became thousands of times more concentrated.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: śānto dvidhaiṣa pārokṣya-sākṣātkāra-vibhedataḥ ||38||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are two types of śānta-rasa: pārokṣya (not visible) and sākṣātkāra (visible).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha pārokṣyam, yathā –
prayāsyati mahat-tapaḥ saphalatāṃ kim aṣṭāṅgikā
munīśvara purātanī parama-yogacaryāpy asau |
narākṛti-navāmbuda-dyuti-dharaṃ paraṃ brahma me
vilocana-camatkṛtiṃ kathaya kiṃ nu nirmāsyati ||39||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Hidden (the Lord is not presently visible):

O lord of the sages! Tell me if my great austerities and my long practice of aṣṭāṅga-yoga will bear fruit? Will that param brahma in human form with the complexion of a new rain cloud ever appear before me and bring astonishment to my eyes?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
kṣetre kuroḥ kim api caṇḍakaroparāge
sāndraṃ mahaḥ pathi vilocanayor yadāsīt |
tan nīrada-dyuti-jayi smarad utsukaṃ me
na pratyag-ātmani mano ramate pureva ||40||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Now that I remember that effulgence that conquers the complexion of a new rain cloud, which I saw at Kurukṣetra at the time of the eclipse of the sun, my restless mind no longer experiences bliss in the impersonal brahman as it did previously.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sākṣātkāro, yathā –
paramātmatayātimedurād
bata sākṣāt-karaṇa-pramodataḥ |
bhagavann adhikaṃ prayojanaṃ
katarad brahma-vido ’pi vidyate ||41||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Seeing the Lord:

O Bhagavān, because you are filled with more bliss than the brahman, being the supreme soul, and because I have experienced intense bliss from seeing You directly, what is the necessity of realizing the impersonal brahman?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
hṛṣṭaḥ kambu-pati-svanair bhuvi luṭhac-cīrāñcalaḥ sañcalan
mūrdhnā ruddha-dṛg-aśrubhiḥ pulakito drāg eṣa līna-vrataḥ |
akṣṇor aṅganam añjana-tviṣi para-brahmaṇy avāpte mudā
mudrābhiḥ prakaṭīkaroty avamatiṃ yogī svarūpa-sthitau ||42||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

One yogī, on hearing the sound of the king of conches, Pāñcajanya, suddenly became full of joy. Tears flowed from his eyes, his hairs stood on end, his head began to shake, his cloth fell on the ground, and he could no longer maintain his vows. Seeing the param brahman with dark complexion, in bliss he showed disregard for the impersonal brahman by his speech.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhavet kadācit kutrāpi nanda-sūnoḥ kṛpā-bharaḥ |
prathamaṃ jñāna-niṣṭho ’pi so ’traiva ratim udvahet ||43||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If Kṛṣṇa is merciful to someone previously fixed in jñāna, that śānta-bhakta then becomes elevated in rati for Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā bilvamaṅgaloktiḥ –
advaita-vīthī-pathikair upāsyāḥ
svānanda-siṃhāsana-labdha-dīkṣāḥ |
śaṭhena kenāpi vayaṃ haṭhena
dāsī-kṛtā gopa-vadhū-viṭena ||44||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
In the words of Bilvamaṅgala:

Though I was respected by the followers of jñāna and was worshipped by them on the throne of realizing the bliss of brahman, forcibly I was made into a female servant (dāsī) by the crafty boyfriend of the cowherd women.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tat-kāruṇya-ślathībhūta-jñāna-saṃskāra-santatiḥ |
eṣa bhakti-rasānanda-nipuṇaḥ syād yathā śukaḥ ||45||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: As in the case of Śukadeva, impressions of jñāna will slacken by the mercy of the Lord, and the person will attain the perfection of bliss in bhakti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śamasya nirvikāratvān nāṭyajñair naiṣa manyate |
śānty-ākhyāyā rater atra svīkārān na virudhyate ||46||
śamo man-niṣṭhatā buddher[6] iti śrī-bhagavad-vacaḥ |
tan-niṣṭhā durghaṭā buddher etāṃ śānta-ratiṃ vinā ||47||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Because of the lack of an object of devotion in the state of śama, the experts in poetics do not consider śānta as a rasa.[7] However, in our opinion, because we accept śānta-rati for the Lord as the sthāyi-bhāva, this objection cannot be made (since the Lord is the object of devotion). The Lord says “śama is having the intelligence fixed in Me.” Without the attainment of śānta-rati, fixing the intelligence in the Lord cannot take place.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kevala-śānto ’pi, śrī-viṣṇu-dharmottare yathā –
nāsti yatra sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ na dveṣo na ca matsaraḥ |
samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu sa śāntaḥ prathito rasaḥ ||48||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Pure śānta according to Viṣṇu-dharmottara:

The condition in which there is no happiness, no suffering, no hatred, no envy, and equality shown to all beings is known as śānta-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sarvathaivam ahaṅkāra-rahitatvaṃ vrajanti cet |
atrāntarbhāvam arhanti dharma-vīrādayas tadā ||49||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When those involved in dharma, charity and compassion (besides the tapasvīs) become completely devoid of being the doer, they become qualified for entering into śānta-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dhṛti-sthāyinam eke tu nirveda-sthāyinaṃ pare |
śāntam eva rasaṃ pūrve prāhur ekam anekadhā ||50||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The previous scholars have described many types of śānta-rasa, saying that dhṛti or nirveda can also be the sthāyi-bhāvas of śānta-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nirvedo viṣaye sthāyī tattva-jñānodbhavaḥ sa cet |
iṣṭāniṣṭa-viyogāpti-kṛtas tu vyabhicāry asau ||51||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If nirveda (self-deprecation) arises from knowledge of the absolute truth, then it can be called a sthāyi-bhāva directed to the Lord. But if it arises from deprivation of dear items or attainment of detested items, it is a vyabhicāri-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śrīdhara-svāmibhiḥ spaṣṭam ayam eva rasottamaḥ |
raṅga-prasaṅge sa-premakākhyaḥ prakīrtitaḥ ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Śrīdhara Svāmī, in talking about rasa,[10] has clearly glorified this as the highest rasa, calling it saprema-bhakti (bhakti filled with prema).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rati-sthāyitayā nāma-kaumudī-kṛdbhir apy asau |
śāntatvenāyam evāddhā sudevādyaiś ca varṇitaḥ ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The writer of Nāma-kaumudī (Lakṣmīdhara) has called this rasa a sthāyi-rati, and Sudeva and other authorities on literary ornament have called it a form of śānta.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ātmocitair vibhāvādyaiḥ prītir āsvādanīyatām |
nītā cetasi bhaktānāṃ prīti-bhakti-raso mataḥ ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When prīti or adoration achieves a pleasurable nature by vibhāvas, anubhāvas, sāttvika-bhāvas and vyabhicāri-bhāvas in the hearts of the devotees, it is called prīti-bhakti-rasa.[11]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
anugrāhyasya dāsatvāl lālyatvād apy ayaṃ dvidhā |
bhidyate sambhrama-prīto gaurava-prīta ity api ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the recipient of mercy acts as a servant, it is called sambhrama-prīti and the recipient identifies himself as the object of parental affection it is called gaurava-prīti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dāsābhimānināṃ kṛṣṇe syāt prītiḥ sambhramottarā |
pūrvavat puṣyamāṇo ’yaṃ sambhrama-prīta ucyate ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those who identify themselves as servants of Kṛṣṇa have sambhrama-prīti for Kṛṣṇa. When this sambhrama-prīti is nourished by vibhāva and other elements it is called sambhrama-prīti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ālambanāḥ –
hariś ca tasya dāsāś ca jñeyā ālambanā iha ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In sambhrama-prīti-rasa, the ālambanas are the Lord (viṣaya) and His servants (āśraya).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra hariḥ –
ālambano ’smin dvibhujaḥ kṛṣṇo gokula-vāsiṣu |
anyatra dvi-bhujaḥ kvāpi kutrāpy eṣu catur-bhujaḥ ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The two-armed form of Kṛṣṇa is the ālambana for the inhabitants of Gokula, and the two-armed or four-armed form of Kṛṣṇa is the ālambana for the devotees of Kṛṣṇa residing elsewhere.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra vraje –
navāmbudhara-bandhuraḥ kara-yugena vaktrāmbuje
nidhāya muralīṃ sphurat-puraṭa-nindi paṭṭāmbaraḥ |
śikhaṇḍa-kṛta-śekharaḥ śikhariṇas taṭe paryaṭana-
prabhur divi divaukaso bhuvi dhinoti naḥ kiṅkarān ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
In Vraja:

The form of Kṛṣṇa with the complexion of a new rain cloud, holding a flute to His mouth with His two hands, wearing a yellow cloth which defeats resplendent gold, with a peacock feather crown on His head, wanders about the bank of the Yamunā near Govardhana and gives bliss to the inhabitants of Svargaloka and to ourselves, His servants on this earth.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
anyatra dvi-bhujo, yathā –
prabhur ayam aniśaṃ piśaṅga-vāsāḥ
kara-yuga-bhāg arikambur ambudābhaḥ |
nava-ghana iva cañcalāpinaddho
ravi-śaśi-maṇḍala-maṇḍitaś cakāsti ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The two-armed outside of Gokula:

Kṛṣṇa, dark-complexioned like a rain cloud, wearing yellow cloth, holding in His two hands the cakra and conch, emits a radiance like a rain cloud flashing lightning and ornamented with the sun and moon.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra caturbhujo, yathā lalita-mādhave (5.70) –
cañcat-kaustubha-kaumudī-samudayaḥ kaumodakī-cakrayoḥ
sakhyenojjvalitais tathā jalajayor āḍhyaś caturbhir bhujaiḥ |
divyālaṅkaraṇena saṅkaṭa-tanuḥ saṅgī vihaṅgeśitur
māṃ vyasmārayad eṣa kaṃsa-vijayī vaikuṇṭha-goṣṭhī-śriyam ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The four-armed form, from Lalita-mādhava:

Kṛṣṇa, victorious over Kaṃsa, wearing around His neck the Kaustubha jewel flashing light, holding at once the club, cakra, lotus and conch in His four hands, whose body is ornamented with extraordinary jewels, and who is accompanied by Garuḍa, makes me forget the splendor of Vaikuṇṭha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
brahmāṇḍa-koṭi-dhāmaika-roma-kūpaḥ kṛpāmbudhiḥ |
avicintya-mahā-śaktiḥ sarva-siddhi-niṣevitaḥ ||11||
avatārāvalī-bījaṃ sadātmārāma-hṛd-guṇaḥ |
īśvaraḥ paramārādhyaḥ sarvajñaḥ sudṛḍha-vrataḥ ||12||
samṛddhimān kṣamā-śīlaḥ śaraṇāgata-pālakaḥ |
dakṣiṇaḥ satya-vacano dakṣaḥ sarva-śubhaṅkaraḥ ||13||
pratāpī dhārmikaḥ śāstra-cakṣur bhakta-suhṛttamaḥ |
vadānyas tejasā yuktaḥ kṛtajñaḥ kīrti-saṃśrayaḥ ||14||
varīyān balavān prema-vaśya ity ādibhir guṇaiḥ |
yutaś catur-vidheṣv eṣa dāseṣv ālambano hariḥ ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The Lord, in whose hair holes reside billions of universes, is an ocean of mercy. He is endowed with inconceivable powers and all perfections. He is the seed of all avatāras, is always attractive to the ātmārāmas, and is the supreme controller. He is most deserving of respect. He is omniscient, fixed in His vows, always increasing, and full of tolerance. He is the protector of those who surrender to Him, compliant, truthful, expert, all auspicious, the afflicter of the evil, and upholder of religious principles. He is the eye of scripture, the friend of the devotee, magnanimous, effulgent, full of gratitude, full of good qualities, the chief among all beings, and is controlled by prema. His form is the ālambana for four types of servants.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha dāsāḥ –
dāsās tu praśritās tasya nideśa-vaśa-vartiṇaḥ |
viśvastāḥ prabhutā-jñāna-vinamrita-dhiyaś ca te ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The servants (dāsas) are well behaved, always ready to follow the order of the Lord. They have full faith in Him and have a sense of humility while recognizing that He is their master.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
prabhur ayam akhilair guṇair garīyān
iha tulanām aparaḥ prayāti nāsya |
iti pariṇata-nirṇayena namrān
hita-caritān hari-sevakān bhajadhvam ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Please worship the servants of the Lord, who are engaged in others’ benefit, and who are humble because they understand that their Lord is most excellent in all qualities and has no equal.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: caturdhāmī adhikṛtāśrita-pāriṣadānugāḥ ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The four types of dāsas are the adhikṛta, āśrita, pāriṣada and anuga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra adhikṛtāḥ –
brahma-śaṅkara-śakrādyāḥ proktā adhikṛtā budhaiḥ |
rūpaṃ prasiddham evaiṣāṃ tena bhaktir udīryate ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wise say that Brahmā, Śaṅkara, Indra and others are called adhikṛta (appointed) dāsas. Since their forms are well known, only their bhakti will be described.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kā paryety ambikeyaṃ harim avakalayan kampate kaḥ śiro ’sau
taṃ kaḥ stauty eṣa dhātā praṇamati viluṭhan kaḥ kṣitau vāsavo ’yam |
kaḥ stabdho hasyate ’ddhā danujabhid-anujaiḥ pūrvajo ’yaṃ mametthaṃ
kālindī jāmbavatyāṃ tridaśa-paricayaṃ jāla-randhrād vyatānīt ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Jāmbavatī asked Kālindī: Who is that person who is circumambulating the Lord?

Kālindī said: It is Durgā.

Jāmbavatī said: Who is that person who is shaking on seeing the Lord?

Kālindī said: It is Śiva.

Jāmbavatī said: Who is that person praising the Lord?

Kālindī: It is Brahmā.

Jāmbavatī said: Who is that lying on the ground offering respects?

Kālindī: That is Indra.

Jāmbavatī: Who is that who has become stunned, thereby becoming the object of laughter by the young members?

Kālindī: It is Yama, my elder brother.

In this way Kālindī introduced the devatās to Jāmbavatī while peering through the lattice work.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha āśritāḥ –
te śaraṇyā jñāni-carāḥ sevā-niṣṭhās tridhāśritāḥ ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Āśrita:

There are three types of āśritas: those who have surrendered to the Lord as their protector (śāraṇya), those who were previously jñānīs (jñāni-cara) but later understood the superiority of the form and qualities of the Lord, and those who are fixed in service (sevā-niṣṭhā), (appreciating the Lord’s sweetness).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kecid bhītāḥ śaraṇam abhitaḥ saṃśrayante bhavantaṃ
vijñātārthās tvad-anubhavataḥ prāsya kecin mumukṣām |
śrāvaṃ śrāvaṃ tava nava-navāṃ mādhurīṃ sādhu-vṛndād
vṛndāraṇyotsava kila vayaṃ deva sevemahi tvām ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O bliss of Vṛndāvana! O Lord! Some people who were fearful, take shelter of You completely, understanding that you are the protector (śaranyas). Some, realizing You are greater than brahman, giving up desire for liberation, surrender to You (jñāni-caras). However, hearing again and again about Your ever-fresh sweetness from the devotees, may we simply become engaged in service to You (sevā-niṣṭhās).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra śaraṇyāḥ –
śaraṇyāḥ kāliya-jarāsandha-baddha-nṛpādayaḥ ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Śaraṇyas:

Kāliya and the kings kept in prison by Jarāsandha are examples of śaraṇyas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
api gahanāgasi nāge prabhu-vara mayy adbhutādya te karuṇā |
bhaktair api durlabhayā yad ahaṃ pada-mudrayojjvalitaḥ ||24||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

O best of masters! You have today shown extraordinary mercy to me, Kāliya, the worst offender, by marking my head with Your footprints, which are rarely attained even by the devotees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā aparādha-bhañjane –
kāmādīnāṃ kati na katidhā pālitā durnideśās
teṣāṃ jātā mayi na karuṇā na trapā nopaśāntiḥ |
utsṛjyaitān atha yadu-pate sāmprataṃ labdha-buddhis
tvām āyātaḥ śaraṇam abhayaṃ māṃ niyuṅkṣvātma-dāsye ||25||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example, from the Aparādha-bhañjana-stotra:

Countless times I have followed the evil orders of lust and anger. They have not shown mercy to me, and I have not shown shame or ceased my sinful actions. Now, however, with acquisition of knowledge, I have given them up. O master of the Yadus! I have surrendered to Your fearless form. Please engage me as Your servant.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha jñāni-carāḥ –
ye mumukṣāṃ parityajya harim eva samāśritāḥ |
śaunaka-pramukhās te tu proktā jñāni-carāḥ budhaiḥ ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Jñāni-caras:

The sages headed by Śaunaka who gave up the desire for liberation and surrendered to the Lord are called jñāni-caras by the wise.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā hari-bhakti-sudhodaye –
aho mahātman bahu-doṣa-duṣṭo ’py
ekena bhāty eṣa bhavo guṇena |
sat-saṅgamākhyena sukhāvahena
kṛtādya no yena kṛśā mumukṣā ||27||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Hari-bhakti-sudhodaya:

Śaunaka speaks to Suta: Though the material world is full of faults, it has one good quality—association with devotees. By this association, we have given up the desire for liberation.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā padyāvalyām (77) –
dhyānātītaṃ kim api paramaṃ ye tu jānanti tattvaṃ
teṣām āstāṃ hṛdaya-kuhare śuddha-cinmātra ātmā |
asmākaṃ tu prakṛti-madhuraḥ smera-vaktrāravindo
megha-śyāmaḥ kanaka-paridhiḥ paṅkajākṣo ’yam ātmā ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example, from Padyāvalī:

Let the ātmā of pure consciousness reside in the hearts of those who know the supreme truth beyond meditation. But may that ātmā with sweet nature, with lotus eyes, yellow cloth, complexion of a rain cloud, and slightly smiling lotus face, remain in our hearts.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sevā-niṣṭhāḥ –
mūlato bhajanāsaktāḥ sevā-niṣṭhā itīritāḥ |
candradhvajo harihayo bahulāśvas tathā nṛpāḥ |
ikṣvākuḥ śrutadevāś ca puṇḍarīkādayaś ca te ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sevā-niṣṭhās:

Those who from the beginning are attached to serving the Lord are called sevā-niṣṭhā, fixed in service. Examples are Candradhvaja (Śiva), Indra, King Bahulāśva, Ikṣvāku, Śrutadeva and Puṇḍarīka.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
ātmārāmān api gamayati tvad-guṇo gāna-goṣṭhīṃ
śūnyodyāne nayati vihagān apy alaṃ bhikṣu-caryām |
ity utkarṣaṃ kam api sa-camatkāram ākarṇya citraṃ
sevāyāṃ te sphuṭam aghahara śraddhayā gardhito ’smi ||30||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

O Kṛṣṇa! When Your qualities are sung in the assembly, even the ātmārāmas become attracted to listen, and the bird-like renunciates residing in lonely gardens take up the activity of begging to hear. Hearing about Your excellent, astonishing qualities, I have become distinctly greedy to serve You with faith.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha pāriṣadāḥ –
uddhavo dāruko jaitraḥ śrutadevaś ca śatrujit |
nandopananda-bhadrādyāḥ pārṣadā yadu-pattane ||31||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In Dvārakā, Uddhava, Dāruka, Sātyaki, Śrutadeva, Śatrujit, Nanda, Upananda and Bhadra are known as pāriṣadas (members of the assembly or followers).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
niyuktāḥ santy amī mantra-sārathyādiṣu karmasu |
tathāpi kvāpy avasare paricaryāṃ ca kurvate |
kauraveṣu tathā bhīṣma-parīkṣid-vidurādayaḥ ||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though they also serve as advisors, charioteers and other functionaries, at other times they act as His retinue. In the same way, among the Kuru dynasty, Bhīṣma, Parīkṣit and Vidura are known as pāriṣadas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
teṣāṃ rūpaṃ, yathā –
sarasāḥ sarasīruhākṣa-veṣās
tridiveśāvali-jaitra-kānti-leśāḥ |
yadu-vīra-sabhāsadaḥ sadāmī
pracurālaṅkaraṇojjvalā jayanti ||33||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their form:

The pāriṣadas always look most splendid, with charming forms, with yellow dress like the Lord, with radiance that defeats the devatās, and with profuse ornaments.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhaktiḥ, yathā –
śaṃsan dhurjaṭi-nirjayādi-virudaṃ bāṣpāvaruddhākṣaraṃ
śaṅkā-pañka-lavaṃ madād agaṇayan kālāgni-rudrād api |
tvayy evārpita-buddhir uddhava-mukhas tvat-pārṣadānāṃ gaṇo
dvāri dvāravatī-purasya purataḥ sevotsukas tiṣṭhati ||34||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their bhakti:

Your pāriṣadas headed by Uddhava, with their intelligence offered to You, constantly uttering glorification of Your victory over Śiva, with words choked up by tears, and with great confidence showing not even a spot of fear from Rudra with his fire of devastation, are standing at the gate of Dvārakā, eager to do service.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: eteṣāṃ pravaraḥ śrīmān uddhavaḥ prema-viklavaḥ ||35||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among these pāriṣadas, Uddhava, controlled by prema, is the best.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tasya rūpaṃ –
kālindī-madhura-tviṣaṃ madhupater mālyena nirmālyatāṃ
labdhenāñcitam ambareṇa ca lasad-gorocanā-rociṣā |
dvandvenārgala-sundareṇa bhujayor jiṣṇum abjekṣaṇaṃ
mukhyaṃ pāriṣadeṣu bhakti-laharī-ruddhaṃ bhajāmy uddhavam ||36||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Uddhava’s form:

I worship Uddhava, with dark complexion like the Yamunā, wearing yellow cloth and the garlands used by Kṛṣṇa. He has arms like door bolts and eyes like lotuses. He is the chief of the pāriṣadas, filled with waves of bhakti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhaktiḥ, yathā –
mūrdhany āhuka-śāsanaṃ praṇayate brahmeśayoḥ śāsitā
sindhuṃ prārthayate bhuvaṃ tanutarāṃ brahmāṇḍa-koṭīśvaraḥ |
mantraṃ pṛcchati mām apeśala-dhiyaṃ vijñāna-vārāṃ nidhir
vikrīḍaty asakṛd vicitra-caritaḥ so ’yaṃ prabhur mādṛśām ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
His bhakti:

Uddhava said: Though He is the controller of Śiva and Brahmā, He carries the order of Ugrasena on His head. Though He is the master of billions of universes, He prays for a small piece of land near the ocean. Though He is the ocean of knowledge, He asks me, an unintelligent fool, for advice. In this way, our amazing master enjoys His pastimes constantly with persons such as me.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha anugāḥ –
sarvadā paricaryāsu prabhor āsakta-cetasaḥ |
purasthāś ca vrajasthāś cety ucyate anugā dvidhā ||38||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Anugas:

Those whose hearts are at all times attached to attending upon the Lord are called anuga-dāsas (attendants). There are two types: those in Dvārakā and those in Vraja.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra purasthāḥ –
sucandro maṇḍanaḥ stambaḥ sutambādyāḥ purānugāḥ |
eṣāṃ pārṣadavat prāyo rūpālaṅkāraṇādayaḥ ||39||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Sucandra, Maṇḍana, Stamba, Sutamba are some of the anugas in Dvārakā. Their forms and ornaments are almost the same as those of the pāriṣadas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sevā yathā –
upari kanaka-daṇḍaṃ maṇḍano vistṛṇīte
dhuvati kila sucandraś cāmaraṃ candra-cārum |
upaharati sutambaḥ suṣṭhu tāmbūla-vīṭīṃ
vidadhati paricaryāḥ sādhavo mādhavasya ||40||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their service:

Maṇḍana holds an umbrella with a gold handle over Kṛṣṇa’s head. Sucandra fans Him with a white cāmara. Sutamba offers betel nut. In this way, the devotees serve Mādhava.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vraja-sthāḥ –
raktakaḥ patrakaḥ patrī madhukaṇṭho madhuvrataḥ |
rasāla-suvilāsāś ca premakando marandakaḥ ||41||
ānandaś candrahāsaś ca payodo bakulas tathā |
rasadaḥ śāradādyāś ca vrajasthā anugā matāḥ ||42||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Raktaka, Patraka, Patrī, Madhukaṇṭha, Madhuvrata, Rasāla, Suvilāsa, Premakanda, Marandaka, Ānanda, Candrahāsa, Payoda, Bakula, Rasada and Śārada are some of the anugas in Vraja.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ rūpaṃ, yathā –
maṇi-maya-vara-maṇḍanojjvalāṅgān
puraṭa-javā-madhuliṭ-paṭīra-bhāsaḥ |
nija-vapur-anurūpa-divya-vastrān
vraja-pati-nandana-kiṅkarān namāmi ||43||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their forms:

I offer my obeisances to Kṛṣṇa’s servants, whose limbs glow with attractive jeweled ornaments, whose complexions are gold, reddish, brown or black like a bee, and who wear clothing suited to their bodies.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sevā, yathā –
drutaṃ kuru pariṣkṛtaṃ bakula pīta-paṭṭāṃśukaṃ
varair agurubhir jalaṃ racaya vāsitaṃ vārida |
rasāla parikalpayoragalatādalair vīṭikāḥ
parāga-paṭalī gavāṃ diśam arundha paurandarīm ||44||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their service:

O Bakula! Please quickly wash the yellow cloth. O Vārida! Please scent the water with this excellent aguru. O Rasāla! Please prepare the betel nut with leaves. See, the eastern horizon is already covered with dust raised by the cows!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: vrajānugeṣu sarveṣu varīyān raktako mataḥ ||45||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among the anugas in Vraja, Raktaka is the chief.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asya rūpaṃ, yathā –
ramya-piṅga-paṭam aṅga-rociṣā
kharvitoru-śata-parvikā-rucam |
suṣṭhu goṣṭha-yuvarāja-sevinaṃ
rakta-kaṇṭham anuyāmi raktakam ||46||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Raktaka’s form:

I am the follower of Raktaka, who is dressed in yellow cloth, whose effulgence conquers that of new grass, who is expertly engaged in Kṛṣṇa’s service, and who has a beautiful voice for singing.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhaktiḥ, yathā –
girivara-bhṛti bhartṛ-dārake ’smin
vraja-yuvarājatayā gate prasiddhim |
śṛṇu rasada sadā padābhisevā-
paṭṭimaratā ratir uttamā mamāstu ||47||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Raktaka’s bhakti:

O Rasada! Listen. May I always have the highest attraction for serving the lotus feet of He who is famous as the son of the King of Vraja, who has held up Govardhana.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: dhūryo dhīraś ca vīraś ca tridhā pāriṣadādikaḥ ||48||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are three types of pāriṣadas and anugas: dhūrya, dhīra and vīra.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra dhūryaḥ –
kṛṣṇe ’sya preyasī-varge dāsādau ca yathāyatham |
yaḥ prītiṃ tanute bhaktaḥ sa dhūrya iha kīrtyate ||49||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That devotee who possesses suitable affection for Kṛṣṇa, His intimates and His servants is called dhūrya-pāriṣada or dhūryānuga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
devaḥ sevyatayā yathā sphurati me devyas tathāsya priyāḥ
sarvaḥ prāṇa-samānatāṃ pracinute tad-bhakti-bhājāṃ gaṇaḥ |
smṛtvā sāhasikaṃ bibhemi tam ahaṃ bhaktābhimānonnataṃ
prītiṃ tat-praṇate khare ’py avidadhad yaḥ svāsthyam ālambate ||50||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Just as Kṛṣṇa appears as the object of service for me, His dear women are also my objects of service. His devotees are all objects of my service. I consider them my very life. I fear even to think of that rash person who simply makes a pretense of being a devotee. However, even the person who has affection for a donkey who has offered respect to Kṛṣṇa is blessed with good health.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha dhīraḥ –
āśritya preyasīm asya nātisevāparo ’pi yaḥ |
tasya prasāda-pātraṃ syān mukhyaṃ dhīraḥ sa ucyate ||51||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That devotee who, taking shelter of Kṛṣṇa’s dear women, though not serving Kṛṣṇa profusely, becomes a chief object of Kṛṣṇa’s mercy, is called a dhīra.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kam api pṛthag-anuccair nācarāmi prayatnaṃ
yadukula-kamalārka tvat-prasāda-śriye ’pi |
samajani nanu devyāḥ pārijātārcitāyāḥ
parijana-nikhilāntaḥ-pātinī me yad-ākhyā ||52||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O sun who opens the lotus of the Yadus! I do not have to exert much effort to gain a wealth of mercy. I have gained a name by being included as an associate of Satyabhāmā, who was worshipped by You when You gave her the pārijāta tree.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vīraḥ –
kṛpāṃ tasya samāśritya prauḍhāṃ nānyam apekṣate |
atulāṃ yo vahana kṛṣṇe prītiṃ vīraḥ sa ucyate ||53||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The devotee who takes shelter of Kṛṣṇa and has incomparable attraction for Kṛṣṇa, though he does not notice others, is called a vīra-pāriṣada or vīrānuga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
pralamba-ripur īśvaro bhavatu kā kṛtis tena me
kumāra-makara-dhvajād api na kiñcid āste phalam |
kim anyad aham uddhataḥ prabhu-kṛpā-kaṭākṣa-śriyā
priyā pariṣad-agrimāṃ na gaṇayāmi bhāmām api ||54||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Balarāma, enemy of Pralamba, may be the Supreme Lord, but what use do I have for him? What can I gain from the prince Pradyumna? Since I have been elevated by the wealth of Kṛṣṇa’s glance of mercy, I do not give regard even to Satyabhāmā, the head of all Kṛṣṇa’s followers.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
caturthe ca –
jagaj-jananyāṃ jagad-īśa vaiśasaṃ
syād eva yat-karmaṇi naḥ samīhitam
karoṣi phalgv apy uru dīna-vatsalaḥ
sva eva dhiṣṇye 'bhiratasya kiṃ tayā ||55||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From the Fourth Canto:

My dear Lord of the universe, the goddess of fortune, Lakṣmī, is the mother of the universe, and yet I think that she may be angry with me because of my intruding upon her service and acting on that very platform to which she is so much attached. Yet I am hopeful that even though there is some misunderstanding, You will take my part, for You are very much inclined to the poor and You always magnify even insignificant service unto You. Therefore, even though she becomes angry, I think that there is no harm for You, because You are so self-sufficient that You can do without her. SB 4.20.28

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eteṣu tasya dāseṣu trividheṣv āśritādiṣu |
nitya-siddhāś ca siddhāś ca sādhakāḥ parikīrtitaḥ ||56||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among the āśritas, pāriṣadas and anugas, there are nitya-siddhas, sādhana-siddhas and sādhakas.[13]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha uddīpanāḥ –
anugrahasya samprāptis tasyāṅghri-rajasāṃ tathā |
bhuktāvaśiṣṭa-bhaktāder api tad-bhakta-saṅgatiḥ |
ity ādayo vibhāvāḥ syur eṣv asādhāraṇā matāḥ ||57||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa’s mercy, attaining His foot dust or food remnants, and association with Kṛṣṇa’s devotees are some of the unique uddīpanas (stimuli) for this rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra anugraha-samprāptiḥ, yathā –
kṛṣṇasya paśyata kṛpāṃ kṛpādyāḥ kṛpaṇe mayi |
dhyeyo ’sau nidhane hanta dṛśor adhvānam abhyagāt ||58||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Attaining mercy:

O Kṛpācarya! O brāhmaṇas! See Kṛṣṇa’s mercy to me, the most fallen! Kṛṣṇa, the object of meditation, has appeared to my eyes at the time of my death.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
muralī-śṛṅgayoḥ svānaḥ smita-pūrvāvalokanam |
guṇotkarṣa-śrutiḥ padma-padāṅka-nava-nīradāḥ |
tad-aṅga-saurabhādyās tu sarvaiḥ sādhāraṇā matāḥ ||59||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The sound of His flute and horn, His glance with a slight smile, hearing the excellence of His qualities, the lotus, the marks on His feet, new clouds, and the fragrance of His body are considered the regular uddīpanas.[14]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra muralī-svano, yathā vidagdha-mādhave (1.30)–
sotkaṇṭhaṃ muralī-kalā-parimalān ākarṇya ghūrṇat-tanor
etasyākṣi-sahasrataḥ surapāter aśrūṇi sasrur bhuvi |
citraṃ vāridharān vināpi tarasā yair adya dhārāmayair
dūrāt paśyata deva-mātṛtkam abhūd vṛndāṭavī-maṇḍalam ||60||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The sound of the flute, from Vidagdha-mādhava:

Hearing the sound of Kṛṣṇa’s flute, the thousand eyes on Indra’s shaking body are shedding tears which are falling on the ground. Though there are no clouds in the sky, Vṛndāvana has been nourished by showers made of those tears.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha anubhāvāḥ –
sarvataḥ svaniyogānām ādhikyena parigrahaḥ |
īrṣyā-lavena cāspṛṣṭā maitrī tat-praṇate jane |
tan-niṣṭhādyāḥ śītāḥ syur eṣv asādhāraṇāḥ kriyāḥ ||61||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The anubhāvas:

Being completely involved in serving according one’s capacity, friendship with the Lord’s servants with absence of even a trace of envy on seeing the excellence of others’ service, and being fixed in their love, which are all considered śīta,[16] are the special anubhāvas of the dāsas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra svaniyogasya sarvata ādhikyaṃ, yathā –
aṅga-stambhārambham uttaṅgayantam
premānandaṃ dāruko nābhyanandat
kaṃsārāter vījane yena sākṣād
akṣodīyān antarāyo vyadhāyi ||62||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Being absorbed in service:

When Dāruka was fanning Kṛṣṇa with a cāmara whisk, overcome with prema, his body became increasingly paralyzed, but because that bliss would be an obstacle in serving Kṛṣṇa, he did not welcome that ecstasy.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
udbhāsvarāḥ puroktā ye tathāsya suhṛd-ādayaḥ |
virāgādyāś ca ye śītāḥ proktāḥ sādhāraṇās tu te ||63||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The ordinary anubhāvas of the dāsas are all the udbhāsvaras mentioned previously,[18] and as well respect for the friends of Kṛṣṇa, and detachment etc. which are all śīta.[19]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra nṛtyam, yathā śrī-daśame–
śrutadevo ’cyutaṃ prāptaṃ svagṛhān janako yathā |
natvā munīṃś ca saṃhṛṣṭo dhunvan vāso nanarta ha ||64||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of dancing, from the Tenth Canto:

Śrutadeva received Lord Acyuta into his home with as much enthusiasm as that shown by King Bahulāśva. After bowing down to the Lord and the sages, Śrutadeva began to dance with great joy, waving his shawl. SB 10.86.38

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
tvaṃ kalāsu vimukho ’pi nartanaṃ
prema-nāṭya-guruṇāsi pāṭhitaḥ |
yad vicitra-gati-caryayāñcitaś
citrayasy ahaha cāraṇān api ||65||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Though you are not an expert at dancing, you have astonished us dancers so much with your spectacular routine that one would think that you learned dancing from the guru of dance called prema.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sāttvikāḥ –
stambhādyāḥ sāttvikāḥ sarve prītādi-tritaye matāḥ ||66||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sāttvika-bhāvas:

It is said that all the sāttvika-bhāvas starting with stambha (paralysis) appear in prīti (dāsya), preyo (sakhya) and madhura-rasas. [20]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
gokulendra-guṇa-gāna-rasena
stambham adbhutam asau bhajamānaḥ |
paśya bhakti-rasa-maṇḍapa-mūla-
stambhatāṃ vahati vaiṣṇava-varyaḥ ||67||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: See how this great devotee, permeated with the sāttvika-bhāva of stambha (paralysis) from hearing sweet songs glorifying Kṛṣṇa, has become the main pillar (stambha) holding up the pavilion of bhakti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śrī-daśame –
sa indraseno bhagavat-padānujaṃ
bibhran muhuḥ prema-vibhinnayā dhiyā |
uvāca hānanda-jalākulekṣaṇaḥ
prahṛṣṭa-romā nṛpa gadgadākṣaram ||68||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From the Tenth Canto:

Taking hold of the Lords’ lotus feet again and again, Bali, the conqueror of Indra’s army, spoke from his heart, which was melting out of his intense love. O King, as tears of ecstasy filled his eyes and the hair on his limbs stood on end, he began to speak with faltering words. SB 10.85.38

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vyabhicāriṇaḥ –
harṣo dhṛtiś cātra nirvedo ’tha viṣaṇṇatā |
dainyaṃ cintā smṛtiḥ śaṅkā matir autsukya-cāpale ||69||
vitarkāvega-hrī-jāḍya-mohonmādāvahitthikāḥ |
bodhaḥ svapnaḥ klamo vyādhir mṛtiś ca vyabhicāriṇaḥ ||70||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Vyabhicāri-bhāvas:

Twenty-four vyabhicāri-bhāvas appear in prīti-rasa: harṣa, garva, dhṛti, nirveda, viṣāda, dainyam, cintā, smṛti, śaṅkā, mati, autsukya, cāpalatā, vitarka, āvega, hrī, jāḍyam, moha, unmāda, avahitthā, bodha, svapna, klama (glāni), vyādhi and mṛti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
itareṣāṃ madādīnāṃ nātipoṣakatā bhavet |
yoge trayaḥ syur dhṛtyas tā ayoge tu klamādayaḥ |
ubhayatra pare śeṣā nirvedādyāḥ satāṃ matāḥ ||71||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The other nine--mada, śrama, trāsa, apasmāra, ālasya, ugratā, krodha, asūyā and nidrā-- do not nourish prīti-rasa. In meeting the Lord, harṣa, garva and dhṛti manifest, and in separation from the Lord, klama (glāni), vyādhi and mṛti manifest. According to the devotees, the remaining eighteen vyabhicāri-bhāvas manifest both in union with and separation from the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra harṣo, yathā prathame –
prīty-utphulla-mukhāḥ procur harṣa-gadgadayā girā |
pitaraṃ sarva-suhṛdam avitāram ivārbhakāḥ ||72||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of harṣa (joy) from the First Canto:

The citizens began to speak to receive the Lord, with choked voices in joy, their faces cheerful with affection, just as wards welcome their guardian and father. SB 1.11.5

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
harim avalokya puro bhuvi
patito daṇḍa-praṇāma-śata-kāmaḥ |
pramada-vimugdho nṛpatiḥ
punar utthānaṃ visasmāra ||73||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Bahulāśva, seeing Kṛṣṇa, fell to the ground, thinking he would offer respects a hundred times, but being agitated by joy, he forgot to rise up.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
klamo, yathā skānde –
aśoṣayan manas tasya mlāpayan mukha-paṅkajam |
ādhis tad-virahe deva grīṣme sara ivāṃśumān ||74||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: An example of klama (also called mlāni or glāni—depletion of energy), from Skanda Purāṇa:

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nirvedo, yathā –
dhanyāḥ sphurati tava sūrya karāḥ sahasraṃ
ye sarvadā yadupateḥ padayoḥ patanti |
bandhyo dṛśāṃ daśa-śatī dhriyate mamāsau
dūre muhūrtam api yā na vilokate tam ||75||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Nirveda (self-deprecation):

O sun! The thousands of rays coming from you are fortunate, because they fall at the lotus feet of the lord of the Yadus. But all my thousand eyes are useless, because they have not seen Him even from far off, and not even for a second.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sthāyī –
sambhramaḥ prabhutā-jñānāt kampaś cetasi sādaraḥ |
anenaikyaṃ gatā prītiḥ sambhrama-prītir ucyate |
eṣā rase ’tra kathitā sthāyi-bhāvatayā budhaiḥ ||76||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The sthāyi-bhāva of prīti-rasa:

Eagerness to execute service with respect and trembling with eagerness, because of knowledge of the Lord’s greatness, is the quality of sambhrama. Prīti combined with this sambhrama is called sambhrama-prīti.[22] The learned call this sambhrama-prīti the sthāyi-bhāva of prīti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
āśritādeḥ puraivoktaḥ prakāro rati-janmani |
tatra pāriṣadādes tu hetuḥ saṃskāra eva hi |
saṃskārodbodhakās tasya darśana-śravaṇādayaḥ ||77||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the chapter on bhāva in general (1.3.6), it was explained that bhāva (sthāyi-bhāva) appears by sādhana (usual) or by mercy (rare). This describes the process by which rati arises for the āśritas and others described in this chapter (who are sādhana-siddhas). However, the rati of pāriṣadas and anugas described in this chapter is caused only by saṃskāras or impressions (since all the examples given were nitya-siddhas). Seeing and hearing about Kṛṣṇa stimulates the impressions.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣā tu sambhrama-prītiḥ prāpnuvaty uttarottaram |
vṛddhiṃ premā tataḥ snehas tato rāga iti tridhā ||78||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Sambhrama-prīti gradually increases in three stages to prema, sneha and finally rāga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra sambhrama-prītiḥ, yathā śrī-daśame–
mamādyāmaṅgalaṃ naṣṭaṃ phalavāṃś caiva me bhavaḥ |
yan namasye bhagavato yogi-dhyeyāṅghri-paṅkajam ||79||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of sambhrama-prīti (sthāyi-bhāva), from the Tenth Canto:

Today all my sinful reactions have been eradicated and my birth has become worthwhile, since I will offer my obeisances to the Supreme Lord’s lotus feet, upon which mystic yogīs meditate. SB 10.38.6

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
kalinda-nandinī-kula-kadamba-vana-vallabham |
kadā namaskariṣyāmi gopa-rūpaṃ tam īśvaram ||80||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

When will I have the good fortune to offer respects to the Supreme Lord in the form of a cowherd boy, fond of the kadamba forests on the bank of the Yamunā River? [24]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha premā –
hrāsa-śaṅkā-cyutā baddha-mūlā premeyam ucyate |
asyānubhāvāḥ kathitās tatra vyasanitādayaḥ ||81||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When sambhrama-prīti becomes firmly fixed, with no doubt that it will decrease, it is called prema. The anubhāvas are such things as complete attachment to the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
aṇimādi-saukhya-vīcīm avīci-duḥkha-pravāhaṃ vā |
naya māṃ vikṛtir na hi me tvat-padakamalāvalambasya ||82||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Since I have taken shelter of Your lotus feet, You may throw me into the waves of suffering in the hell called Avīci, or throw me in the waves of happiness by bestowing siddhis like aṇima, but I will not change my prema for You.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
ruṣājvalita-buddhinā bhṛgu-sutena śapto ’py alaṃ
mayā hṛta-jagat-trayo ’py atanukaitavaṃ tanvatā |
vinindya kṛta-bandhano ’py uraga-rāja-pāśair balād
arajyata sa mayy aho dviguṇam eva vairocaniḥ ||83||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Though Bali was severely cursed by Śukrācarya, who was flaming with anger, and though I stole from Bali the three worlds while playing a trick on him as Vāmanāvatāra, and though I then criticized him for not being able to fulfill his promise, and finally bound him up with ropes made of snakes, his attachment to me increased two-fold.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha snehaḥ –
sāndraś citta-dravaṃ kurvan premā 'sneha' itīryate |
kṣaṇikasyāpi neha syād visleṣasya sahiṣṇutā ||84||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sneha:

When prema becomes extremely thick and the heart melts, it is called sneha. In this state, one cannot tolerate even a moment’s separation from the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
dambhena bāṣpāmbu-jharasya keśavaṃ
vīkṣya dravac-cittam asusruvat tava |
ity uccakair dhārayato vicittatāṃ
citrā na te dāruka dāru-kalpatā ||85||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O Dāruka! Seeing Kṛṣṇa, your heart used to turn liquid on the pretext of your gushing tears. Now, in separation from Kṛṣṇa, it is not astonishing that you, Dāruka, have become like a doll made out of wood (dārukalpatā) out of extreme loss of consciousness.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
patnīṃ ratna-nidheḥ parām upaharan pūreṇa bāṣpāmbhasāṃ
rajyan-mañjula-kaṇṭha-garbha-luṭhita-stotrākṣaropakramaḥ |
cumban phullakadamba-ḍambara-tulāṅgaiḥ samīkṣiyācyutaṃ
stabdho ’py abhyadhikāṃ śriyaṃ praṇamatāṃ vṛndād dadhāroddhavaḥ ||86||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

On seeing Kṛṣṇa, Uddhava offered a gift in the form of the best river of tears, and his voice choked when he began to recite prayers using sweet attractive words and sweet tone. His body erupted in goose bumps. Becoming completely stunned, he radiated superiority over all other devotees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha rāgaḥ –
snehaḥ sa rāgo yena syāt sukhaṃ duḥkham api sphuṭam
tat-sambandha-lave ’py atra prītiḥ prāṇa-vyayair api ||87||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Rāga:

That sneha which turns sorrow into happiness by a slight appearance of Kṛṣṇa is called rāga. In this state of rāga, the devotee will do anything for the pleasure of Kṛṣṇa, even giving up his life.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
gurur api bhujagād bhīs takṣakāt prājya-rājya-
cyutir ati-śāyinī ca prāyacaryā ca gurvī |
atasanuta mudam uccaiḥ kṛṣṇa-līlā-sudhāntar-
viharaṇa-sacivatvād auttareyasya rājñaḥ ||88||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though extremely terrified of the snake Takṣaka, Parīkṣit, son of Uttarā, giving up his great kingdom and undertaking the extreme vow of fasting till death, became most blissful through drinking the nectar of Kṛṣṇa’s pastimes.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
keśavasya karuṇā-lave ’pi ced
bāḍavo ’pi kila ṣaḍavo mama |
asya yady adayatā kuśa-sthalī
pūrṇa-siddhir api me kuśasthalī ||89||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

If Keśava shows even a little mercy to me, the vāḍaba fire will be like a sweet drink. If He does not show me mercy, even Dvārakā, full of wealth and power, will be like a piece of grassy earth.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prāya ādya-dvaye premā snehaḥ pāriṣadeṣv asau |
parīkṣiti bhaved rāgo dāruke ca tathoddhave ||90||
vrajānugeṣv anekeṣu raktaka-pramukheṣu ca |
asminn abhyudite bhāvaḥ prāyaḥ syāt sakhya-leśa-bhāk ||91||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This sambhrama-prīti manifests as prema in the adhikṛtas and āsritas and in the pāriṣadas, it manifests as sneha, and in Parīkṣit, Dāruka and Uddhava (pāriṣadas) it manifests as rāga. (Rāga also manifests in the anugas.) When rāga appears in many anugas in Vraja such as Raktaka, the dāsya-bhāva becomes mixed with sakhya.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
śuddhāntān militaṃ bāṣpa-ruddha-vāg uddhavo harim |
kiñcit-kuñcita-netrāntaḥ svāntena pariṣasvaje ||92||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

Seeing the Lord coming out of the inner chambers, Uddhava’s eyes become filled with tears and his voice choked up. Closing his eyes slightly, he embraced the Lord in his heart.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: ayoga-yogāv etasya prabhedau kathitāv ubhau ||93||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Prīti-rasa has two types: ayoga (separation) and yoga (meeting).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha ayogaḥ –
saṅgābhāvo harer dhīrair ayoga iti kathyate |
ayoge tan-manaskatvaṃ tad-guṇādy-anusandhayaḥ ||94||
tat prāpty-apāya-cintādyāḥ sarveṣāṃ kathitāḥ kriyāḥ |
utkaṇṭhitaṃ viyogaś cety ayoge ’pi dvidhocyate ||95||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Lack of association with the Lord is called ayoga (separation). In this state, the anubhāvas are concentration on the Lord within the heart, reviewing His qualities, and thinking of means of attaining His association. There are two types of ayoga: utkaṇṭhitam (longing before ever meeting the Lord) and viyoga (separation after meeting).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra utkaṇṭhitam –
adṛṣṭa-pūrvasya harer didṛkṣotkaṇṭhitaṃ matam ||96||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The desire to see the Lord when the devotee has not seen Him at all is called utkaṇṭhitam.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā nārasiṃhe –
cakāra meghe tad-varṇe bahu-māna-ratiṃ nṛpaḥ |
pakṣapātena tan-nāmni mṛge padme ca tad-dṛśi ||97||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of longing before meeting, from Narasiṃha Purāṇa:

King Ikṣvāku, because of his extreme attachment to the Lord, developed great rati for the black cloud because of its color, the kṛṣna-sāra deer because of its name, and the lotus which resembled Kṛṣṇa’s eyes.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā va, śrī-daśame –
apy adya viṣṇor manujatvam īyuṣor
bhārāvatārāya bhuvo nijecchayā
lāvaṇya-dhāmno bhavitopalambhanaṃ
mahyaṃ na na syāt phalam añjasā dṛśaḥ ||98||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example of longing before meeting (utkaṇṭḥitam), from the Tenth Canto:

I am going to see the Supreme Lord Viṣṇu, the reservoir of all beauty, who by His own sweet will has now assumed a humanlike form to relieve the earth of her burden. Thus there is no denying that my eyes will achieve the perfection of their existence. SB 10.38.10

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atrāyoga-prasaktānāṃ sarveṣām api sambhave |
autsukya-dainya-nirveda-cintānāṃ cāpalasya ca |
jaḍatonmāda-mohānām api syād atiriktatā ||99||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though it is possible for all the vyabhicāri-bhāvas to appear in ayoga (utkaṇṭḥitam) within prīti-bhakti-rasa, autsukya, dainyam, nirveda, cintā, cāpalatā, jaḍatā, unmāda and moha are more common.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra autsukyaṃ, yathā śrī-kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛte (41) –
amūny adhanyāni dināntarāṇi
hare tvad-ālokanam antareṇa |
anātha-bandho karuṇaika-sindho
hā hanta hā hanta kathaṃ nayāmi ||100||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Autsukya (impatience) in utkaṇṭḥitam, from Kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛta:

O Lord! O protector of the unprotected! O ocean of mercy! How can I pass my unfortunate days without having seen You?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
vilocana-sudhāmbudhes tava padāravinda-dvayī
vilocana-rasa-cchaṭām anupalabhya vikṣubhyataḥ |
mano mama manāg api kvacid anāpnuvan nirvṛtiṃ
kṣaṇārdham api manyate vraja-mahendra varṣa-vrajam ||101||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O Kṛṣṇa! You are an ocean of nectar for the eyes. Not having the pleasure of seeing Your two lotus feet, I am disturbed and my mind cannot find any happiness. A moment seems like many years.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dainyaṃ, yathā tatraiva –
nibaddha-mūrdhāñjalir eṣa yāce
nīrandhra-dainyonnati-mukta-kaṇṭham |
dayāmbudhe deva bhavat-kaṭākṣa-
dākṣiṇya-leśena sakṛn niṣiñca ||102||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of dainyam (lowness) in utkaṇṭḥitam:

O Lord! O ocean of mercy! With my hand folded over my head, I am praying in a humble mood with crying voice that You should sprinkle me with a particle of Your generous glance.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
asi śaśi-mukuṭādyair apy alabhyekṣaṇas tvaṃ
laghur aghahara kīṭād apy ahaṃ kūṭa-karmā |
iti visadṛśatāpi prārthane prārthayāmi
snapaya kṛpaṇa-bandho mām apāṅga-cchaṭābhiḥ ||103||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O slayer of Agha! Even Śiva cannot see You, but I am more insignificant than an insect and full of sinful acts. Though I am unqualified for prayer, I am still praying, O friend of the fallen, that You bathe me with your glance.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nirvedo, yathā –
sphuṭaṃ śritavator api śruti-niṣevayā ślāghyatāṃ
mamābhavanir etayor bhavatu netrayor mandayoḥ |
bhaven na hi yayoḥ padaṃ madhurima-śriyām āspadaṃ
padāmbujanakhāṅkurād api visāri rocis tava ||104||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Nirveda (self-deprecation) in utkaṇṭḥitam:

Though my eyes are praised because of seeing so many scriptures, they are unfortunate, because they do not see the wealth of sweetness in the form of the effulgence coming from the toe nails of Your lotus feet. Let those eyes be destroyed!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
cintām, yathā –
hari-pada-kamalāvaloka-tṛṣṇā
taralamater api yogyatām avīkṣya |
avanata-vadanasya cintayā me
hari hari niḥśvasato niśāḥ prayāti ||105||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of cintā (pondering) in utkaṇṭḥitam:

I spend my nights expelling sighs of grief with bowed head, thinking myself unqualified for seeing the lotus feet of the Lord, though I have such longing to see them.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
cāpalaṃ, yathā śrī-kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛte (32) –
tvac-chaiśavaṃ tri-bhuvanādbhutam ity avehi
mac-cāpalaṃ ca tava vā mama vādhigamyam |
tat kiṃ karomi viralaṃ muralī-vilāsi
mugdhaṃ mukhāmbujam udīkṣitum īkṣaṇābhyām ||106||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of cāpalam (insolence) utkaṇṭḥitam, from Kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛta:

You know that Your sweet youth astonishes all three worlds. Thus, You and I can understand my insolence arising from my desire to see You. Give me some instruction. What should I do to see Your rare lotus face with the attractive flute?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
hriyam aghahara muktvā dṛk-pataṅgī mamāsau
bhayam api damayitvā bhakta-vṛndāt tṛṣārtā |
niravadhim avicārya svasya ca kṣodimānaṃ
tava caraṇa-sarojaṃ leḍhum anvicchatīśa ||107||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example of cāpalam:

O destroyer of Agha! O Lord! I have give up shyness and become fearless of devotees. With thirsty heart, not considering my insignificant position, I desire, like a bee, to taste constantly Your lotus feet.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
jaḍatā, yathā saptame (7.4.37) –
nyasta-krīḍanako bālo jaḍavat tan-manastayā |
kṛṣṇa-graha-gṛhītātmā na veda jagad īdṛśam ||108||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of jaḍatā (inability to make decisions) in utkaṇṭḥitam, from the Seventh Canto:

From the very beginning of his childhood, Prahlāda Mahārāja was uninterested in childish playthings. Indeed, he gave them up altogether and remained silent and dull, being fully absorbed in Kṛṣṇa consciousness. Since his mind was always affected by Kṛṣṇa consciousness, he could not understand how the world goes on being fully absorbed in the activities of sense gratification. SB 7.4.37

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
nimeṣonmuktākṣaḥ katham iha parispanda-vidhurāṃ
tanuṃ bibhrad bhavyaḥ pratikṛtir ivāste dvija-patiḥ |
aye jñātaṃ vaṃśī-rasika-nava-rāga-vyasaninā
puraḥ śyāmāmbhode bata vinihitā dṛṣṭir amunā ||109||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Why does this qualified brāhmaṇa remain with body unmoving, without blinking his eyes, like a statue? It seems he has become addicted to fresh prema for the enjoyer of the flute, and his eyes are fixed on the black cloud in front of him.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
unmādo, yathā saptame –
nadati kvacid utkaṇṭho vilajjo nṛtyati kvacit |
kvacit tad-bhāvanā-yuktas tanmayo 'nucakāra ha ||110||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of unmāda (insanity) in utkaṇṭḥitam, from the Seventh Canto:

Sometimes, upon seeing the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Prahlāda Mahārāja would loudly call in full anxiety. He sometimes lost his shyness in jubilation and began dancing in ecstasy, and sometimes, being fully absorbed in thoughts of Kṛṣṇa, he felt oneness and imitated the pastimes of the Lord. SB 7.4.40

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
kvacin naṭati niṣpaṭaṃ kvacid asambhavaṃ stambhate
kvacid vihasati sphuṭaṃ kvacid amandam ākrandati |
lasaty analasaṃ kvacit kvacid apārtham ārtāyate
harer abhinavoddhura-praṇaya-sīdhum atto muniḥ ||111||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Nārada, drunk with the nectar of new love for the Lord, sometimes danced naked, sometimes became paralyzed inappropriately, sometimes laughed loudly, sometimes shouted intensely, sometimes played about without fatigue, and sometimes wandered around as if in pain, when there was no cause for pain.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
moha, yathā hari-bhakti-sudhodaye –
ayogyam ātmānam itīśa-darśane
sa manyamānas tad-anāpti-kātaraḥ |
udvela-duḥkhārṇava-magna-mānasaḥ
śrutāśrudhāro dvija mūrcchitāpatat ||112||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of moha (blank mind, fainting) in utkaṇṭḥitam, from Hari-bhakti-sudhodaya:

O brāhmaṇa! Thinking himself unqualified for seeing the Lord, Prahlāda, grieved because he could not attain Him, drowned in an ocean of intense suffering. Tears flowing from his eyes, he fainted.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
hari-caraṇa-vilokābdhi-tāpāvalībhir
bata vidhūta-cid-ambhasy atra nas tīrtha-varye |
śruti-puṭa-parivāheneśa nāmāmṛtāni
kṣipata nanu satīrthāś ceṣṭatāṃ prāṇa-haṃsaḥ ||113||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O fellow students! Our revered guru has dried up like a tīrtha devoid of its sacred water, which is its very life. This has happened because of the heat of separation on not seeing the feet of the Lord. Pour into that tīrtha the nectar of the Lord’s name by means of the canal of his ear, and then the swan of his life will be revived.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha viyogaḥ –
viyogo labdha-saṅgena vicchedo danuja-dviṣā ||114||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When one attains the association of the Lord and then becomes separated from him that separation is called viyoga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
bali-suta-bhuja-ṣaṇḍa-khaṇḍanāya
kṣataja-puraṃ puruṣottame prayāte |
vidhuta-vidhura-buddhir uddhavo ’yaṃ
viraha-niruddha-manā niruddhavo ’bhūt ||115||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of viyoga:

When Kṛṣṇa went to Śonitapura to cut of the arms of Bāṇa (the son of Bali), Uddhava’s mind became overcome with separation. His intelligence was shaken because of suffering, and he became devoid of bliss.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
aṅgeṣu tāpaḥ kṛśatā jāgaryālamba-śūnyatā |
adhṛtir jaḍatā vyādhir unmādo mūrcchitaṃ mṛtiḥ
viyoge sambhrama-prīter daśāvasthāḥ prakīrtitāḥ ||116||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are ten conditions of sambhrama-prīti in viyoga: heat in the body, thinness, insomnia, instability of the mind, lack of interest in anything, dullness (jaḍatā), sickness (vyādhi), insanity (unmāda), fainting and death-like symptoms (mṛti).[26]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
anavasthitir ākhyātā cittasyālamba-śūnyatā |
arāgitā tu sarvasminn adhṛtiḥ kathitā budhaiḥ |
anye ’ṣṭau prakaṭārthatvāt tāpādyā na hi lakṣitāḥ ||117||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Alamba-śūnyatā means instability of the mind and adhṛti means lack of attraction for anything. Since the meanings of the other words are clear, they are not explained here.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra tāpo, yathā –
asmān dunoti kamalaṃ tapanasya mitraṃ
ratnākaraś ca vaḍabānala-gūḍha-mūrtiḥ |
indīvaraṃ vidhu-suhṛt katham īśvaraṃ vā
taṃ smārayan munipate dahatīha sabhyān ||118||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of heat in viyoga:

O best of sages! The lotus, (though it reminds us of Your eyes), will give us pain because it is the friend of the sun, and the ocean (though it reminds us of Your complexion), gives us pain because it is dominated by the vāḍaba fire. But why does the blue lotus, friend of the moon, making us remember the Lord, also cause us burning pain?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kṛśatā, yathā –
dadhati tava tathādya sevakānāṃ
bhuja-parighāḥ kṛśatāṃ ca pāṇḍutāṃ ca |
patati bata yathā mṛṇāla-buddhyā
sphuṭam iha pāṇḍava-mitra pāṇḍu-pakṣaḥ ||119||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of thinness in viyoga:

O friend of the Pāṇḍavas! The arms of all your servants have become thin and pale in separation from You. The swans have come down thinking that those arms are stems of the water lily.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
jāgaryā, yathā –
virahān mura-dviṣaś ciraṃ vidhurāṅge parikhinna-cetasi |
kṣaṇadāḥ kṣaṇa-dāyitojjhitā bahulāśve bahulās tadābhavan ||120||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of sleeplessness in viyoga:

King Bahulāśva, in long separation from Kṛṣṇa, became distressed in body and mind. Many of his nights gave up their pleasurable nature of sleep.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
āvalambana-śūnyatā, yathā –
vijaya-ratha-kuṭumbinā vinānyan
na kila kuṭumbam ihāsti nas trilokyām |
bhramad idam anavekṣya yat-padābjaṃ
kvacid api na vyavatiṣṭhate ’dya cetaḥ ||121||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of instability of mind in viyoga:

I do not have other family in the three worlds except Kṛṣṇa, the charioteer of Arjuna. Not seeing His lotus feet, my mind wanders about, not being fixed anywhere.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
adhṛtiḥ, yathā –
prekṣya piñcha-kulam akṣi pidhatte
naicikī-nicayam ujjhati dūre |
vaṣṭi yaṣṭim api nādya murāre
raktakas tava padāmbuja-raktaḥ ||122||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of detachment viyoga:

O Murāri! In separation from You, Your servant Raktaka, devoted to Your lotus feet, closed his eyes on seeing a peacock feather. He has given up all the attractive cows and has no desire to take up his herding stick.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
jaḍatā, yathā –
yaudhiṣṭhiraṃ puram upeyuṣi padmanābhe
khedānala-vyatikarair ativiklavasya |
svedāśrubhir na hi paraṃ jalatām avāpur
aṅgāni niṣkriyatayā ca kiloddhavasya ||123||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of jaḍatā (lack of judgment) in viyoga:

When Kṛṣṇa went to Indraprastha, Uddhava, disturbed by grief, became wet with tears and perspiration, and ceased to move at all.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vyādhir, yathā –
cirayati maṇim anveṣṭuṃ
calite murabhidi kuśasthalī-purataḥ |
samajani dhṛta-nava-vyādhiḥ
pavana-vyādhir yathārthākhyaḥ ||124||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of vyādhi (sickness) in viyoga:

When Kṛṣṇa went to find the Syamantaka jewel and was absent a long time, Uddhava became afflicted with a new sickness out of separation, and thus was appropriately called “he who was afflicted by wind disease.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
unmādo, yathā –
proṣite bata nijādhidaivate
raivate navam avekṣya nīradam |
bhrānta-dhīr ayam adhīram uddhavaḥ
paśya nauti ramate namasyati ||125||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of unmāda (insanity) in viyoga:

When his personal deity went away, Uddhava became confused in mind. Look, seeing a fresh cloud on Raivata Mountain, he is praising it, frolicking about, and offering his respects to it.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mūrcchitaṃ, yathā –
samajani daśā viśleṣāt te padāmbuja-sevināṃ
vraja-bhuvi tathā nāsīn nidrā-lavo ’pi yathā purā |
yadu-vara dara-śvāsenāmī vitarkita-jīvitāḥ
satatam adhunā niśceṣṭāṅgās taṭāny adhiśerate ||126||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of moha or mūrcchitam (fainting) in viyoga:

O best of the Yadus! Those who were serving Your lotus feet got no sleep when You were in Vraja. In separation from You, they had reached a similar state. But now, by their slight breathing, it is questionable whether they are living or not. With unconscious bodies, they lie on the bank of the Yamunā.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mṛtiḥ, yathā –
danuja-damana yāte jīvane tvayy akasmāt
pracura-viraha-tāpair dhvanta-hṛt-paṅkajāyām |
vrajam abhi paritas te dāsa-kāsāra-paṅktau
na kila vasatim ārtāḥ kartum icchanti haṃsāḥ ||127||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of mṛti (death-like symptoms) in viyoga:

O killer of demons! You are their very life! Since you departed so suddenly from Vraja, the lotuses of their hearts in the lakes of their bodies have been dried up by the fire of separation. The swans of their life airs, being unhappy in that situation, no longer desire to live there.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
aśivatvān na ghaṭate bhakte kutrāpy asau mṛtiḥ |
kṣobhakatvād viyogasya jāta-prāyeti kathyate ||128||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Because of its inauspiciousness, death is impossible for these devotees. When there are death-like symptoms caused by extreme disturbance due to separation from Kṛṣṇa, it is called mṛti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha yogaḥ –
kṛṣṇena saṅgamo yas tu sa yoga iti kīrtyate |
yoge ’pi kathitaḥ siddhis tuṣṭiḥ sthitir iti tridhā ||129||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Meeting with Kṛṣṇa is called yoga. There are three types of yoga: siddhi, tuṣṭi, and sthiti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra siddhiḥ –
utkaṇṭhite hareḥ prāptiḥ siddhir ity abhidhīyate ||130||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Attaining the Lord after a state of great longing (and not having met Him before) is called siddhi (attaining one’s desires).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrī-kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛte (57) –
mauliś candraka-bhūṣaṇo marakata-stambhābhirāmaṃ vapur
vaktraṃ citra-vimugdha-hāsa-madhuraṃ bāle vilole dṛśau |
vācaḥ śaiśava-śītayā mada-gaja-ślāghyā vilāsa-sthitir
mandaṃ mandam aye ka eṣa mathurā-vīthīṃ mitho gāhate ||131||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of siddhi, from Kṛṣṇa-kārṇāmṛta:

On his head there is a peacock feather ornament. His body is firm and blue, like an emerald[27] pillar. His face is sweet with an attractive, variegated smile. His eyes are soft and playful. His childish voice removes all suffering. The movements of his body are more praiseworthy than the gait of a mad elephant. Who is this solitary person who moves softy about Vṛndāvana?[28]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā śrī-daśame –
rathāt tūrṇam avaplutya so ’krūraḥ prema-vihvalaḥ |
papāta caraṇopānte daṇḍavad rāma-kṛṣṇayoḥ ||132||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example of siddhi, from the Tenth Canto:

Akrūra, overwhelmed with affection, quickly jumped down from his chariot and fell at the feet of Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma like a rod. SB 10.38.34

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tuṣṭiḥ –
jāte viyoge kaṃsāreḥ samprāptis tuṣṭir ucyate ||133||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Meeting Kṛṣṇa after separation from Him is called tuṣṭi.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā prathame –
kathaṃ vayaṃ nātha ciroṣite tvayi
prasanna-dṛṣṭyākhila-tāpa-śoṣaṇam |
jīvema te sundara-hāsa-śobhitam
apaśyamānā vadanaṃ manoharam ||134||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of tuṣṭi, from the First Canto:

O master, if You live abroad all the time, then we cannot look at Your attractive face, whose smiles vanquish all our sufferings. How can we exist without Your presence? [29] SB 1.11.10

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
samakṣam akṣamaḥ prekṣya harim añjali-bandhane |
dāruko dvārakā-dvāri tatra citra-daśāṃ yayau ||135||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example of tuṣṭi:

Seeing Kṛṣṇa in front of him at the gate of Dvārakā, Dāruka reached such an astonishing condition that he could not even greet Kṛṣṇa with folded hands.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sthitiḥ –
saha-vāso mukundena sthitir nigaditā budhaiḥ ||136||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wise call living together with Kṛṣṇa permanently sthiti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā haṃsadūte (50) –
purastād ābhīrī-gaṇa-bhayada-nāmā sa kaṭhino
maṇi-stambhālambī kuru-kula-kathāṃ saṅkalayitā |
sa jānubhyām aṣṭāpada-bhuvanam avaṣṭabhya bhavitā
guroḥ śiṣyo nūnaṃ pada-kamala-saṃvāhana-rataḥ ||137||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of sthiti, from Haṃsadūta:

That cruel person given a fearful name by the gopīs (Akrūra), leaning against a jewel pillar, is discussing with Kṛṣṇa about the Kurus. The disciple of Bṛhaspati, Uddhava, is sitting on the golden floor and is absorbed in massaging the lotus feet of the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nijāvasara-śuśrūṣā-vidhāne sāvadhānatā |
puras tasya niveśādyā yoge ’mīṣāṃ kriyā matāḥ ||138||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In association with Kṛṣṇa, the anubhāvas of the dāsa-bhaktas are such things as carefully performing their services and sitting in front of Him.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kecid asyā rateḥ kṛṣṇa-bhakty-āsvāda-bahirmukhāḥ |
bhavatvam eva niścitya na rasāvasthatāṃ jaguḥ ||139||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Some persons with no taste for Kṛṣṇa bhakti, on considering dāsya-rati, conclude that it does not reach a state of rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
iti tāvad asādhīyo yat purāṇeṣu keṣucit |
śrīmad-bhāgavate caiṣa prakaṭo dṛśyate rasaḥ ||140||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Their opinion is unfounded, because in many purāṇas and in the Bhāgavatam, this dāsya-rasa is clearly seen.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathā hi ekādaśe –
kvacit rudanty acyuta-cintayā kvacid
dhasanti nandanti vadanty alaukikāḥ |
nṛtyanti gāyanty anuśīlayanty ajaṃ
bhavanti tūṣṇīṃ param etya nirvṛtāḥ ||141||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Thus it says in the Eleventh Canto:

Having achieved love of Godhead, the devotees sometimes cry out loud, absorbed in thought of the infallible Lord. Sometimes they laugh, feel great pleasure, speak out loud to the Lord, dance or sing. Such devotees, having transcended material, conditioned life, sometimes imitate the unborn Supreme by acting out His pastimes. And sometimes, achieving His personal audience, they remain peaceful and silent. SB 11.3.32

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
saptame ca –
niśamya karmāṇi guṇān atulyān
vīryāṇi līlā-tanubhiḥ kṛtāni |
yadātiharṣotpulakāśru-gadgadaṃ
protkaṇṭha udgāyati rauti nṛtyati ||142||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Also it says in the Seventh Canto:

One who is situated in devotional service is certainly the controller of his senses, and thus he is a liberated person. When such a liberated person, the pure devotee, hears of the transcendental qualities and activities of the Lord’s incarnations for the performance of various pastimes, his hair stands on end on his body, tears fall from his eyes, and in his spiritual realization his voice falters. Sometimes he very openly dances, sometimes he sings loudly, and sometimes he cries. Thus he expresses his transcendental jubilation. SB 7.7.34

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣātra bhaktābhāvānāṃ prāyakī prakriyoditā |
kintu kālādi-vaiśiṣṭhyāt kvacit syāt sīma-laṅghanam ||143||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In these verses, the usual emotions of these dāsya-bhaktas have been given. According to particular times, there will be exceptions to the general behavior.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha gaurava-prītiḥ –
lālyābhimānināṃ kṛṣṇe syāt prītir gauravottarā |
