sā vibhāvādibhiḥ puṣṭā gaurava-prītir ucyate ||144||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Affection for Kṛṣṇa by those who identify themselves as his objects of indulgence is called gauravottara. When this affection is nourished by elements starting with vibhāva it is called gaurava-prīti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ālambanāḥ –
hariś ca tasya lālyāś ca bhavanty ālambanā iha ||145||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The ālambanas of this rasa are the Lord (viṣaya) and the sons or younger brothers and sisters (āśraya) under His affectionate care.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra hariḥ, yathā –
ayam upahita-karṇaḥ prastute vṛṣṇi-vṛddhair
yadupatir itihāse manda-hāsojjvalāsyaḥ |
upadiśati sudharmā-madhyam adhyāsya dīvyan
hitam iha nijayāgre ceṣṭayaivātmajān naḥ ||146||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The Lord:

When the elder Vṛṣṇis raise some proposal Kṛṣṇa hears attentively. When a humorous topic arises, a gentle smile appears on His face. Enjoying himself in this way while sitting in the assembly hall, He is giving beneficial instructions to us, His sons, by such conduct.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mahā-gurur mahā-kīrtir mahā-buddhir mahā-balaḥ |
rakṣī lālaka ity ādyair guṇair ālambano hariḥ ||147||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In this gaurava-prīti-rasa, Kṛṣṇa is the viṣaya, endowed with qualities of being a great father, having great fame, great intelligence, great strength, being a protector and caresser of His children.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha lālyāḥ –
lālyāḥ kila kaniṣṭhatva-putratvādy-abhimāninaḥ |
kaniṣṭhāḥ sāraṇa-gada-subhadrā-pramukhāḥ smṛtāḥ |
pradyumna-cārudeṣṇādyāḥ sāmbādyāś ca kumārakāḥ ||148||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those who think that they are the younger brothers or sisters or that they are his sons, are the āśrayas of this rasa. Among them Sāraṇa, Gada, Subhadrā are chief younger brothers and sisters, and Pradyumna, Cārudeṣṇa and Sāmba are his sons.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ rūpaṃ, yathā –
api murāntaka-pārṣada-maṇḍalād
adhika-maṇḍana-veśa-guṇa-śriyaḥ |
āsata-pīta-sita-dyutibhir yutā
yadu-kumāra-gaṇāḥ puri remire ||149||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: These children possess more clothing, ornaments, qualities and effulgence than the pāriṣadas. They have black, yellow and white complexions, and they play in Dvārakā.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ bhaktiḥ, yathā –
sagdhiṃ bhajanti hariṇā mukham unnamayya
tāmbūla-carvitam adanti ca dīyamānam |
ghrātāś ca mūrdhni parirabhya bhavanty udasrāḥ
sāmbādayaḥ kati purā vidadhus tapāṃsi ||150||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their devotion:

Sāmba and others eat with Kṛṣṇa, with their heads raised. They eat His betel remnants. When Kṛṣṇa embraces them and smells their heads, they burst into tears. How much austerity they must have done previously to attain this position!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: rukmiṇī-nandanas teṣu lālyeṣu pravaro mataḥ ||151||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among all the lālyas, Rukmiṇī’s son Pradyumna is the chief.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tasya rūpam –
sa jayati śambara-damanaḥ sukumāro yadu-kumāra-kula-mauliḥ |
janayati janeṣu janaka-bhrāntiṃ yaḥ suṣṭhu rūpeṇa ||152||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
His form:

All glories to the young boy, crown jewel of the young Yadu princes, Pradyumna, killer of Śambara, who is mistaken for Kṛṣṇa because of his beautiful form.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asya bhaktiḥ –
prabhāvati samīkṣyatāṃ divi kṛpāmbudhir mādṛśāṃ
sa eṣa paramo gurur garuḍa-go yadūnāṃ patiḥ |
yataḥ kim api lālanaṃ vayam avāpya daroddhurāḥ
purārim api saṅgare guru-ruṣaṃ tiraskurmahe ||153||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
His devotion:

O Prabhāvatī! See in the sky the lord of the Yadus, our guru, the ocean of mercy, riding on Garuḍa. Cared for by Him with the greatest affection, I have attained such confidence that I defeated the ferocious Tripurāri in battle.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ubhayeṣāṃ sadārādhya-dhiyaiva bhajatām api |
sevakānām ihaiśvarya-jñānasyaiva pradhānatā ||
lālyānāṃ tu sva-sambandha-sphūrter eva samantataḥ ||154||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though both the servants and the lālyas worship Kṛṣṇa constantly as the most worthy person of worship, awareness of the Lord’s majesty is more prominent in the servants. Identification as the relatives (His children) of Kṛṣṇa is more prominent in the minds of the lālyas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vraja-sthānāṃ paraiśvarya-jñāna-śūnya-dhiyām api |
asty eva vallavādhīśa-putratvaiśvarya-vedanam ||155||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though awareness of Kṛṣṇa as the Lord is absent in the servants in Vraja, they are aware that He has powers, since He is the son of the cowherd leader.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha uddīpanāḥ –
uddīpanās tu vātsalya-smita-prekṣādayo hareḥ ||156||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The uddīpanas for gaurava-prīti-bhaktas are such things as the Lord’s glance mixed with affection and smiling.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
agre sānugrahaṃ paśyann agrajaṃ vyagra-mānasaḥ |
gadaḥ padāravinde ’sya vidadhe daṇḍavan-natim ||157||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Seeing Kṛṣṇa with His affectionate glance in front of him, Gada, with confused mind, offered his respects at Kṛṣṇa’s lotus feet.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha anubhāvāḥ –
anubhāvās tu tasyāgre nīcāsana-niveśanam |
guror vartmānusāritvaṃ dhuras tasya parigrahaḥ |
svairācāra-vimokṣādyāḥ śītā lālyeṣu kīrtitāḥ ||158||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Anubhāvas:

Sitting on a seat lower than Kṛṣṇa’s, following after Kṛṣṇa, following His order and giving up their own wishes are the śīta anubhāvas of the lālyas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra nīcāsana-niveśanam, yathā –
yadu-sadasi surendrair drāg upavrajyamānāḥ
sukhada-karaka-vārbhir brahmaṇābhyukṣitāṅgaḥ |
madhuripum abhivandya svarṇa-pīṭhāni muñcan
bhuvam abhi makarāṅko rāṅkavaṃ svīcakāra ||159||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sitting lower than Kṛṣṇa:

Following behind the best of devatās and sprinkled with water by Brahmā, Pradyumna entered the assembly and offered respects to Kṛṣṇa. Avoiding a golden seat, he sat on deer skin spread on the floor.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dāsaiḥ sādhārāṇāś cānye procyante ’mīṣu kecana |
praṇāmo mauna-bāhulyaṃ saṅkocaṃ praśrayāḍhyatā |
nija-prāṇa-vyayenāpi tad-ājñā-paripālanam ||160||
adho-vadanatā sthairyaṃ kāsa-hāsādi-varjanam |
tadīyātirahaḥ-keli-vārtādy-uparamādayaḥ ||161||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The lālyas share other anubhāvas in common with the dāsas: that is, offering respects, keeping very silent in front of the Lord, crouching down, humility, carrying out the Lord’s order even if it means giving up their lives, lowering their heads, firmness, not laughing or coughing in front of the Lord, and not listening to Kṛṣṇa’s private joking conversations.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sāttvikāḥ –
kandarpa vindati mukunda-padāravinda-
dvandve dṛśoḥ padam asau kila niṣprakampā |
prāleya-bindu-nicitaṃ dhṛta-kaṇṭakā te
svinnādya kaṇṭaki-phalaṃ tanur anvakārṣīt ||162||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of sāttvika-bhāvas:

Having seen the lotus feet of Mukunda, your body has become immobile, covered with perspiration, with hairs standing on end. Your body is imitating a flowering thorn bush spread with snow.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vyabhicāriṇaḥ –
anantaroktāḥ sarve ’tra bhavanti vyabhicāriṇaḥ ||163||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: All vyabhicāri-bhāvas described for those with sambhrama-prīti manifest in those with gaurava-prīti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra harṣo, yathā –
dūre darendrasya nabhasy udīrṇe
dhvanau sthitānāṃ yadu-rājadhanyām |
tanūruhais tatra kumārakāṇāṃ
naṭaiś ca hṛṣyadbhir akāri nṛtyam ||164||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Harṣa (joy):

When the conch Pāñcajanya sounded in the sky from far off, all the hairs on the bodies of young Yadu princes in Dvārakā began to dance with the dancers.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nirvedo, yathā –
dhanyaḥ sāmba bhavān sa-riṅgaṇam ayan pārśve rajaḥ-karbūro
yas tātena vikṛṣya vatsalatayā svotsaṅgam āropitaḥ |
dhiṅ māṃ durbhagam atra śambara-mayair durdaiva-visphūrjitaiḥ
prāptā na kṣaṇikāpi lālana-ratiḥ sā yena bālye pituḥ ||165||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Nirveda (self-deprecation):

O Sāmba! You are very fortunate, because when you crawl to Kṛṣṇa with your body covered with dust, He picks you up in great affection and puts you on His lap. I am unfortunate. Because of the unfortunate actions of the expansions of the demon Śambara, I did not receive even for a moment that loving care from my father.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sthāyī –
deha-sambandhitā-mānād guru-dhīr atra gauravam |
tanmayī lālake prītir gaurava-prītir ucyate ||166||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sthāyi-bhāva:

The mentality that the Lord is one’s loving protector, born from the feeling that one belongs to Him as His son is called gaurava. Such prīti towards the protector is called gaurava-prīti-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sthāyi-bhāvo ’tra sā caiṣām āmūlāt svayam ucchritā |
kañcid viśeṣam āpannā premeti sneha ity api |
rāga ity ucyate cātra gaurava-prītir eva sā ||167||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This gaurava-prīti-rati which spontaneously appears and spreads in the devotee’s heart is the sthāyi-bhāva of the rasa. This prīti, becoming excellent, becomes prema. When it becomes more elevated it becomes sneha, and it becomes even more elevated it is called rāga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra gaurava-prītiḥ, yathā –
mudrāṃ bhinatti na rada-cchadayor amandāṃ
vaktraṃ ca nonnamayati sravad-asra-kīrṇam |
dhīraḥ paraṃ kim api saṅkucatīṃ jhaṣāṅko
dṛṣṭiṃ kṣipaty aghabhidaś caraṇāravinde ||168||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Gaurava-prīti-Sthāyi-bhāva:

The most sober Pradyumna could not open his mouth in order to speak to his father. He did not lift his tear-filled eyes, and threw only furtive glances at Kṛṣṇa’s lotus feet.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
premā, yathā –
dviṣadbhiḥ kṣodiṣṭhair jagad-avihitecchasya bhavataḥ
karād ākṛṣyaiva prasabham abhimanyāv api hate |
subhadrāyāḥ prītir danuja-damana tvad-viṣayikā
prapede kalyāṇī na hi malinimānaṃ lavam api ||169||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Prema:

O killer of demons! The feeble enemies managed to take Abhimanyu from Your hands and killed him, though You desire to protect the whole world. However, Subhadrā’s auspicious affection for You did not decrease by one degree.[30]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sneho, yathā –
vimuñca pṛthu-vepathuṃ visṛja kaṇṭhākuṇṭhāyitaṃ
vimṛjya mayi nikṣipa prasarad-aśru-dhāre dṛśau |
karaṃ ca makara-dhvaja prakaṭa-kaṇṭakālaṅkṛtaṃ
nidhehi savidhe pituḥ kathaya vatsa kaḥ sambhramaḥ ||170||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O Pradyumna! Stop that excessive trembling and relieve your choking voice. Wipe the tears from your eyes and look at Me. Give me your hand full of goose bumps. O child! What is this veneration for Your father?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rāgo, yathā –
viṣam api sahasā sudhām ivāyaṃ
nipibati cet pitur iṅgitaṃ jhaṣāṅkaḥ |
visṛjati tad-asammatir yadi syād
viṣam iva tāṃ tu sudhāṃ sa eva sadyaḥ ||171||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If indicated by his father, Pradumnya will immediately drink poison as if it were nectar, and if his father does not permit it, he will reject nectar as if it were poison.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: triṣv evāyoga-yogādyā bhedāḥ pūrvavad īritāḥ ||172||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The different definitions of ayoga and yoga and their subdivisions for gaurava-prīti, preyo and vatsala-rasa are the same as those of sambhrama-prīti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra utkaṇṭhitam,[31] yathā –
śambaraḥ sumukhi labdha-durvipaḍ-
ḍambaraḥ sa ripur ambarāyitaḥ |
ambu-rāja-mahasaṃ kadā guruḥ
kambu-rāja-karam īkṣitāsmahe ||173||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Separation before first meeting:

O beautiful faced Rati! The ferocious enemy Śambara, who was a pile of dangers, has been destroyed, but when will we see Kṛṣṇa, my father, with complexion of a rain cloud, holding the Pāñcajanya conch?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha viyogaḥ –
mano mameṣṭām api geṇḍu-līlāṃ
na vaṣṭi yogyāṃ ca tathāstra-yogyām |
gurau puraṃ kauravam abhyupete
kārām iva dvāravatīm avaiti ||174||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Separation after meeting:

Since our father went to Indraprastha, my mind has no desire to practice weaponry or to play ball, though I am very fond of it. Dvārakā now seems like a prison.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
siddhiḥ –
militaḥ śambara-purato madanaḥ purato vilokayan pitaram |
ko ’ham iti svaṃ pramadān na dhīr adhīr apy asau veda ||175||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Meeting for the first time:

When Pradyumna, arriving from Śambara’s city, saw his father in front of him (for the first time), though he was by nature very sober, out of great joy, he forgot himself and thought, “Who am I?”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tuṣṭiḥ –
militam adhiṣṭhita-garuḍaṃ prekṣya yudhiṣṭhira-purān murārātim |
ajani mudā yadu-nagare sambhrama-bhūmā kumārāṇām ||176||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Meeting Kṛṣṇa again:

When Kṛṣṇa arrived from Indraprastha riding on Garuḍa, all His sons became very excited with bliss.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sthitiḥ –
kuñcayann akṣiṇī kiñcid bāṣpa-niṣpandi-pakṣiṇī |
vandate pādayor dvandvaṃ pituḥ prati-dinaṃ smaraḥ ||177||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Permanent association:

Slightly contracting his eyes while his eyelids became soaked with tears, Pradyumna offered respects to his father’s two feet every day.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
utkaṇṭhita-viyogādye yad yad vistāritaṃ na hi |
sambhrama-prītivaj jñeyaṃ tat tad evākhilaṃ budhaiḥ ||178||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wise will understand that utkaṇṭhitam, viyoga and yoga for the gaurava-prīti-rasa which have been here described very briefly are similar to the utkaṇṭhitam, viyoga and yoga of sambhrama-prīti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sthāyi-bhāvo vibhāvādyaiḥ sakhyam ātmocitair iha |
nītaś citte satāṃ puṣṭiṃ rasaḥ preyān udīryate ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the sthāyi-bhāva of sakhya-rati is nourished by suitable vibhāvas and the other elements in the devotee, it is known as preyo-bhakti-rasa (or sakhya-rasa).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ālambanāḥ –
hariś ca tad-vayasyāś ca tasminn ālambanā matāḥ ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The Lord and His friends of the same age are the ālambanas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra hariḥ –
dvibhujatvādi-bhāg atra prāgvad ālambano hariḥ ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The Lord:

The two-armed and four-armed forms of the Lord as previously described in prīti-bhakti-rasa are the forms of the Lord as ālambana.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra vraje, yathā –
mahendra-maṇi-mañjula-dyutir amanda-kunda-smitaḥ
sphurat-puraṭa-ketakī-kusuma-ramya-paṭṭāmbaraḥ |
srag-ullasad-uraḥ-sthalaḥ kvaṇita-veṇur atrāvrajan
vrajād aghaharo haraty ahaha naḥ sakhīnāṃ manaḥ ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The form in Vraja:

As the slayer of Agha returns from the cow pens, with his complexion like a delicate blue sapphire, His brilliant white smile like a kunda flower, His cloth yellow like the golden ketakī flower, forest garland shining on His chest, He steals the minds of us cowherd friends.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
anyatra, yathā –
cañcat-kaustubha-kaumudī-samudayaṃ kaumodakī-cakrayoḥ
sakhyenojjvalitais tathā jalajayor āḍhyaṃ caturbhir bhujaiḥ |
dṛṣṭvā hāri-harin-maṇi-dyuti-haraṃ śauriṃ hiraṇyāmbaraṃ
jagmuḥ pāṇḍu-sutāḥ pramoda-sudhayā naivātma-sambhāvanām ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
In other places:

Seeing Kṛṣṇa in a yellow cloth, with complexion defeating the effulgence of a beautiful emerald, with Kaustubha jewel flashing rays of moonlight, holding the club, cakra, lotus and conch in His four hands, the Pāṇḍavas forgot themselves in the sweet experience of bliss.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
suveṣaḥ sarva-sal-lakṣma-lakṣito balināṃ varaḥ |
vividhādbhuta-bhāṣā-vid vāvadūkaḥ supaṇḍitaḥ ||6||
vipula-pratibho dakṣaḥ karuṇo vīra-śekharaḥ |
vidagdho buddhimān kṣantā rakta-lokaḥ samṛddhimān |
sukhī varīyān ity ādyā guṇās tasyeha kīrtitā ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The qualities of the Lord in preyo-bhakti-rasa are: dressing attractively, possessing all good qualities, being best of the strong, being knowledgeable of many languages, being talkative, fully knowledgeable of all branches of learning, quick-witted, skillful, merciful, most courageous, learned in arts, intelligent, tolerant, attractive to all people, prosperous, jovial, and the most prominent among all persons.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha tad-vayasyāḥ –
rūpa-veṣa-guṇādyais tu samāḥ samyag-ayantritāḥ |
viśrambha-sambhṛtātmāno vayasyās tasya kīrtitāḥ ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
His friends (āśraya):

Those whose form, qualities and dress are similar to Kṛṣṇa’s, who are not restricted like the dāsas because they are endowed with deep trust and familiarity (viśrambha) in relation to the Lord, are known as vayasya or friends.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
sāmyena bhīti-vidhureṇa vidhīyamāna-
bhakti-prapañcam anudañcad-anugraheṇa |
viśrambha-sāra-nikuramba-karambitena
vandetarām aghaharasya vayasya-vṛndam ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
This is expressed in the following verse:

I offer my respects to the friends of Kṛṣṇa, who serve Him, with deep trust, without restriction, without fear, while thinking that He is their equal.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: te pura-vraja-sambandhād dvi-vidhāḥ prāya īritāḥ ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are two types of friends: those in the city and those in Vraja.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra pura-sambandhinaḥ –
arjuno bhīmasenaś ca duhitā drupadasya ca |
śrīdāma-bhūsurādyāś ca sakhāyaḥ pura-saṃśrayāḥ ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Arjuna, Bhīmasena, Draupadī, Śrīdāma brāhmaṇa[32] are some of the friends in the city.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ sakhyam, yathā –
śirasi nṛpatir drag aghrāsīd aghārim adhīra-dhīr
bhuja-parighayoḥ śliṣṭau bhīmārjunau pulakojjvalau |
pada-kamalayoḥ sāsrau dasrātmajau ca nipetatus
tam avaśādhiyaḥ prauḍhānandād arundhata pāṇḍavāḥ ||12||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their friendship:

When Kṛṣṇa arrived in Indraprastha, Yudhiṣṭhira, with unsteady mind, immediately smelled his head.[33] Bhīma and Arjuna, with hairs standing on end, embraced His iron arms. Nakula and Sahadeva fell at His lotus feet with tears in their eyes. In this way, the Pāṇḍavas, with hearts agitated with joy, impeded Kṛṣṇa’s entry into the city.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: śreṣṭhaḥ pura-vayasyeṣu bhagavān vānaradhvajaḥ ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among the friends in the city, the Pāṇḍava Arjuna is the best.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asya rūpaṃ, yathā –
gāṇḍīva-pāṇiḥ kari-rāja-śuṇḍā
ramyorur indīvara-sundarābhaḥ |
rathāṅginā ratna-rathādhirohī
sa rohitākṣaḥ sutarām arājīt ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Arjuna’s form:

Arjuna, with Gāṇḍiva bow in hand, with thighs like elephant trunks, with the complexion of a blue lotus, and with reddish eyes, looked splendid as he mounted the jewel-studded chariot with Kṛṣṇa, holder of the cakra.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sakhyaṃ, yathā –
paryaṅke mahati surāri-hantur aṅke
niḥśaṅka-praṇaya-nisṛṣṭa-pūrva-kāyaḥ |
unmīlan-nava-narma-karmaṭho ’yaṃ
gāṇḍīvī smita-vadanāmbujo vyarājīt ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Arjuna’s friendship:

Arjuna, with smiling face, looked splendid as he sat on the same large seat as Kṛṣṇa, placing his head on His bosom in affection without fear, expertly joking in original ways.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vraja-sambandhinaḥ –
kṣaṇādarśanato dīnāḥ sadā saha-vihāriṇaḥ |
tad-eka-jīvitāḥ proktā vayasyā vraja-vāsinaḥ |
ataḥ sarva-vayasyeṣu pradhānatvaṃ bhajanty amī ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The friends in Vraja:

The friends in Vraja suffer from not seeing Kṛṣṇa even for a moment. They play with Him at all times and their very life depends on Kṛṣṇa. For this reason, they are considered the chief among all the friends.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ rūpaṃ, yathā –
balānuja-sadṛg-varo-guṇa-vilāsa-veṣa-śriyaḥ
priyaṅkaraṇa-vallakī-dala-viṣāṇa-veṇv-aṅkitāḥ |
mahendra-maṇi-hāṭaka-sphaṭika-padma-rāga-tviṣaḥ
sadā praṇaya-śālinaḥ sahacarā hareḥ pāntu vaḥ ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their forms:

The cowherd friends of Kṛṣṇa are the same age as Kṛṣṇa, with similar qualities, activities, clothing and beauty. They are equipped with pleasing flutes, horns and vallakī leaves,[34] and have complexions of dark blue, gold, crystal and ruby. Always filled with love for Kṛṣṇa, may they protect you!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sakhyaṃ, yathā –
unnidrasya yayus tavātra viratiṃ sapta kṣapās tiṣṭhato
hanta śrānta ivāsi nikṣipa sakhe śrīdāma-pāṇau girim |
ādhir vidhyati nas tvam arpaya kare kiṃ vā kṣaṇaṃ dakṣiṇe
doṣṇas te karavāma kāmam adhunā savyasya saṃvāhanam ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their friendship:

You have spent seven nights standing constantly without sleep holding up Govardhana. You must be very tired. O my friend Kṛṣṇa, throw the mountain into Śrīdāma’s hand.[35] Our minds are very disturbed seeing you in this condition. Or at least place mountain in Your right hand. Then we will be able to massage Your left hand.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā śrī-daśame–
itthaṃ satāṃ brahma-sukhānubhūtyā
dāsyaṃ gatānāṃ para-daivatena |
māyāśritānāṃ nara-dārakeṇa
sārddhaṃ vijahruḥ kṛta-puṇya-puñjāḥ ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example, from the Tenth Canto:

Here is the Supreme Person, who is considered the impersonal Brahman by great sages, a most worshipable form by the dāsya-bhaktas, and the son of a human being by ordinary men. Now these boys, who have performed many, many pleasing activities in their past lives, are playing with that Supreme Personality of Godhead in the form of a cowherd boy. SB 10.12.11

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣu kṛṣṇasya sakhyaṃ, yathā –
sahacara-nikurambaṃ bhrātar ārya praviṣṭaṃ
drutam agha-jaṭharāntaḥ-koṭare prekṣamāṇaḥ |
skhalad-aśiśira-bāṣpa-kṣālita-kṣāma-gaṇḍaḥ
kṣaṇam aham avasīdan śūnya-cittas tad āsam ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Kṛṣṇa’s friendship with the boys:

O brother! O respected one! On seeing all My friends quickly enter the stomach of the Agha demon, hot tears began flowing from My eyes, and My cheeks became thin. Being despondent, for some time My mind became vacant.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
suhṛdaś ca sakhāyaś ca tathā priya-sakhāḥ pare |
priya-narma-vayasyāś cety uktā goṣṭhe catur-vidhāḥ ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are four types of friends in Gokula: suhṛt, sakha, priya-sakha and priya-narma-sakha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra suhṛdaḥ –
vātsalya-gandhi-sakhyāṃ tu kiñcit te vayasādhikāḥ |
sāyudhās tasya duṣṭebhyaḥ sadā rakṣā-parāyaṇāḥ ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The suhṛts have friendship mixed with a tinge of vatsala. They are slightly older than Kṛṣṇa, and, carrying weapons, they are always ready to protect Kṛṣṇa from demons.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
subhadra-maṇḍalībhadra-bhadravardhana-gobhaṭāḥ |
yakṣendrabhaṭa-bhadrāṅga-vīrabhadrā mahā-guṇāḥ |
vijayo balabhadrādyāḥ suhṛdas tasya kīrtitāḥ ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Subhadra, Maṇḍalībhadra, Bhadravardhana, Gobhaṭa, Yakṣa, Indrabhaṭa, Bhadrāṅga, Vīrabhadra, Mahāguṇa, Vijaya and Balabhadra are some of the suhṛts.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ sakhyaṃ, yathā –
dhunvan dhāvasi maṇḍalāgram amalaṃ tvaṃ maṇḍalībhadra kiṃ
gurvīṃ nārya gadāṃ gṛhāṇa vijaya kṣobhaṃ vṛthā mā kṛthāḥ |
śaktiṃ na kṣipa bhadravardhana puro govardhanaṃ gāhate
garjann eṣa ghano balī na tu balīvardākṛtir dānavaḥ ||24||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their friendship:

O Maṇḍalībhadra! Why are you running around swinging that sword? O respectable Balarāma! Don’t take up that heavy club. Vijaya! Do not uselessly become agitated. Bhadravardhana! Do not throw your strength around. What is approaching Govardhana and making a roaring sound is not the powerful demon Ariṣṭāsura with a bull shape, but only a cloud.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: suhṛtsu maṇḍalībhadra-balabhadrau kilottamau ||25||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among the suhṛts, Maṇḍalībhadra and Balarāma are the best.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra maṇḍalībhadrasya rūpam, yathā –
pāṭala-paṭala-sad-aṅgo lakuṭa-karaḥ śekharī śikhaṇḍena |
dyuti-maṇḍalīm ali-nibhāṃ bhāti dadhan maṇḍalībhadraḥ ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The form of Maṇḍalībhadra:

Maṇḍalībhadra appears with pink cloth decorating his shining limbs, club in hand, peacock feather on his head, with the shining black complexion of a bee.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asya sakhyaṃ, yathā –
vana-bhramaṇa-kelibhir gurubhir ahni khinnīkṛtaḥ
sukhaṃ svapitu naḥ suhṛd vraja-niśānta-madhye niśi |
ahaṃ śirasi mardanaṃ mṛdu karomi karṇe kathāṃ
tvam asya visṛjann alaṃ subala sakthinī lālaya ||27||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The friendship of Maṇḍalībhadra:

Our friend Kṛṣṇa has become very tired because of wandering around playing in the forest for a long time. Now, He should sleep comfortably in His house in Vraja at night. I will gently massage His head. Subala! You should massage His legs while speaking in His ear.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
baladevasya rūpaṃ, yathā –
gaṇḍāntaḥ-sphurad-eka-kuṇḍalam ali-cchannāvataṃsotpalaṃ
kastūrī-kṛta-citrakaṃ pṛthu-hṛdi bhrājiṣṇu guñjā-srajam |
taṃ vīraṃ śarad-ambuda-dyuti-bharaṃ saṃvīta-kālāmbaraṃ
gambhīra-svanitaṃ pralamba-bhujam ālambe pralamba-dviṣam ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The form of Balarāma:

I take shelter of Baladeva, enemy of Pralamba. On the edge of His cheek dangles one earring. The lotuses decorating His ears are covered with bees. His tilaka is made of musk. On His broad chest shines a guñja berry necklace. His complexion is as white as an autumn cloud. His cloth is blue, His voice is deep, and His arms hang down to His knees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asya sakhyaṃ, yathā –
jani-tithir iti putra-prema-saṃvītayāhaṃ
snapayitum iha sadmany ambayā stambhito ’smi |
iti subala girā me sandiśa tvaṃ mukundaṃ
phaṇi-pati-hrada-kacche nādya gaccheḥ kadāpi ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Baladeva’s friendship:

Today is My birth tithi. Therefore, My affectionate mother has kept Me at home in order to bathe Me. Subala! Tell Kṛṣṇa that I said that He should by no means go to Kāliya’s lake today.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra sakhāyaḥ –
kaniṣṭha-kalpāḥ sakhyena sambandhāḥ prīti-gandhinā |
viśāla-vṛṣabhaurjasvi-devaprastha-varūthapāḥ ||30||
maranda-kusumāpīḍa-maṇibandha-karandhamāḥ |
ity-ādayaḥ sakhāyo ’sya sevā-saukhyaika-rāgiṇaḥ ||31||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The sakhas:

Friends who are slightly younger than Kṛṣṇa, who have a touch of dāsya in relationship with Kṛṣṇa are called sakhas. They are very attached to the happiness derived from serving Kṛṣṇa. Among them are Viśāla, Vṛṣabha, Ojasvī, Devaprastha, Varūthapa, Maranda, Kusumāpīḍa, Maṇibandha, and Karandhama.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ sakhyaṃ, yathā –
viśāla visinī-dalaiḥ kalaya bījana-prakriyāṃ
varūthapa vilambitālaka-varūtham utsāraya |
mṛṣā vṛṣabha jalpitaṃ tyaja bhajāṅga-saṃvāhanaṃ
yad-ugra-bhuja-saṅgare gurum agāt klamaṃ naḥ sakhā ||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their friendship:

Today our friend has become very tired from intense combat. Viśāla! Fan Him with a lotus leaf. Varūthapa, tidy up His hair! Vṛṣabha, massage his limbs without unnecessary talk!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: sarveṣu sakhiṣu śreṣṭho devaprastho ’yam īritaḥ ||33||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among the sakhas, Devaprastha is known as the best.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tasya rūpaṃ, yathā –
bibhrad geṇḍuṃ pāṇḍurodbhāsa-vāsāḥ
pāśā-baddhottuṅga-maulir balīyān |
bandhūkābhaḥ sindhur aspardhi-līlo
devaprasthaḥ kṛṣṇa-pārśvaṃ pratasthe ||34||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Devaprastha’s form:

With a ball in his hand, with light yellow cloth, top-knot tied with a cord, and red complexion, strong Devaprastha, romping around like the king of the elephants, walks by Kṛṣṇa’s side.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asya sakhyaṃ, yathā –
śrīdāmnaḥ pṛthulāṃ bhumām abhi śiro vinyasya viśrāmiṇaṃ
dāmnaḥ savya-kareṇa ruddha-hṛdayaṃ śayyā-virājat-tanum |
madhye sundari kandarasya padayoḥ saṃvāhanena priyaṃ
devaprastha itaḥ kṛtī sukhayati premṇā vrajendrātmajam ||35||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Devaprastha’s friendship:

O beautiful young woman! In a mountain cave, Kṛṣṇa lay down and rested His head in the broad arms of Śrīdāma, with Dāma’s left hand covering His chest. Expert Devaprastha gave pleasure to his beloved Kṛṣṇa by massaging His feet in affection.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha priya-sakhāḥ –
vayas-tulyāḥ priya-sakhā sakhyaṃ kevalam āśritāḥ |
śrīdāmā ca sudāmā ca dāmā ca vasudāmakaḥ ||36||
kiṅkiṇi-stokakṛṣṇāṃśu-bhadrasena-vilāsinaḥ |
puṇḍarīka-viṭaṅkākhya-kalabiṅkādayo ’py amī ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Priya-sakhas:

Those who are the same age as Kṛṣṇa, with only pure sakhya, are called priya-sakhas. Śrīdāma, Sudāma, Dāma, Vasudāma, Kiṅkiṇi, Stokakrṣṇa, Aṃśu, Bhadrasena, Vilāsī, Puṇḍarīka, Viṭaṅka and Kalabiṇka are priya-sakhas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ramayanti priya-sakhāḥ kelibhir vividhaiḥ sadā |
niyuddha-daṇḍa-yuddhādi-kautukair api keśavam ||38||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The priya-sakhas give pleasure to Kṛṣṇa by hand to hand combat, stick fighting and other sports.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ sakhyaṃ, yathā –
sagadgada-padair hariṃ hasati ko ’pi vakroditaiḥ
prasārya bhujayor yugaṃ pulaki kaścid āśliṣyati |
kareṇa calatā dṛśau nibhṛtam etya rundhe paraḥ
kṛśāṅgi sukhayanty amī priya-sakhāḥ sakhāyaṃ tava ||39||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their friendship:

One priya-sakha jokes using puns in a choked voice. Another embraces Kṛṣṇa with arms full of goose bumps. Another sneaks up behind Kṛṣṇa and covers His eyes with his quick hand. O thin-waisted woman! In this way, the priya-sakhas give pleasure to Your friend Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: eṣu priya-vayasyeṣu śrīdāmā pravaro mataḥ ||40||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among the priya-sakhas, Śrīdāma is the chief.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tasya rūpam, yathā –
vāsaḥ piṅgaṃ bibhrataṃ śṛṅga-pāṇiṃ
baddha-spardhaṃ sauhṛdān mādhavena |
tāmroṣṇīṣaṃ śyāma-dhāmābhirāmaṃ
śrīdāmānaṃ dāma-bhājaṃ bhajāmi ||41||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Śrīdāma’s form:

I worship Śrīdāma, with yellow-greenish cloth and dark blue complexion, holding a horn in his hand, wearing a copper colored turban and a flower garland. Out of friendship, he competes with Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sakhyaṃ, yathā –
tvaṃ naḥ projjhya kaṭhora yāmuna-taṭe kasmād akasmād gato
diṣṭyā dṛṣṭim ito ’si hanta niviḍāśleṣaiḥ sakhīn prīṇaya |
brūmaḥ satyam adarśane tava manāk kā dhenavaḥ ke vayaṃ
kiṃ goṣṭhaṃ kim abhīṣṭam ity acirataḥ sarvaṃ viparyasyati ||42||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
His friendship:

You are so cruel! Why did you suddenly leave us at the bank of the Yamunā and go away? We are very fortunate to have seen You. Now, please us by giving us a strong embrace. Not seeing You for even a short time, the cows, ourselves, the cowsheds, and Your gopīs all fall into the worst misfortune.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha priya-narma-vayasyāḥ –
priya-narma-vayasyās tu pūrvato ’py abhito varāḥ |
ātyantika-rahasyeṣu yuktā bhāva-viśeṣiṇaḥ |
subalārjuna-gandharvās te vasantojjvalādayaḥ ||43||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The priya-narma-sakhas are superior to the suhṛts, sakhas and priya-sakhas, and possess a very special bhāva. They engage in the most confidential pastimes. Subala, Arjuna, Gandharva, Vasanta and Ujjvala are priya-narma-sakhas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣāṃ sakhyaṃ, yathā –
rādhā-sandeśa-vṛndaṃ kathayati subalaḥ paśya kṛṣṇasya karṇe
śyāmā-kandarpa-lekhaṃ nibhṛtam upaharaty ujjvalaḥ pāṇi-padme |
pālī-tāmbūlam āsye vitarati caturaḥ kokilo mūrdhni dhatte
tārā-dāmeti narma-praṇayi-sahacarās tanvi tanvanti sevām ||44||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Their friendship:

O thin-waisted woman! Subala is whispering information about Rādhā’s in Kṛṣṇa’s ear. Ujjvala is handing Śyāmā’s love letter to the lotus hand of Kṛṣṇa in a solitary place. Catura is offering betel nut given by Pālī into Kṛṣṇa’s mouth. Kokila is putting a garland sent by Tārā over Kṛṣṇa’s head. In this way, the priya-narma-sakhas serve Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: priya-narma-vayasyeṣu prabalau subalojjvallau ||45||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among the priya-narma-sakhas, Subala and Ujjvala are the chief.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra subalasya rūpaṃ, yathā –
tanu-ruci-vijita-hiraṇyaṃ hari-dayitaṃ hāriṇaṃ harid-vasanam |
subalaṃ kuvalaya-nayanaṃ naya-nandita-bāndhavaṃ vande ||46||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Subala’s form:

I offer my respects to Subala, with a complexion that conquers gold, who wears a pearl necklace and green cloth, whose eyes are like blue lotuses, and who gives joy to his friends by his conduct.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asya sakhyaṃ, yathā –
vayasya-goṣṭhyām akhileṅgiteṣu
viśāradāyām api mādhavasya |
anyair durūhā subalena sārdhaṃ
saṃjñā-mayī kāpi babhūva vārtā ||47||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Subala’s friendship:

Mādhava discussed with Subala topics full of allusions difficult to understand by others in the assembly skilful at interpreting gestures.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ujjvalasya rūpaṃ, yathā –
aruṇāmbaram uccalekṣaṇaṃ
madhu-puṣpa-balibhiḥ prasādhitam |
hari-nīla-ruciṃ hari-priyaṃ
maṇi-hārojjvalam ujjvalaṃ bhaje ||48||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The form of Ujjvala:

I worship Ujjvala, dear to the Lord, with his orange cloth, restless eyes, complexion dark blue like Kṛṣṇa’s. He is decorated with spring flowers and wears a jewel necklace.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asya sakhyaṃ, yathā –
śaktāsmi mānam avituṃ katham ujjvalo ’yaṃ
dūtaḥ sameti sakhi yatra milaty adūre |
sāpatrapāpi kulajāpi pativratāpi
kā vā vṛṣasyati na gopa-vṛṣaṃ kiśorī ||49||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Ujjvala’s friendship:

O friend! Kṛṣṇa’s messenger Ujjvala is approaching. How will I protect my honor? Is there any chaste, shy respectable woman here who would remain without desiring the best of cowherd boys, when Ujjvala is nears?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: ujjvalo ’yaṃ viśeṣeṇa sadā narmokti-lālasaḥ ||50||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Ujjvala particularly always has a longing for joking.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
sphurad-atanu-taraṅgā vardhitānalpa-velaḥ
sumadhura-rasa-rūpo durgamāvāra-pāraḥ |
jagati yuvati-jātir nimnagā tvaṃ samudras
tad iyam aghahara tvām eti sarvādhvanaiva ||51||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O killer of Agha! You are an incomparable ocean of sweet rasa, and like the high tide, You transgress all the boundaries of morality. The young women of this world are rivers filled with waves of love. Whatever path they take, they end up meeting You.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: eteṣu ke ’pi śāstreṣu ke ’pi lokeṣu viśrutāḥ ||52||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among all the friends, some are famous in the scriptures and some are famous through local stories.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nitya-priyāḥ sura-carāḥ sādhakāś ceti te tridhā |
kecid eṣu sthirā jātyā mantrivat tam upāsate ||53||
taṃ hāsayanti cāpalāḥ kecid vaihāsikopamāḥ |
kecid ārjava-sāreṇa saralāḥ śīlayanti tam ||54||
vāmā vakrima-cakreṇa kecid vismāyayanty amum |
kecit pragalbhāḥ kurvanti vitaṇḍām amunā |
saumyāḥ sūnṛtayā vācā dhanyā dhinvanti taṃ pare ||55||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are three types of friends: nitya-priya (nitya-siddha), devatā (previously) and sādhana-siddha. Some, steady and acting like advisors, worship Kṛṣṇa. Others have a fickle nature and make Kṛṣṇa laugh in the manner of jesters. Others, with meek nature, make Him happy by their serious behavior. Others astonish Him by crooked behavior. Other confident friends argue with Him. Some peaceful, fortunate boys please Him with truthful, pleasing words.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
evaṃ vividhayā sarve prakṛtyā madhurā amī |
pavitra-maitrī-vaicitrī-cārutām upacinvate ||56||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: By possessing various natures, the sweet friends increase the wonder and pleasure of pure friendship with Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha uddīpanāḥ –
uddīpanā vayo-rūpa-śṛṅga-veṇu-darā hareḥ |
vinoda-narma-vikrānti-guṇāḥ preṣṭha-janās tathā |
rāja-devāvatārādi-ceṣṭānukaraṇādayaḥ ||57||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The uddīpanas for preyo-rasa are the Lord’s age, form, horn, flute, conch, sports, joking, heroism, His intimate devotees, and imitating the actions of kings and devatās.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vayaḥ –
vayaḥ kaumāra-paugaṇḍa-kaiśoraṃ ceha sammatam |
goṣṭhe kaumāra-paugaṇḍe kaiśoraṃ pura-goṣṭhayoḥ ||58||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: According to the wise, Kṛṣṇa has three ages: kaumāra, paugaṇḍa and kaiśora.[36] Kṛṣṇa is visible in kaumāra and paugaṇḍa ages only in Gokula, but He is visible in kaiśora age in Gokula, Mathurā and Dvārakā.[37]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra kaumāraṃ, yathā –
kaumāraṃ vatsale vācyaṃ tataḥ saṅkṣipya likhyate ||59||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kaumāra age is suitable for vatsala-rasa. Therefore, it will be described here only briefly.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrī-daśame –
bibhrad veṇuṃ jaṭhara-paṭayoḥ śṛṅga-vetre ca kakṣe
vāme pāṇau masṛṇa-kavalaṃ tat-phalāny aṅgulīṣu |
tiṣṭhan madhye sva-parisuhṛdo hāsayan narmabhiḥ svaiḥ
svarge loke miṣati bubhuje yajña-bhug bāla-keliḥ ||60||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From the Tenth Canto:

Kṛṣṇa is yajña-bhuk—that is, He eats only offerings of yajña—but to exhibit His childhood pastimes, He now sat with His flute tucked between His waist and His tight cloth on His right side and with His horn bugle and cow-driving stick on His left. Holding in His left hand a very nice preparation of yogurt and rice, he held a small portion in His right hand ready to eat it. He sat like the whorl of a lotus flower, looking forward toward all His friends, personally joking with them and creating jubilant laughter among them as He ate. At that time, the denizens of heaven were watching, struck with wonder at how the Personality of Godhead, who eats only in yajña, was now eating with His friends in the forest.[38] SB 10.13.11

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha paugaṇḍam –
ādyaṃ madhyaṃ tathā śeṣaṃ paugaṇḍaṃ ca tridhā bhavet ||61||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Paugaṇḍa age has three divisions: beginning, middle and end.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyaṃ paugaṇḍaṃ –
adharādeḥ sulauhityaṃ jaṭharasya ca tānavam |
kambu-grīvodgamādyaṃ ca paugaṇḍe prathame sati ||62||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the first part of paugaṇḍa age, His lips are very red, His waist is thin, and three lines appear on His throat like a conch.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
tundaṃ vindati te mukunda śanakair aśvatta-patra-śriyaṃ
kaṇṭhaṃ kambuvad ambujākṣa bhajate rakhā-trayīm ujjvalām |
ārundhe kuruvinda-kandala-ruciṃ bhū-candra danta-cchado
lakṣmīr ādhunikī dhinoti suhṛdām akṣīṇi sā kāpy asau ||63||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O Kṛṣṇa! Your abdomen has the beauty of an aśvattha leaf. O lotus eyed one! Your throat is beautified by three lines like a conch. O moon on earth! Your lips conquer the red effulgence of fresh rubies. What can be said? This new, indescribably beauty is giving bliss to the eyes of Your friends.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
puṣpa-maṇḍana-vaicitrī citrāṇi giri-dhātubhiḥ |
pīta-paṭṭa-dukūlādyam iha proktaṃ prasādhanam ||64||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the beginning of the paugaṇḍa age, there are varieties of flower ornaments on Kṛṣṇa, marking on His body made of minerals, and yellow silk cloth.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sarvāṭavī-pracāreṇa naicikī-caya-cāraṇam |
niyuddha-keli-nṛtyādi-śikṣārambho ’tra ceṣṭitam ||65||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The activities of this age are herding the calves in all the forests, learning hand to hand combat, playing games and dancing.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
vṛndāraṇye samastāt surabhiṇi surabhī-vṛnda-rakṣā-vihārī
guñjāhārī śikhaṇḍa-prakaṭita-mukuṭaḥ pīta-paṭṭāmbara-śrīḥ |
karṇābhyāṃ karṇikāre dadhad alam urasā phulla-mallīka-mālyaṃ
nṛtyan dor-yuddha-raṅge naṭavad iha sakhīn nandayaty eṣa kṛṣṇaḥ ||66||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa wore a guñja berry necklace, a crown of peacock feathers, karṇika flowers in His ears, a jasmine garland on His chest, and yellow silk cloth. Absorbed in the pastime of protecting the cows of glorious Vṛndāvana, dancing like an actor in the combat arena, He gave bliss to His friends.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha madhya-paugaṇḍam –
nāsā suśikharā tuṅgā kapolau maṇḍalākṛtī |
pārśvādy-aṅgaṃ suvalitaṃ paugaṇḍe sati madhyame ||67||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the middle of the paugaṇḍa age, the tip of His nose becomes raised, His cheeks become round and His sides and other bodily parts become graceful.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
tila-kusuma-vihāsi-nāsikā-śrīr
nava-maṇi-darpaṇa-darpa-nāśi-gaṇḍaḥ |
harir iha parimṛṣṭa-pārśva-sīmā
sukhayati suṣṭhu sakhīn sva-śobhayaiva ||68||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The beauty of His nose derides the sesame flower, His cheeks crush the pride of a mirror made of fresh jewels, and His smooth thorax became raised. Kṛṣṇa gave happiness to His friends by this beauty.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
uṣṇīṣaṃ paṭṭa-sūtrottha-pāśenātra taḍit-tviṣā |
yaṣṭiḥ śyāmā tri-hastoccā svarṇāgrety ādi-maṇḍanam |
bhāṇḍīre krīḍanaṃ śailoddhāraṇādyaṃ ca ceṣṭitam ||69||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The decorations of the middle paugaṇḍa age are a turban tied with silk cords shining like lightning and a black staff four and half feet long with a golden tip. The activities of the middle paugaṇḍa age are playing in Bhāṇḍira forest and climbing Govardhana.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
yaṣṭiṃ hasta-traya-parimitāṃ prāntayoḥ svarṇa-baddhāṃ
bibhral-līlāṃ caṭula-camarī-cāru-cūḍojjvala-śrīḥ |
baddhoṣṇīṣaḥ puraṭa-rucinā paṭṭi-pāśena pārśve
paśya krīḍan sukhayati sakhe mitra-vṛndaṃ mukundaḥ ||70||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O friend! Look, in that direction. Mukunda, shining brightly with a clump of flower buds on His head, wearing a turban bound by a golden cloth cord, holding a black staff four and a half feet long, capped at both ends with gold, is giving bliss to His friends.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
paugaṇḍa-madhya evāyaṃ harir dīvyan virājate |
mādhuryādbhuta-rūpatvāt kaiśorāgrāṃśa-bhāg iva ||71||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the middle of the paugaṇḍa age the Lord, engaged in playing, seems to partake of the first part of kaiśora age because of having an astonishing form caused by His sweetness.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha śeṣa-paugaṇḍam –
veṇī nitamba-lambāgrā līlālaka-latā-dyuti |
aṃsayos tuṅgatety ādi paugaṇḍe carame sati ||72||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: At the end of the paugaṇḍa age, Kṛṣṇa has a long braid extending to His waist, locks of hair falling playfully, and strong shoulders.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
agre līlālaka-latikayālaṅkṛtaṃ bibhrad-āsyaṃ
cañcad-veṇī-śikhara-śikhayā cumbita-śreṇi-bimbaḥ |
uttuṅgāṃsa-cchavir aghaharo raṅgam aṅga-śriyaiva
nyasyann eva priya-savayasāṃ gokulān nirjihīte ||73||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: With His locks of hair falling playfully over His face, with His braid at the back reaching His hips, and with His full shoulders, the killer Agha departs from Gokula, giving pleasure to His friends by His physical beauty.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
uṣṇīṣe vakrimā līlā-sarasī-ruha-pāṇitā |
kāśmīreṇordhva-puṇḍrādyam iha maṇḍanam īritam ||74||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The ornaments of the late paugaṇḍa age are His tilted turban, the play lotus in His hand, and tilaka on His forehead made of saffron.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
uṣṇīṣe dara-vakrimā kara-tale vyājṛmbhi-līlāmbujaṃ
gaura-śrīr alike kilordhva-tilakaḥ kastūrikā-bindumān |
veṣaḥ keśava peśalaḥ subalam apy āghūrṇayaty adya te
vikrāntaṃ kim uta svabhāva-mṛdulāṃ goṣṭhābalānāṃ tatim ||75||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O Keśava! Your attractive clothing—the tilted turban, the play lotus in Your hand, the golden tilaka on the forehead, and the dot of musk—are making me, courageous Subala, completely dizzy! How much more this will affect the naturally soft gopīs!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra bhaṅgī girāṃ narma-sakhaiḥ karṇa-kathā-rasaḥ |
eṣu gokula-bālānāṃ śrī-ślāghety-ādi-ceṣṭitam ||76||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The activities of the late paugaṇḍa age are word play, enjoying intimate conversations with narma-sakhas, and praising the forms of the young girls of Gokula in front of His friends.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
dhūrtas tvaṃ yad avaiṣi hṛd-gatam ataḥ karṇe tava vyāhare
keyaṃ mohanatā-samṛddhir adhunā godhuk-kumārī-gaṇe |
atrāpi dyuti-ratna-rohaṇa-bhuvo bālāḥ sakhe pañca-ṣāḥ
pañceṣur jagatāṃ jaye nija-dhurāṃ yatrārpayan mādyati ||77||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa! You are crafty, because You have understood what is in my heart. Therefore, I am saying in Your ear, that the young gopīs are now showing increased power of bewildering. Among them, five or six young girls have become most attractive. O friend! It seems that Cupid has become mad with bliss, giving them his own duty of conquering the world.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha kaiśoram –
kaiśoraṃ pūrvam evoktaṃ saṅkṣepeṇocyate tataḥ ||78||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The kaiśora age was previously described (starting with 2.1.313) and thus here only it will be described in brief.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
paśyotsikta-balī-trayī-vara-late vāsas taḍin-mañjule
pronmīlad-vana-mālikā-parimala-stome tamāla-tviṣi |
ukṣaty ambaka-cātakān smita-rasair dāmodarāmbhodhare
śrīdāmā ramaṇīya-roma-kalikākīrṇāṅga-śākhī babhau ||79||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: See! The best of creepers with three prominent folds, clothed in attractive lightning, replete with the fragrance of blossoming forest flowers, with the radiance of the black tamāla tree, the cloud in the form of Dāmodara, quenches with full delight the devotees’ eyes, which are like cātaka birds seeing immanent rain. And consequently, Śrīdāma remains standing like a tree whose branches are erupting with pleasing bristles.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prāyaḥ kiśora evāyaṃ sarva-bhakteṣu bhāsate |
tena yauvana-śobhāsya neha kācit prapañcitā ||80||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa of kaiśora age is most attractive for all the devotees. Thus, the beauty of yauvana age does not appear in Kṛṣṇa, and is not described here at all.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha rūpaṃ, yathā –
alaṅkāram alaṅkṛtvā tavāṅgaṃ paṅkajekṣaṇa |
sakhīn kevalam evedaṃ dhāmnā dhīman dhinoti naḥ ||81||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The form of Kṛṣṇa as uddīpana:

O lotus eyed Kṛṣṇa! Intelligent boy! What is the use of decorating Your body with ornaments? Your body pleases us friends with its own luster.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha śṛṅgaṃ, yathā –
vraja-nija-vaḍabhī-vitardikāyām
uṣasi viṣāṇa-vare ruvaty udagram |
ahaha savayasāṃ tadīya-romṇām
api nivahāḥ samam eva jāgrati sma ||82||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The horn as uddīpana:

When the horn sounded loudly from the steps of the roof-top pavilion in the summer season in Vraja, all the cowherd boys woke up with hairs standing on end.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
veṇur, yathā –
suhṛdo na hi yāta kātarā
harim anveṣṭum itaḥ sutāṃ raveḥ |
kathayann amum atra vaiṇava-
dhvani-dūtaḥ śikhare dhinoti naḥ ||83||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The flute as uddīpana:

O friends! Do not go the bank of the Yamunā to search for Kṛṣṇa out of despair, for the messenger in the form of the flute sound from the top of Govardhana is telling us about Him and is giving us pleasure.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śaṅkho, yathā –
pāñcālī-patayaḥ śrutvā pāñcajanyasya nisvanam |
pañcāsya paśya muditāḥ pañcāsya-pratimāṃ yayuḥ ||84||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The conch as uddīpana:

Look, the five Pāṇḍavas, hearing the sound of the Pāñcajanya conch, became white like Śiva in great joy.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vinoda, yathā –
sphurad-aruṇa-dukūlaṃ jāguḍair gaura-gātraṃ
kṛta-vara-kavarīkaṃ ratna-tāṭaṅka-karṇam |
madhuripum iha rādhā-veṣam udvīkṣya sākṣāt
priya-sakhi subalo ’bhūd vismitaḥ sa-smitaś ca ||85||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Diversions as uddīpana:

Dear friend! When Kṛṣṇa put on Rādhā’s clothing, and appeared with bright pink cloth, golden limbs smeared with kuṃkuma, hair bound up beautifully, and earrings, Subala was astonished and smiled.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
athānubhāvāḥ –
niyuddha-kanduka-dyūta-vāhya-vāhādi-kelibhiḥ |
laguḍālaguḍi-krīḍā-saṅgaraiś cāsya toṣaṇam ||86||
palyaṅkāsana-dolāsu saha-svāpopaveśanam |
cāru-citra-parīhāso vihāraḥ salilāśaye ||87||
yugmatve lāsya-gānādyāḥ sarva-sādhāraṇāḥ kriyāḥ ||88||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Pleasing Kṛṣṇa by hand to hand combat, playing ball and dice, riding on each other’s shoulders, stick fighting, sleeping and sitting with Kṛṣṇa on a bed, seat or swing, telling pleasing jokes, playing in the water, and singing and dancing with Kṛṣṇa are the activities (anubhāvas) common to all types of friends.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra niyuddhena toṣaṇaṃ, yathā –
aghahara jita-kāśī yuddha-kaṇḍūla-bāhus
tvam aṭasi sakhi-goṣṭhyām ātma-vīryaṃ stuvānaḥ |
kathaya kim u mamoccaiś caṇḍa-dor-daṇḍa-ceṣṭā-
viramita-raṇa-raṅgo niḥsahāṅgaḥ sthito ’si ||89||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Pleasing Kṛṣṇa by combat:

Killer of Agha! Thinking that You are victorious, with arms itching for battle, You wander amongst Your friends praising Your prowess. But tell me, seeing the action of my thick arms, having given up Your eagerness to fight, can You remain standing there with weak limbs?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yuktāyuktādi-kathanaṃ hita-kṛtye pravartanam |
prāyaḥ puraḥsaratvādyāḥ suhṛdām īritāḥ kriyāḥ ||90||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The activities of the suhṛts (those friends older than Kṛṣṇa) are giving advice on what should be done or not done, engaging in beneficial acts, and being eager to perform all types of service.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tāmbulādy-arpaṇaṃ vaktre tilaka-sthāsaka-kriyā |
patrāṅkura-vilekhādi sakhīnāṃ karma kīrtitam ||91||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Putting tāmbula in Kṛṣṇa’s mouth, applying His tilaka, smearing sandalwood on His body, and painting designs on His face are the activities of the sakhas (those friends younger than Kṛṣṇa).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nirjitī-karaṇaṃ yuddhe vastre dhṛtvāsya karṣaṇam |
puṣpādyācchedanaṃ hastāt kṛṣṇena sva-prasādhanam |
hastāhasti-prasaṅgādyāḥ proktāḥ priya-sakha-kriyāḥ ||92||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Defeating Kṛṣṇa in battle, pulling His cloth, stealing flowers and other things from His hand, being decorated by Kṛṣṇa, and hand to hand combat are the activities of the priya-sakhas (those friends of equal age as Kṛṣṇa).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dūtyaṃ vraja-kiśorīṣu tāsāṃ praṇaya-gāmitā |
tābhiḥ keli-kalau sākṣāt sakhyuḥ pakṣa-parigrahaḥ ||93||
asākṣāt sva-sva-yūtheśā-pakṣa-sthāpana-cāturī |
karṇākarṇi-kathādyāś ca priya-narma-sakha-kriyāḥ ||94||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Carrying messages among the young women of Vraja, encouraging the gopīs’ love for Kṛṣṇa, taking Kṛṣṇa’s side when the gopīs are present and Kṛṣṇa is not present, showing skill at siding with the chief gopīs when Kṛṣṇa is present but the gopīs are not, and arguing intensely by whispering in each other’s ears, are the activities of the priya-narma-sakhas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vanya-ratnālaṅkārair mādhavasya prasādhanam |
puras tauryatrikaṃ tasya gavāṃ sambhālana-kriyāḥ ||95||
aṅga-saṃvāhanaṃ mālya-gumphanaṃ bījanādayaḥ |
etāḥ sādhāraṇā dāsair vayasyānāṃ kriyā matāḥ |
pūrvokteṣv aparāś cātra jñeyā dhīrair yathocitam ||96||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Decorating Kṛṣṇa with forest flowers and jewel ornaments, singing and dancing in front of Kṛṣṇa, caring for the cows, massaging His body, fanning Him as well as other activities are activities that the friends share in common with the dāsas. Among all the anubhāvas (activities) mentioned previously, some which are suitable, though not mentioned here, are also accepted as the anubhāvas of the friends by the wise.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sāttvikāḥ, tatra stambho, yathā –
niṣkrāmantaṃ nāgam unmathya kṛṣṇaṃ
śrīdāmāyaṃ drāk pariṣvaktu-kāmaḥ |
labdha-stambhau sambhramārambha-śālī
bāhu-stambhau paśya notkṣeptum īṣṭe ||97||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sāttvika-bhāvas, paralysis:

When Kṛṣṇa emerged from the water after conquering Kāliya, Śrīdāma wanted to embrace Him immediately, but his thick arms became paralyzed and he was unable to lift them.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
svedo, yathā –
krīḍotsavānanda-rasaṃ mukunde
svāty-ambude varṣati ramya-ghoṣe |
śrīdāma-mūrtir vara-śuktir eṣā
svedāmbu-muktā-paṭalīṃ prasūte ||98||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Perspiration:

When the cloud called Mukunda appeared during the period when the moon entered Svāti constellation, made pleasant sounds (of rumbling or from the flute), and rained blissful showers of joyful pastimes, the oyster in the form of Śrīdāma (giver of wealth) began to produce pearls as perspiration.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
romāñco, yathā dāna-keli-kaumudyām (37) –
api guru-puras tvām utsaṅge nidhāya visaṅkaṭe
vipula-pulakollāsaṃ svarā pariṣvajate hariḥ |
praṇayati tava skandhe cāsau bhujaṃ bhujagopamaṃ
kva subala purā siddha-kṣetre cakartha kiyat-tapaḥ ||99||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Hair standing on end, from Dāna-keli-kaumudī:

O Subala! You are fortunate because in the presence of the elders you embrace Kṛṣṇa freely, spreading out your arms with hairs standing on end, and Kṛṣṇa places His arm on your shoulder. Tell me at which pure holy place did you perform innumerable austerities?

In the presence of elders Kṛṣṇa puts you on his lap embraces you freely with his snake-like arms.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
svara-bhedādi catuṣkaṃ, yathā –
praviṣṭavati mādhave bhujaga-rāja-bhājaṃ hradaṃ
tadīya-suhṛdas tadā pṛthula-vepathu-vyākulāḥ |
vivarṇa-vapuṣaḥ kṣaṇād vikaṭa-gharghara-dhmāyino
nipatya nikaṭa-sthalī-bhuvi suṣuptim ārebhire ||100||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Trembling, change of color, choking of the voice, fainting:

When Kṛṣṇa entered into Kāliya’s lake, the friends became afflicted with trembling. They lost their color and from some time could not speak properly. They fell on the ground and became unconscious.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
aśru, yathā –
dāvaṃ samīkṣya vicarantam iṣīka-tūlais
tasya kṣayārtham iva bāṣpa-jharaṃ kirantī |
svām apy upekṣya tanum ambuja-māla-bhāriṇy
ābhīra-vīthir abhito harim āvariṣṭa ||101||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Tears:

Seeing the forest fire spread around through the cotton from dry reeds, Kṛṣṇa’s friends, not considering their own bodies, surrounded Kṛṣṇa on all sides, appearing to extinguish the fire with the tears from their eyes.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vyabhicāriṇaḥ –
augryaṃ trāsaṃ tathālasyaṃ varjayitvākhilāḥ pare |
rase preyasi bhāva-jñaiḥ kathitā vyabhicāriṇaḥ ||102||
tatrāyoge madaṃ harṣaṃ garvaṃ nidrāṃ dhṛtiṃ vinā |
yoge mṛtiṃ klamaṃ vyādhiṃ vināpasmṛti-dīnate ||103||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Those knowledgeable of bhāva say that all the vyabhicāri-bhāvas except augrya, trāsa and ālasya are present in preyo-rasa. However, in separation from Kṛṣṇa, mada, harṣa, garva, nidrā and dhṛti do not appear. In union with Kṛṣṇa, mṛti, klama (glāni), vyādhi, apasmṛti and dīnatā (dainyam) do not appear.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra harṣo, yathā –
niṣkramayya kila kāliyoragaṃ
vallaveśvara-sute samīyuṣi |
sammadena suhṛdaḥ skhalat-padās
tad-giraś ca vivaśāṅgatāṃ dadhuḥ ||104||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Joy:

When Kṛṣṇa returned to His friends after driving out Kāliya, they lost control of their bodies in great joy. They could not stand on their feet and their words became incoherent.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sthāyī –
vimukta-sambhramā yā syād viśrambhātmā ratir dvayoḥ |
prāyaḥ samānayor atra sā sakhyam sthāyi-śabda-bhāk ||105||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sthāyi-bhāva:

When there is a rati (attraction) based on mutual trust between two friends as equals without respect, it is called sakhya-rati. This becomes the sthāyi-bhāva of sakhya-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
viśrambho gāḍha-viśvāsa-viśeṣaḥ yantraṇojjhitaḥ |
eṣā sakhya-ratir vṛddhiṃ gacchantī praṇayaḥ kramāt |
premā snehas tathā rāga iti pañca-bhidoditā ||106||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Deep, familiar trust without restriction is called viśrambha (confidence). This viśrambha or sakhya-rati increases gradually through praṇaya, prema, sneha and rāga. Thus, sakhya-rati has five types.[40]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra sakhya-ratiḥ, yathā –
mukundo gāndinī-putra tvayā sandiśyatām iti |
garuḍāṅka guḍākeśas tvāṃ kadā parirapsyate ||107||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sakhya-rati:

O Akrūra, son of Gāndinī! You should tell Mukunda, ’’Possessor of the mark of Garuḍa! When will Arjuna be able to embrace You?’’

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
praṇayaḥ –
prāptāyāṃ sambhramādīnāṃ yogyatāyām api sphuṭam |
tad-gandhenāpy asaṃspṛṣṭā ratiḥ praṇaya ucyate ||108||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When sakhya rati is completely free of any touch of respect, though there are clear opportunities to show respect, it is called praṇaya.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
surais tripura-jin mukhair api vidhīyamāna-stuter
api prathayataḥ parām adhika-pārameṣṭhya-śriyam |
dadhat-pulakinaṃ harer adhi-śirodhi savyaṃ bhujaṃ
samaskuruta pāṃśumān śirasi candrakān arjunaḥ ||109||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though the devatās headed by Śiva began praising Kṛṣṇa announcing His supreme position as the Lord, Arjuna, Kṛṣṇa’s friend in Vraja,[42] just put his arm with hairs standing on end upon Kṛṣṇa’s shoulder and wiped the dust from His peacock feather.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prema, yathā –
bhavaty udayatīśvare suhṛdi hanta rājya-cyutir
mukunda vasatir vane para-gṛhe ca dāsya-kriyā |
iyaṃ sphuṭam amaṅgalā bhavatu pāṇḍavānāṃ gatiḥ
parantu vavṛdhe tvayi dvi-guṇam eva sakhyāmṛtam ||110||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O Mukunda! Though You, the Supreme Lord, have appeared as the friend of the Pāṇḍavas, they met with so many calamities such as losing the kingdom, living in the forest, and acting as servants in someone else’s house. However, their sweet friendship for You doubled in spite of this.[43]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sneho, yathā śrī-daśame –
anye tad-anurūpāṇi manojñāni mahātmanaḥ |
gāyanti sma mahārāja sneha-klinna-dhiyaḥ śanaiḥ ||111||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sneha, from the Tenth Canto:

My dear King, other boys would sing enchanting songs appropriate to the occasion, and their hearts would melt out of love for the Lord. [44] SB 10.15.18

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
ārdrāṅga-skhalad-accha-dhātuṣu suhṛd-gotreṣu līlā-rasaṃ
varṣaty ucchvasiteṣu kṛṣṇa-mudire vyaktaṃ babhūvādbhutam |
yā prāg āsta sarasvatī drutam asau līnopakaṇṭha-sthale
yā nāsīd udagād dṛśoḥ pathi sadā nīrorudhārātra sā ||112||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the black cloud (Kṛṣṇa) sprinkled a rain of pastimes, all the mountains (cowherd friends) crumbling because of getting wet, colored with various mineral dyes, in disheveled array (breathing heavily), revealed something astonishing. The river Sarasvatī, in charge of words, got blocked up the throat (valley) of the friend (mountains), and streams of water, which were previously not visible, began to continuously flow (from their eyes).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rāgo, yathā –
astreṇa duṣpariharā haraye vyakāri
yā patri-paṅktir akṛpeṇa kṛpī-sutena |
utplutya gāṇḍiva-bhṛtā hṛdi gṛhyamāṇā
jātāsya sā kusuma-vṛṣṭir ivotsavāya ||113||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the cruel Aśvatthāmā showered invincible arrows at Kṛṣṇa, Arjuna jumped forward and received the arrows on his chest. Those arrows gave pleasure to Arjuna like a shower of flowers.[45]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
kusumāny avacinvataḥ samantād
vana-mālā-racanocitāny araṇye |
vṛṣabhasya vṛṣārkajā marīcir
divasārdhe ’pi babhūva kaumudīva ||114||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example of rāga:

When Vṛṣabha, Kṛṣṇa’s friend, arrived at noon to pick flowers for making garlands for Kṛṣṇa by wandering all over the forest, the hot rays of the summer sun in Vṛṣabha zodiac sign (Taurus) were like the cool rays of the moon for him.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha ayoge utkaṇṭhitaṃ, yathā –
dhanur-vedam adhīyāno madhyamas tvayi pāṇḍavaḥ |
bāṣpa-saṅkīrṇayā kṛṣṇaḥ girāśleṣaṃ vyajijñapat ||115||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Utkaṇṭhitam (separation, not having met Kṛṣṇa before)

O Kṛṣṇa! The middle Pāṇḍava, Arjuna, while studying the military arts, embraced You by means of words mixed with his tears.[46]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha viyoge, yathā –
aghasya jaṭharānalāt phaṇi-hradasya ca kṣveḍato
davasya kavalād api tvam avitātra yeṣām abhūḥ |
itas tritayato ’py atiprakaṭa-ghora-dhāṭī-dharāt
kathaṃ na viraha-jvarād avasitān sakhīn adya naḥ ||116||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Viyoga (separation after meeting):

You protected us, your friends, from the fire of digestion of Aghāsura, from the poison of Kāliya and from the appetite of the forest fire. But how will You protect us despairing friends from the attack of the fever of separation—which is far more ferocious than these three dangers?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: atrāpi pūrvavat proktās tāpādyās tā daśā daśa ||117||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: As previously explained (3.2.116), the ten conditions starting with tāpa (pain or heat) appear in this condition of viyoga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra tāpaḥ –
prapanno bhāṇḍīre ’py adhika-śiśire caṇḍim abharaṃ
tuṣāre ’pi prauḍhiṃ dinakara-sutā-srotasi gataḥ |
apūrvaḥ kaṃsāre subala-mukha-mitrāvalim asau
balīyān uttāpas tava viraha-janmā jvalayati ||118||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Heat in the body:

O enemy of Kaṃsa! The strong heat generated by separation from You is most amazing, because it causes even more intense heat in a very cool place. It has multiplied its heat in the waters of the cool Yamunā River, and is burning Your friends headed by Subala.[47]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kṛśatā –
tvayi prāpte kaṃsa-kṣitipati-vimokṣāya nagarīṃ
gabhīrād ābhīrāvali-tanuṣu khedād anudinam |
catūrṇāṃ bhūtānām ajani tanimā dānava-ripo
samīrasya ghrānādhvani pṛthulatā kevalam abhūt ||119||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Thinness:

O enemy of the demons! When You went to Mathurā to liberate Kaṃsa, the four elements, earth, water, fire and ether in the cowherd boys became meager due to their intense sorrow. Only the air element passing through their nostrils became strong.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
jāgaryā, yathā –
netrāmbuja-dvandvam avekṣya pūrṇaṃ
bāṣpāmbu-pūreṇa varūthapasya |
tatrānuvṛttiṃ kila yādavendra
nirvidya nidrā-madhupī mumoca ||120||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Insomnia:

O king of the Yadus! Seeing the tears in the lotus eyes of Your friend Varūthapa, the bees of sleep have given up those lotus eyes out of disgust.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ālambana-śūnyatā –
gate vṛndāraṇyāt priya-suhṛdi goṣṭheśvara-sute
laghu-bhṛtaṃ sadyaḥ patad-atitarām utpatad api |
na hi bhrāmaṃ bhrāmaṃ bhajati caṭulaṃ tulam iva me
nirālambaṃ cetaḥ kvacid api vilambaṃ lavam api ||121||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Vacant mind:

Since the son of the king of Vraja, my dear friend, left Vṛndāvana, my fickle mind, without support, has become so light that it rises and falls like a piece of cotton, spins around constantly and cannot find a point of concentration.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
adhṛtiḥ –
racayati nija-vṛttau pāśupālye nivṛttiṃ
kalayati ca kalānāṃ vismṛtau yatna-koṭim |
kim aparam iha vācyaṃ jīvite ’py adya dhatte
yaduvara virahāt te nārthitāṃ bandhu-vargaḥ ||122||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Detachment:

O best of the Yadus! In separation from You, Your friends are no longer interested in their occupation of herding the cows. They make millions of attempts to forget about singing and dancing. What more can I say? They do not desire to remain alive.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
jaḍatā –
anāśrita-paricchadāḥ kṛśa-viśīrṇa-rukṣāṅgakāḥ
sadā viphala-vṛttayo virahitāḥ kila cchāyayā |
virāva-parivarjitās tava mukunda goṣṭhāntare
sphurati suhṛdāṃ gaṇāḥ śikhara-jāta-vṛkṣā iva ||123||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Dullness:

O Mukunda! Your friends appear like trees on the top of a mountain. As the trees have no leaves, they have no clothing. As the tree has no beauty, they have no luster. As the tree is silent without birds, they are silent. As the tree is without fruit, they are without actions. They are thin, with worn, rough limbs.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vyādhiḥ –
viraha-jvara-saṃjvareṇa te jvalitā viślatha-gātra-bandhanā |
yaduvīra taṭe viceṣṭate ciram ābhīra-kumāra-maṇḍalī ||124||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sickness:

Hero of the Yadus! Suffering from heat of the fever in separation from You, all Your young cowherd friends have been lying on the bank of the Yamunā with limp limbs for many days.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
unmādaḥ –
vinā bhavad-anusmṛtiṃ viraha-vibhrameṇādhunā
jagad-vyavahṛti-kramaṃ nikhilam eva vismāritāḥ |
luṇṭhanti bhuvi śerate bata hasanti dhāvanty amī
rudanti mathurā-pate kim api vallavānāṃ gaṇāḥ ||125||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Insanity:

Lord of Mathurā! Unable to think of You because of the confusion caused by separation, the cowherd boys have now forgotten how to conduct themselves in this world. Sometimes they fall on the ground. Sometimes they lie down, sometimes they laugh, sometimes they run, and sometimes they weep.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mūrcchitam –
dīvyatīha madhure mathurāyāṃ
prāpya rājyam adhunā madhu-nāthe |
viśvam eva muditaṃ ruditāndhe
gokule tu muhur ākulatābhūt ||126||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Fainting:

The Lord of the Madhus, having attained a kingdom at sweet Mathurā, now enjoys Himself and gives bliss to the world. But crying and fainting repeatedly, Gokula has become completely confused.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mṛtiḥ –
kaṃsārer viraha-jvarormi-janita-jvālāvalī-jarjarā
gopāḥ śaila-taṭe tathā śithilita-śvāsāṅkurāḥ śerate |
vāraṃ vāram akharva-locana-jalair āplāvya tān niścalān
śocanty adya yathā ciraṃ paricaya-snigdhāḥ kuraṅgā api ||127||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Death-like state:

O enemy of Kaṃsa! Worn out with the pain arising from the waves of fever of separation, the cowherd boys have fallen down on the plateau of Govardhana and are breathing only slightly. Just as people lament with tears in their eyes when they see that acquaintances have fallen into difficulty, today, the deer lament for them, soaking their motionless bodies with their profuse tears.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prokteyaṃ virahāvasthā spaṣṭa-līlānusārataḥ |
kṛṣṇena viprayogaḥ syān na jātu vraja-vāsinām ||128||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The separation which has been described here is in accordance with the pastimes of the Lord when He appears on earth. However, the inhabitants of Vraja are never separated from Kṛṣṇa (in His eternal pastimes.)

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathā ca skānde mathurā-khaṇḍe –
vatsair vatsatarībhiś ca sadā krīḍati mādhavaḥ |
vṛndāvanāntara-gataḥ sa-rāmo bālakair vṛtaḥ ||129||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
This is stated in the Skanda Purāṇa, Mathurā-khaṇḍa:

Kṛṣṇa, along with Balarāma, surrounded by the boys of Vraja, plays eternally in Vṛndāvana with the cows and calves.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha yoge siddhir, yathā –
pāṇḍavaḥ puṇḍarīkākṣaṃ prekṣya cakri-niketane |
citrākāraṃ bhajann eva mitrākāram adarśayat ||130||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Siddhi (meeting Kṛṣṇa for the first time):

Arjuna, seeing Kṛṣṇa in the house of the potter in Drupada’s town, became motionless like a picture, and thus revealed himself as a true friend.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tuṣṭir, yatha śrī-daśame–
taṃ mātuleyaṃ parirabhya nirvṛto
bhīmaḥ smayan prema-javākulendriyaḥ |
yamau kirīṭī ca suhṛttamaṃ mudā
prabṛddha-bāṣpaḥ parirebhire ’cyutam ||131||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Tuṣṭi (meeting Kṛṣṇa after some separation), from the Tenth Canto:

Then Bhīma, his eyes brimming with tears, laughed with joy as he embraced his maternal cousin, Kṛṣṇa. Arjuna and the twins—Nakula and Sahadeva—also joyfully embraced their dear most friend, the infallible Lord, and they cried profusely. SB 10.71.27

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
kurujāṅgale harim avekṣya puraḥ
priya-saṅgamaṃ vraja-suhṛn-nikarāḥ |
bhuja-maṇḍalena maṇi-kuṇḍalinaḥ
pulakāñcitena pariṣaṣvajire ||132||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

His friends from Vraja, who had longed to see Him for so long, seeing Kṛṣṇa at Kurukṣetra, embraced Him with all their arms erupting in goose bumps.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sthitir, yathā śrī-daśame –
yat-pāda-pāṃsur bahu-janma-kṛcchrato
dhṛtātmabhir yogibhir apy alabhyaḥ |
sa eva yad-dṛg-viṣayaḥ svayaṃ sthitaḥ
kiṃ varṇyate diṣṭam ato vrajaukasām ||133||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sthiti (permanent association), from the Tenth Canto:

Yogīs may undergo severe austerities and penances for many births by practicing yama, niyama, āsana and prāṇāyāma, none of which are easily performed. Yet in due course of time, when these yogīs attain the perfection of controlling the mind, they will still be unable to taste even a particle of dust from the lotus feet of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. What then can we describe about the great fortune of the inhabitants of Vrajabhūmi, these cowherd boys, with whom the Supreme Personality of Godhead personally lives permanently and who see the Lord face to face? SB 10.12.12

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dvayor apy eka-jātīya-bhāva-mādhurya-bhāg asau |
preyān kām api puṣṇāti rasaś citta-camatkṛtim ||134||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This preyo-rasa or sakhya-rasa, in which Kṛṣṇa and the devotee partake of the sweetness of one type of love, nourishes astonishing bliss in the heart

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prīte ca vatsale cāpi kṛṣṇa-tad-bhaktayoḥ punaḥ |
dvayor anyonya-bhāvasya bhinna-jātīyatā bhavet ||135||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In prīti-rasa (dāsya rasa) and vātsalya-rasa, Kṛṣṇa’s and the devotee’s love are of different types.[51]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
preyān eva bhavet preyān ataḥ sarva-raseṣv ayam |
sakhya-sampṛkta-hṛdayaiḥ sadbhir evānubudhyate ||136||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: For this reason, those devotees devoted to sakhya-rasa understand that this sakhya-rasa is the best among all rasas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vibhāvādyais tu vātsalyaṃ sthāyī puṣṭim upāgataḥ |
eṣa vatsala-nāmātra prokto bhakti-raso budhaiḥ ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When vatsala-sthāyi-bhāva is nourished by vibhāva and the other elements, it is called vatsala-bhakti-rasa by the wise.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ālambanāḥ –
kṛṣṇaṃ tasya gurūṃś cātra prāhur ālambanān budhāḥ ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Ālambanas:

The learned say that Kṛṣṇa, and the devotees acting as elders, are the ālambanas for this rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra kṛṣṇo, yathā –
nava-kuvalaya-dāma-śyāmalaṃ komalāṅgaṃ
vicalad-alaka-bhṛṅga-krānta-netrāmbujāntam |
vraja-bhuvi viharantaṃ putram ālokayantī
vraja-pati-dayitāsīt prasnavotpīḍa-digdhā ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Kṛṣṇa as the viṣaya of vātsalya-rasa:

On seeing her son, who had the complexion of a fresh, blue lotus, who had a soft body, with lotus eyes touched by bee-like locks of hair, Yaśodā became wet with milk flowing forcefully from her breast.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śyāmāṅgo ruciraḥ sarva-sal-lakṣaṇa-yuto mṛduḥ |
priya-vāk saralo hrīmān vinayī mānya-māna-kṛt |
dātety-ādi-guṇo kṛṣṇo vibhāva iti kathyate ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The vibhāva of vātsalya-rasa is Kṛṣṇa, with dark, attractive, soft body, endowed with all wonderful characteristics, speaking lovely words. He is honest, shy, obedient, respectful and generous.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
evaṃ guṇasya cāsyānugrāhyatvād eva kīrtitā |
prabhāvānāspadatayā vedyasyātra vibhāvatā ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Kṛṣṇa, endowed with the above-mentioned qualities, but without manifesting powers, is famous as the vibhāva of vatsala-rasa by accepting Himself as the object of compassion.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathā śrī-daśame –
trayyā copaniṣadbhiś ca sāṅkhya-yogaiś ca sātvataiḥ |
upagīyamāna-māhātmyaṃ hariṃ sāmanyatātmajam ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From the Tenth Canto:

The glories of the Supreme Personality of Godhead are studied through the three Vedas, the Upaniṣads, the literature of Sāṅkhya-yoga, and other Vaiṣṇava literature, yet mother Yaśodā considered that Supreme Person her ordinary child. SB 10.8.45

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
viṣṇur nityam upāsyate sakhi mayā tenātra nītāḥ kṣayaṃ
śaṅke pūtanikādayaḥ kṣiti-ruhau tau vātyayonmūlitau |
pratyakṣaṃ girir eṣa goṣṭa-patinā rāmeṇa sārddhaṃ dhṛtas
tat-tat-karma duranvayaṃ mama śiśoḥ kenāsya sambhāvyate ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Because I and my husband have worshiped Viṣṇu constantly, Pūtana and other demons have been destroyed. The two arjuna trees were uprooted by the wind (and Viṣṇu protected Him). I saw with my own eyes that Govardhana was held up by my husband, along with Balarāma’s help, because of worshipping Viṣṇu. How would it be possible for my baby to perform all these difficult tasks?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha guravaḥ –
adhikaṃ-manya-bhāvena śikṣā-kāritayāpi ca |
lālakatvādināpy atra vibhāvā guravo matāḥ ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The elders (āśraya):

The elders become vibhāva of vatsala-rasa by thinking themselves greater than Kṛṣṇa, protecting Him and teaching Him.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
bhūry-anugraha-citena cetasā
lālanotkam abhitaḥ kṛpākulam |
gauraveṇa guruṇā jagad-guror
gauravaṃ gaṇam agaṇyam āśraye ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

I take shelter of the countless elders, full of desire to remove Kṛṣṇa’s sorrow, who are eager to care for Him, with hearts filled with great desire to protect Him, though He is the guru for the universe.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
te tu tasyātra kathitā vraja-rājñī vrajeśvaraḥ |
rohiṇī tāś ca vallavyo yāḥ padmaja-hṛtātmajāḥ ||10||
devakī tat-sapatnyaś ca kuntī cānakadundubhiḥ |
sāndīpani-mukhāś cānye yathā-pūrvam amī varāḥ |
vrajeśvarī-vrajādhīśau śreṣṭhau gurujaneṣv imau ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The elders of Kṛṣṇa are Yaśodā, Nanda, Rohiṇī, the gopīs whose sons were stolen by Lord Brahmā, Devakī and the other wives of Vasudeva, Kunti, Vasudeva, and Sāndīpani Muni. Among the elders, the first ones on the list are superior to the later ones.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra vrajeśvaryā rūpaṃ, yathā śrī-daśame –
kṣaumaṃ vāsaḥ pṛthu-kaṭi-taṭe bibhratī sūtra-naddhaṃ |
putra-sneha-snuta-kuca-yugaṃ jāta-kampaṃ ca subhrūḥ ||12||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The form of Yaśodā, from the Tenth Canto:

Dressed in a saffron-yellow sari, with a belt tied about her full hips, mother Yaśodā pulled on the churning rope, laboring considerably, her bangles and earrings moving and vibrating and her whole body shaking. Because of her intense love for her child, her breasts were wet with milk. Her face, with its very beautiful eyebrows, was wet with perspiration, and mālatī flowers were falling from her hair. SB 10.9.3

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
ḍorī-juṭita-vakra-keśa-paṭalā sindūra-bindūllasat-
sīmānta-dyutir aṅga-bhūṣaṇa-vidhiṃ nāti-prabhūtaṃ śritā |
govindāsya-nisṛṣṭa-sāśru-nayana-dvandvā navendīvara-
śyāma-śyāma-rucir vicitra-sicayā goṣṭheśvarī pātu vaḥ ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another description of her form:

The queen of the cowherds, Yaśodā, has wavy hair bound by cords, with a dot of sindhura at the parting of her hair. She does not wear many ornaments. Her eyes become filled with tears on seeing Kṛṣṇa. Her complexion is dark like a blue lotus, radiating a dark glow, and she wears colorful clothing. May she protect us!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vātsalyam, yathā –
tanau mantra-nyāsaṃ praṇayati harer gadgadamayī
sa-bāṣpākṣī rakṣā-tilakam alike kalpayati ca |
snuvānā pratyūṣe diśati ca bhuje kārmaṇam asau
yaśodā mūrteva sphurati suta-vātsalya-paṭalī ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Yaśodā’s motherly affection:

Early in the morning, with milk dripping from her breasts, with choked voice and tears in her eyes, Yaśodā uttered mantras on Kṛṣṇa’s limbs, placed tilaka on his forehead for his protection, and tied protective herbs on His arm. She was the personification of maternal love.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vrajādhīśasya rūpaṃ, yathā –
tila-taṇḍulitaiḥ kacaiḥ sphurantaṃ
nava-bhāṇḍīra-palāśa-cāru-celam |
ati-tundilam indu-kānti-bhājaṃ
vraja-rājaṃ vara-kūrcam arcayāmi ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Nanda’s form:

I worship Nanda, the corpulent king of Vraja, who has a mixture of white and black hair on his head, an attractive beard as white as moon, and clothing the color of a new banyan leaf.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vātsalyam, yathā –
avalambya karāṅguliṃ nijāṃ
skhalad-aṅghri prasarantam aṅgane |
