urasi sravad-aśru-nirjharo
mumude prekṣya sutaṃ vrajādhipaḥ ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
His parental affection:

Kṛṣṇa held the fingers of Nanda as He walked about the yard on unsteady feet. Seeing his son begin to walk, Nanda became inundated with bliss, and his chest became covered with tears.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha uddīpanāḥ –
kaumārādi-vayo-rūpa-veśāḥ śaiśava-cāpalam |
jalpita-smita-līlādyāḥ budhair uddīpanāḥ smṛtāḥ ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Uddīpanas:

The uddīpanas for vatsala-rasa are said to be His three ages starting with kaumāra, His form, His cloth, His naughty actions, His laughing and His playing.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra kaumāram –
ādyaṃ madhyaṃ tathā śeṣaṃ kaumāraṃ tri-vidhaṃ matam ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The kaumāra age has three stages: beginning, middle and end.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyam –
sthūla-madhyorutāpāṅga-śvetimā svalpa-dantatā |
pravyakta-mārdavatvaṃ ca kaumāre prathame sati ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The first part of kaumāra:

At the beginning of the kaumāra age, His waist and thighs are chubby. The edges of His eyes are white; His teeth begin to appear slightly and His body is very tender.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
tri-catura-daśana-sphuran-mukhenduṃ
pṛthutara-madhya-kaṭi-rakoru-sīmā |
nava-kuvalaya-komalaḥ kumāro
mudam adhikāṃ vraja-nāthayor vyatānīt ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

This young boy, showing three or four teeth, with chubby waist and thighs, who was as soft as a fresh blue lotus, gave extraordinary joy to Nanda and Yaśodā.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asmin muhuḥ pada-kṣepe kṣaṇike rudita-smite |
svāṅguṣṭha-pānam uttāna-śayanādyaṃ ca ceṣṭitam ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The activities of Kṛṣṇa at the beginning of the kaumāra age are kicking up His legs, momentary crying or laughing, sucking His thumb, and sleeping on His back.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
mukha-puṭa-kṛta-pādāmbhoruhāṅguṣṭha-mūrdha-
pracala-caraṇa-yugmaṃ putram uttāna-suptam |
kṣaṇam iha virudantaṃ smera-vaktraṃ kṣaṇaṃ sā
tilam api viratāsīn nekṣituṃ goṣṭha-rājñī ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

Yaśodā could not stop looking at her son sucking His big toe, throwing His two feet in the air, lying on His back, sometimes crying and sometimes laughing.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra vyāghra-nakhaṃ kaṇṭhe rakṣā-tilaka-maṅgalam |
paṭṭa-ḍorī kaṭau haste sūtram ity ādi maṇḍanam ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The ornaments of the early kaumāra age are a tiger claw around His neck, tilaka for protection, mascara on His eyes, a cord around His waist and a string tied on His wrist.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
tarakṣu-nakha-maṇḍalaṃ nava-tamāla-patra-dyutiṃ
śiśuṃ rucira-rocanā-kṛta-tamāla-patra-śriyam |
dhṛta-pratisaraṃ kaṭi-sphurita-paṭṭa-sūtra-srajaṃ
vrajeśa-gṛhiṇī sutaṃ na kila vīkṣya tṛptiṃ yayau ||24||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

Looking at her son possessing the complexion of a new tamāla leaf, with a tiger claw around His neck, with yellow gorocana tilaka, with a string tied on His wrist and silk cord around His waist, Yaśodā could never satisfy her eyes.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha madhyamam –
dṛk-taṭī-bhāg-alakatānagnatā cchidri-karṇatā |
kalokti-riṅganādyaṃ ca kaumāre sati madhyame ||25||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Middle kaumāra age:

In the middle kaumāra age, Kṛṣṇa has locks of hair falling to His eyes. He appears with partial clothing. He has pierced ears, He utters sweet, unclear words, and beings to crawl.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
vicalad-alaka-ruddha-bhrū-kuṭī cañcalākṣaṃ
kala-vacanam udañcan nūtana-śrotra-randhram |
alaghu-racita-riṅgaṃ gokule dig-dukūlaṃ
tanayam amṛta-sindhau prekṣya mātā nyamāṅkṣīt ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

Mother Yaśodā drowned in an ocean of bliss on seeing her partially naked son with locks hanging down to His brow, with darting eyes, inarticulate words, and fresh holes in His ears, as He crawled about more frequently.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ghrāṇasya śikhare muktā nava-nītaṃ karāmbuje |
kiṅkiṇy-ādi ca kaṭyādau prasādhanam ihoditam ||27||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The decorations of the middle kaumāra period are a pearl at the tip of His nose, butter in His hand, and bells around His waist.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kvaṇita-kanaka-kiṅkiṇī-kalāpaṃ
smita-mukham ujjvala-nāsikāgram uktam |
kara-dhṛta-navanīta-piṇḍam agre
tanayam avekṣya nananda nanda-patnī ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

The wife of Nanda derived great pleasure from seeing Kṛṣṇa in front of her, with small gold bells jingling around His waist, with the pearl in His nose and butter in His hand.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha śeṣam –
atra kiñcit kṛśaṃ madhyam īṣat-prathima-bhāg uraḥ |
śiraś ca kāka-pakṣāḍhyaṃ kaumāre carame sati ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Late kaumāra:

During the last part of the kaumāra age, Kṛṣṇa’s waist becomes slightly thinner, His chest becomes slightly broader, and three braids hang down His back.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
sa manāg apacīyamāna-madhyaḥ
prathimopakrama-śikṣaṇārthi-vakṣāḥ |
dadhad-ākula-kāka-pakṣa-lakṣmīṃ
jananīṃ stambhayati sma divya-ḍimbhaḥ ||30||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

The sight of the charming child with thin waist, slightly broadened chest, and three braids tied at the back of His head completely stunned His mother.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dhaṭī phaṇa-paḍī cātra kiñcid-vanya-vibhūṣaṇam |
laghu-vetra-karatvādi maṇḍanaṃ parikīrtitam ||31||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A long narrow cloth wrapped around the waist, cloth folded to resemble a serpent’s hood at the front, flower ornaments and a small stick in His hand are considered the ornaments of the last part of the kaumāra age.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vatsa-rakṣā vrajābhyarṇe vayasyaiḥ saha khelanam |
pāva-śṛṅga-dalādīnāṃ vādanādy atra ceṣṭitam ||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The activities of the last part of the kaumāra age are herding the calves near Vraja, playing with friends, and playing on small flutes, horns and leaves.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
śikhaṇḍa-kṛta-śekharaḥ phaṇa-paṭīṃ dadhat
kare ca laguḍīṃ laghuṃ savayasāṃ kulair āvṛtaḥ |
avann iha śakṛt-karīn parisare vrajasya priye
sutas tava kṛtārthayaty ahaha paśya netrāṇi naḥ ||33||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of activities of late kaumāra:

O dear wife! Look! Your son, with a peacock feather on His head and the hood-shaped cloth decoration, carrying a stick, and surrounded by His friends, is herding the calves at the edge of Vraja. By seeing Him, our eyes have become successful.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha paugaṇḍam –
paugaṇḍādi puraivoktaṃ tena saṅkṣipya likhyate ||34||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Paugaṇḍa age:

Since the paugaṇḍa age was previously described (as the uddīpana for sakhya-rasa, 3.3.61-77), it is described in summary here.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
pathi pathi surabhīṇām aṃśukottaṃsi-mūrdhā
dhavalim ayug-apāṅgo maṇḍitaḥ kañcukena |
laghu laghu pariguñjan-mañju-mañjīra-yugmaṃ
vraja-bhuvi mama vatsaḥ kacca-deśād upaiti ||35||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

My son with white edges on His eyes, wearing a turban and jacket, is returning to Vraja from the river bank, following behind the cows, while His attractive ankle bells jingle.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha kaiśoram –
aruṇima-yug-apāṅgas tuṅga-vakṣaḥ-kapāṭī-
viluṭhad-amala-hāro ramya-romāvali-śrīḥ |
puruṣa-maṇir ayaṃ me devaki śyāmalāṅgas
tvad-udara-khani-janmā netram uccair dhinoti ||36||

navyena yauvanenāpi dīvyan goṣṭhendra-nandanaḥ |
bhāti kevala-vātsalya-bhājāṃ paugaṇḍa-bhāg iva ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though Kṛṣṇa is beautiful in the age of nava-yauvana (late kaiśora), He appears as if He were still in the paugaṇḍa age for those fixed in pure vātsalya-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sukumāreṇa paugaṇḍa-vayasā saṅgato ’py asau |
kiśorābhaḥ sadā dāsa-viśeṣāṇāṃ prabhāsate ||38||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: And when Kṛṣṇa is of the delicate paugaṇḍa age, He appears to be of the kaiśora age for certain types of persons in dāsya-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha śaiśava-cāpalam –
pārīr bhinatti vikiraty ajire dadhīni
santānikāṃ harati kṛntati mantha-daṇḍam |
vahnau kṣipaty avirataṃ nava-nītam itthaṃ
mātuḥ pramoda-bharam eva haris tanoti ||39||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Insolence of His kaiśora age:

Kṛṣṇa gave bliss to mother Yaśodā by breaking the milk pots, scattering the yogurt, stealing the cream off the milk, breaking the churning rod, and constantly throwing butter into the fire.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
prekṣya prekṣya diśaḥ sa-śaṅkam asakṛn mandaṃ padaṃ nikṣipan
nāyāty eṣa latāntare sphuṭam ito gavyaṃ hariṣyan hariḥ |
tiṣṭha svairam ajānatīva mukhare caurya-bhramad-bhrū-lataṃ
trasyal-locanam asya śuṣyad-adharaṃ ramyaṃ didṛkṣe mukham ||40||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O Mukharā! Kṛṣṇa, looking all around in fear and walking softly, has certainly come to steal the butter by darting out from behind the creepers. Remain here, pretending not to know. I desire to see His attractive face with dry face and frightened eyes, as He moves His brows---thinking how to steal the butter.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha anubhāvāḥ –
anubhāvāḥ śiro-ghrāṇaṃ kareṇāṅgābhimārjanam |
āśīrvādo nideśaś ca lālanaṃ pratipālanam |
hitopadeśa-dānādyā vatsale parikīrtitāḥ ||41||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The anubhāvas in vatsala-rasa are smelling Kṛṣṇa’s head, rubbing His body with one’s hands, giving blessings to Him, giving Him orders, caring for Him, protecting Him, and giving beneficial instructions.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra śiro-ghrāṇam, yathā śrī-daśame –
tad-īkṣaṇotprema-rasāplutāśayā
jātānurāgā gata-manyavo ’rbhakān |
uduhya dorbhiḥ parirabhya mūrdhani
ghrāṇair avāpuḥ paramāṃ mudaṃ te ||42||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Smelling His head, from the Tenth Canto:

At that time, all the thoughts of the cowherd men merged in the mellow of paternal love, which was aroused by the sight of their sons. Experiencing a great attraction, their anger completely disappearing, they lifted their sons, embraced them in their arms and enjoyed the highest pleasure by smelling their sons’ heads. SB 10.13.33

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
dugdhena digdhā kuca-vicyutena
samagram āghrāya śiraḥ sapiccham |
kareṇa goṣṭheśitur aṅganeyam
aṅgāni putrasya muhur mamārja ||43||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

The wife of Nanda, with milk flowing from her breasts and soaking her body, after smelling His head bedecked with peacock feather, began to rub His limbs with her hands repeatedly.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
cumbāśleṣau tathāhvānaṃ nāma-grahaṇa-pūrvakam |
upālambhādayaś cātra mitraiḥ sādhāraṇāḥ kriyāḥ ||44||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The elders’ activities of kissing him, embracing him, calling out His name and criticizing Him are anubhāvas in common with the friends as well.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sāttvikāḥ –
navātra sāttvikāḥ stanya-srāvaḥ stambhādayaś ca te ||45||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sāttvika-bhāvas:

The sāttvika-bhāvas of vātsalya-bhakti-rasa are the eight starting with stambha (paralysis) and as well, milk flowing from the breasts of the women.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra stanya-srāvo, yathā śrī-daśame –
tan-mātaro veṇu-rava-tvarotthitā
utthāpya dorbhiḥ parirabhya nirbharam |
sneha-snuta-stanya-payaḥ-sudhāsavaṃ
matvā paraṃ brahma sutān apāyayan ||46||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Milking flowing from the breasts, from the Tenth Canto:

The mothers of the boys, upon hearing the sounds of the flutes and bugles being played by their sons, immediately rose from their household tasks, lifted their boys onto their laps, embraced them with both arms and began to feed them with their breast milk, which flowed forth because of extreme love specifically for Kṛṣṇa. Actually, Kṛṣṇa is everything, but at that time, expressing extreme love and affection, they took special pleasure in feeding Kṛṣṇa, the Parabrahman, and Kṛṣṇa drank the milk from His respective mothers as if it were a nectarean beverage. SB 10.13.23

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā lalita-mādhave (1.46) –
niculita-giri-dhātu-sphīta-patrāvalīkān
akhila-surabhi-reṇūn kṣālayadbhir yaśodā |
kuca-kalasa-vimuktaiḥ sneha-mādhvīka-medhyais
tava navam abhiṣekaṃ dugdha-pūraiḥ karoti ||47||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example, from Lalita-mādhava:

O Kṛṣṇa! Yaśodā, by most pure streams of milk mixed with the honey of her affection flowing from the pots in the form of her breasts, is washing away the dust raised by the cows’ hooves which covers the painted patterns on Your body, derived from minerals found in the mountains. In this way, she performs a first bath for You.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
stambhādayo, yathā –
katham api parirabdhuṃ na kṣamā stabdha-gātrī
kalayitum api nālaṃ bāṣpa-pura-plutākṣī |
na ca sutam upadeṣṭuṃ ruddha-kaṇṭhī samarthā
dadhatam acalam āsīd vyākulā gokuleśā ||48||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Other sāttvika bhāvas:

The disturbed queen of Gokula could not embrace Kṛṣṇa, the lifter of Govardhana, because her limbers were paralyzed. Nor could she gaze upon Him, because her eyes were filled with tears. Nor could she instruct Him, because her voice was choked up.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vyabhicāriṇaḥ –
tatrāpasmāra-sahitāḥ prītoktāḥ vyabhicāriṇaḥ ||49||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Vyabhicāri-bhāvas:

The vyabhicāri-bhāvas of vātsalya-bhakti-rasa are the same as those for prīti-bhakti-rasa with the addition of apasmāra.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra harṣo, yathā śrī-daśame–
yaśodāpi mahābhāgā naṣṭa-labdha-prajā satī |
pariṣvajāṅkam āropya mumocāśru-kalāṃ muhuḥ ||50||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Harṣa (joy), from the Tenth Canto:

The greatly fortunate mother Yaśodā, having lost her son and then regained Him, placed Him on her lap. That chaste lady cried constant torrents of tears as she repeatedly embraced Him. SB 10.17.20

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā vidagdha-mādhave (1.20) –
jita-candra-parāga-candrikā
naladendīvara-candana-śriyam |
parito mayi śaitya-mādhurīṃ
vahati sparśa-mahotsavas tava ||51||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example, from Vidagdha-mādhava:

O Kṛṣṇa! The great bliss generated from Your touch produces in me in the most pleasurable coolness, which conquers the coolness of abundant camphor, the moonlight, the herb uśīra (spikenard), the blue lotus or sandalwood.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sthāyī –
sambhramādi-cyutā yā syād anukampe ’nukampituḥ |
ratiḥ saivātra vātsalyaṃ sthāyī bhāvo nigadyate ||52||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sthāyi-bhāva:

The rati of a person dispensing compassion which is directed towards a worthy object is called vatsala-rati. This vatsala-rati is said to be the sthāyi-bhāva for vatsala-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yaśodādes tu vātsalya-ratiḥ prauḍhā nisargataḥ |
premavat snehavad bhāti kadācit kila rāgavat ||53||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The vatsala-rati of Yaśodā and others is at the stage of very intense rāga by its very nature. Just as other ratis gradually increase to prema, and then sneha, sometimes vatsala-rati also appears externally to increase to prema, sneha and rāga, but internally it is always on the level of rāga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra vātsalya-ratir, yathā śrī-daśame
nandaḥ sva-putram ādāya pretyāgatam udāra-dhīḥ |
mūrdhny upāghrāya paramāṃ mudaṃ lebhe kurūdvaha ||54||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Vatsala-rati from the Tenth Canto:

O Mahārāja Parīkṣit, best of the Kurus, Nanda Mahārāja was very liberal and simple. He immediately took his son Kṛṣṇa on his lap as if Kṛṣṇa had returned from death, and by formally smelling his son’s head, Nanda Mahārāja undoubtedly enjoyed transcendental bliss. SB 10.6.43

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
vinyasta-śruti-pālir adya muralī-nisvāna-śuśrūṣā
bhūyaḥ prasrava-varṣiṇī dviguṇitotkaṇṭhā pradoṣodaye |
gehād aṅganam aṅganāt punar asau gehaṃ viśanty ākulā
govindasya muhur vrajendra-gṛhiṇī panthānam ālokate ||55||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Today, in order to hear the sound of the flute, Yaśodā has pricked up her ears. But as evening approached, her longing doubled in intensity. Milk flowed from her breasts, and repeatedly she went out of the house into the yard and then reentered the house. In complete anxiety, she constantly gazed at the path by which Govinda would return.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
premavad, yathā –
prekṣya tatra muni-rāja-maṇḍalaiḥ
stūyamānam api mukta-sambhramā |
kṛṣṇam aṅkam abhi gokuleśvarī
prasnutā kuru-bhuvi nyavīviśat ||56||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Yaśodā’s appearing to have prema:

Knowing from talking to others that Kṛṣṇa was being glorified by the sages at that place, Yaśodā, milk flowing from her breasts, entered Kurukṣetra without reverence for Him as the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
devakyā vivṛta-prasū-caritayāpy unmṛjyamānānane
bhūyobhir vasudeva-nandanatayāpy udghūṣyamāṇe janaiḥ |
govinde mihira-grahotsukatayā kṣetraṃ kuror āgate
premā vallava-nāthayor atitarām ullāsam evāyayau ||57||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

When Kṛṣṇa went with an eager heart to Kurukṣetra to observe the solar eclipse, people glorified Him as the son of Vasudeva. Devakī, performing activities suitable for a mother, wiped the tears from His face, while Nanda and Yaśodā’s prema simply increased even more.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
snehavat, yathā –
pīyūṣa-dyutibhiḥ stanādri-patitaiḥ kṣīrotkarair jāhnavī
kālindī ca vilocanābja-janitair jātāñjana-śyāmalaiḥ |
ārān-madhyama-vedim āpatitayoḥ klinnā tayoḥ saṅgame
vṛttāsi vraja-rājñi tat-suta-mukha-prekṣāṃ sphuṭaṃ vāñchasi ||58||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Yaśodā’s appearing to have sneha:

O queen of Vraja! The Gaṅgā River has sprung up through streams of sweet milk falling from the mountains of your breasts, and the Yamunā has sprung up through the tears mixed with black mascara falling from your lotus eyes.[56] Those rivers are falling on the altar of your torso. Being bathed at the joining of the two rivers, you clearly desire to see the face of your son.[57]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rāgavat, yathā –
tuṣārati tuṣānalo ’py upari tasya baddha-sthitir
bhavantam avalokate yadi mukunda goṣṭheśvarī |
sudhāmbudhir api sphuṭaṃ vikaṭa-kāla-kūṭaty alaṃ
sthitā yadi na tatra te vadana-padmam udvīkṣyate ||59||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Yaśodā’s appearing to manifest rāga (though she always has rāga):

O Mukunda! If Yaśodā is placed on a fire made from grain chaff--but can see You--the fire becomes as cool as ice. If she is placed in an ocean of nectar--but cannot see You--the ocean of nectar becomes as sharp as Kālakūṭa poison.[58]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha ayoge utkaṇṭhitam, yathā –
vatsasya hanta śarad-indu-vinindi-vaktraṃ
sampādayiṣyati kadā nayanotsavaṃ naḥ |
ity acyute viharati vraja-bāṭikāyām
ūrvī tvarā jayati devaka-nandinīnām ||60||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Utkaṇṭhita (separation, not having met for the first time):

When Kṛṣṇa was playing in the grounds of Vraja, the daughters of Devaka[59] thought, “O! When will we see the face of Kṛṣṇa, which conquers the autumn moon?” All glories to their great impatience.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
bhrātas tanayaṃ bhrātur
mama sandiśa gāndinī-putra |
bhrātṛvyeṣu vasantī
didṛkṣate tvāṃ hare kuntī ||61||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O Akrūra! Tell Kṛṣṇa, the son of my brother, that Kuntī, dwelling among the enemy, is longing to see Him.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
viyogo, yathā śrī-daśame–
yaśodā varṇyamānāni putrasya caritāṇi ca |
śṛṇvaty aśrūṇy asrākṣīt sneha-snuta-payodharā ||62||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Separation after meeting, from the Tenth Canto:

As mother Yaśodā heard the descriptions of her son’s activities in Mathurā, she poured out her tears, and milk flowed from her breasts out of love. SB 10.46.28

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
yāte rāja-puraṃ harau mukha-taṭī vyākīrṇa-dhūmrālakā
paśya srasta-tanuḥ kaṭhora-luṭhanair dehe vraṇaṃ kurvatī |
kṣīṇā goṣṭha-mahī-mahendra-mahiṣī hā putra putrety asau
krośantī karayor yugena kurute kaṣṭād uras-tāḍanam ||63||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Just see! Now that Kṛṣṇa has gone to Mathurā, thin Yaśodā, her face covered by grey hair scattered here and there, injures her body by falling roughly on the ground. She shouts “O my son! O my son!” and beats her chest with her two hands.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bahūnām api sad-bhāve viyoge ’tra tu kecana |
cintā viṣāda-nirveda-jāḍya-dainyāni cāpalam |
unmāda-mohāv ity ādyā atyudrekaṃ vrajanty amī ||64||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though many vyabhicāri-bhāvas are possible in vatsala-rasa, during viyoga only cintā, viṣāda, nirveda, jāḍyam, dainyam, cāpalya, unmāda and moha are prominent.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atra cintā –
manda-spandam abhūt klamair alaghubhiḥ sandānitaṃ mānasaṃ
dvandvaṃ locanayoś cirād avicala-vyābhugna-tāraṃ sthitam |
niśvāsaiḥ sravad eva pākam ayate stanyaṃ ca taptair idaṃ
nūnaṃ vallava-rājñi putra-virahodghūrṇābhir ākramyase ||65||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Cintā (pondering):

O Yaśodā! You mind, tied up with heavy fatigue, has become devoid of function. The pupils of your eyes remain motionless, staring in a crooked manner. The milk flowing from your breasts has become cooked by your hot breathing. Certainly, you are afflicted with unsteadiness due to separation from your son.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
viṣādaḥ –
vadana-kamalaṃ putrasyāhaṃ nimīlati śaiśave
nava-taruṇimārambhonmṛṣṭaṃ na ramyam alokayam |
abhinava-vadhū-yuktaṃ cāmuṃ na harmyam aveśayaṃ
śirasi kuliśaṃ hanta kṣiptaṃ śvaphalka-sutena me ||66||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Viṣāda (remorse):

I have not seen the attractive, shining face of my son in His period of new youth (late kaiśora period), after His childhood. I have not performed the gṛha-praveśa of my son when He got married. Oh! Akrūra has struck my head with a thunderbolt.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nirvedaḥ –
dhig astu hata-jīvitaṃ niravadhi-śriyo ’py adya me
yayā na hi hareḥ śiraḥ snuta-kucāgram āghrāyate |
sadā nava-sudhā-duhām api gavāṃ parārdhaṃ ca dhik
sa luñcati na cañcalaḥ surabhi-gandhi yāsāṃ dadhi ||67||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Nirveda (self-disgust):

What an unfortunate life, in spite of all unlimited wealth, for I did not smell Kṛṣṇa’s head while milk flows from my breast. How unfortunate are the millions of cows which give continual milk like fresh nectar, for fickle Kṛṣṇa does not steal their fragrant yogurt?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
jāḍyam –
yaḥ puṇḍarīkekṣaṇa tiṣṭhatas te
goṣṭhe karāmbhoruha-maṇḍano ’bhūt |
taṃ prekṣya daṇḍa-stimitendriyād yad
daṇḍākṛtis te jananī babhūva ||68||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Jāḍyam (inaction):

O lotus-eyed one! Seeing Your stick which was the ornament of Your lotus hand while you were in Gokula, You mother has today become like a stick, with all senses stopped.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dainyam –
yācate bata vidhātar udasrā tvāṃ radais tṛṇam udasya yaśodā |
gocare sakṛd api kṣaṇam adya matsaraṃ tyaja mamānaya vatsam ||69||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Dainyam (lowness):

O selfish Lord! With tears in her eyes and grass in her teeth, Yaśodā prays to you, “Give up hatred of me, and bring within my vision, just once for a short time today, my son.”

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
cāpalam –
kim iva kurute harmye tiṣṭhann ayaṃ nirapatrapo
vrajapatir iti brūte mugdho ’yam atra mudā janaḥ |
ahaha tanayaṃ prāṇebhyo ’pi priyaṃ parihṛtya taṃ
kaṭhina-hṛdayo goṣṭhe svairī praviśya sukhīyati ||70||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Cāpalam (insolence):

What is this shameless person doing sitting in the mansion? Ignorant people call him the lord of Vraja while smiling. How astonishing! (It is true.) Giving up a son dearer than life, this hard-hearted person, entering the village freely, thinks he is happy.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
unmādaḥ –
kva me putro nīpāḥ kathayata kuraṅgāḥ kim iha vaḥ
sa babhrāmābhyarṇe bhaṇata tam udantaṃ madhukarāḥ |
iti bhrāmaṃ bhrāmaṃ bhrama-bhara-vidūnā yadupate
bhavantaṃ pṛcchantī diśi diśi yaśodā vicarati ||71||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Unmāda (insanity):

“O kadamba trees, where is my son? O deer, is my son wandering about near you? O bees! Please tell me some news about Him.” O Lord of the Yadus, in this way, with completely confused mind, the suffering Yaśodā wandered about in all directions asking news of You.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mohaḥ –
kuṭumbini manas taṭe vidhuratāṃ vidhatse kathaṃ
prasāraya dṛśaṃ manāk tava sutaḥ puro vartate |
idaṃ gṛhiṇi gṛhaṃ na kuru śūnyam ity ākulaṃ
sa śocati tava prasūṃ yadu-kulendra nandaḥ pitā ||72||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Moha (vacant mind):

“Dear wife! Why are you suffering so much in your mind? Open your eyes and see. Your son is standing in front of you. Dear wife! Do not make this house empty.” O king of the Yadu dynasty! In this way, your father Nanda reveals his grief to Your mother.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha yoge siddhiḥ –
vilokya raṅga-sthala-labdha-saṅgamaṃ
vilocanābhīṣṭa-vilokanaṃ harim |
stanyair asiñcan nava-kañcukāñcalaṃ
devyaḥ kṣaṇād ānakadundubhi-priyāḥ ||73||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Seeing Kṛṣṇa for the first time:

When Vasudeva’s wives saw that Kṛṣṇa, object of their eyes’ desire, had entered the arena, for a short time their flowing breast milk dampened their new bodices.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tuṣṭir, yathā prathame –
tāḥ putram aṅkam āropya sneha-snuta-payodharāḥ |
harṣa-vihvalitātmānaḥ siṣicur netrajair jalaiḥ ||74||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Meeting Kṛṣṇa after separation, from the First Canto:

The mothers, after embracing their son, sat Him on their laps. Due to pure affection, milk sprang from their breasts. They were overwhelmed with delight, and the tears from their eyes moistened the Lord. SB 1.11.30

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā lalita-mādhave (10.14) –
nayanayoḥ stanayor api yugmataḥ
paripatadbhir asau payasāṃ jharaiḥ |
ahaha vallava-rāja-gṛheśvarī
sva-tanayaṃ praṇayād abhiṣiñcati ||75||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example, from Lalita-mādhava:

Out of affection, the wife of Nanda began to bathe her son in the water streaming from her eyes and the milk streaming from her breasts.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sthitir, yathā vidagdha-mādhave (1.19) –
ahaha kamala-gandher atra saundarya-vṛnde
vinihita-nayaneyaṃ tvan-mukhendor mukunda |
kuca-kalasa-mukhābhyām ambara-knopam ambā
tava muhur atiharṣād varṣati kṣīra-dhārām ||76||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Permanent association, from Vidagdha-mādhava:

O Mukunda! Our mother, fixing her eyes on the beauty of Your moon-like face, fragrant as a lotus, out of great jubilation continually pours streams of milk from the mouths of the two pots in the form of her breasts. In this manner, her clothing becomes soaked.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: svīkurvate rasam imaṃ nāṭya-jñā api kecana ||77||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Some literary experts accept vatsala-bhakti-rasa as one of the rasas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathāhuḥ [Sāhitya-darpaṇa 3.201] –
sphuṭaṃ camatkāritayā vatsalaṃ ca rasaṃ viduḥ |
sthāyī vatsalatāsyeha putrādy-ālambanaṃ matam ||78||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The learned recognize vatsala-rasa as one of the rasas because of its clear expression of bliss. In this rasa, the sthāyi-bhāva is vatsala-rati and the ālambana is the son or other offspring.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kiṃ ca –
apratītau hari-rateḥ prītasya syād apuṣṭatā |
preyasas tu tirobhāvo vatsalaysāsya na kṣatiḥ ||79||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Moreover:

When a dāsya or sakhya-bhakta cannot discern if the Lord has rati for himself, his prīti-rasa weakens, and preyo-rasa disappears, but even if the vatsala-bhakta cannot discern the Lord’s rati, vatsala-rasa is not affected at all.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣā rasa-trayī proktā prītādiḥ paramādbhutā |
tatra keṣucid apy asyāḥ saṅkulatvam udīryate ||80||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: What is astonishing is that all the three rasas just described—prīti-rasa, preyo-rasa and vatsala-rasa--are said to appear mixed together in some devotees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
saṅkarṣaṇasya sakhyas tu prīti-vātsalya-saṅgatam |
yudhiṣṭhirasya vātsalyaṃ prītyā sakhyena cānvitam ||81||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Balarāma’s sakhya-rasa is mixed with dāsya and vatsala-rasa. Yudhiṣṭhira’s vatsala is mixed with dāsya and sakhya.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
āhuka-prabhṛtīnāṃ tu prītir vātsalya-miśritā |
jarad-ābhīrikādīnāṃ vātsalyaṃ sakhya-miśritam ||82||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Ugrasena and others have dāsya, mixed with some vatsala. Aged gopīs have vatsala mixed with sakhya.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
mādreya-nāradādīnāṃ sakhyaṃ prītyā karambitam |
rudra-tārkṣyoddhavādīnāṃ prītiḥ sakhyena miśritā ||83||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Nakula, Sahadeva, Nārada and others have sakhya mixed with prīti (dāsya). Śiva, Garuḍa, Uddhava and others have prīti (dāsya) mixed with sakhya-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
aniruddhādi-naptèṇām evaṃ kecid babhāṣire |
evaṃ keṣucid anyeṣu vijñeyaṃ bhāva-miśraṇam ||84||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Some say that Aniruddha and other grandsons have dāsya mixed with some sakhya-rasa. In this way, the mixture of rasas should be understood in other devotees also.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ātmocitair vibhāvādyaiḥ puṣṭiṃ nītā satāṃ hṛdi |
madhurākhyo bhaved bhakti-raso ’sau madhurā ratiḥ ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When madhura-rati is nourished by suitable vibhāva and other elements in the devotees’ hearts, it is called madhura-bhakti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
nivṛttānupayogitvād durūhatvād ayaṃ rasaḥ |
rahasyatvāc ca saṃkṣipya vitatāṇgo vilikhyate ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Though this rasa has many components, it will be described in summary because it is difficult to describe, because it is very confidential, and because it is unsuitable for those who do not have the taste for madhura-rasa--since it appears similar to the rasa of mundane love,

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ālambanāḥ –
asmin ālambanaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ priyās tasya tu subhruvaḥ ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Ālambanas:

The ālambanas in this rasa are Kṛṣṇa and His dear, beautiful women.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra kṛṣṇaḥ –
tatra kṛṣṇaḥ asamānordhva-saundarya-līlā-vaidagdhya-sampadām |
āśrayatvena madhure harir ālambano mataḥ ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Kṛṣṇa:

Kṛṣṇa, who is the abode of abundant beauty, pastimes and skill in the arts which cannot be equaled or surpassed, is considered the ālambana (viṣaya).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrī-gīta-govinde (1.11) –
viśveṣām anurañjanena janayann ānandam indīvara-
śreṇī-śyāmala-komalair upanayann aṅgair anaṅgotsavam |
svacchandaṃ vraja-sundarībhir abhitaḥ praty-aṅgam āliṅgitaḥ
śṛṅgāraḥ sakhi mūrtimān iva madhau mugdho hariḥ krīḍati ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From Gīta-govinda:

O friend! Attractive Kṛṣṇa, who produces bliss in everyone within the universe by inducing their love, who produces the bliss of Cupid in the women by His soft limbs which have the color of a forest of blue lotuses, and whose every limb is embraced spontaneously by the young women of Vraja, sports in the spring season like the incarnation of madhura-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha tasya preyasyaḥ –
nava-nava-vara-mādhurī-dhurīṇāḥ
praṇaya-taraṅga-karambitāntaraṅgāḥ |
nija-ramaṇatayā hariṃ bhajantiḥ
praṇamata tāḥ paramādbhutāḥ kiśorīḥ ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The dear women:

I offer my respects to the most astonishing young women, who are endowed with the most excellent, freshest sweetness, whose hearts are mixed with waves of prema, and who adore Kṛṣṇa as their own lover.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: preyasīṣu harer āsu pravarā vārṣabhānavī ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Among these lovers of Kṛṣṇa, Rādhā, daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, is the chief.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asyā rūpaṃ –
mada-cakita-cakorī-cārutā-cora-dṛṣṭir
vadana-damita-rākārohiṇī-kānta-kīrtiḥ |
avikala-kala-dhautoddhūti-dhaureyaka-śrīr
madhurima-madhu-pātrī rājate paśya rādhā ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Her form:

Just look at Rādhā, the abode of sweet nectar, whose blissful eyes steals the beauty of a cakorī bird quivering with excitement, whose face defeats the fame of the full moon, and whose excellent beauty exceeds the luster of pure gold.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asyā ratiḥ –
narmoktau mama nirmitoru-paramānandotsavāyām api
śrotrasyānta-taṭīm api sphuṭam anādhāya sthitodyan-mukhī |
rādhā lāghavam apy anādara-girāṃ bhaṅgībhir ātanvatī
maitrī-gauravato ’py asau śata-guṇāṃ mat-prītim evādadhe ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Rādhā’s rati:

I derive a hundred times more delight from Rādhā, than from respectful friendship, even when she does not hear my jokes filled with the highest pleasure, and instead, looking skyward, ignores those jokes with clever words which indicate her contempt.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra kṛṣṇa-ratir, yathā śrī-gīta-govinde (3.1) –
kaṃsārir api saṃsāra-vāsanābaddha-śṛṅkhalām |
rādhām ādhāya hṛdaye tatyāja vraja-sundarīḥ ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Kṛṣṇa’s rati, from Gīta-govinda:

When Rādhā left the arena of the rasa dance out of pride, Kṛṣṇa gave up the other beautiful women and left the arena, thinking only of Rādhā who was bound up by chains of the highest love.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha uddīpanāḥ |
uddīpanā iha proktā muralī-nisvanādayaḥ ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Uddīpanas:

In madhura-bhakti-rasa, the sound of the flute and other similar items are the uddīpanas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā padyāvalyām (172)[65] –
guru-jana-gañjanam ayaśo
gṛha-pati-caritaṃ ca dāruṇaṃ kim api |
vismārayati samastaṃ
śiva śiva muralī murārāteḥ ||12||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From Padyāvalī:

How astonishing! The sound of Kṛṣṇa’s flute makes the gopīs forget the scolding of elders, the infamy from breaking social rules, and the terrifying treatment from their husbands.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha anubhāvāḥ –
anubhāvās tu kathitā dṛg-natekṣā-smitādayaḥ ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Anubhāvas:

The anubhāvas of madhura-rasa are such things as glancing from the corner of the eye and smiles.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā lalita-mādhave (1.14) –
kṛṣṇāpāṅga-taraṅgita-dyumaṇijā-sambheda-veṇī-kṛte
rādhāyāḥ smita-candrikā-suradhunī-pure nipīyāmṛtam |
antas toṣa-tuṣāra-samplava-lava-vyālīḍhatāpodgamāḥ
krāntvā sapta jaganti samprati vayaṃ sarvordhvam adhyāsmahe ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example, from Lalita-mādhava:

By drinking the nectar of the Gaṅgā in the form of Rādhā’s sweet smile, as it mixes at the Triveṇī junction with the waves of the Yamunā in the form of the dark tips of Kṛṣṇa’s eyes,[66] and by extinguishing the rising heat by bathing slightly in the cool water of the satisfied mind, we have surpassed the seven worlds, and now live in the topmost planet.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sāttvikāḥ, yathā padyāvalyām (181) –
kāmaṃ vapuḥ pulakitaṃ nayane dhṛtāsre
vācaḥ sa-gadgada-padāḥ sakhi kampi vakṣaḥ |
jñātaṃ mukunda-muralī-rava-mādhurī te
cetaḥ sudhāṃśu-vadane taralīkaroti ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sāttvika-bhāvas, from Padyāvalī:

O moon-faced friend! Seeing your body with hairs on end, the tears in your eyes, your choked voice and your trembling chest, I can understand that the sound of Kṛṣṇa’s flute has melted your heart.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vyabhicāriṇaḥ –
ālasyaugrye vinā sarve vijñeyā vyabhicāriṇaḥ ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Vyabhicāri-bhāvas:

In madhura-bhakti-rasa, all the vyabhicāri-bhāvas except ālasya (sloth) and augrya (ferocity) manifest.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra nirvedo, yathā padyāvalyām (221) –
mā muñca pañcaśara pañca-śarīṃ śarīre
mā siñca sāndra-makaranda-rasena vāyo |
aṅgāni tat-praṇaya-bhaṅga-vigarhitāni
nālambituṃ katham api kṣamate’dya jīvaḥ ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Nirveda (self-deprecation):

O Cupid! Do not shoot my body with your five arrows. O Vāyu! Do not wet my body with the sap of forest flowers. How can my wretched limbs which have caused a break in Kṛṣṇa’s love maintain their life?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
harṣo, yathā dāna-keli-kaumudyām (34) –
kuvalaya-yuvatīnāṃ lehayann akṣi-bhṛṅgaiḥ
kuvalaya-dala-lakṣmī-laṅgimāḥ svāṅga-bhāsaḥ |
mada-kala-kalabhendrollaṅghi-līlā-taraṅgaḥ
kavalayati dhṛtiṃ me kṣmādharāraṇya-dhūrtaḥ ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Harṣa (joy), from Dāna-keli-kaumudī:

This cheater, standing in the forest on Govardhana Hill, performing pastimes that surpass those of a young, intoxicated elephant, whose bodily effulgence causes attraction for the bee-like eyes of all the women of the earth, and whose complexion surpasses the luster of a blue lotus petal, has destroyed my self-control.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sthāyī –
sthāyī bhāvo bhavaty atra pūrvoktā madhurā ratiḥ ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Sthāyi-bhāva:

The Sthāyi-bhāva of madhura-rasa is the previously described madhura-rati.[70]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā padyāvalyām (158) –
bhrūvalli-tāṇḍava-kalā-madhurānana-śrīḥ
kaṅkelli-koraka-karambita-karṇa-pūraḥ |
ko ’yaṃ navīna-nikaṣopala-tulya-veṣo
vaṃśīraveṇa sakhi mām avaśīkaroti ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From Padyāvalī:

O friend! Who is this person wearing cloth the color of a gold streak on a touchstone, whose face is sweet with dancing eyebrows, whose ears are decorated with aśoka buds, who has brought me under His control by the sound of His flute?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rādhā-mādhavayor eva kvāpi bhāvaiḥ kadāpy asau |
sajātīya-vijātīyair naiva vicchidyate ratiḥ ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: That love of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa is never at any time or in any circumstance covered by other similar relationships or by different sthāyi-bhāvas.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
ito dūre rājñī sphurati parito mitra-paṭalī
dṛśor agre candrāvalir upari śailasya danujaḥ |
asavye rādhāyāḥ kusumita-latā saṃvṛta-tanau dṛg-
anta-śrīr lolā taḍid iva mukundasya valate ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

At a short distance stands the queen of Vraja, and all around are Kṛṣṇa’s friends. Candrāvalī is standing right in front of Him, and Arisṭāsura is standing at the edge of Vraja on stony soil. But Kṛṣṇa’s restless glance falls only upon the form of Rādhā, covered by flowering creepers.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ghorā khaṇḍita-śaṅkhacūḍam ajiraṃ rundhe śivā tāmasī
brahmiṣṭha-śvasanaḥ śama-stuti-kathā prāleyam āsiñcati |
agre rāmaḥ sudhā-rucir vijayate kṛṣṇa-pramodocitaṃ
rādhāyās tad api praphullam abhajan mlāniṃ na bhāvāmbujam ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: On one side lay dead Śaṅkhacūḍa, whose body was like an arena for pastimes, surrounded by frightful, ferocious, ignorant jackals. In another place, the wind, like a group of sages situated in brahman realization, recited soothing praises, cool as falling snow. In front stood Balarāma, glowing like the full moon. But the lotus of Rādhā’s love suitable for giving joy to Kṛṣṇa did not fade, but rather sprang into full bloom.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: sa vipralambha-sambhoga-bhedena dvi-vidho mataḥ ||24||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Madhura-bhakti-rasa has two types: vipralambha (in separation) and sambhoga (in union).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra vipralambhaḥ –
sa pūrva-rāgo mānaś ca pravāsādi-mayas tathā |
vipralambho bahu-vidho vidvadbhir iha kathyate ||25||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wise describe many types of vipralambha, such as pūrva-rāga, māna and pravāsa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra pūrva-rāgaḥ –
prāg-asaṅgatayor bhāvaḥ pūrva-rāgo bhaved dvayoḥ ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Pūrva-rāga:

The love in separation of the two lovers before they ever meet each other is called pūrva-rāga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā padyāvalyām (181) –
akasmād ekasmin pathi sakhi mayā yāmuna-taṭaṃ
vrajantyā dṛṣṭo yo nava-jaladhara-śyāmala-tanuḥ |
sa dṛg-bhaṅgyā kiṃ vākuruta na hi jāne tata idaṃ
mano me vyālolaṃ kvacana gṛha-kṛtyo na lagate ||27||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Padyāvalī:

One day while going to the bank of the Yamunā, I suddenly saw one man with the complexion of a new rain cloud. I do not know what He did by moving His eyes playfully, but since then my heart has become unstable, and I no longer partake of household duties.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrī-daśame –
yathā vinidrā yac cittā rukmiṇī kamalekṣaṇā |
tathāham api tac-citto nidrāṃ ca na labhe niśi |
vedāhaṃ rukmiṇyā dveṣān mamodvāho nivāritaḥ ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from the Tenth Canto:

Just as Rukmiṇī’s mind is fixed on Me, My mind is fixed on her. I can’t even sleep at night. I know that Rukmī, out of envy, has forbidden our marriage. SB 10.53.2

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha mānaḥ |
mānaḥ prasiddha evātra ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Māna, which is described in the example following, is well known, (and thus need not be explained.)[74]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā śrī-gīta-govinde (2.1) –
viharati vane rādhā sādhāraṇa-praṇaye harau
vigalita-nijotkarṣād īṛsyā-vaśena gatānyataḥ |
kvacid api latā-kuñje guñjan-madhu-vrata-maṇḍalī-
mukhara-nikhare līnā dīnāpy uvāca rahaḥ sakhīm ||30||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Gīta-govinda:

When Kṛṣṇa was sporting with all the gopīs in the forest, treating them all with equal affection, Rādhā left that place in anger because of His slighting her superior position. Later, while hiding in a grove of creepers while bees were buzzing overhead, in a grief-stricken state, she spoke to a friend in private.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha pravāsaḥ
pravāsaḥ saṅga-vicyutiḥ ||31||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Pravāsa refers to separation after meeting.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā padyāvalyām (350) –
hastodare vinihitaika-kapola-pāler
aśrānta-locana-jala-snapitānanāyāḥ |
prasthāna-maṅgala-dināvadhi mādhavasya
nidrā-lavo ’pi kuta eva saroruhākṣyāḥ ||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Padyāvalī:

Since the auspicious day when Kṛṣṇa departed for Mathurā, the lotus-faced Rādhā, putting her left hand on her left cheek, soaked her face with unremitting tears. How could she sleep at all?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā prahlāda-saṃhitāyām uddhava-vākyam –
bhagavān api govindaḥ kandarpa-śara-pīḍitaḥ |
na bhuṅkte na svapiti ca cintayan vo hy ahar-niśam ||33||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From Prahlāda-saṃhitā, the words of Uddhava:

Lord Govinda also, being afflicted by the arrows of Cupid, thinking of you alone day and night, cannot eat or sleep.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha sambhogaḥ –
dvayor militayor bhogaḥ sambhoga iti kīrtyate ||34||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The enjoyment experienced when the two lovers meet is called sambhoga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā padyāvalyām (199) –
paramānurāga-parayātha rādhayā
parirambha-kauśala-vikāśi-bhāvayā
sa tayā saha smara-sabhājanotsavaṃ
niravāhayac chikhi-śikhaṇḍa-śekharaḥ ||35||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example from Padyāvalī:

Kṛṣṇa, with peacock feather on His head, executed the festival of Cupid with Rādhā, who was endowed with the highest attachment for Kṛṣṇa, and who expressed her love with skilful embraces.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śrīmad-bhāgavatādy-arha-śāstra-darśitayā dṛśā |
iyam āviṣkṛtā mukhya-pañca-bhakti-rasā mayā ||36||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: I have described the five principle types of bhakti-rasa according to the knowledge given in suitable scriptures such as Bhāgavatam.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
gopāla-rūpa-śobhāṃ dadhad api raghunātha-bhāva-vistārī |
tuṣyatu sanātanātmā paścima-bhāge rasāmbu-nidheḥ ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
May the eternal soul who has the beautiful form of a cowherd boy and who spread the bhāvas to Rāma and other forms be pleased with this western section of the ocean of nectar.

May Sanātana who nourishes the bhāva of Gopāla Bhaṭṭa and distributes the bhāva of Raghunātha dāsa be pleased with this western section!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhakti-bhareṇa prītiṃ kalayann urarīkṛta-vrajāsaṅgaḥ |
tanutāṃ sanātanātmā bhagavān mayi sarvadā tuṣṭim ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
May the Lord with eternal form who accepts Vraja as His eternal home, being merciful out of great affection for the devotee, be satisfied with me.

May the person called Sanātana Gosvāmī who, having great joy because of his devotion, was attached to living in the land of Vraja, be pleased with me.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
rasāmṛtābdher bhāge ’tra turīye tūttārābhidhe |
rasaḥ sapta-vidho gauṇo maitrī-vaira-sthitir mithaḥ ||2||
rasābhāsāś ca tenātra laharyo nava kīrtitāḥ |
prāg atrāniyatādhārāḥ kadācit kvāpy uditvarāḥ ||3||
gauṇā bhakti-rasāḥ sapta lekhyā hāsyādayaḥ kramāt ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the fourth division of the sweet ocean of rasa, called the northern section, the seven secondary rasas, as well as mutually friendly and antagonistic rasas and, as well, rasābhāsa will be discussed in nine waves (chapters). Previously it was said that these secondary rasas do not have a fixed ālambana (unlike the primary rasas) and appear occasionally in any of the persons with the primary rasas. The seven secondary rasas will be described in order starting with hāsya.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhaktānāṃ pañcadhoktānām eṣāṃ madhyata eva hi |
kvāpy ekaḥ kvāpy anekaś ca gauṇeṣv ālambano mataḥ ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: These secondary rasas appear only within the five types of persons possessing the five types of sthāyi-bhāva. Sometimes (in a particular situation) one among these five types of persons may experience a particular secondary rasa, and at other times all of these types may experience a particular secondary rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra hāsya-bhakti-rasaḥ –
vakṣyamāṇair vibhāvādyaiḥ puṣṭiṃ hāsa-ratir gatā |
hāsya-bhakti-raso nāma budhair eṣa nigadyate ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When hāsa-rati is nourished by the presence of vibhāva and other elements, it is called hāsya-bhakti-rasa by the wise.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
asminn ālambanaḥ kṛṣṇas tathānyo ’pi tad-anvayī |
vṛddhāḥ śiśu-mukhāḥ prāyaḥ proktā dhīrais tad-āśrayāḥ |
vibhāvanādi-vaiśiṣṭhyāt pravarāś ca kvacin matāḥ ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In hāsya-rasa, Kṛṣṇa and persons performing humorous actions directed to Kṛṣṇa are the ālambana. The wise say that the elders and the chief young children are usually the āśrayas of hāsya-rasa. In special circumstances, the chief personalities become the āśraya of this rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra kṛṣṇo, yathā –
yāsyāmy asya na bhīṣaṇasya savidhaṃ jīrṇasya śīrṇākṛter
mātar neṣyati māṃ pidhāya kapaṭād ādhārikāyām asau |
ity uktvā cakitākṣam adbhuta-śiśāv udvīkṣyamāṇe harau
hāsyaṃ tasya niruddhato ’py atitarāṃ vyaktaṃ tadāsīn muneḥ ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Kṛṣṇa as ālambana:

“O mother! I will not go towards the frightful, old withered person! He will craftily put Me in his begging bag.” When Nārada saw baby Kṛṣṇa with frightened eyes say this, though he tried to suppress his laughter, he could not do so.[77]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha tad-anvayī –
yac ceṣṭā kṛṣṇa-viṣayā proktaḥ so ’tra tad-anvayī ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: A person whose action (of laughter) is directed towards Kṛṣṇa is called tad-anvayī (person related to Kṛṣṇa).[78]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
dadāmi dadhi-phāṇitaṃ vivṛṇu vaktram ity agrato
niśamya jaratī-giraṃ vivṛta-komalauṣṭhe sthite |
tayā kusumam arpitaṃ navam avetya bhugnānane
harau jahasur uddhuraṃ kim api suṣṭhu goṣṭhārbhakāḥ ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Devotee as ālambana:

“Open Your mouth. I will give you some yogurt mixed with sugar.” Hearing the words of the old woman, when Kṛṣṇa opened His tender lips, she put a fresh flower in His mouth. Tasting the bitterness, He puckered His mouth. The young cowherd boys laughed loudly.[79]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
asya prekṣya karaṃ śiśor munipate śyāmasya me kathyatāṃ
tathyaṃ hanta cirāyur eṣa bhavitā kiṃ dhenu-koṭīśvaraḥ |
ity ukte bhagavan mayādya paritaś cīreṇa kiṃ cāruṇā
drāg āvirbhavad-uddhura-smitam idaṃ vaktraṃ tvayā rudhyate ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O master of the sages! When I asked you to look at the hand of my dark-complexioned child, and to tell me if He will be the lord of a million cows and have a long life, why did you suddenly hide your laughing face completely with your beautiful cloth?[80]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
uddīpanā hares tādṛg-vāg-veṣa-caritādayaḥ |
anubhāvās tu nāsauṣṭha-gaṇḍa-niṣpandanādayaḥ ||12||
harṣālasyāvahitthādyā vijñeyā vyabhicāriṇaḥ |
sā hāsa-ratir evātra sthāyi-bhāvatayoditā ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The uddīpanas for hāsya-rasa are the humorous words, clothing and behavior of Kṛṣṇa. The anubhāvas are quivering of the nose, lips and cheeks. The vyabhicāri-bhāvas are harṣa (jubilation), ālasya (sloth), and avahitthā (concealing oneself). The sthāyi-bhāva is hāsa-rati.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ṣoḍhā hāsa-ratiḥ syāt smita-hasite vihasitāvahasite ca |
apahasitātihasitake jyeṣṭhādīnāṃ kramād dve dve ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are six types of hāsya-rati: smita, hasita, vihasa, avahasita, apahasita, and atihasita. The first two appear in superiors (such as sages or confidential friends), the second two appear in persons of medium status (messengers or servants) and the third two appear in those of lowest status (such as children).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vibhāvanādi-vaicitryād uttamasyāpi kutracit |
bhaved vihasitādyaṃ ca bhāvajñair iti bhaṇyate ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wise say that when there are special attractions in the vibhāva and other elements, the lower types of hāsya such as vihasa appear in the superior person as well.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra smitam –
smitaṃ tv alakṣya-daśanaṃ netra-gaṇḍa-vikāśa-kṛt ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the teeth are not seen and the eyes and cheeks become joyful it is called smita (slight smile).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kva yāmi jaratī khalā dadhi-haraṃ didhīrṣanty asau
pradhāvati javena māṃ subala maṅkṣu rakṣāṃ kuru |
iti skhalad-udīrite dravati kāndiśīke harau
vikasvara-mukhāmbujaṃ kulam abhūn munīnāṃ divi ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

“O strong brother! Because I stole some yogurt, the wicked old lady is chasing Me to tie Me up. Where can I go? Please protect Me quickly!” Saying these words to His elder brother with choked voice, Kṛṣṇa fled in fear. Seeing this, the faces of the sages in the sky blossomed.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
hasitam –
tad eva dara-saṃlakṣya-dantāgraṃ hasitaṃ bhavet ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When teeth are slightly visible, it is called hasita (smile).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
mad-vaśena puraḥ-sthito harir asau putro ’ham evāsmi te
paśyety acyuta-jalpa-viśvasitayā saṃrambha-rajyad-dṛśā |
mām eti skhalad-akṣare jaṭilayā vyākruśya niṣkāsite
putre prāṅgataḥ sakhī-kulam abhūd dantāṃśu-dhautādharam ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

“O mother! I am your son Abhimanyu. Kṛṣṇa is coming here wearing my clothing.” Believing Kṛṣṇa’s words, Jaṭilā, her eyes red with anger, began shouting and driving her real son from the yard, as he tried to say “Mother! Mother!” The lips of Rādhā’s friends became gleaming white with the luster of their teeth.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vihasitam –
sa-svanaṃ dṛṣṭa-daśanaṃ bhaved vihasitaṃ tu tat ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When laughing sounds are heard and the teeth are visible it is called vihasita (audible laughing).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
muṣāṇa dadhi meduraṃ viphalam antarā śaṅkase
sa-niḥśvasita-ḍambaraṃ jaṭilayātra nidrāyate |
iti bruvati keśave prakaṭa-śīrṇa-danta-sthalaṃ
kṛtaṃ hasitam utsvanaṃ kapaṭa-suptayā vṛddhayā ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

“My friends! Steal the thick yogurt. Why are you uselessly showing fear in this house? Jaṭilā is sleeping with loud breathing.” When Kṛṣṇa said this, the old lady, only pretending to be sleeping, laughed audibly, showing her worn teeth.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
avahasitam –
tac cāvahasitaṃ phulla-nāsaṃ kuñcita-locanam ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the nose swells and eyes squint, it is called avahasita (intense laughter).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
lagnas te nitarāṃ dṛśor api yuge kiṃ dhātu-rāgo ghanaḥ
prātaḥ putra balasya vā kim asitaṃ vāsas tvayāṅge dhṛtam |
ity ākarṇya puro vrajeśa-gṛhiṇī-vācaṃ sphuran-nāsikā
dūtī saṅkucad-īkṣaṇāvahasitaṃ jātā na roddhuṃ kṣamā ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

“O son! How have Your two eyes become tainted with strong colors early in the morning? Why are you wearing Baladeva’s blue cloth?” Hearing these words of Yaśodā, servants standing there could not suppress their laughter. Their eyes squinted and their noses swelled.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
apahasitam –
tac cāpahasitaṃ sāśru-locanaṃ kampitāṃsakam ||24||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Laughter with tears in the eyes and shaking shoulders is called apahasita (uncontrollable laughter).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
udasraṃ devarṣir divi dara-taraṅgad-bhuja-śirā
yad abhrāṇy uddaṇḍo daśana-rucibhiḥ pāṇḍarayati |
sphuṭaṃ brahmādīnāṃ naṭayitari divye vraja-śiśau
jaratyāḥ prastobhān naṭati tad anaiṣīd dṛśam asau ||25||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

Seeing the effulgent child of Vraja Kṛṣṇa, who makes the devatās dance, dancing to the verses of an old lady, Nārada, with tears in his eyes and shaking shoulders, began to dance in an extraordinary fashion, and turned all the clouds white with his flashing white teeth.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atihasitam –
sahasta-tālaṃ kṣiptāṅgaṃ tac cātihasitaṃ viduḥ ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Laughter combined with clapping the hands and bending the body is called atihasita (extreme laughter).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
vṛddhe tvaṃ valitānanāsi valibhiḥ prekṣya suyogyām atas
tvām udvoḍhum asau vale-mukha-varo māṃ sādhayaty utsukaḥ |
ābhir vipluta-dhīr vṛṇe na hi paraṃ tvatto bali-dhvaṃsanād
ity uccair mukharā-girā vijahasuḥ sottālikā bālikāḥ ||27||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

“Old lady! Your skin is all wrinkled. You look like a monkey! The king of the monkeys (with wrinkled faces) has sent me to arrange for your marriage to a suitable groom.” Hearing this, the old woman said, “By all these wrinkles, I have lost my intelligence. Thus, I will not accept anyone except you as a groom, since you are the destroyer of demons (and the destroyer of wrinkles).” Hearing Mukharā’s words, all the young boys began to clap their hands and laugh loudly.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yasya hāsaḥ sa cet kvāpi sākṣān naiva nibadhyate |
tathāpy eṣa vibhāvādi-sāmarthyād upalabhyate ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If the person instigating hāsya is not directly discernable, that person can be understood by the similarity of the vibhāva and the other elements with other cases.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
śimbī-lambi-kucāsi dardura-vadhū-vispardhi nāsākṛtis
tvaṃ jīryad-duli-dṛṣṭir oṣṭha-tulitāṅgārā mṛdaṅgodarī |
kā tvattaḥ kuṭile parāsti jaṭilā-putri kṣitau sundarī
puṇyena vraja-subhruvāṃ tava dhṛtiṃ hartuṃ na vaṃśī kṣamā ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O Kuṭilā (sister of Abhimanyu)! Your breasts are long like beans! Your nostrils are like those of a female frog! Your eyes are like old turtles. Your lips are like coals. Your stomach is like a drum. Who is there in this world more beautiful than you? By the strength of your good works, only you, among all the beautiful women of Vraja, have withstood the attraction of the flute.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
eṣa hāsya-rasas tatra kaiśikī-vṛtti-vistṛtau |
śṛṅgārādi-rasodbhedo bahudhaiva prapañcitaḥ ||30||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: This hāsya-rasa, which manifests in madhura and other rasas, has been described in detail with many forms in the works of Bharata Muni and the work Nātāka-candrikā,[81] in the section dealing with kaiśikī vṛtti (dramatic form suitable for love).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
ātmocitair vibhāvādyaiḥ svādyatvaṃ bhakta-cetasi |
sā vismaya-ratir nītādbhuta-bhakti-raso bhavet ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When vismaya-rati becomes pleasurable in the devotee’s heart by suitable vibhāvas and other elements it is called adbhuta-bhakti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhaktaḥ sarva-vidho ’py atra ghaṭate vismayāśrayaḥ |
lokottara-kriyā-hetur viṣayas tatra keśavaḥ ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: All types of devotees are the ālambana for the vismaya-rati of adbhuta-rasa, and Kṛṣṇa because of His superhuman actions, is the viṣaya.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tasya ceṣṭā-viśeṣādyās tasminn uddīpanā matāḥ |
kriyās tu netra-vistāra-stambhāśru-pulakādayaḥ ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Particular actions of Kṛṣṇa are the uddīpanas. Opening the eyes wide is the anubhāva. Paralysis, tears, hair standing on end are the sāttvika-bhāvas.[82]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
āvega-harṣa-jāḍyādyās tatra syur vyabhicāriṇaḥ |
sthāyī syād vismaya-ratiḥ sā lokottara-karmataḥ |
sākṣād anumitaṃ ceti tac ca dvividham ucyate ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Āvega, harṣa, jāḍyam etc. are the vyabhicāri-bhāvas. Vismaya-rati caused by Kṛṣṇa’s superhuman actions is the sthāyi-bhāva. There are two types of superhuman actions: direct and inferred.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra sākṣāt, yathā –
sākṣād aindriyakaṃ dṛṣṭa-śruta-saṅkīrtitādikam ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the superhuman action is perceived by the eye, ear, mouth or other senses, it is called direct.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra dṛṣṭaṃ, yathā –
ekam eva vividhodyama-bhājaṃ
mandireṣu yugapan nikhileṣu |
dvārakām abhi samīksya mukundaṃ
spandanojjhita-tanur munir āsīt ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example of direct perception of wonder by seeing:

When Nārada saw Kṛṣṇa simultaneously performing various actions with one body in each of the queens’ palaces in Dvārakā, his body began to quiver.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathoktaṃ śrī-daśame–
citraṃ bataitad ekena vapuṣā yugapat pṛthak |
gṛheṣu dvy-aṣṭa-sāhasraṃ striya eka udāvahat ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
This is described in the Tenth Canto:

It is quite amazing that in a single body Lord Kṛṣṇa simultaneously married sixteen thousand women, each in a separate palace. SB 10.69.2

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
kva stanya-gandhi-vadanendur asau śiśus te
govardhanaḥ śikhara-ruddha-ghanaḥ kva cāyam |
bhoḥ paśya savya-kara-kandukitācalendraḥ
khelann iva sphurati hanta kim indra-jālam ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

O Yaśodā! See here your little son with the moon-like face slightly smelling of milk! And see here Govardhana, whose summit pierces the clouds! See, with His left hand, He holds the king of mountains if it were just play. Is this some magic?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śrutaṃ, yathā –
yāny akṣipan praharaṇāni bhaṭāḥ sa devaḥ
pratyekam acchinad amūni śara-trayeṇa |
ity ākalayya yudhi kaṃsa-ripoḥ prabhāvaṃ
sphārekṣaṇaḥ kṣitipatiḥ pulakī tadāsīt ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From hearing about Kṛṣṇa’s superhuman acts:

Kṛṣṇa with only three arrows cut down all the arrows fired by eleven akṣauhiṇī of troops under Narakāsura. When Parīkṣit heard of the prowess of Kṛṣṇa in battle, his eyes opened wide and his hairs stood on end.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
saṅkīrtitaṃ, yathā –
ḍimbāḥ svarṇa-nibhāmbarā ghana-ruco jātāś caturbāhavo
vatsāś ceti vadan kṛto ’smi vivaśaḥ stambha-śriyā paśyata |
āścaryaṃ kathayāmi vaḥ śṛṇuta bhoḥ pratyekam ekaikaśaḥ
stūyante jagad-aṇḍavadbhir abhitas te hanta padmāsanaiḥ ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
By speaking about Kṛṣṇa’s superhuman acts:

All the boys and calves has four arms. They were dark in complexion like clouds, and wore yellow cloth. See, speaking in this way, I have lost control and become stunned. I will tell you something astonishing. Please listen. Each of the masters of the universes on lotus seats, all the Lord Brahmās, were praising every one of these four-armed forms.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
anumitaṃ, yathā –
unmīlya vraja-śiśavo dṛśaṃ purastād
bhāṇḍīraṃ punar atulya vilokayantaḥ |
sātmānaṃ paśu-paṭalīṃ ca tatra dāvād
unmuktāṃ manasi camatkriyām avāpuḥ ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
By inference of Kṛṣna’s superhuman acts:

When all the children opened their eyes, they were astonished to see in front of them the incomparable banyan tree called Bhāṇḍīra, and that they and all the cows had been rescued from the forest fire.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
apriyādeḥ kriyā kuryān nālaukiky api vismayam |
asādhāraṇy api manāk karoty eva priyasya sā ||12||

priyāt priyasya kim uta sarva-lokottarottarā |
ity atra vismaye proktā raty-anugraha-mādhurī ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Therefore, it goes without saying that if the dearest person performs the most extraordinary actions, it will certainly produce astonishment. Thus, the sweetness produced from rati (through a primary rasa of love) has also been mentioned in this discussion of vismaya-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
saivotsāha-ratiḥ sthāyī vibhāvādyair nijocitaḥ |
ānīyamānā svādyatvaṃ vīra-bhakti-raso bhavet ||1||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When utsāha-rati attains an enjoyable nature by suitable vibhāvas and other elements, it is called vīra-bhakti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yuddha-dāna-dayā-dharmaiś caturdhā-vīra ucyate |
ālambana iha prokta eṣa eva caturvidhaḥ ||2||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are four types of vīra or valiant persons: yuddha-vīra, dāna-vīra, dayā-vīra and dharma-vīra. These devotees are the ālambana for vīra-bhakti-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: utsāhas tv eṣa bhaktānāṃ sarveṣām eva sambhavet ||3||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: It is possible for utsāha-rati to appear in all types of devotees.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra yuddha-vīraḥ –
paritoṣāya kṛṣṇasya dadhad utsāham āhave |
sakhā bandhu-viśeṣo vā yuddha-vīra ihocyate ||4||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Yuddha-vīra:

Special friends endowed with fortitude in fighting for satisfying Kṛṣṇa are called yuddha-vīra.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pratiyoddhā mukundo vā tasmin vā prekṣake sthite |
tadīyecchāveśenātra bhaved anyaḥ suhṛd-varaḥ ||5||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In this rasa, the friends fight against Kṛṣṇa, or fight against His best friends in Kṛṣṇa’s presence, according to Kṛṣṇa’s desire.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra kṛṣṇo, yathā –
aparājita-māninaṃ haṭhāc
caṭulaṃ tvām abhibhūya mādhava |
dhinuyām adhunā suhṛd-gaṇaṃ
yadi na tvaṃ samarāt parāñcasi ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Fighting with Kṛṣṇa:

O Mādhava! You are very unsteady, but You think that no one can beat You. If You do not flee from the fight, I will now defeat You and please Your friends!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā –
saṃrambha-prakaṭīkṛta-pratibhaṭārambha-śriyoḥ sādbhutaṃ
kālindī-puline vayasya-nikarair ālokyamānas tadā |
avyutthāpita-sakhyayor api varāhaṅkāra-visphūrjitaḥ
śrīdāmnaś ca bake-dviṣaś ca samarāṭopaḥ paṭīyān abhūt ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

Śrīdāma and Kṛṣṇa who were the very best of friends displayed the splendor of fighting with anger on the bank of the Yamunā. This intense pride in fighting with increased desire to win was seen by all their friends with astonishment.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
suhṛd-varo, yathā –
sakhi-prakara-mārgaṇān agaṇitān kṣipan sarvatas
tathādya laguḍaṃ kramād bhramayati sma dāmā kṛtī |
amaṃsta racita-stutir vrajapates tanujo ’py amuṃ
samṛddha-pulako yathā laguḍa-pañjarāntaḥ-sthitam ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Fighting with His best friends:

Skilful Śrīdāma stopped the countless arrows with leather tips fired by all his friends by whirling his stick around. The son of Nanda, with hairs standing on end, praised him, because it appeared that Śrīdāma was enclosed in a cage made by the whirling stick.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
prāyaḥ prakṛta-śūrāṇāṃ sva-pakṣair api karhicit |
yuddha-keli-samutsāho jāyate paramādbhutaḥ ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Sometimes there are astonishing, intense mock battles between two natural fighters on the same side.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathā ca hari-vaṃśe –
tathā gāṇḍīva-dhanvānaṃ vikrīḍan madhusūdanaḥ |
jigāya bharata-śreṣṭhaṃ kuntyāḥ pramukhato vibhuḥ || iti | ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From Hari-vaṃśa:

The Lord Madhusūdana in front of Kuntī defeated Arjuna, wielder of the Gāṇḍīva, the best of the Bharata dynasty, in mock battle.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
katthitāsphoṭa-vispardhā-vikramāstra-grahādayaḥ |
pratiyodha-sthitāḥ santo bhavanty uddīpanā iha ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The uddīpanas of yuddha-vīra-rasa are boasting, striking the arms in challenge, rivalry, showing strength, taking up weapons, and being aroused by the words of the opponent.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra katthitam –
piṇḍīśūras tvam iha subalaṃ kaitavenābalāṅgaṃ
jitvā dāmodara yudhi vṛthā mā kṛthāḥ katthitāni |
mādyann eṣa tvad-alaghu-bhujāsarpa-darpāpahārī
mandradhvāno naṭati nikaṭe stokakṛṣṇaḥ kalāpī ||12||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Boasting as uddīpana:

O Dāmodara! You are expert only at eating. Do not boast, after winning over weak Subala by cheating. This peacock in the form of Stoka-kṛṣṇa, destroyer of the snake of Your long arms, is dancing madly nearby while making loud sounds.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
katthitādyāḥ sva-saṃsthāś ced anubhāvāḥ prakīrtitāḥ |
tathaivāhopuruṣikā kṣveḍitākrośa-valganam ||13||
asahāye ’pi yuddhecchā samarād apalāyanam |
bhītābhaya-pradānādyā vijñeyāś cāpare budhaiḥ ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If the above actions such as boasting are only for one’s own awareness they are anubhāvas. The wise say the other anubhāvas are self-pride, roaring like a lion, proud words, bounding about, enthusiasm to fight even without assistance, not fleeing from battle, and making the fearful fearless.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra katthitam, yathā –
protsāhayasyatitarāṃ kim ivāgraheṇa
māṃ keśisūdana vidann api bhadrasenam |
yoddhuṃ balena samam atra sudurbalena
divyārgalā pratibhaṭas trapate bhujo me ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Boasting as anubhāva:

O killer of Keśi! You know that I am Bhadrasena. Why are you eager to fight with weak Baladeva? My arm rivaling a shining door bolt is ashamed of this.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
āhopuruṣikā, yathā –
dhṛtāṭope gopeśvara-jaladhi-candre parikaraṃ
nibadhnaty ullāsād bhuja-samara-caryā-samucitam |
sa-romāñcaṃ kṣveḍā-niviḍa-mukha-bimbasya naṭataḥ
sudāmnaḥ sotkaṇṭhaṃ jayati muhur āhopuruṣikā ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Show of self-pride:

O lord of the cowherds! All glories to Sudāma, who, displaying great self-confidence, and roaring like a lion, danced around till his hairs stood on end and his eagerness of fighting increased, when the proud Kṛṣṇa tightened His belt with great show.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
catuṣṭaye ’pi vīrāṇāṃ nikhilā eva sāttvikāḥ |
garvāvega-dhṛti-vrīḍā-mati-harṣāvahitthikāḥ |
amarṣotsukatāsūyā-smṛty-ādyā vyabhicāriṇaḥ ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: All the four types of heroes show all the sāttvika-bhāvas and all the vyabhicāri-bhāvas such as garva, āvega, dhṛti, vrīḍā, mati, harṣa, avahitthā, amarṣa, utsukatā, asūyā and smṛti.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yuddhotsāha-ratis tasmin sthāyi-bhāvatayoditā |
yā svaśakti-sahāyādyair āhāryā sahajāpi vā |
jigīṣā stheyasī yuddhe sā yuddhotsāha īryate ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The steady desire to win in fighting, either acquired or natural, induced either by one’s own strength or with encouragement of others’ help, which arises when yuddhotsāha-rati becomes the sthāyi-bhāva, is called yuddhotsāha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra sva-śaktyā āhāryotsāha-ratir, yathā –
sva-tāta-śiṣṭyā sphuṭam apy anicchann
āhūyamānaḥ puruṣottamena |
sa stoka-kṛṣṇo dhṛta-yuddha-tṛṣṇaḥ
prodyamya daṇḍaṃ bhramayāñcakāra ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Regaining desire to fight on one’s own:

When his father discouraged him, Stoka-kṛṣṇa was not willing to fight with Kṛṣṇa. However, when challenged by Kṛṣṇa, he became eager to fight. He took up his stick and began to whirl it around.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sva-śaktyā sahajotsāha-ratir, yathā –
śukākāraṃ prekṣya me bāhu-daṇḍaṃ
mā tvaṃ bhaiṣīḥ kṣudra re bhadrasena |
helārambheṇādya nirjitya rāmaṃ
śrīdāmāhaṃ kṛṣṇam evāhvayeya ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Natural desire to fight encouraged only by oneself:

O wimpy Bhadrasena! I am Śrīdāma. Do not be afraid on seeing my arm thick like an elephant’s trunk. Today I will defeat Balarāma in a mock battle, and then challenge Kṛṣṇa to fight.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā va –
balasya balino balāt suhṛd-anīkam āloḍayan
payodhim iva mandaraḥ kṛta-mukunda-pakṣa-grahaḥ |
janaṃ vikaṭa-garjitair vadhirayan sa dhīra-svaro
hareḥ pramadam ekakaḥ samiti bhadraseno vyadhāt ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Another example:

The deep voiced Bhadrasena, on Kṛṣṇa’s side, deafening the friends on strong Balarāma’s side by his harsh roaring, churned them all by himself like the Mandara Mountain churning the ocean. This gave great pleasure to Kṛṣṇa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sahāyenāhāryotsāha-ratir, yathā –
mayi valgati bhīma-vikrame
bhaja bhaṅgaṃ na hi saṅgarāditaḥ |
iti mitra-girā varūthapaḥ
sa-virūpaṃ vibruvan hariṃ yayau ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Regaining enthusiasm for battle by other person’s assistance:

“Since I am bursting with fierce prowess, do not accept defeat in this battle.” Hearing these words from a friend, Varūthapa, uttering unnatural sounds, charged towards the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sahāyena sahajotsāha-ratir, yathā –
saṅgrāma-kāmuka-bhujaḥ svayam eva kāmaṃ
dāmodarasya vijayāya kṛtī sudāmā |
sāhāyyam atra subalaḥ kurute balī cej
jāto maṇiḥ sujaṭito vara-hāṭakena ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Natural enthusiasm for battle, with additional encouragement:

Expert Sudāma with arms eager for battle had sufficient strength to conquer Kṛṣṇa in combat. If he were to take the help of strong Subala, he would be like a jewel set in gold.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
suhṛd eva pratibhaṭo vīre kṛṣṇasya na tv ariḥ |
sa bhakta-kṣobha-kāritvād raudre tv ālambano rase |
rāgābhāvo dṛg-ādīnāṃ raudrād asya vibhedakaḥ ||24||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In vīra-bhakti-rasa the friends are the opponents of Kṛṣṇa. They are not the opponents of enemies. When there is an enemy giving agitation to the devotee, the devotee becomes the ālambana of raudra-rasa. In raudra-rasa the eyes become red etc. This is absent in vīra-rasa. This is the difference between vīra and raudra-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha dānavīraḥ –
dvi-vidho dāna-vīraḥ syād ekas tatra bahu-pradaḥ |
upasthita-durāpārtha-tyāgī cāpara ucyate ||25||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are two types of dāna-vīras: the person who gives in plenty (bahu-prada) and the renounced person who does not want to accept what is offered by the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra bahu-pradaḥ –
sahasā dīyate yena svayaṃ sarvasvam apy uta |
dāmodarasya saukhyāya procyate sa bahu-pradaḥ ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who will give everything immediately for Kṛṣṇa’s pleasure is called the bahu-prada (generous giver).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sampradānasya vīkṣādyā asminn uddīpanā matāḥ |
vāñchitādhika-dātṛtvaṃ smita-pūrvābhibhāṣaṇam ||27||
sthairya-dākṣiṇya-dhairyādyā anubhāvā ihoditāḥ |
vitarkautsukya-harṣādyā vijñeyā vyabhicāriṇaḥ ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The uddīpana for the dāna-vīra is seeing a person worthy of charity. The anubhāvas are giving more than asked, talking with a slight smile, steadiness, kindness, and patience etc. The vyabhicāri-bhāvas are vitarka, autsukya and harṣa and others.[86]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dānotsāha-ratis tv atra sthāyi-bhāvatayoditā |
pragāḍhā stheyasī ditsā dānotsāha itīryate ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the dāna-vīra, dānotsāha-rati is the sthāyi-bhāva. A very steady desire to give is called dānotsāha.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dvidhā bahu-prado ’py eṣa vidvadbhir iha kathyate |
syād ābhyudayikas tv ekaḥ paras tat-sampradānakaḥ ||30||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There are two types of plentiful givers: ābhyudayika (giving on auspicious occasions) and kṛṣṇa-sampradānaka (giving in surrender to Kṛṣṇa).

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ābhyudāyikaḥ –
kṛṣṇasyābhyudayārthaṃ tu yena sarvasvam arpyate |
arthibhyo brāhmaṇādibhyaḥ sa ābhyudāyiko bhavet ||31||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who gives everything to the requesters such as brāhmaṇas for the sake of Kṛṣṇa is called the ābhyudāyika-dāna-vīra.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
vrajapatir iha sūnor jātakārthaṃ tathāsau
vyatarad amala-cetāḥ sañcayaṃ naicikīnām |
pṛthur api nṛga-kīrtiḥ sāmprataṃ saṃvṛtāsīd
iti nijagadur uccair bhūsurā yena tṛptāḥ ||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
An example:

When the pure hearted Nanda gave all the best cows to the brāhamaṇas on the occasion of cerebrating Kṛṣṇa’s birth, the surrounding brāhmaṇas were so satisfied that they exclaimed loudly that it eclipsed the charity of King Nṛga.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha tat-sampradānakaḥ –
jñātaye haraye svīyam ahaṃtā-mamatāspadam |
sarvasvaṃ dīyate yena sa syāt tat-sampradānakaḥ ||33||
tad-dānaṃ prīti-pūjābhyāṃ bhaved ity uditaṃ dvidhā ||34||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When a person realizes the real nature of the Lord and gives to the Lord all his possessions which are sources of material identity and possessiveness, he is called tat-sampradānaka. There are two types: giving out of affection and giving as worship.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra prīti-dānam –
prīti-dānaṃ tu tasmai yad dadyād bandhv-ādi-rūpiṇe ||35||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Giving in affection:

What is given to the Lord as an affectionate friend is called prīti-dāna.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
cārcikyaṃ vaijayantīṃ paṭam uru-puraṭodbhāsuraṃ bhūṣaṇānāṃ
śreṇiṃ māṇikya-bhājaṃ gaja-ratha-turagān karburān karbureṇa |
dattvā rājyaṃ kuṭumbaṃ svam api bhagavate ditsur apy anyad uccair
deyaṃ kutrāpy adṛṣṭvā makha-sadasi tadā vyākulaḥ pāṇḍavo ’bhūt ||36||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: After completing all the ceremonial functions at the rājasūya sacrifice, Yudhiṣṭhira desired to give Kṛṣṇa sandalwood, a vaijayantī garland, ornaments with the best rubies and gold, elephants, chariots, and horses decked in gold, his family and himself. Not seeing anything else worthy of giving, he became afflicted.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pujā-dānaṃ –
pujā-dānaṃ tu tasmai yad vipra-rūpāya dīyate ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: What is given to the Lord Himself, or His form representing the devatās or the brāhmaṇas is called pujā-dāna.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā aṣṭame –
yajanti yajñaṃ kratubhir yam ādṛtā
bhavanta āmnāya-vidhāna-kovidāḥ |
sa eva viṣṇur varado 'stu vā paro
dāsyāmy amuṣmai kṣitim īpsitāṃ mune ||38||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From the Eighth Canto:

O great sage, great saintly persons like you, being completely aware of the Vedic principles for performing ritualistic ceremonies and yajñas, worship Lord Viṣṇu in all circumstances. Therefore, whether that same Lord Viṣṇu has come here to give me all benedictions or to punish me as an enemy, I must carry out His order and give Him the requested tract of land without hesitation. SB 8.20.11

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā vā daśa-rūpake –
lakṣmī-payodharotsaṅga-kuṅkumāruṇito hareḥ |
balinaiva sa yenāsya bhikṣā-pātrīkṛtaḥ karaḥ ||39||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
From Daśa-rūpaka:

Bali offered the ball of grains to the hand of the Lord which was colored with the kuṃkuma from the breast of Lakṣmī.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha upasthita-durāpārtha-tyāgī –
upasthita-durāpārtha-tyagy asau yena neṣyate |
hariṇā dīyamāno ’pi sārṣṭy-ādis tuṣyatā varaḥ ||40||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The person who does not want to accept even the five types of liberation offered by the Lord who is pleased with him is called the upasthita-durāpārtha-tyagī (one who is resolute to reject things rarely attained by others).[87]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pūrvato ’tra viparyasta-kārakatvaṃ dvayor bhavet |
asminn uddīpanāḥ kṛṣṇa-kṛpālāpa-smitādayaḥ ||41||
anubhāvās tad-utkarṣa-varṇana-draḍhimādayaḥ |
atra sañcāritā bhūmnā dhṛter eva samīkṣyate ||42||
