Seeing You, the ancient person, today, I have realized the bliss of impersonal brahman which arises in samādhi purified by the splendor of knowledge.[123]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
dvitīyaṃ, yathā –
yatra yatra viṣaye mama dṛṣṭis
taṃ tam eva kalayāmi bhavantam |
yan nirañjana parāvara-bījaṃ
tvāṃ vinā kim api nāparam asti ||6||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Contemplating oneness:

Whatever objects I see, I see as You alone, because there is nothing other than You as the cause of all causes and effect, O pure one!

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha prītoparasaḥ –
kṛṣṇasyāgre ’tidhārṣṭyena tad-bhakteṣv avahelayā |
svābhīṣṭa-devatānyatra paramotkarṣa-vīkṣayā |
maryādātikramādyaiś ca prītoparasatā matā ||7||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Prītoparasa (dāsyoparasa) arises by showing audacity in the presence of the Lord, disrespecting His devotees, seeing the devatās as more attractive than the Lord, and ignoring the rules.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyaṃ, yathā –
prathayan vapor vivaśatāṃ satāṃ kulair
avadhīryamāṇa-naṭano ’py anargalaḥ |
vikira prabho dṛśam ihety akuṇṭha-vāk
caṭulo baṭur vyavṛṇutātmano ratim ||8||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Showing audacity:

After showing off his lack of bodily control and being ignored by the devotees when he danced, a fickle brāhmaṇa boy without shame addressed the deity of Kṛṣṇa in a bold voice. “O Lord! Look at me!” In this way, he showed his love for the Lord.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha preya-uparasaḥ –
ekasminn eva sakhyena hari-mitrādy-avajñayā |
yuddha-bhūmādinā cāpi preyānuparaso bhavet ||9||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Preyānuparasa (sakhyoparasa) arises by one-sided friendship, disrespect for Kṛṣṇa’s friends and excessive fighting.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyaṃ, yathā –
suhṛd ity udito bhiyā cakampe
chalito narma-girā stutiṃ cakāra |
sa nṛpaḥ pariripsato bhujābhyāṃ
hariṇā daṇḍavad agrataḥ papāta ||10||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
One-sided friendship:

When Kṛṣṇa addressed a king as His friend, the King replied, “I am not qualified to be Your friend.” When Kṛṣṇa joked with him, the King began to recite prayers of praise to Him. When Kṛṣṇa embraced him, the king began offering his respects on the ground.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha vatsaloparasaḥ –
sāmarthyādhikyābhijñānāl lālanādy-aprayatnataḥ |
karuṇasyātirekādes turyāś coparaso bhavet ||11||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Vatsaloparasa arises from recognizing too much power in the Lord, from making no effort to affectionately care for the Lord as child, and from excessive suffering.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyaṃ, yathā –
mallānāṃ yad-avadhi parvatodbhaṭānām
unmāthaṃ sapadi tavātmajād apaśyam |
nodvegaṃ tad-avadhi yāmi jāmi tasmin
drāghiṣṭhām api samitiṃ prapadyamāne ||12||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Seeing too much power in the Lord:

O sister! Ever since the time that I saw your son kill the wrestlers as hard as mountains, I cannot be disturbed even if He engages in extended battles.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha śṛṅgāroparasaḥ | tatra sthāyi-vairūpyam –
dvayor ekatarasyaiva ratir yā khalu dṛśyate |
yānekatra tathaikasya sthāyinaḥ sā virūpatā |
vibhāvasyaiva vairūpyaṃ sthāyiny atropacaryate ||13||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
madhuroparasa:

The sthāyi-bhāva of madhura-rasa is distorted where the love between two persons is one-sided or where one of the persons has many lovers. The distortion of the vibhāva brings about a distortion in the sthāyi-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ekatra ratir, yathā lalita-mādhave –
manda-smitaṃ prakṛti-siddham api vyudantaṃ
saṅgopitaś ca sahajo ’pi dṛśos taraṅgaḥ |
dhūmāyite dvija-vadhū-madanārti-vahnāv
ahnāya kāpi gaitri aṅkuritām ayāsīt ||14||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
One-sided love, from Lalita-mādhava:

Though the fire of Cupid began to smolder in the brāhmaṇas’ wives, Kṛṣṇa avoided their spontaneous, sweet smiles. He hid the restless glances from His eyes. An indescribable state of peace appeared in Kṛṣṇa’s mind.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atyantābhāva evātra rateḥ khalu vivakṣitaḥ |
etasyāḥ prāg-abhāve tu śucir noparaso bhavet ||15||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
The intention of the previous verse is also to point out the temporary nature of the wives’ rati (atyanta abhāvaḥ). However, madhuroparasa does not arise here solely because the rati did not exist previously.

Though the fire of Cupid began to smolder in the brāhmaṇas’ wives, I suppressed my spontaneous, sweet smile and hid the restless glances in my eyes. An indescribable state of peace appeared in my mind.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
anekatra ratir, yathā –
gāndharvi kurvāṇam avekṣya līlām
agre dharaṇyāṃ sakhi kāma-pālam |
ākarṇayantī ca mukunda-veṇuṃ
bhinnādya sādhvi smarato dvidhāsi ||16||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Rati for several persons:

O Rādhā! O friend! O chaste woman! Seeing Balarāma perform pastimes on this earth, and hearing the sound of Mukunda’s flute, today you have been divided into two parts by Cupid.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
kecit tu nāyakasyāpi sarvathā tulya-rāgataḥ |
nāyikāsv apy anekāsu vadanty uparasaṃ śucim ||17||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Some say that madhuroparasa arises where the male lover does not distinguish between many types of women lovers and treats them all equally.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vibhāva-vairūpyam –
vaidagdhyaujjvalya-viraho vibhāvasya virūpatā |
latā-paśu-pulindīṣu vṛddhāsv api sa vartate ||18||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: There is irregularity in the vibhāva (āśrayas) where there is lack of cleverness, suitability, good dress and purity. This irregularity is present in creepers, animals, the Pulinda women and old women.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra latā, yathā –
sakhi madhu-kiratī niśamya vaṃśīṃ
madhu-mathanena kaṭākṣitātha mṛdvī |
mukula-pulakitā latāvalīyaṃ
ratim iha pallavitāṃ hṛdi vyanakti ||19||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Creepers:

The creepers which had become soft by the glance of Kṛṣṇa are oozing honey on hearing the sound of His flute. They reveal their love for Kṛṣṇa which has sprouted in their hearts by displaying goose bumps in the form of buds sprouting on their limbs.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
paśur, yathā –
paśyādbhutās tuṅga-madaḥ kuraṅgīḥ
pataṅga-kanyā-puline ’dya dhanyāḥ |
yāḥ keśavāṅge tad-apāṅga-pūtāḥ
sānaṅga-raṅgāṃ dṛśam arpayanti ||20||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Animals:

O friend! See these fortunate deer, experiencing extraordinary bliss on the bank of the Yamunā. Today, purified by the side glance of Kṛṣṇa, they are casting playful glances of conjugal love at His limbs.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
pulindī, yathā –
kālindī-puline paśya pulindī pulakācitā |
harer dṛk-cāpalaṃ vīkṣya sahajaṃ yā vighūrṇate ||21||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Pulinda women:

Look here! The Pulinda women, seeing the restless eyes of Hari on the bank of the Yamunā, are wandering about with their hairs standing on end.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
vṛddhā, yathā –
kajjalena kṛta-keśa-kālimā
bilva-yugma-racitonnata-stanī |
paśya gauri kiratī dṛg-añcalaṃ
smerayaty aghaharaṃ jaraty asau ||22||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Old women:

Look, Rādhā! This old woman has dyed her hair black using kajjala and has raised her breasts by inserting bilva fruits in her bodice. She makes Kṛṣṇa smile as she throws amorous glances at Him.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
sthāyino ’tra virūpatvam eka-rāgatayāpi cet |
ghaṭetāsau vibhāvasya virūpatve ’py udāhṛtiḥ ||23||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If there is irregularity in the sthāyi-bhāva because of one-sided love, then the example will also show irregularity in the vibhāva. It was previously said that irregularity in the vibhāva will produce irregularity in the sthāyi-bhāva.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
śucitvaujjvalya-vaidagdhyāt suveśatvāc ca kathyate |
śṛṅgārasya vibhāvatvam anyatrābhāsatā tataḥ ||24||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: It is said that proper condition for madhura-rasa arises from purity, appropriate form and species, cleverness in love and good dress. Lack of these qualities in the vibhāva creates ābhāsa in the vibhāva of madhura-rasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha anubhāva-vairūpyam –
samayānāṃ vyatikrāntir grāmyatvaṃ dhṛṣṭāpi ca |
vairūpyam anubhāvāder manīṣibhir udīritam ||25||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The wise say that irregularity in the anubhāva consists of transgressing the codes of conduct, vulgarity and audacity.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra samaya-vyatikrāntiḥ –
samayāḥ khaṇḍitādīnāṃ priye roṣoditādayaḥ |
puṃsaḥ smitādayaś cātra priyayā tāḍanādiṣu |
eteṣām anyathā-bhāvaḥ samayānāṃ vyatikramaḥ ||26||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The proper conduct for a woman who has been neglected by her lover is to show anger. The proper conduct of the man when hit by flowers thrown by his lover is to smile. For the man and woman to act otherwise is called transgression of normal conduct.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyaṃ, yathā –
kāntā-nakhāndhito ’py adya parihṛtya hare hriyam |
kailāsa-vāsinīṃ dāsīṃ kṛpā-dṛṣṭyā bhajasva mām ||27||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Improper conduct:

O lord! Though you are marked by the nails of other women lovers, I give up all shyness, and request that you please accept me, who am Your servant residing on Mount Kailāsa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha grāmyatvam –
bāla-śabdādy-upanyāso virasokti-prapañcanam |
kaṭī-kaṇḍūtir ity ādyaṃ grāmyatvaṃ kathitaṃ budhaiḥ ||28||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Vulgarity:

Using unsuitable words such as bāla (child), using disagreeable language, and scratching the buttocks are called vulgarity by the wise.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra ādyaṃ, yathā –
kiṃ naḥ phaṇi-kiśorīṇāṃ tvaṃ puṣkara-sadāṃ sadā |
muralī-dhvaninā nīvīṃ gopa-bāla vilumpasi ||29||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Unsuitable words:

O baby cowherd! How is it that you loosen the belts of us young snake girls living in the lake of Kāliya, by the sound of Your flute?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha dhṛṣṭatā –
prakaṭa-prārthanādiḥ syāt sambhogādes tu dhṛṣṭatā ||30||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
Audacity:

To request for enjoyment with Kṛṣṇa overtly is called boldness.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
yathā –
kānta kailāsa-kuñjo ’yaṃ ramyāhaṃ nava-yauvanā |
tvaṃ vidagdho ’si govinda kiṃ vā vācyam ataḥ param ||31||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: O my lover! O Govinda! Living in bower on Mount Kailāsa, I am of the late kaiśora age and attractive, and You are clever in love affairs. What more can be said?

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
evam eva tu gauṇānāṃ hāsādīnām api svayam |
vijñeyoparasatvasya manīṣibhir udāhṛtiḥ ||32||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In a similar manner, the wise are able to understand uparasas of the secondary rasas such as hāsya.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha anurasāḥ –
bhaktādibhir vibhāvādyaiḥ kṛṣṇa-sambandha-varjitaiḥ |
rasā hāsyādayaḥ sapta śāntaś cānurasā matāḥ ||33||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If the devotees as ālambanas and other elements are devoid of a relationship with Kṛṣṇa when expressing a certain rasa, then anurasa arises for the seven secondary rasas and śānta-rasa.[126]

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tatra hāsyānurasaḥ –
tāṇḍavaṃ vyadhita hanta kakkhaṭī
markaṭī bhrū-kuṭībhis tathoddhuram |
yena vallava-kadambakaṃ babhau
hāsa-ḍambara-karambitānanam ||34||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: When the female monkey Kakkhaṭī moved her eyebrows, and danced furiously, all the cowherd boys laughed loudly.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha adbhutānurasaḥ –
bhāṇḍīra-kakṣe bahudhā vitaṇḍāṃ
vedānta-tantre śuka-maṇḍalasya |
ākarṇayan nirnimiṣākṣi-pakṣmā
romāñcitāṅgaś ca surarṣir āsīt ||35||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: Hearing the prolonged debate among the parrots on Vedānta in creepers climbing the Bhāṇdira tree, Nārada eyes stopped blinking in wonder, and his hairs stood on end.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration: evam evātra vijñeyā vīrāder apy udāhṛtiḥ ||36||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In the same way, anurasa for vīra and the other secondary rasas should be understood.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
aṣṭāv amī taṭastheṣu prākaṭyaṃ yadi bibhrati |
kṛṣṇādibhir vibhāvādyair tadāpy anurasā matāḥ ||37||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If the seven secondary rasas or śānta-rasa appear in neutral devotees[128] with Kṛṣṇa as the vibhāva etc. it is still considered anurasa.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
atha aparasāḥ –
kṛṣṇa-tat-pratipakṣaś ced viṣayāśrayatāṃ gatāḥ |
hāsādīnāṃ tadā te ’tra prājñair aparasā matāḥ ||38||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: If Kṛṣṇa becomes the viṣaya and His enemies become the āśraya of hāsya and other secondary rasas,[129] it is called aparasa by the wise.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
evam anye ’pi vijñeyās te ’dbhutāparasādayaḥ |
uttamās tu rasābhāsāḥ kaiścid rasatayoditāḥ ||40||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: In a similar way, one should understand examples of aparasa for adbhūta and other rasas. Some persons consider superior rasābhāsa (uparasa) to be rasa, because it has a relishable nature.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
tathā hi –
bhāvāḥ sarve tad-ābhāsā rasābhāsāś ca kecana |
amī prokta-rasābhijñaiḥ sarve ’pi rasanād rasāḥ ||41||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation:
For instance, it is said:

Some experts in rasa say that all bhāvas, even bhāvābhāsas and rasābhāsas are rasa since they are enjoyable.

bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.transliteration:
bhāratādyāś catasras tu rasāvasthāna-sūcikāḥ |
vṛttayo nāṭya-mātṛtvād uktā nāṭaka-lakṣaṇe ||42||
bhakti_rasamrta_sindhu.translation: The four styles[130] such as bharatī which indicate a condition of rasa in speech and drama have been explained in the work Nāṭaka-candrikā, since they are suitable only for dramas.
